explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-9, Snap. Replay will void the play entirely and go to the Two-Minute Timeout, treated the same as the end of a period. Replay communicates with the TV truck for the embedded clock, or uses the score bug clock if none is available. This process applies regardless of the outcome of the play run after the clock hit 2:00.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Returner B3 fields the kick at the B-7 and runs to the B-45 where he is tackled, and the ball comes loose. The on-field crew rules the runner down. There is a flag down for offside on Team A. Replays show B3 lost possession before he was down and A36 recovered at the B-42.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Whether B3 was down or fumbled is reviewable. Since the on-field ruling would allow Team B to tack on the five-yard offside penalty and get the ball at the 50, but overturning the call on the field means Team A recovered the fumble, the offside penalty forces Team A to re-kick.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A98's punt is first touched by A80 at the B-25, then picked up by B40 at the B-20, who runs 10 yards and fumbles. B70 holds at the B-40 during B40's run. A20 picks up the fumble at the B-30 and runs for a touchdown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. The score by A20 does not count; five and ten-yard penalties are not administered on the try or the succeeding kickoff. Because the illegal touching provides an enforcement spot, the penalty for Team B's holding foul may be enforced. Team A accepts the penalty, giving Team B the ball at the B-20 after enforcement from the end of B40's run.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. It is late in the fourth quarter and Team A leads by 3 points. B44 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-20 and carries the ball out of bounds at the B-30. B79 was in the neutral zone at the snap. When the game clock is stopped it reads 2:02.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-35, Ready. Team A will accept the penalty for B79's offside foul and retain possession of the ball. The game clock starts on the Referee's signal, because Team B will not next snap the ball.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. It is late in the fourth quarter after the Two-Minute Timeout and Team B leads by 3 points. B44 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-20 and carries the ball out of bounds at the B-30. B79 was in the neutral zone at the snap. When the game clock is stopped it reads 1:25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-35, Snap. Team A will accept the penalty for B79's offside foul and retain possession of the ball. The game clock starts on the snap, at the option of Team A, since the foul is by the team ahead in the score after the Two-Minute Timeout.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. A12's legal forward pass is toward A80 who is airborne near the sideline at the 50. When he receives the pass, he comes to the ground facing the field of play, his toe touches the ground inbounds and then his heel comes down on the sideline in a continuous motion. He maintains firm control of the ball as he goes to the ground at that point.
choice_c: A, 4/10, A-30, Snap. Illegal Touching penalty enforced.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, A-30, Snap. This is an incomplete pass. The continuous toe-heel touching is part of a single process, and by interpretation he has landed out of bounds, so he has not executed a catch.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-5. A98's scrimmage kick is partially blocked and lands at the A-8, where B21 muffs the ball and it rebounds back into Team A's endzone. A98 picks up the ball in the endzone and runs out to the A-15 where he steps out of bounds.
choice_a: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. The ball is dead where A98 recovers in the end zone, and there can be no advance. The result of the play is a safety.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-5. A98's scrimmage kick is partially blocked and lands at the A-4, where B21 muffs the ball and it rebounds back into Team A's endzone. A98 picks up the ball in the endzone and runs out to the A-15 where he steps out of bounds.
choice_a: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-15, Snap. Since the kick did not cross the neutral zone, it remains in play and players of either team may recover and advance. The clock starts on the snap after a legal kick down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A98's punt is rolling along the ground near players A44 and B27 who are engaged. The ball bounces against B27's leg at the B-25 and is then recovered by A55 at the B-28.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-28, Snap. The ball rolled into B27's leg, but A44's contact did not cause him to touch the ball, so this is not forced touching. The recovery by A55 is legal since B27 touched the ball beyond the neutral zone, restoring recovery eligibility to either team.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A98's punt is rolling along the ground near players A44 and B27 who are engaged. A44 blocks B27 into the ball, and the ball bounces against B27's leg at the B-25 and is then recovered by A55 at the B-28.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-28, Snap. When B27 touched the ball, it is considered forced touching because the block by A44 caused B27 to touch it. Since A55 recovered the scrimmage kick that had crossed the neutral zone at the B-28, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at that spot.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-28 and is hit by B6 knocking him to the ground. The Back Judge throws a flag and after the play the Referee announces, "Personal foul, targeting." Replays show B6 lead with his right shoulder to attack with forcible contact to the side of A80's head. The contact occurred after A80 completed the catch, but before he had time to clearly protect himself.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B6, Ready. The targeting foul is reviewable, and replay must confirm all aspects to enforce the penalty. Although A80 completed the catch, he is still defenseless until he clearly has time to protect himself. B6 led with the shoulder and attacked with forcible contact to the head/neck, so this is targeting.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. B13 catches A1's punt at the B-20 and returns it to the 50 where he is pushed out of bounds. A56 is on the ground at the end of the play after contact with B8 at the B-35 during the return. There is a flag down and the Referee announces, "Personal foul, illegal blindside block, with targeting." Replays show B8 approaching A56 clearly from the front and making forcible contact with his shoulder to A56's head.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. The targeting aspect of the play is reviewable. Since B8 did not approach from the blindside, A56 is not considered defenseless and only the crown-of-helmet standard applies. The contact with the shoulder is not targeting, so B8 remains in the game. The illegal blindside block foul itself is not reviewable and is enforced from the spot of the foul.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-5. A12's backward pass in flight is batted by B55 at the A-2, and the ball goes out of bounds behind Team A's goal line.
choice_a: No foul, no score. A, 1/10, A-2, Snap.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for team B. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. There is no foul by B55. A pass in flight, forward or backward, may be batted in any direction by any player eligible to touch the ball, except that a backward pass in flight may not be batted forward by the passing team. The impetus is charged to Team A's pass, resulting in a safety.

question_text: Try @ B-3. B21 intercepts A12's legal forward pass in the end zone and runs it back to midfield where B21 is downed. During the return of the interception, B50 clips in Team B's end zone.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The penalty for a foul on a try after Team B gains possession is declined by rule and the try is simply over. Team A will kick off.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A98 punts and the ball lands at the B-30 and bounces toward receiver B21. At the B-25, the ball bounces by B21 and touches the towel hanging down from his waistline. A80 recovers the ball at the B-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Since the ball has crossed the neutral zone and been touched by B21 (a uniform attachment counts as part of the player), any player may catch or recover the ball. Team A's recovery is legal.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air and A80 catches the ball as he crosses in front of A12 and A80 turns up-field and is out of bounds at the A-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The short flip of the ball by A12 is considered a legal forward pass unless it is clearly and obviously thrown backward. It is a completed forward pass to A80, and the run up-field results in a 1st down for the offense.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air and A80 muffs the ball and the ball drops to the ground. A12 recovers at the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-25, Snap. The short flip of the ball by A12 is considered a legal forward pass unless it is clearly and obviously thrown backward. When the ball strikes the ground, it is ruled an incomplete forward pass and the play is dead.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Early in the 4th quarter, back A21 takes a handoff and runs for a 15-yard gain and is down inbounds. During the play, A88 is guilty of holding at the A-30, and B54 is injured on the play and down on the field requiring assistance from the medical staff. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/15, A-20, Snap. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-25, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
choice_d: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds. The holding foul by A88 is penalized 10 yards from the spot of the foul, the A-30, and repeats 1st down. The play clock is set to 40, and both the play clock and game clock start on the Referee's signal after the injury timeout.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Late in the 4th quarter, back A21 takes a handoff and runs for a 15-yard gain and is down inbounds. During the play, A88 is guilty of holding at the A-30, and B54's helmet comes off during the play (not a direct result of a foul). The game clock is stopped at 1:59 after the play. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/15, A-20, Snap. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
choice_b: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: A, 1/15, A-20, Snap. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: A, 1/10, A-25, Snap. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, A-20, Snap. Play clock set to 25 seconds. The holding foul by A88 is penalized 10 yards from the spot of the foul, the A-30, and repeats 1st down. Since the stoppage is for a Team B helmet off near the Two-Minute Timeout, the play clock is set to 25 and the game clock starts on the snap.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B21 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B21 catches the kick right at the goal line and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The "T signal", if executed properly, is not considered a valid or invalid fair catch signal and is not a signal at all from a rules perspective. It does not cause the ball to become dead and does not deprive the receiving team the opportunity to advance the ball.

tags: instant replay;illegal forward pass;pass interference;line of scrimmage;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week6;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QB A1 scrambles to the B-40 and throws a forward pass to receiver A80 who catches it at the B-30 and runs to the B-20 where he is tackled. There are two flags on the play and after a discussion the Referee announces there is no foul for offensive pass interference because there is a foul for an illegal forward pass from beyond the line of scrimmage and that penalty will be enforced.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, A-45, Ready. The illegal forward pass is reviewable, and since the pass was thrown from the line of scrimmage, it is legal. The OPI penalty is then enforced instead, since that rule applies when a legal forward pass crosses the neutral zone.

tags: coach's challenge;out of bounds spot;line to gain;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week6;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/8 @ B-48. QB A1 scrambles toward the sideline and runs toward the line to gain and he is marked out of bounds at the B-41. Replays show A1 stepped out of bounds at the B-44 and the Team B head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling.
choice_c: A, 4/4, B-44, Ready. Team B loses their timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/4, B-44, Ready. Whether a player stepped out of bounds prior to where the officials spotted the ball is reviewable regardless of the line to gain. Team B wins the challenge and keeps their timeout.

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. With the game clock running, snapper A55 lifts the ball before passing it backward, B50 bats the ball away, and B55 recovers the ball at the A-38.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-35, Ready. The action by A55 is an illegal snap and the ball remains dead. The penalty is five yards from the succeeding spot, and Team A retains possession after the penalty.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. With the game clock running before the snap, B97 crosses the neutral zone and, without making contact, continues his charge behind the Team A linemen and directly toward the quarterback. A12 takes the snap and is tackled for a 3-yard loss.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, A-40, Ready. No Team B player may cross the neutral zone and, without making contact, continue their charge toward any back. This is a dead ball foul, so officials shut the play down and penalize Team B 5 yards for offside rather than taking the result of the live play.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A lines up for a two-point try attempt and Team B has eleven players in its defensive formation. The 12th Team B player runs onto the field just before the ball is snapped. A22 takes the handoff from the quarterback and is tackled at the one-yard line.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-1 1/2, No clock. The action by the 12th Team B player is a live-ball foul for illegal substitution. Team A accepts the penalty; Team B is penalized half the distance to the goal, and the try is repeated at the B-1 1/2.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A lines up for a two-point try attempt and Team B has eleven players in its defensive formation. The 12th Team B player runs onto the field just before the ball is snapped. A22 takes the handoff from the quarterback and carries the ball into the end zone.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, Snap.
choice_d: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The action by the 12th Team B player is a live-ball foul for illegal substitution. Team A declines the penalty since the try is successful, and the penalty does not carry over to the kickoff.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. With the game clock running, Team B has 12 players in the formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field. The ball is ready for play, the game clock is running, both teams are in formation and the snap is imminent. Quarterback A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense and calls timeout.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. Team A may keep their time out.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. Team A's timeout is charged.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-30, Snap. Team A may keep their time out.
choice_d: A, 2/5, B-35, Ready. Team A may keep their time out.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. Team A may keep their time out. When the deep officials count 12 Team B players, both teams are in formation, no Team B player is attempting to leave the field, and the snap is imminent, the crew will offer Team A their timeout back and penalize Team B for a substitution foul.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A21 is offside and the kick goes out of bounds at the B-15 after it has been touched by B44 at the B-25.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules -- or -- B, 1/10, B-20, Snap.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules -- or -- B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. For A21's offside foul, Team B may elect to have the kick repeated at Team A's 30-yard line, or snap the ball at the inbounds spot plus five yards from where the ball went out of bounds.

question_text: 3/20 @ B-40. A12 throws a legal forward pass toward A88. During the pass defensive end B90 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct. The pass is complete for a 10-yard gain to the B-30 where A88 is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Team B unsportsmanlike conduct fouls occurring during a legal forward pass play are enforced from the end of the last run when the run ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of possession during the down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Early in the 4th quarter, receiver A83 has just leaped and received a forward pass. As A83 is about to regain his balance, B45 launches and attacks into A83's head with his shoulder at the A-47. A83 completes the catch and is down at the 50.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. Disqualify B45. The action by B45 is targeting - initiating contact with a defenseless opponent above the shoulders. B45 is disqualified for the remainder of the game and the first half of the next game.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, A1's low pass to A80 is ruled incomplete in the field of play at the B-31 (1:30). Receiver A80 is injured on the play and the training staff comes out to attend to him. Replays show A80 gained firm control of the ball before it touched the ground, and he maintained control throughout the catch process.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/1, B-31, 1:30, Ready. Whether the pass is complete or incomplete is reviewable. A80 did complete the process, so the call is overturned to a catch. The overturn results in a running clock, but since there is also an injury, there is no 10-second runoff.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, the score is tied. Back A2 runs to the B-32 where he is hit and knocked to the ground in bounds by B14 (0:50). There are no flags on the play. Replay stops the game to review potential targeting by B14 (0:43). Replays show B14 launch with his head down and make forcible contact with the crown of the helmet to the side of A2's head.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-32, 0:50, Snap or Ready at Team A's option.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-17, 0:50, Snap or Ready at Team A's option.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-17, 0:43, Snap or Ready at Team A's option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-17, 0:50, Snap or Ready at Team A's option. The hit by B14 is reviewable for targeting. The elements of targeting are present, so replay adds the foul and enforces the 15-yard penalty from the end of the run. The overturn creates a stopped clock, reset to the time of the foul, and Team A as the offended team has the option to go on the snap or the ready for play.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field.
choice_a: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/5, B-20, Ready - reset the game clock to 0:12.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12. This is a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot. At the option of Team A, the game clock is reset to 0:12 and starts on the snap.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense had 12 players on the field at the snap but B21 was hustling to get off the field and the ball was snapped just before B21 exited the field.
choice_a: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap. This is still a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction, penalized 5 yards from the previous spot. But since B21 had no influence on the play, there is no clock adjustment this time.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and runs for 10 yards and is downed inbounds and the clock is stopped with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field.
choice_a: A, 1/15, B-10, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready - reset the game clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:12. This is a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction, but there is no requirement to accept the penalty in order to have the clock reset. The offense may decline the 5-yard penalty and still keep the option to reset the game clock to 0:12, starting on the next snap.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-22. Team A is trailing by nine points with 0:35 showing on the game clock. At the snap, B21, B40 and B44 blatantly hold, wrapping both arms around Team A's wideouts and take them to the ground. Quarterback A12 has no receiver in the route, scrambles and then legally throws the ball away. After the play, the game clock reads 0:26. The back judge, field judge and side judge have a flag down for Team B holding on each of their initial responsibilities.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-17, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:35.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-11, Ready - reset the game clock to 0:35.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-11, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-11, Snap - reset the game clock to 0:35. This is a blatant and obvious unfair act designed to take time off the clock. The Referee converts the holding fouls to an unsportsmanlike conduct foul, penalized half the distance to the goal. The game clock is reset to 0:35 and starts on the next snap. B21, B40 and B44 each have one unsportsmanlike foul counter.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. B33, defending against a legal forward pass at the B-15, has his back to the ball and is waving his arms in the face of eligible A88, but does not make contact. The pass falls incomplete.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Pass Interference on B33.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-40, Snap. No foul by B33. Physical contact is required to establish pass interference.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-8. Ball carrier A21, advancing in the field of play, becomes airborne at the two-yard line. His first contact with the ground is out of bounds three yards beyond the goal line. The ball, in possession of the ball carrier, passes over the pylon.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The plane of the goal line extends between and includes the pylons. The ball in possession of A21 breaks the plane of the goal line, so it is a touchdown even though he lands out of bounds beyond it.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Midway through the 2nd quarter, A44 runs and gains 10 yards before stepping out of bounds. During the down B55's helmet comes off without a helmet foul by Team A. B55 immediately picks up his helmet, puts it on and continues to chase A44 and is beside him when A44 runs out of bounds.
choice_d: A, 1/10, 50, Snap. No foul, B55 may remain in the game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Ready. This is a personal foul by B55 for continuing to participate after losing his helmet. The clock stops at the end of the down, and B55 must leave the game for the next down. The play clock is set to 40 seconds and the game clock starts on the Referee's signal.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98 is prepared to kick off. The ball is on the tee and the referee has signaled it ready for play. As the kicker approaches the ball it begins to roll off the tee just as he starts his kicking motion. The kicker follows through and kicks the ball as it continues to roll off or near the tee. The ball squibs along the ground and is recovered by A21 at the A-45.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. There is no foul, and the ball never becomes alive since it was not kicked from a stationary position on the tee as intended. Officials should shut the play down as soon as possible, and the teams line up for a new kickoff.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Team A trails 31-27 and has one timeout remaining late in the 4th quarter (1:30). QB A1's pass to A80 at the B-30 hits the ground and is incomplete (1:26). There are no flags on the play. Replays show Team B had 12 players on the field participating during the down.
choice_a: A, 3/5, B-35, Snap - Reset clock to 1:30 at option of offended team.
choice_c: A, 3/10, B-30, Snap - Reset clock to 1:30 at option of offended team.
choice_d: A, 3/5, B-35, Ready - Reset clock to 1:30 at option of offended team.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-35, Snap - Reset clock to 1:30 at option of offended team. The number of players on the field during a live ball is reviewable. Since Team B played with 12 (the 12th player was not attempting to leave the field), Team A has the option to reset the clock to where it was at the snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-40. Late in the 1st quarter Team A snaps the ball and A2 runs to the A-45 where B4 tackles him by the facemask. The Umpire throws a flag for the personal foul. The score bug shows the game clock was clearly at 0:00 before the snap.
choice_d: 1st quarter over, no penalty carries to 2nd quarter. A, 1/10, A-40, Snap, 2nd quarter 15:00.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Snap, 2nd quarter 15:00. Whether time expired prior to the snap is reviewable, and replay will communicate to the on-field crew to void the play. However, the personal foul cannot be ignored; it is enforced from the line of scrimmage as a dead ball foul carried into the next quarter.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 4/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A98's field goal attempt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A88 recovers the ball at the B-28 and runs for a touchdown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-3, No Clock. A88's advance is legal since the kick never crossed the neutral zone, so Team A scores a touchdown. Team B begins its possession series at the 25-yard line after the try.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/1 @ B-6. During the first possession series of the first extra period, the offense did not score. In the second possession series, Team A lines up to try a short field goal on 3rd down. The attempt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A23 recovers the ball at the B-10 and is tackled at the B-7.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/2, B-7, No clock. The kick did not cross the neutral zone, so A23's advance is legal. Since it was 3rd down, Team A keeps the ball for the next down, which is 4th down.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
tags: extra period;late hit;change of possession;line to gain;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week9;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/20 @ B-35. During the first possession series of the first extra period, the offense goes for it on 4th down. A12 completes a pass to A88 at the B-20 where he is legally hit and knocked out of bounds. B54 crosses the boundary, and hits A88 clearly after the play is over.
choice_a: B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No clock. New possession series for Team B.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-20, No clock. Team A retains possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No clock. New possession series for Team B. A88 is stopped short of the line to gain, so Team A's possession series is over. Team B starts its possession series after enforcement of the 15-yard personal foul penalty from the 25-yard line, giving them 1st and 10 at the 40.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is late in the half and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team A head coach requests a timeout, and the timeout is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation and the ball is ready for play. Just before the play clock hits 0, the Team A head coach requests a timeout.
choice_a: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Team A charged a 2nd timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. The officials will not acknowledge the second timeout request by the Team A head coach. The delay of game penalty against Team A is enforced instead. Team A has 2 timeouts remaining.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. Late in the 4th quarter with the score tied and the game clock running, B77 is in the neutral zone at the snap, but does not make contact. QB A12 rolls out to pass, runs to the B-17 and throws a forward pass, which falls incomplete. The game clock is stopped with 15 seconds remaining.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-20, Snap. A12's illegal forward pass and the offside by B77 create offsetting fouls. There is no 10-second runoff because the fouls offset. The game clock remains at 15 seconds and starts on the snap.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-45. A98 punts and the kick is partially blocked and goes out of bounds at the A-46. At the snap, Team A has five players in the backfield.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-41, Snap - or - A, 4/12, A-40, Snap. Team A has committed a foul for illegal formation. Team B may have the ball, first and 10 at the A-41 after the five-yard penalty is enforced from the A-46, the dead-ball spot, or have the penalty enforced at the previous spot with fourth down repeated at the A-40.

question_text: 4/6 @ A-24. Team A is in scrimmage kick formation and punts. B50 is lined up inside the frame of the snapper and is within one yard of the line of scrimmage. The punt rolls out of bounds at the B-40.
choice_c: A, 4/1, A-29, Ready - or - B, 1/10, B-40, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, A-29, Snap - or - B, 1/10, B-40, Snap. B50 commits a live ball foul by lining up within 1 yard of the line of scrimmage inside the frame of the snapper. Team A may accept the penalty, keep the ball, and have 4th and 1, or decline the penalty and take the result of the play, with Team B taking the ball at the B-40.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, Team A has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws a legal forward pass to A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete at the B-31. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31. After the ball is ready at the B-31, Team A breaks the huddle late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach attempts to call a timeout just before the play clock displays zero.
choice_c: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A charged a 2nd timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A commits a dead-ball foul for delay of game as the play clock expires. Team A had 4/1 @ B-31 after the overturn, then 4/6 @ B-36 following the delay of game penalty. Consecutive called team timeouts are not allowed in any individual dead ball period, so the second timeout request is not honored. Team A retains their challenge and their timeout.

tags: instant replay;catch process;down by rule;line to gain;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week9;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ 50. With 3:03 remaining in the 4th quarter, the score is tied at 24. A80 controls A1's forward pass at the B-42, gets his right foot down, and as he goes to the ground, he extends the ball toward the line to gain. The ball comes loose and goes out of bounds at the B-46. The officials rule fumble. Replays show A80's left knee down before he lost control, and the ball at the B-41 at that point.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-41, Ready. Catch/no catch, fumble/down by rule, and the line to gain are all reviewable aspects of the play. A80 completed the catch since he got control, a body part down, and performed an act common to the game. Even though the line to gain was not made, the 5-yard difference is significant in this situation, so it is placed at the spot of the knee-down.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-30. QB A1 under heavy pressure scrambles to the numbers and throws a pass from the A-21 that lands near the line of scrimmage with no eligible receivers in the area. The Line Judge confers with the Referee and the Referee drops a flag for intentional grounding. The line feed shows the ball hit the ground at the A-31.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, A-30, Snap. Where the ball touched the ground in relation to the line of scrimmage is reviewable. Replay uses O2O to inform the Referee the ball made it to the line of scrimmage, so the pass is legal and the flag is picked up.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. A12's entire body and the ball is beyond the neutral zone when he throws a pass downfield and B75 is in the neutral zone at the snap. B21 intercepts the pass at the B-10, and B55 blocks below the waist at the B-20 during the return. B21 is tackled at the 50.
choice_b: A, 2/10, 50, Ready. Team B declines and keeps the interception.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-40, Ready. There is no option; the fouls offset and the down is replayed. Team B may not decline offsetting fouls here because it had fouled before getting possession of the ball.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. During the kick, B50 holds at the B-30 and the untouched kick goes out of bounds at the B-3.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-40, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The teams commit offsetting fouls (illegal kick out of bounds and holding). There is no option, and Team A re-kicks at the previous spot.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. B44 receives the kick at the B-5 and returns the ball for a touchdown. Substitutes B21, B80, and B88 leave the Team B team area, and with no impact on the play and no intention of entering the game, run to the end zone to congratulate B44.
choice_c: B, Try, A-18, No Clock - or - B, Try, A-3, No Clock & Free Kick @ 20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, Try, A-18, No Clock - or - B, Try, A-3, No Clock & Free Kick @ 20. B21, B80 and B88 have committed a foul for Unsportsmanlike Conduct. The touchdown counts; the 15-yard penalty is enforced on the try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods, at Team A's choice.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. A86 is legally blocked out of bounds by B18 at Team B's two-yard line. A86, while attempting an immediate return to the field of play, leaps from out of bounds and is airborne as he receives A16's legal forward pass. He lands in Team B's end zone with the ball firmly in his grasp.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-15, Snap. The pass is incomplete. A86 did not lose his eligibility when legally blocked out of bounds, but he must establish inbounds before touching the pass. Since he did not, the ball is out of bounds when he touches it.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A98's kick is blocked by B55, and B80 recovers the ball at the B-10 and advances across Team A's goal line. During B80's run, B21 blocks A98 below the waist at the B-10.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. Penalty carries to the kickoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The score by B80 is cancelled, the try is ended, and the penalty is declined by rule. There is no carryover of the personal foul by B21 since it was not flagrant.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-6. A98's punt is fair caught at the B-40. Team A is flagged for having two players wearing Number 11.
choice_c: A, 4/13, A-3, Ready - or - B, 1/10, B-45, Snap.
choice_d: A, 4/8, A-8, Snap - or - B, 1/10, B-45, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/13, A-3, Snap - or - B, 1/10, B-45, Snap. The penalty for two players wearing identical numbers may be enforced either 5 yards from the previous spot or 5 yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (the succeeding spot), at the option of Team B.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-6. Ball carrier A21, advancing toward Team B's goal line, fumbles when B55 bats the ball forward from A21's hand. A21 loses possession at the B-2, and the ball goes into Team B's end zone, where B44 recovers.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Even though B55 bats the ball forward into the end zone, the impetus for the ball being there is charged to A21's fumble, since A21 was in possession at the time B55 contacted the ball. The result is a touchback.

tags: roughing the passer;low hit;passing posture;spot of enforcement;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week10;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Quarterback A11 drops back in the pocket and sets up to pass. He then scrambles to his right, sets up again at the A-20 and is in a passing posture when he is hit by end B98 who drives his shoulder into A11's knee. A11 then pulls the ball in and goes to the ground because of being tackled by B88 at the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The contact into the knee area is a foul by B98, since A11's vulnerability as a potential passer in a passing posture is protected even though he did not release the ball. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot.

tags: instant replay;clock correction;end of half;field position guideline;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz;2024-cfo-weekly-quiz-week10;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the 2nd quarter Team A kicks off and B12 catches the ball at the B-5. He runs to the B-20 where he is tackled, and time expires. The game clock was at 0:01 when B12's right knee was down.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-20, No Clock. Untimed down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Half over. Although there was time remaining when the returner was down, replay will not reset the clock in this situation due to field position. The guideline is the 40-yard line of the team putting the ball in play; the ball would be snapped well short of that, so the half stands as over.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline at the B-30 and runs for a touchdown. The FJ has a hat off, but no flag. The officials get together and the Referee announces the ruling on the field is the receiver was forced out of bounds and came back in legally to catch the pass - TD. Replays show the receiver did step out of bounds and re-establish before catching the pass, but there was no contact by a defensive player at any point prior to him stepping out.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, 50, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, 50, Snap. Whether the receiver stepped out of bounds is reviewable. Replay can look at the defensive contact, and since there was no contact, replay adds a foul for illegal touching, overturning the touchdown.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. The offense substitutes in their backup QB, A7 and he lines up in the backfield with A12 who is the starting QB. The ball is snapped to A12, he fakes to A7 and then throws a pass to A88 who is in the end zone and makes the catch. The CJ notices that both A12 and A7 are wearing green dot helmets.
choice_b: Touchdown stands. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-10, Snap. Each team may have only 1 player on the field wearing a green dot helmet. This live ball equipment violation is enforced 5 yards from the previous spot, nullifying the touchdown, and the clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a touchdown.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A and Team B line up in position and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. The high short kick is fair caught by B21 at the B-23.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. There is no foul on the play. On free kick downs, the coach-to-player communications limit is not in effect. The fair catch allows Team B to put the ball in play at the B-25.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A98 punts and the kick is untouched beyond the neutral zone when A88 reaches across Team B's goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play. B22 recovers at the B-2 and advances to the B-12 where A66 tackles him by pulling his facemask.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-27, Snap. No foul by A88 for batting the ball in the end zone; this is illegal touching. Team B may accept the penalty for the facemask foul, which cancels the illegal touching privilege, giving them a first down after the 15-yard penalty enforced at the B-12.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-18. A98's punt is illegally touched by A47 at the A-48. B7 bats the loose ball forward from the 50 to the A-43 where A39 falls on it.
choice_d: B, 1/10, A-48, Ready - or - A, 4/5, A-28, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-48, Snap - or - A, 4/5, A-28, Snap. Team A may decline the penalty for Team B's illegal bat, which would allow Team B to take the ball at the spot of illegal touching. If Team A accepts the penalty instead, it is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot; postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply since Team A is in legal possession at the end of the down.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. A12 throws a pass to A88 at the B-30 who makes the catch while grounded. Ineligible lineman A70 runs more than three yards beyond the neutral zone and does not make contact with an opponent. He circles toward the flank and returns back across the neutral zone before A12's pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, A-45, Ready. No originally ineligible receiver shall be or "have been" more than three yards beyond the neutral zone until the pass is thrown. A70 is illegally downfield even though he returned before the pass. The penalty is enforced 5 yards from the previous spot, and the down is repeated.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Defender B21 is in press coverage, and at the snap strikes wideout A88 in the face mask at the line of scrimmage. A88 is initially disrupted from running the route. QB A12 is sacked for a 7-yard loss.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The action by B21 is a personal foul for striking A88; continuous contact is not required to be a foul. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot. B21 could be ejected only if the contact is viewed as flagrant.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, quarterback A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-35 that falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball at the B-30 in an attempt to catch it. There was no eligible receiver in the area. The clock shows 1:01 after the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. A10 commits a foul for intentional grounding; there is no foul for illegal touching since the pass is illegal. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass. Since this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, the 10-second runoff rule applies.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards and is tackled short of the line to gain. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54's helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. Play clock?
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap. The game clock is stopped at 1:58 for the Two-Minute Timeout and for B54's helmet being off. Even with a Team B helmet off, the play clock is set to 25 seconds following the Two-Minute Timeout, and the game clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-20. QB A1 scrambles out of the pocket and runs down the sideline. He is hit near the goal line causing the ball to come loose and go out of bounds beyond the sideline. The Side Judge rules TD. Replays show the ball came loose at the B-1, but there is no video evidence of whether the ball crossed inside/over or outside the pylon.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, B-1, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-1, Ready. The ball's relation to the goal line and pylon is reviewable. It is clear the ball was fumbled short of the line, so replay overturns the touchdown, but without video evidence of the ball's relation to the pylon, it is placed at the spot of the fumble.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A1's punt lands at the B-25 where it hits B3 in the leg and is then recovered by A14 at the B-23. The officials huddle before the Referee announces B3 was passive and blocked into the football, so it is Team B's ball. Replays show B3 was actively blocking when the ball touched his leg.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-23, Snap. Whether B3 touched the ball or not is reviewable, but whether he was forced into the ball is not. Replay will not stop the game but should communicate to the TV truck that the contact determination is not reviewable.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-35. A98's punt travels seven yards beyond the neutral zone where B33 touches the ball. The ball then rebounds behind the neutral zone, where A21 recovers it at the A-30 and is downed.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-30, Snap. The ball is dead when recovered by A21 and may not be advanced. Since the ball was touched beyond the neutral zone by B33, the ball remains alive and the recovery by A21 is legal, resulting in a first down for Team A.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. A12 throws a legal forward pass to airborne A88 who gains firm control of the ball one yard within the opponent's end zone. As A88 gains firm control of the ball he is contacted by B21 and A88 first comes to the ground on his feet at the one-yard line and maintains firm control of the ball. A88 falls to the ground at the 1/2 yard line.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. When airborne A88 gains firm control in the end zone and then completes the catch, it is a touchdown.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-35. Late in the 4th quarter Team A trails by 2 points and is out of timeouts. A44 rushes for 12 yards, and the clock is stopped with 0:02 remaining on the clock. QB A12 gets his team lined up in a legal formation, and just after the referee appropriately blows his whistle and signals, which starts the game clock, the quarterback takes the snap and spikes the ball. The ball hits the ground and the clock shows 0:01, and replay can confirm that the ball hit the ground with 0:01 on the clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Game Over. If the game clock is stopped and will start on the referee's signal with three or more seconds remaining, the offense may reasonably expect enough time for another play after a spike. With two seconds or one second on the clock, there is enough time for only one play, which was used.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The game is tied 20-20, and on the first possession series of the first extra period, Team A scores a touchdown. On the try, B21 intercepts A12's pass and returns it across Team A's goal line.
choice_a: Score is Team A 26 - Team B 20. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_b: Score is Team A 26 - Team B 22. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, Ready.
choice_c: No score. A retains a 1-point lead. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_d: Score is Team A 26 - Team B 22. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score is Team A 26 - Team B 22. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. The touchdown on the try counts 2 points for Team B. Team B now begins their possession series at the 25-yard line, trailing 26-22.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. Team B has 12 players in the formation, and B44 recognizes that he is the 12th player and is attempting to leave the field. When the ball is snapped, B44 is on the field but his next stride takes him off the field. Team A snaps the ball and the run by A21 gains 3 yards.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. Since B44 did not get off the field before the snap, it is a live ball substitution foul. The penalty is enforced 5 yards from the previous spot, resulting in a first down.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. Ball carrier A22 heads for the right pylon at the goal line. At the B-2 he dives and the ball, in A22's right hand, crosses the sideline at the B-1 and the ball passes outside the pylon. A22 then touches the pylon with his left hand while still airborne with the ball beyond the goal line extended.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. When A22 touches the pylon, the ball becomes dead and the ball carrier gets goal line extended. Since the ball is beyond the goal line extended when declared dead, it is a touchdown.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. Ball carrier A22 heads for the right pylon at the goal line. At the B-2 he dives and the ball, in A22's right hand, crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. A22 first touches the ground out of bounds three yards beyond the goal line.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-1, Ready. The goal-line plane is not extended here because A22 did not touch either the pylon or the ground in the end zone. Since he is airborne and the ball crossed the boundary at the B-1, it is marked out of bounds at that point.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B21 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B21 catches the kick right at the goal line and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The "T signal", if executed properly, is not considered a valid or invalid fair catch signal. It does not cause the ball to become dead and does not deprive the receiving team the opportunity to advance the ball.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QB A1 is hit as he attempts to pass and the ball lands near the sideline at the B-36 where B12 picks it up and returns it across Team A's goal line. The Referee announces the ruling on the field is a fumble returned for a TD. While the ball was loose the LJ signaled incomplete. Replays show the ball came loose before A1's hand came forward and B12 recovered it in bounds.
choice_a: TD for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
choice_b: No play. Down replayed at the original spot.
choice_c: TD for Team B. B, Try, A-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock. The pass/fumble determination and the recovery at the sideline are reviewable, confirming the fumble and the return for a touchdown. The LJ's inadvertent incomplete signal is not itself reviewable and does not override the correct ruling.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. B2 fields the kick at the B-10 and returns it to the A-40 where he is pushed out of bounds. There is a flag down and the Referee announces the return team had two players with number 34 on the field during the down. Replay has video evidence that there was only one 34 on the field.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-40, Snap. This foul is not reviewable, but replay can assist via O2O when it is clear there are not duplicate numbers on the field, allowing the crew to pick up the flag.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, team A has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws to A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31. After the ball is ready at the B-31, Team A breaks the huddle late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach attempts to call a timeout just before the play clock displays zero.
choice_c: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A charged a 2nd timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. After the review, Team A retains their challenge and their timeout. The officials will not honor the coach's request for a second timeout in the same dead ball period. Team A had 4/1 @ B-31 after the overturn, then 4/6 @ B-36 following the dead ball delay of game penalty.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. A21 gains 3 yards and is tackled inbounds and B55's helmet comes off during the down. When the ball becomes dead the game clock is stopped and reads 0:45 in the second quarter. Neither team has a time out remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 4/2, A-33, Snap. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 4/2, A-33, 10-Second Runoff. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_c: A, 4/2, A-33, 10-Second Runoff. Play Clock set to 25.
choice_d: A, 4/7, A-38, 10-Second Runoff. Play Clock set to 40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/2, A-33, 10-Second Runoff. Play Clock set to 40. Team A has the option for a 10-second runoff. If exercised, the game clock is set to 0:35 and starts on the Referee's signal. If declined, the game clock remains at 0:45 and starts on the snap. The play clock is set to 40 seconds.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. A22 runs around left end and is tackled hard by B44 at the B-5. After the ball is dead, A22 gets up and punches B44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. The action by A22 is a dead ball personal foul, resulting in a 15-yard penalty, and A22 is ejected. Team A has 1st and 10 because the foul occurs after the series ended and before the ball is ready for play.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A98's punt is untouched beyond the neutral zone, bounces up at the B-2 and then A88 reaches across Team B's goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play. B22 recovers at the B-2 and advances to the B-12 where A66 tackles him by pulling his facemask.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-27, Snap. There is no foul by A88 for batting in the end zone; it is illegal touching with the spot of violation at the B-20. It is more advantageous for Team B to accept the penalty for the facemask foul at the B-12, since accepting a live-ball foul penalty cancels the illegal touching privilege.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-35. A98 punts and at the snap Team A has five players in the backfield. The kick is partially blocked, crosses the neutral zone and lands at the A-40. The ball then bounces back behind the neutral zone and rolls out of bounds at the A-32.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-27, Snap – or – A, 4/12, A-30, Snap. Since the ball crossed the neutral zone, Team B may elect to have the five-yard illegal formation penalty enforced at the dead-ball spot, or to have the penalty enforced at the previous spot with fourth down repeated.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A11 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the pass defensive end B78 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct. The pass to A88 is complete for a 10-yard gain where A88 is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Ready. The penalty for unsportsmanlike conduct is enforced at the end of the run, the A-30, resulting in a first down for Team A.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. B31 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-5 and returns it to the B-13 where he is tackled. During the return, B63 clips in his end zone.
choice_b: Safety, two points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, two points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, two points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The basic spot is the end of B31's run, the B-13, but the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, which is in Team B's own end zone, resulting in a safety.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. During the first possession series of the first extra period, B31 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-5 and returns it to the B-13 where he is tackled. During the return, B63 clips in his end zone.
choice_a: Safety, two points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. In extra periods, the penalty for B63's clipping foul is declined by rule since it is not a flagrant foul. Team A's possession series is over, and Team B begins its own possession series at the 25-yard line.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter, team A trails 24-21 and is out of timeouts. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline at the B-32 and is immediately hit by B13 who drives him backward out of bounds (0:04). The Line Judge rules progress and gives a wind signal and time expires.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Game over. Team B wins 24-21. Forward progress is not reviewable for purposes of the game clock. If there is any question as to whether the pass was complete or the line to gain was made, replay should wait until the clock hits 0:00 and then immediately alert officials to hold the teams.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter, team A trails 24-21 and is out of timeouts. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline at the B-32 and is immediately tackled by B13 (0:04). The Line Judge rules down in bounds and gives a wind signal and time expires. Replays show after completing the catch A80's right knee came down in the white before anything else touched the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/2, B-32, 0:04, Snap. Whether A80 was down in bounds or out of bounds is reviewable in this situation, since time expired but there should have been time remaining. There is no minimum number of seconds required to put back on the clock since it will not start on the next ready for play.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. A88 catches a pass at the B-5 and is down there. The clock displays 0:00 just before the play ends. Team A then commits 4 distinct and separate Unsportsmanlike Conduct fouls as teams are heading off the field. Team A is set to kick off the second half.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-5, FK Rules. All 4 UNS enforced on the free kick.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-5, Snap. One UNS carries over to the succeeding spot after the Free Kick.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-5, FK Rules. One UNS carries over to the succeeding spot after the Free Kick.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-5, FK Rules. One UNS carries over to the succeeding spot after the Free Kick. If any penalty would extend a team's free kick restraining line behind its own five-yard line, it is enforced from the next succeeding spot instead. Three of the four UNS penalties are enforced on the kickoff, and the fourth is enforced at the succeeding spot after the kick.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. A88 catches a pass at the B-5 and is down there. The clock displays 0:00 just before the play ends. Team A then commits 4 distinct and separate Unsportsmanlike Conduct fouls as teams are heading off the field. B21 reacts and retaliates by shoving A88 to the ground. Team A is set to kick off the second half.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-5, FK Rules. One UNS carries over.
choice_c: B, Free Kick, B-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. Since unsportsmanlike conduct fouls occur by both teams during the dead ball period and none of the penalties have been completed, the yardage penalties cancel.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-30. Team A is in a no-huddle offense and is moving to the line when the ball is made ready for play. Although some players settle into their positions and stop, at least one player never stops and is still moving when the ball is snapped. A12 takes the snap and gains 2 yards.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/6, B-35, Ready. This is an illegal shift that converts to a false start since Team A never satisfied the one-second rule before the snap. Officials should shut the play down and penalize Team A five yards.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-5. A98's punt from behind its own goal line strikes a Team B player at the A-15 and rebounds back across Team A's goal line. A32 recovers two yards deep in the end zone, and dives back into the field of play and is down at the A-2.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. The ball is dead when A32 recovers it in his own end zone; even though he is in legal possession, he cannot advance it. The ball belongs to the kicking team in their own end zone, so the result is a safety.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. B21 commits a personal foul after the untouched kick goes out of bounds at the B-10.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-45, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-20, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-45, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. The free kick out of bounds is a live ball foul, and B21's action is a dead ball foul. Team B may elect a re-kick following the 5-yard penalty for the kick out of bounds and then have the 15-yard dead ball penalty enforced, or retain the ball at the B-35 (spot for the kick out of bounds) with the 15-yard penalty enforced from there.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-30. A98's untouched, field goal attempt sails wide left of the upright. B21 holds A88 at the 20-yard line during the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-10, Snap. Following the unsuccessful field goal, it is Team B's ball and the penalty falls under postscrimmage kick enforcement. The postscrimmage kick spot is the 30-yard line, and the enforcement is from the 20-yard line, the spot of the foul, with no possible replay.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A98 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B's end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Score 2 points for Team A w/ B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team A w/ B, Free Kick, B-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 2 points for Team A w/ B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap. B25's kicking the ball provides new impetus, so the result of the play is a safety. B25 also commits a foul for illegally kicking the ball. Team A may decline the penalty and take the safety and two points, or accept the penalty, governed by postscrimmage kick rules, giving Team B the ball at the B-2.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-45. QB A12 drops back and looks downfield for A88 who is the primary receiver and defender B21 is holding A88 at the B-35. A12 throws a forward pass to his checkdown receiver A44 but the ball falls incomplete at the B-46.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The penalty for B21's holding foul is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot and carries an automatic 1st down.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, the score is tied and both teams have two timeouts remaining. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline and gets tackled at the B-32 (0:53). The ball comes loose and B56 recovers it on the ground at the B-29 (0:50). The officials rule fumble and recovery in bounds for Team B. Replays show it was a fumble and when B56 first touched the ball his left foot was out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/2, B-32, 0:50, Ready. Whether A80 fumbled and the recovery at the sideline are both reviewable. Since B56 was out of bounds when he touched the loose ball, it is a forward fumble out of bounds and the ball goes back to the spot of the fumble. There is no runoff since the clock would stop temporarily when the ball is out of bounds.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, the score is tied and both teams have two timeouts remaining. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline and gets tackled at the B-32 (0:53). The ball comes loose and B56 recovers it on the ground at the B-29 (0:50). The officials rule fumble and recovery in bounds for Team B. Replays show A80's right knee was down before he lost possession of the football.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/2, B-32, 0:53, 10-Second Runoff. Whether A80 fumbled and the recovery at the sideline are both reviewable. Since A80 was down prior to the fumble, the call is overturned to down by rule short of the line to gain. The result is a running clock, so a 10-second runoff applies. Either team can take a timeout to prevent the runoff.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Midway through the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-30. Both teams have all timeouts remaining.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready. Team B is charged with a timeout.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. No penalty, no timeout charged.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout. Based on the 2025 rules change, if a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, that team is charged with a timeout and there is no yardage penalty. Because of the team timeout, the clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Midway through the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-30. Team B is out of timeouts.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, and their team is out of timeouts, they are charged with a delay penalty. It remains 1st and 10 because the action occurred before the ball was ready for play. The clock starts on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Midway through the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted, B55 crosses back to his side of the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-20 after the ball was ready for play.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready (Team B is charged with a timeout) - or - A, 1/5, B-20, Ready (if Team B has no timeout remaining).
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap (Team B is charged with a timeout) - or - A, 1/5, B-20, Ready (if Team B has no timeout remaining).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap (Team B is charged with a timeout) - or - A, 1/5, B-20, Ready (if Team B has no timeout remaining). If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, that team is charged with a timeout and there is no yardage penalty, with the clock starting on the snap. If Team B has no timeout remaining, they are charged a delay penalty of 5 yards since the action occurred after the ball was ready for play, and the clock starts on the Referee's signal.

tags: extra period;timeout limit;2025 rule change;delay of game;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Try @ B-3. First possession series of the 4th extra period. After calling a team timeout during the 3rd extra period, Team A substitutes late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach calls timeout just before the play clock goes to zero.
choice_c: A, Try, B-8, No Clock. Timeout granted.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-8, No Clock. Based on the 2025 rules change, each team is allowed only one timeout from the beginning of the 3rd extra period until the game ends. Officials ignore the coach's request for a second timeout, and Team A is penalized 5 yards for delay of game.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. While the kick is in the air, B21 gives a "T signal" thinking he will not return the kick. The ball is caught by B21 at the B-5 and the officials blow the play dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Based on the 2025 rules change, the "T signal" given during a free kick is now considered an invalid signal, and the ball is dead when caught. Team B puts the ball in play at the B-25.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Team A sends in their punt team, and has no players numbered 50 - 79. They initially line up in a scrimmage kick formation, but before the snap, back A44 shifts to a position five yards behind the snapper in the clear path from the snapper to the potential kicker. The ball is snapped to A44 who throws a legal forward pass to end A88 who catches the ball at the B-45 and runs out of bounds at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, A-40, Ready. Because A44 is in the clear path between the snapper and potential kicker, Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation at the snap under the 2025 definition. Since there are no players numbered 50-79 on the line, Team A is not in a legal formation, a live ball foul penalized 5 yards from the previous spot.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A is down by 4 points and snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12.
choice_d: A, 1/5, B-20, Ready – reset the clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12. This is a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot. At the option of Team A, the game clock is reset to 0:12 and starts on the snap; this 2024 interpretation is now codified into the rule for 2025.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A is down by 4 points and snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense had 12 players on the field at the snap but B21 was hustling to get off the field and the ball was snapped just before B21 exited the field.
choice_a: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/5, B-20, Ready – no clock adjustment.
choice_d: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap – no clock adjustment.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap – no clock adjustment. This is still a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction, penalized 5 yards from the previous spot, and the clock starts on the snap. Since B21 had no influence on the play, there is no option for a clock adjustment.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. B13 catches the kick at the B-2 and returns it to the B-40 where he is pushed out of bounds. The officials spot the ball there, 1st and 10 for Team B. During the kick B8 made a "T-signal" with both arms out to each side at the B-10.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-2, Snap - adjust clock if the kickoff occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap - adjust clock if the kickoff occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-2, Ready - adjust clock if the kickoff occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-2, Snap - adjust clock if the kickoff occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout. The signal by Team B is reviewable for validity. A "T-signal" during a free kick is considered an invalid signal under the 2025 rule, and the ball is dead once possessed by any Team B player, so the return doesn't count.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the 3rd quarter, receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-20 and runs to the B-15 where he is tackled in bounds. Team A rushes to the line and gets lined up, but before they can snap it the Back Judge shuts the play down after noticing B76 on the ground injured back at the B-28. The Referee charges Team B with a timeout for the injury after the ball was spotted. Replays show B76 went to the ground before the ball was spotted.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Team B is charged with a timeout.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play clock 0:40 and no charged TO Team B.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Play clock 0:40 and no charged TO Team B.
choice_d: A, 1/5, B-15, Ready. No charged TO Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play clock 0:40 and no charged TO Team B. The 2025 injury-timeout charge rule only applies when the player presents as injured after the ball is spotted for the next down. Since B76 went down before the ball was spotted, there is no charge; replay should communicate via O2O.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Team A leads by 2 points. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-47 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-30 and the clock is stopped at 1:10. Both teams have all timeouts remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-47, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout. Play clock 40 seconds.
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-47, 10-Second Runoff. Team B is charged with a timeout. Play clock 25 seconds.
choice_c: A, 3/2, B-47, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout. Play clock 25 seconds.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-47, Ready. Team B is charged with a timeout. Play clock 25 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-47, Snap. Team B is charged with a timeout. Play clock 25 seconds. If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, that team is charged with a timeout and there is no yardage penalty. Because of the team timeout, there is no 10-second runoff, and the clock starts on the snap. With the timeout, the play clock is set to 25 seconds.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Team A leads by 2 points. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-47 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-30 and the clock is stopped at 1:10. Both teams have no timeouts remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-42, Ready. Play clock 40 seconds.
choice_b: A, 2/5, B-42, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock 40 seconds.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-42, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock 25 seconds.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-42, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock 40 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-42, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock 40 seconds. If a player presents as injured and their team is out of timeouts, they are charged a delay penalty; it is 1st and 10 because the 5-yard delay penalty takes the ball beyond the line to gain. Since the injury is the only reason the clock was stopped, the 10-second runoff applies, and the play clock is set to 40 seconds.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. Late in the game with the game clock running, Team A lines up to kick a game-tying field goal. The kick is blocked, never crosses the neutral zone, and holder A15 falls on the ball back at the B-25. When A15 falls on the ball, the clock reads 0:03 and then time expires on the game clock.
choice_a: A, 4/10, B-25, Snap. Reset the game clock to 0:03.
choice_c: A, 4/10, B-25, Ready. Reset the game clock to 0:03.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Reset the game clock to 0:03.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, B-25, Snap. Reset the game clock to 0:03. When A15 recovers the ball back at the B-25, the kick down has ended and the game clock stops, resetting to 0:03 to start on the next snap. Since the ball never crossed the neutral zone and it was 3rd down, the recovery gives Team A 4th down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. Midway through the 3rd quarter, A12 drops deep in the pocket, and A77 legally blocks edge rusher B79. At the A-35, A77 stands over B79 and brandishes a weapon. A12 throws a pass that is complete to A88 at midfield, and he runs to the B-40 where he is pushed out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25, A-25, Ready. A77's actions are taunting and must be penalized for unsportsmanlike conduct. Under a new 2025 editorial change, offensive unsportsmanlike conduct behind the neutral zone is added to the list of fouls enforced from the previous spot. A second UNS by A77 results in ejection.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98 kicks the ball high and B21 gives the "T" signal while the ball is in the air standing one yard in the endzone. B21 muffs the kick while standing in the endzone, the ball bounces forward and then he recovers the ball at the B-5. He runs to the B-20 where he is down.
choice_d: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-5, Snap. The "T" signal given during a free kick is an invalid signal under the 2025 rule. The ball is dead when caught or recovered, so officials should shut down the play as soon as B21 possesses the ball in the end zone, and the return run doesn't count.
