explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, 10-Second Runoff. A12's intentional grounding foul from the end zone results in a safety. Team A free kicks from the A-20, and Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-45. Early in the 2nd quarter, A1's punt is fielded by B3 at the B-14 and returned to the B-45 where he is pushed out of bounds. There is a flag down at the B-25 and the Referee announces holding during the return on B56. B3 gave an invalid fair catch signal prior to catching the punt.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Whether B3 made a valid or invalid signal during the kick is reviewable, but since the holding penalty already puts the ball at the B-15, there is no significant competitive effect that would require replay to stop the game over the signal question.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Receiver A80 catches a pass inbounds near the sideline at the B-25 and runs for a TD. The Side Judge has a hat off and communicates on O2O that he has the receiver being forced out of bounds by B2 and re-establishing back inbounds before touching the ball. Replays show there was minimal contact between B2 and A80 prior to A80 stepping out of bounds.
choice_b: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. Whether A80 stepped out of bounds and re-established before touching the ball is reviewable. Since there was some contact by B2, even if minimal, replay cannot second-guess its severity or effect, so the on-field "forced out" ruling stands.

question_text: 3/2 @ B-45. With the clock running, Team A is in formation, and the snap is imminent. QB A12 is in shotgun formation and is working to change the play. The Team B defensive coordinator in his team area claps causing the Team A wideout to false start just before the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. The clap is reserved for the offense and may not be used by the defense. The action by the Team B assistant coach is a dead-ball foul for delay of game for disconcerting signals.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-6. Team A trails by 1 point and it is late in the 4th quarter. QB A12 drops straight back, is under heavy duress and intentionally grounds a forward pass from his end zone that lands at the A-10 with no receiver in the area. When the play ends, the clock reads 2:00.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team B and A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-6, Snap.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team B and A, Free Kick, A-20, 10-Second Runoff.
choice_d: Score 2 points for Team B and A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 2 points for Team B and A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-6, Snap. Intentional grounding from the end zone results in a safety. Since it was 4th down, if Team B declines the penalty, it is Team B's ball 1st and Goal at the previous spot. There is no 10-second runoff since the play did not occur after the Two-Minute Timeout.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. A12 throws a legal forward pass to A80 at the B-22. A80 has position on B21 and is about to touch the pass when B21 pushes A80 in the back above the waist to get to the ball and the pass falls incomplete.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-8, Snap. Offensive pass interference declined by Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5, B-20, Snap. The action by B21 is legal since interference rules do not apply behind the neutral zone; an eligible receiver there may push an opponent in the back above the waist to get to a forward pass.

question_text: 4/8 @ B-40. A91's untouched field goal attempt rolls dead at the B-7 with no one near the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap. When the untouched kick crosses the neutral zone and is declared dead there, the ball belongs to Team B at the previous spot.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. Ball carrier A22 heads for the right-hand pylon at the goal line. The ball in his right hand crosses the sideline at the 1/2 yard line and then breaks the plane of the extension of the goal line outside (i.e., to the right of) the pylon. A22 then steps on the goal line and falls out of bounds.
choice_a: Touchdown for A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The goal-line plane is extended for the ball carrier because A22 touches the ground in the end zone, even though the ball itself crossed outside the pylon.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. Ball carrier A22 heads for the right-hand pylon at the goal line. The ball in his right hand crosses the sideline at the 1/2 yard line and then breaks the plane of the extension of the goal line outside (i.e., to the right of) the pylon, and then A22 steps on the sideline 1 inch short of the goal line.
choice_a: Touchdown for A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-1/2, Ready. Since the ball carrier did not touch in the end zone or the pylon, the goal-line plane is not extended. The ball is ruled out of bounds at the crossing point of the sideline.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-40. Early in the 4th quarter with the clock running, Snapper A51 lifts the ball before passing it backward, B52 bats the ball away, and B53 recovers the ball at the B-39.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-45, Ready. The action by A51 is an illegal snap. The ball remains dead because it was not put in play by a legal snap, so Team A retains possession after the 5-yard penalty.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-40. Early in the 4th quarter with the clock running, Snapper A51 legally begins the snap, but B52 bats the ball before A51 completes the snap, and B53 recovers the ball at the B-39.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. The action by B52 is a foul, and the ball remains dead since Team B may not touch the ball until it has been snapped. Team A retains possession after the 5-yard penalty is enforced against Team B.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. With the clock running in the 2nd quarter, Team A gets lined up and snaps the ball very close to the Two-Minute Timeout. The officials allow the snap and Back A2 runs to the B-35 where he is tackled inbounds (1:55). The TV graphic of the game clock shows it clearly at 2:00 prior to the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-40, 2:00, Snap. If the game clock clearly hits 2:00 before the snap but the officials allow the play to occur, replay will void the play. During the media break, replay should call the TV truck for the embedded game clock and make the decision based on what it shows.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Back A2 takes a pitch and runs around right end toward the sideline. He turns upfield and runs into Team B's end zone. The Line Judge rules A2 stepped out of bounds at the B-7. Replays show A2 did not step OOB at the B-7 and advanced the ball into the end zone in the immediate continuing action.
choice_b: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. If a ball carrier is ruled out of bounds but did not actually step out, replay can award an advance only if the player advances the ball into the opponent's end zone in the immediate continuing action.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, Team A is flagged for an illegal shift, QB A12 throws a legal forward pass, and B25 intercepts the pass at the B-10. During the return, B38 commits a foul for an illegal blindside block at midfield before B25 crosses Team A's goal line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 25, No Clock. Team B's score is not allowed. The fouls by both teams cancel, and the down is not repeated since Team A's possession series has already ended. Team B begins its own possession series at the 25-yard line.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-40. QB A12's legal forward pass is intercepted by B21 at the B-20, who is downed inbounds at the B-30. B72 was offside on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. Team A retains possession after enforcement of the offside penalty. Although the clock was stopped to award Team B a first down, Team B will not next snap the ball, so the clock starts on the referee's signal.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-30. QB A12 is in shotgun formation, takes the snap and hands to A21. He takes the ball, stops short of the line, and pitches the ball back to A12 in the tackle box. A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles out of the tackle box, and at the A-25, throws the ball away out of bounds and over the bench area. There were no Team A receivers in the area, but the ball clearly crossed the Neutral Zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, A-25, Snap. The exception to intentional grounding applies to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession before throwing the forward pass. Since A12 never gave up possession to another player, his throwaway is legal.

tags: pass protection;block in the back;tackle box;legal contact;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week8;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-35. A12 takes the snap and retreats to pass. Defensive end B95 gets past A75 and is about to tackle A12 at the B-42, who is still inside the tackle box. A75 pushes B95 in the back at the numbers to prevent him from making the tackle. A12's pass is completed to A88 at the B-10, and A88 runs for a touchdown.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-10, Snap. Block in the back on A75.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
choice_d: A, 4/10, B-42, Snap. Block in the back on A75.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. Continuing action involving pass protection while the passer remains inside the tackle box falls within the spirit of the exceptions to the blocking-in-the-back rule and is legal.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A lines up for a two-point try attempt and Team B has 12 players in its defensive formation. The 12th Team B player runs toward the sideline and is 1-yard inbounds just as the ball is snapped. A22 takes the handoff from the quarterback and is tackled at the one-yard line.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-1 1/2, No Clock. This is a live-ball foul against Team B for illegal substitution. Team B is penalized half the distance to the goal, and Team A repeats the try.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A lines up for a two-point try attempt and Team B has 12 players in its defensive formation. The 12th Team B player runs toward the sideline and is 1-yard inbounds just as the ball is snapped. A22 takes the handoff from the quarterback and carries the ball into the end zone.
choice_b: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, Snap.
choice_d: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. This is a live-ball foul against Team B for illegal substitution. Team A declines the penalty and keeps the successful try; there is no carryover.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score by 3 points late in the 4th quarter after the Two-Minute Timeout. Ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play after gaining three yards and the game clock reads 1:27. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-26.
choice_a: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap – or – A, 3/4, A-28, Snap (at the option of Team B).
choice_c: A, 2/16, A-16, Ready – or – A, 3/4, A-28, Snap (at the option of Team B).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap – or – A, 3/4, A-28, Snap (at the option of Team B). The holding penalty against A55, if accepted, is enforced 10 yards from the spot of the foul, repeating 2nd down. The defense may instead decline the penalty, bringing up 3rd down. Either way, the clock starts on the snap or the referee's signal, at Team B's option.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score by 3 points late in the 4th quarter after the Two-Minute Timeout. Ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play after gaining nine yards and the game clock reads 1:27. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-26.
choice_a: A, 2/16, A-16, Ready (at the option of Team B).
choice_b: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap (at the option of Team B).
choice_c: A, 3/1, A-25, Snap (at the option of Team B).
choice_d: A, 2/6, A-26, Snap (at the option of Team B).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap (at the option of Team B). The holding penalty against A55 is enforced 10 yards from the spot of the foul, repeating 2nd down. The game clock starts on the snap or the referee's signal, at Team B's option.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A1 throws a swing pass from the B-39 to back A1 who attempts to catch it at the B-39 but drops the ball. He immediately picks it up and runs to the B-34, but the officials kill the play ruling the pass forward and incomplete.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. Whether the pass was forward or backward is reviewable, but if replay makes it backward there can be no advance allowed. Replay should not stop the game here since Team A was already denied an opportunity to advance the ball by the on-field ruling.

tags: instant replay;catch process;going to the ground;maintain control;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week8;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Receiver A80 attempts to catch a forward pass near the sideline at the B-20. The ball comes loose, and the officials rule the pass incomplete. A80 is going to the ground to make the catch and on the way to the ground he gets control and his right foot down inbounds. B14 who was covering A80 pulls the ball loose after the right foot is down, but before A80 lands.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, B-40, Snap. Whether the pass was complete or incomplete is reviewable. Since A80 was going to the ground to make the catch, he must maintain control when he lands; since he does not, the pass is incomplete regardless of how the ball came loose.

question_text: 3/1/2 yard @ A-44. With the clock running just before the snap, center A55 slides the ball forward so the ball is at the line to gain. The QB takes the snap and sneaks the ball to the A-45.
choice_a: The play is shut down before the snap – Reset the ball to A, 3/1/2 yard, A-44, Ready.
choice_b: A, 4/1, A-45, Ready. False start on A55.
choice_c: The play is shut down before the snap – Reset the ball to A, 3/1/2 yard, A-44, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The play is shut down before the snap – Reset the ball to A, 3/1/2 yard, A-44, Ready. The snapper's action of sliding the ball forward to the line to gain is a dead ball foul. The play is shut down, the ball is reset, and the player and head coach are warned; a false start foul is only considered after a warning.

tags: roughing the passer;fumble;spot of recovery;automatic first down;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week9;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. A88 catches a legal forward pass and fumbles when tackled after gaining nine yards. The fumble is recovered by grounded A24 five yards in advance of the fumble. During the pass, B54 is guilty of roughing the passer.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-46, Ready. The penalty for B54's roughing the passer foul is enforced 15 yards from the spot of recovery by A24, the end of the last run, and results in a first down for Team A.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, B37 intercepts A12's forward pass at the B-15 and has a clear field to the goal line when he makes an obscene gesture at the A-15 toward the nearest opponent.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 40, No Clock. Team B's score is cancelled, and the penalty is carried over to Team B's own possession series, which begins on the 40-yard line with 1st and 10.

tags: consecutive timeouts;false start;delay of game;dead ball period;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week9;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is midway in the 3rd quarter with the clock running and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team A head coach requests a timeout, and the time out is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation, and the ball is ready for play. Just before the snap, A77 commits a false start. Both teams are back in formation with the ball ready for play and just before the play clock expires, the Team A head coach requests a timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, B-40, Snap. The officials will not acknowledge the second timeout request because the intervening false start, a dead ball foul, keeps everything within the same dead ball period as the first timeout. The play clock expires for delay of game instead.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 3 points and neither team has a timeout. Team A completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15. B27 remains on the ground, and the Back Judge declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 on the game clock. Play Clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap at the option of Team A. Play clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap at the option of Team A. Play clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap at the option of Team A. Play clock set to 40. Team A has the option of a 10-second runoff, which they may decline, and the clock will then start on the snap. The play clock is set to 40 seconds.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. Game clock and play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap w/ clock reset to 0:12 at the option of team A. Play clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap w/ clock reset to 0:12 at the option of team A. Play clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap w/ clock reset to 0:12 at the option of team A. Play clock set to 25.
choice_d: A, 1/5, B-20, Ready w/ clock reset to 0:12 at the option of team A. Play clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap w/ clock reset to 0:12 at the option of team A. Play clock set to 25. With 12 players on the field at the snap, Team B commits a substitution infraction, penalized 5 yards from the previous spot. At the option of Team A, the game clock is reset to 0:12 and starts on the snap; the play clock is set to 25 seconds.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A91's punt is untouched and is bouncing at the B-3. A33 blocks B21 into the ball, forcing it through the end zone and over the end line.
choice_b: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Since A33 blocked B21 into the ball, B21 is deemed not to have touched the kick. The impetus remains from the kick itself, not from the forced touch, so the ball is declared dead in the end zone for a touchback.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A and Team B line up in position and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. The kick is fair caught at the B-5 by B21.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. There is no foul on the play. On free kick plays, the coach-to-player communications rule is not in effect. The ball is put in play by the receiving team at the B-25 because of the fair catch.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A1's punt is fielded by B3 at the B-35 and returned to the 50 where he is downed. During the return A1 was running toward B3 at the A-45, but B54 approached him from the front and launched into him with his right shoulder making forcible contact to A1's head.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap, DQ B54. A1, the kicker, is considered defenseless throughout the down. B54 attacks the head with forcible contact, confirming targeting and disqualification.

question_text: 4/2 @ B-42. QB A1 attempts to hand the ball to back A2 but they muff the exchange. The ball bounces off A2's hands, but before it touches the ground, he gains possession and runs to the B-38 where he is tackled.
choice_a: A, 4/2, B-42, Ready. Fumble, ball spotted at spot of recovery.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-38, Running. This is not considered a fumble since the handoff is considered successful. The live-ball run counts normally, resulting in a first down.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 4/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A98's field goal attempt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. Holder A12 recovers the ball at the B-30 and runs for a touchdown.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. Since the ball did not cross the neutral zone, it continues in play and all players can recover and advance. The advance by A12 is legal and results in a touchdown.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A executes an on-side kick and A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. At the A-47 he leaps from out of bounds and bats the ball forward, and it rolls out of bounds at the A-49.
choice_c: B, 1/10, A-42, Ready - or - A, Re-kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_d: B, 1/10, A-42, Snap - or - A, Re-kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-42, Snap - or - A, Re-kick, A-30, FK Rules. Airborne A33 is out of bounds when he touches the ball since he has not established himself inbounds. There is no foul for batting the ball forward since the ball is dead as soon as A33 touches it; this is instead a free kick out of bounds, giving Team B the standard enforcement choices.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. In the second quarter with the score tied, the game clock reads 0:45 at the snap. During the play, A55 loses his helmet. Right tackle A77 is flagged for holding at the B-25. The ball carrier is tackled inbounds at the B-21. Neither team has a timeout remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/15, B-35, Ready, at the option of Team B. A55 must leave the game for one play, and play clock set to 25.
choice_b: A, 1/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. A55 must leave the game for one play, and play clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 1/15, B-35, Ready, at the option of Team B. A55 must leave the game for one play, and play clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready, at the option of Team B. A55 must leave the game for one play, and play clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, B-35, Ready, at the option of Team B. A55 must leave the game for one play, and play clock set to 25. There is no option for a 10-second runoff since the clock is stopped both for the helmet off and to administer the holding penalty. The play clock is set to 25 seconds. The game clock starts on the Referee's signal or snap at the option of the offended team.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running in the fourth quarter. Team A trails by two points and is out of timeouts. After the ball is ready for play lineman A66 commits a false start, and the officials stop the game clock at 0:08.
choice_d: A, 2/10, B-30, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Game over, Team B wins. The false start causes the clock to stop immediately, and Team B accepts the yardage penalty and the 10-second runoff. Since there were only 8 seconds remaining, the runoff ends the game.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A32 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B's end zone and over the end line.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, Free Kick, B-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap. B25's kicking the ball provides new impetus, resulting in a safety, and is also a foul for illegally kicking the ball. Team A may decline and take the safety, or accept the penalty, governed by postscrimmage kick rules, giving Team B the ball at the B-2.

tags: dead ball foul;personal foul;turnover on downs;succeeding spot;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week10;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/8 @ B-35. Team A gains five yards and the ball is declared dead, after which B21 is called for a dead ball personal foul at the B-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Team A's series is over since they failed to make the line to gain. B21's dead ball personal foul is enforced 15 yards from the succeeding spot, giving Team B a first down at the B-15. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with the score tied, Team A completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. B27 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:15 on the game clock. Neither team has a timeout remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, 10-Second Runoff. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play Clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play Clock set to 40. Since Team A made a first down after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team B's injury is not the only reason the clock was stopped, so there is no option for a 10-second runoff. The clock starts on the Referee's signal following the injury timeout.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with the score tied, Team A completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. B27 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 on the game clock. Neither team has a timeout remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Play Clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Play Clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Play Clock set to 40. Team A has the option of a 10-second runoff, which they may decline, and the clock will then start on the snap. The play clock is set to 40 seconds.

question_text: Try @ B-3. On a two-point attempt receiver A80 attempts to catch a low pass near the goal line, but the ball comes loose and hits the ground. The officials rule the try no good. Replays show A80 get his hands underneath the ball and control it as he goes to the ground. He has control when he lands and then as he rolls over the ball comes loose. The ball was breaking the plane of the goal line when A80 hit the ground.
choice_a: Team A Try no good. Free Kick, A-35.
choice_b: Team A Try successful. Free Kick, A-20.
choice_c: Team A Try successful. Free Kick, A-35.
choice_d: Team A Try successful. B, 1/10, B-25, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A Try successful. Free Kick, A-35. Whether the pass was complete and the ball's relation to the goal line are both reviewable. A80 completes the catch by maintaining control when he lands, and the catch process is over once he begins to roll over, so the try is overturned to successful.

tags: instant replay;intentional grounding;significance standard;forward or backward pass;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week10;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/Goal @ B-5. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A1 under heavy pressure scrambles to the B-14 where he throws a pass that first hits the ground out of bounds at the B-14 1/2. The Referee drops a flag for intentional grounding. There are no other flags on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-14, Snap. Whether the pass was forward or backward is reviewable, but the half-yard difference has no significant competitive effect, so replay should not get involved.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Midway through the 4th quarter, QB A12 throws downfield, and the ball is intercepted by B21 at the B-45. B21 returns to the A-35 where he fumbles and A55 recovers at that point while grounded.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-35, Running. Since there has been a change of possession during the down, when Team A recovers the ball it results in a first down. The clock will not stop to award the first down.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98's kick is caught in the end zone and B21 runs it out. During the return, B55 holds at the B-40 and subsequently B21 fumbles at the B-35 and the ball is recovered by A80 at the B-37. Just after the play, replay stops the game and creates Targeting with the crown of the helmet on A44 who hit the ball carrier just before the fumble. The receiving team head coach wants to take the ball and does not want a re-kick.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. Disqualify A44.
choice_b: Replay the down. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. Disqualify A44.
choice_d: Replay the down. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Replay the down. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. Disqualify A44. Live-ball fouls by both teams cause the fouls to cancel and the down to be repeated. The change-of-possession exception cannot apply here because Team A's targeting foul occurred before Team A last gained possession, so there is no option even though the receiving coach wanted to keep the ball.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. Midway through the 4th quarter, Team A punts and receiver B21 muffs the ball near the sideline at the B-30. Gunner A80 scoops and recovers the ball at the B-32 and runs out of bounds at the B-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-32, Snap. A80's recovery of the muffed punt is legal, and the ball becomes dead at that spot with no advance allowed. The clock starts on the snap because of the legal kick down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. Midway through the 4th quarter, Team A punts and receiver B21 catches the ball near the sideline at the B-30 and returns to the B-35 where he fumbles. Gunner A80 scoops and recovers the ball at the B-35 and runs out of bounds at the B-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Snap. Since B21 completed the catch before fumbling, this is a genuine fumble rather than a muffed kick, so A80's recovery and advance are both legal. The clock starts on the snap.

tags: fumble;out of bounds;spot of the fumble;dead ball foul;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week11;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. A22 takes the direct snap, runs and fumbles the ball on Team A's 38-yard line, with the ball going out of bounds at the A-40. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, A55 commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-23, Ready. A22 made the line to gain and then fumbled forward out of bounds; since it went out ahead of the fumble spot, the ball reverts to the spot of the fumble, still a first down. The dead ball foul penalty is then enforced before the new line to gain is set.

tags: fumble;out of bounds;spot of the fumble;change of possession;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week11;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. A22 takes the direct snap, runs and fumbles the ball on Team A's 38-yard line, with the ball going out of bounds on Team A's 30-yard line. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, A55 commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-15, Snap. The runner made the line to gain but then fumbled backward out of bounds; since the fumble went out behind the spot of the fumble, short of the line to gain, the ball belongs to Team B there. The 15-yard penalty is enforced from that spot, giving Team B a first down.

question_text: 3/8 @ A-26. Early in the 3rd quarter, airborne A82 receives a legal forward pass and has firm control at the A-35. As A82 receives the ball, he is contacted by B21 and first comes to the ground with the ball at the A-33, where he is down and the ball is declared dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-35, Running. Although A82 completed the catch when he got a body part down at the A-33, the ball is spotted at the A-35, the spot of forward progress.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-20. Midway through the 4th quarter, ball carrier A22 is tackled hard by B44 at the B-5. Just after the ball is dead, A22 gets up and punches B44.
choice_a: A, 2/5, B-15, Ready. A22 is ejected.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-20, Snap. A22 is ejected.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected. A22's run made the line to gain, so a new series is awarded to Team A first. The 15-yard penalty for the dead ball personal foul is enforced, and A22 is ejected. The clock starts on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. B3 catches A1's punt at the B-20 and returns it to the B-32 where he is tackled by A56 and fumbles the ball. A14 falls on the loose ball at the B-30. There is a flag on the play, and the Referee announces holding during the return by the return team (at the B-25) and the penalty is declined. Replays show B3 was not down when he lost possession of the ball and that A56 lowered his head and clearly attacked with the crown of the helmet making forcible contact.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, A-35, Snap. DQ A56. Fumble/down by rule and potential targeting are both reviewable. Since Team A had possession at the end of the down but did not gain it with clean hands, the only option is to replay the down, with the targeting disqualification still applied.

tags: instant replay;goal line;camera angle;clear and obvious standard;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week11;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/Goal @ B-2. With 0:30 remaining in the game Team A trails 21-17 and has one timeout remaining. QB A1 takes the snap and on a designed run heads toward the goal line pylon. He dives for the pylon with the ball extended toward the goal line and his right knee hits down inbounds. The Line Judge signals TD. The cart cam is positioned at the B-1 and from that angle it is clear A1's right knee is down and it appears the nose of the ball breaks the plane. There is also a handheld camera positioned one yard into the end zone and from that angle it appears the ball is short. No other angles provide anything else definitive.
choice_b: TD. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The ball's relation to the goal line is reviewable, but with no camera angle directly down the goal line, the ruling on the field is upheld since off-angle camera positions distort the ball's position relative to the line.

tags: late substitution;delay of game;umpire position;defense matching up;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/3 at the B-20. With the clock running and 4 minutes left in the 4th quarter, Team A is behind by 4 points. The ball is ready for play and the umpire is in his regular position. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The umpire is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. The Referee shuts down the action. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 3/3 @ B-20, Snap. The late substitution is legal and the play stands as run.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-25, Snap. Team A is penalized for illegal substitution and loses possession.
choice_d: A, 3/3 @ B-25, Running. The umpire's inability to reach the ball voids any penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/8 @ B-25, Ready. The play is shut down, the game clock is stopped, and the defense is allowed to substitute in response to Team A's late substitutions. Team A is penalized 5 yards for delay of game. The play clock is set to 25 seconds and starts on the ready-for-play signal.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. QB A12 takes the snap, rolls out right, finds no receiver open, turns up field and runs. After making the line to gain, A12 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet first at the B-27, stays upright, and runs for a touchdown. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. The runner never actually went to the ground, so the play continues to the end zone.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-30, Ready. The ball is blown dead three yards short of where the fake slide began.
choice_d: A, 4/10 @ B-40, Ready. The fake slide is treated as a foul for unsportsmanlike conduct that negates the run.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-27, Ready. The live ball becomes dead at the point where A12 simulates or fakes as if he will begin a feet-first slide. An official shall sound the whistle and declare the ball dead at that point. A fake slide is not reviewable under Rule 12-3-3.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-45. After the ball is ready, Team A comes to the line and the clock goes to 0:00 to end the 3rd quarter. A21 was disqualified late in the 3rd quarter for Targeting and legally remained in the team area. Between the end of the 3rd quarter and the start of the 4th quarter, A21 is flagged in the team area for Unsportsmanlike Conduct. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Ready. A21's prior disqualification means the new foul cannot be penalized further.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. The dead ball foul results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_c: A, 1/25 @ A-40, Snap. A21 is ejected.
choice_d: A, 1/25 @ A-40, Ready. A21 remains in the team area since he was already disqualified once.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25 @ A-40, Snap. A21 is ejected. The action by A21 is a dead ball foul, a 15-yard penalty from the succeeding spot. Since the ball was ready for play, the penalty is enforced after the line to gain is established. A21 is ejected from the game and must leave the playing enclosure. Any player who has been disqualified and is subsequently penalized for Unsportsmanlike Conduct is automatically ejected.

tags: fumble recovery;grounded player;roughing the passer;end of last run;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 at the A-35. A21 catches a forward pass and fumbles when tackled at the A-44. The fumble is recovered by grounded A24 five yards in advance of the fumble. During the pass, passer A12 is roughed by B75. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-49, Ready. The recovery in advance of the fumble spot is illegal, so the ball returns to A24's grounded spot.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-44, Snap. The roughing the passer foul is declined in favor of Team B taking over on the turnover.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-49, Ready. B75's foul is enforced from the spot of A21's fumble rather than the end of the run.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-36, Ready. The recovery in advance of the spot of A21's fumble is legal because it is not 4th down. The penalty for B75's foul is enforced from the end of Team A's last run, which is the A-49.

tags: punt touched by kicking team;end zone;touchback;kick down clock start;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the A-45. A91's punt lands at the B-8, is then touched by A33 at the B-4, and the ball rolls into Team B's end zone. Team B recovers it two yards deep in the end zone and advances the ball into the field of play, where it is downed at the B-3. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-3, Snap. Team B keeps the ball at the spot where it was downed rather than taking a touchback.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap. Team A's illegal touching gives Team A the ball at the spot of the touch.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal instead of the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The ball is dead when it strikes the ground in the end zone untouched by Team B. Team B could take the ball at the spot of Team A's illegal touching, but will take the touchback and put the ball in play at the B-20. The clock starts on the snap following the scrimmage kick down.

question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. A91's punt is high and deep, and returner B21 drifts back 2 yards deep in his own end zone and muffs the punt in flight. The loose ball is recovered and downed by A80 with the ball still in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchback for Team B. B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Once the ball enters the end zone on a punt, only Team B may recover it.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try @ B-3, No clock.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-1, Ready. Team A gains possession but must snap from just outside the goal line.
choice_d: Safety for Team A, score 2 points. A muffed punt recovered in the end zone by the kicking team is a safety.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try @ B-3, No clock. The ball is muffed by B21 in his own end zone before the ball strikes the ground, so the ball remains live and any player of either team may recover. A80 legally recovers the ball in Team B's end zone, resulting in a touchdown.

tags: 12 men on the field;substitution infraction;no clock adjustment;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 1/10 at the B-25. Team A, down by 3 points, snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 finds no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball away beyond the neutral zone, and the play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense had 12 players on the field at the snap, but B21 was hustling to get off the field, and the ball was snapped just before B21 exited. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/5 @ B-20, Ready. Since B21 was leaving the field, there is no foul, but the clock is set to reflect a stoppage.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-25, 10-Second Runoff. The intentional grounding penalty carries a 10-second runoff.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-25, Snap. Team B accepts the penalty and elects to take the down over on defense.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5 @ B-20, Snap. Since B21 did not get off the field at the snap, the defense has 12 players on the field, which is a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot. Since B21 had no influence on the play, there is no clock adjustment.

question_text: 2/10 at the B-40. Late in the game, neither team has a timeout. A22 runs for 8 yards and is tackled short of the line to gain. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54's helmet comes off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. What is the ruling on the game clock and play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Ready. Play clock set to 40, since the helmet-off stoppage overrides the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_b: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Snap. Play clock set to 15, matching a normal injury timeout inside two minutes.
choice_c: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Running. The clock never stops because the helmet coming off was not the result of a foul.
choice_d: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Snap. Play clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Snap. Play clock set to 25. The game clock is stopped at 1:58 for the Two-Minute Timeout and for B54's helmet being off. Even with a Team B helmet off, the play clock is set to 25 seconds following the Two-Minute Timeout, and the game clock starts on the snap. B54 must sit out the next play.

tags: instant replay;out of bounds;re-establish inbounds;first touch eligibility;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. QB A1's pass near the sideline intended for A80 is tipped first by B6. A80 then steps out of bounds with his right foot before re-establishing inbounds with his left foot and catching the pass at the B-30. He runs to the B-25, where he is pushed out of bounds. The officials spot the ball there with no flags on the play. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-25, Ready, but A80 is flagged for illegal touching since he stepped out of bounds before re-establishing.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-30, Snap. The tip by B6 makes A80 ineligible, so the catch is disallowed and Team B takes over.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-30, Ready. The spot is placed at the catch point rather than where A80 was pushed out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-25, Ready. Whether A80 was out of bounds and re-established and who touched the pass first are both reviewable aspects of the play. The first touch is by B6, so all players become eligible and remain eligible throughout the rest of the down.

tags: targeting review;dead ball personal foul;line to gain review;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week12;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. A80 catches a forward pass and runs to the B-37, where he dives for the line to gain and is hit by B23. The officials spot the ball at the B-35 and the Referee announces a dead ball personal foul with targeting for a late hit by B23 to the head/neck area. Replays show B23 lead with his shoulder and attack with forcible contact to the middle of A80's back after he was down. They also show the ball at the B-36 when A80's left knee hit the ground. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-21, Snap, and B23 is disqualified since replay confirms forcible contact to a defenseless player.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Since targeting is overturned, the dead ball personal foul is also wiped out entirely.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-35, Snap. The line to gain is upheld as reviewed, giving Team A a first down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-21, Snap. Targeting and the ball in relation to the line to gain are both reviewable aspects of the play. Since the contact was not to the head/neck area or with the crown, there is no foul for targeting. The dead ball personal foul penalty is still enforced, but since Team A did not reach the line to gain, Team B gets possession.

tags: interception in end zone;hold in end zone;safety;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/Goal at the B-8. B21 intercepts A12's legal forward pass deep in his end zone and advances but does not get out of the end zone, where he is downed. During B21's run, B52 holds A80 in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The interception stands as a touchback and the holding foul is enforced from there.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-16, Snap. The 10-yard holding penalty is enforced from the interception spot, giving Team A a first down.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick @ B-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick @ A-35, FK Rules. Team A kicks off after its own safety score.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick @ B-20, FK Rules. The result of the play is a touchback, but since the foul by B52 is in the end zone, the penalty leaves the ball in Team B's possession in the end zone, resulting in a safety.

question_text: 3/5 at the B-20. Defensive back B44 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone and, while still in the end zone, fumbles the ball. The ball rolls forward, goes into the field of play, and in the scramble at the B-2, A33 intentionally kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-2, Snap. Team A recovers the loose ball at the spot where A33 last touched it before it went into the end zone.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. Kicking the ball out of the end zone results in a safety against Team B.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-12, Snap. The 10-yard penalty for the illegal kick is enforced from the B-2 rather than the basic touchback spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-30, Snap. The result of the play is a touchback, and therefore the basic spot for enforcement of the 10-yard penalty for illegally kicking the ball by A33 is the B-20. The touchback results because of the new impetus given by A33 kicking the ball.

question_text: Free Kick at the A-35. A91's kick is rolling on the ground at the B-3 when B21 kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick @ B-20, FK Rules – or – A, Free Kick @ A-45, FK Rules.
choice_b: Touchback for Team B. B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Kicking a rolling free kick out of the end zone is treated as a normal touchback.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick @ A-35, FK Rules only, with no option to decline the penalty.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-13, Snap. The 10-yard penalty for the illegal kick is enforced from the B-3 with no safety awarded.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick @ B-20, FK Rules – or – A, Free Kick @ A-45, FK Rules. The result of the play is a safety due to the new impetus by B21. B21 also commits a foul for illegally kicking the ball. If the 10-yard penalty is accepted, the down is repeated with the penalty enforced at the previous spot.

question_text: 1/10 at the A-40. QB A12 drops back in the pocket and sets up to pass. He then scrambles right, sets up again, and is in the passing posture when he is hit by end B88, who drives his shoulder into A12's knee. A12 then pulls the ball down and goes to the ground because of being tackled by B88 at the A-38. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Ready. Since A12 did not release the ball, he is not a passer and no roughing foul applies.
choice_c: A, 2/2 @ A-38, Ready. The tackle is legal and only the resulting sack yardage counts.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the dead ball spot at the A-38 rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Ready. The action by B88 is a foul for roughing the passer. While A12 is technically not a passer because he did not release the ball, the action by B88 is a foul because the contact was in the knee area or below and A12 was in a passing posture. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot.

tags: blocked punt;recovery behind neutral zone;legal forward pass;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/8 at the A-12. Late in the 4th quarter, A98's punt is blocked and the ball does not cross the neutral zone. At the A-10, back A22 recovers the ball and throws a forward pass to eligible A88, who is tackled at the B-3. The game clock reads 0:03. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-10, Snap. A blocked punt that doesn't cross the neutral zone gives Team B the ball at the recovery spot.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Running. The clock continues to run since the pass is a legal continuation of the kick play.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Ready. A22's forward pass after recovering a blocked punt is an illegal second forward pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Snap. The recovery and pass by A22 are legal since the punt did not cross the neutral zone. The clock stops when A88 is tackled at the B-3, and the game clock starts on the snap because of the legal kick play.

question_text: 4/1 at the B-30. It is midway through the 4th quarter with the clock running, and both teams have their full allotment of timeouts remaining. Before the snap, legal substitute B21, running from the bench area, requests a timeout just after being within the nine-yard marks. A12 then takes the snap and drives for a 1-yard gain on the QB sneak. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-29, Snap. B21's timeout request was too late, so the QB sneak counts and Team A gains a first down.
choice_b: A, 4/1 @ B-30, Ready. The timeout is denied since B21 was still coming from the bench area when he requested it.
choice_c: B, 4/1 @ B-30, Snap. B21's request results in a delay of game charged to Team A, and Team B takes over on downs.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-30, Snap. The timeout request by B21 is granted, and there is no play.

tags: punt return;holding before end of kick;postscrimmage kick enforcement;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the A-25. During A98's punt, B21 holds A55 at the A-19 after the kick crosses the neutral zone and before it was caught by B80 at the B-35. B80 returns the ball back to the 50. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ 50, Snap. The return to the 50 stands and the holding foul during the kick is disregarded.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-29, Snap. The 10-yard holding penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul at the A-19.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot rather than the end of the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25, Snap. The penalty for the foul by B21 is subject to postscrimmage kick enforcement since the foul occurred before the end of the kick and Team B will be the next to put the ball in play. The 10-yard penalty is enforced from the end of the kick, which is the B-35.

tags: 12 men on the field;two-minute timeout;game clock reset options;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 1/10 at the B-25. Team A, down by 4 points, snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. A12 finds no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and runs for 10 yards, downed inbounds with the clock stopped at 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. What is the ruling on the game clock and play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-15, Ready. Team A must accept the 5-yard penalty in order to have the game clock reset to 0:12.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. Game clock reset to 0:12 at the option of Team A, play clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot and the game clock stays at 0:06.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. Game clock stays at 0:06, since resetting the clock is only available before the Two-Minute Timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. Game clock reset to 0:12 at the option of Team A, play clock set to 25. Since there were 12 defenders participating, Team B committed a foul for a substitution infraction, and since this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team A has additional options. There is no requirement to accept the penalty to have the clock reset. The offense may decline the 5-yard penalty, which would be enforced from the previous spot, and keep the option to reset the game clock to 0:12 with the game clock starting on the next snap.

tags: instant replay;down by contact;immediate continuing action;touchdown overturn;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/Goal at the B-5. A2 takes a handoff and gets hit at the line of scrimmage by B98, and they go to the ground. A2 appears to land on top of B98 as he rolls over, then advances the ball into the end zone in the immediate continuing action. The Line Judge rules A2's left elbow touched the ground as he rolled over and spots the ball at the B-4. Replays show A2's elbow did not touch the ground, nor any other body part that would put him down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/Goal @ B-4, Ready. Whether a runner is down by contact is a judgment call and is not reviewable.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal @ B-4, Ready. Replay confirms the ruling on the field since A2's elbow was close enough to the ground.
choice_c: TD for Team A, Try @ B-3, No Clock.
choice_d: TD for Team A, but only after a coach's challenge is filed, since this is not automatically reviewed.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team A, Try @ B-3, No Clock. Whether A2 was down is reviewable, and if he is not down and the ball crosses the goal line in the immediate continuing action, replay will overturn to a TD.

tags: instant replay;out of bounds;immediate continuing action;touchback overturn;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week13;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 at the B-15. A2 takes a handoff and runs around right end down the sideline toward the end zone. He dives from the 3-yard line toward the goal line, and the ball comes loose, hitting the pylon. The Line Judge rules that A2 stepped out of bounds at the 3-yard line before he dove for the end zone and spots the ball there. Replays show A2 did not step out of bounds. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Ready. The out of bounds ruling on the field stands since sideline calls are not reviewable.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. Since A2 did not step out of bounds, the dive into the end zone counts as a score.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-3, Snap. Team B recovers the fumble at the pylon rather than receiving a touchback.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Whether A2 stepped out of bounds is reviewable, and since he did not and the ball made it to the end zone in the immediate continuing action, replay will overturn to a touchback.

tags: holding on punt block attempt;automatic first down;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/12 at the A-35. Just after the snap, lineman B77 grabs guard A66 and pulls him to one side, allowing linebacker B43 to shoot the gap to try to block the kick. B44 catches the kick at the B-25 and returns to the B-40, where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 4/12 @ A-45, Snap. The holding penalty is enforced but does not include an automatic first down.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-40, Snap. The return to the B-40 stands and the holding foul during the punt is disregarded.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. B77's holding is subject to postscrimmage kick enforcement since it occurred during the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. The action by B77 is a foul for holding. B77's holding foul is not covered by postscrimmage kick enforcement since it is before the kick. The 10-yard penalty for B77's holding foul is enforced at the previous spot. The penalty yardage alone does not give Team A a first down, but defensive holding includes an automatic first down. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: Try at the B-3. A91's kick is successful and B77 lined up in the neutral zone. After the ball is dead, B54 commits a personal foul. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Try is good, A, Free Kick @ B-35, FK Rules only. There is no option to instead replay the try.
choice_b: Try is good, A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules – or – A, Retry @ B-¾, No clock.
choice_c: The try is nullified by B54's dead ball foul, and Team A must retry from the B-¾ with no kickoff option.
choice_d: Try is good, A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules, but the dead ball foul against B54 is disregarded once the try is scored.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Try is good, A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules – or – A, Retry @ B-¾, No clock. If Team A declines the offside penalty and accepts the point, the penalty for Team B's dead-ball foul is then enforced on the kickoff. If Team A elects to replay the try, both penalties against Team B are enforced before the snap.

tags: intentional grounding;tackle box exception;backward pass;loss of down;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/5 at the A-40. Midway through the 4th quarter, QB A12 drops back in the pocket to pass. Under a heavy rush, he flips a backward pass to back A22, who carries the ball outside the tackle box. About to be tackled, A22 at the A-34 throws a forward pass that crosses the neutral zone and lands in an area 20 yards away from the nearest Team A eligible receiver. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/11 @ A-34, Snap. A22's pass is legal because he is treated as the original passer under the tackle box exception.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. The pass is an incomplete legal forward pass since A22 received a backward pass first.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-34, Snap. The intentional grounding foul results in a turnover on downs handled as a change of possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/11 @ A-34, Snap. The throw by A22 is a foul for intentional grounding. There is no eligible receiver in the area, and the tackle-box rule exception applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul.

question_text: 3/5 at the A-40. Midway through the 4th quarter, QB A12 is in a shotgun formation. He muffs the snap at the A-35 and the ball is picked up by A22 inside the tackle box. Under a heavy rush, A22 gets outside the tackle box and at the A-33 throws the ball, and it falls incomplete beyond the neutral zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/16 @ A-24, Snap. A22's pass is intentional grounding since he threw it while outside the tackle box.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-33, Ready. The muffed snap is treated as a backward pass reception, awarding Team A a new set of downs.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-33, Snap. Since A22 was outside the tackle box, the incomplete pass results in loss of down and possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5 @ A-40, Snap. The pass by A22 is an incomplete legal forward pass. A22 controlled the backward pass that resulted from the snap and did not relinquish possession until he made the pass.

question_text: 3/10 at the A-30. Midway through the 2nd quarter, QB A11 drops back to pass. As he is about to be tackled at the A-20, A11 throws the ball forward to an area where there are no eligible receivers, and tackle A77 catches the pass at the A-28 and is tackled at the A-32. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-28, Snap. A77's catch is illegal touching, giving Team B the ball at the spot of the catch.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-32, Snap. A77's catch counts as a legal completion since the pass reached a Team A player.
choice_d: A, 4/20 @ A-20, Snap. The clock starts on the snap rather than remaining on the ready-for-play signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/20 @ A-20, Ready. The pass by A11 is an illegal forward pass, and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass. This is intentional grounding since A11 throws the ball into an area where there are no eligible Team A receivers to save loss of yardage. This is not illegal touching by A77, because the rule for illegal touching applies only to a legal forward pass.

tags: fumble caught airborne;out of bounds;no recovery;spot of fumble;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 at the A-25. During A21's run, he fumbles the ball at the A-33, which strikes the ground and bounces into the air. B49, in pursuit, jumps from well inbounds at the A-35 and gains firm control of the ball while airborne at the A-37, crosses the boundary, and returns to the ground out of bounds at the A-38. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-38, Ready. B49's airborne control completes the recovery even though he lands out of bounds.
choice_c: A, 3/2 @ A-38, Ready. The ball is returned to the out-of-bounds spot rather than the spot of the fumble.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-35, Ready. B49's recovery is completed at the point of initial control at the A-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2 @ A-33, Ready. There is no recovery by B49, as he returned to the ground out of bounds and did not complete the recovery. Since there was no recovery, this is considered a forward fumble out of bounds, and the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble and belongs to the fumbling team at that spot.

tags: 4th down short of line to gain;dead ball personal foul;succeeding spot;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/8 at the B-35. A21's run gains four yards and the ball is declared dead, after which B54 is called for a dead ball personal foul. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-16, Snap. The 15-yard penalty against B54 gives Team A a new series instead of Team B.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-31, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the dead ball spot at B-31 rather than the succeeding spot.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-16, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-16, Snap. The 4th down run by A21 was short of the line to gain and Team A's series is over, with a new series awarded to Team B. B54's penalty will be enforced 15 yards from the succeeding spot and will result in a 1st and 10 since the foul was before the ready for play of the new series. The clock starts on the snap.

tags: offsetting dead ball personal fouls;ejection stands;down unaffected;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/Goal at the B-3. Ball carrier A21 is downed at the one-yard line, and then B67 piles on. A21 retaliates by punching B67. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/Goal @ B-16, Ready. Both 15-yard penalties are enforced consecutively against Team B and Team A.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-1, Ready. Since both fouls are dead ball personal fouls, Team B is awarded a new series.
choice_c: A, 3/Goal @ B-1, Ready, but A21 is not ejected since the fouls offset and cancel each other.
choice_d: A, 3/Goal @ B-1, Ready. A21 is ejected.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal @ B-1, Ready. A21 is ejected. Both B67's and A21's fouls are dead ball personal fouls, and when dead ball personal fouls are reported to the Referee and none of the penalties have been completed, the yardage penalties cancel. The new down (3rd down) had been established before the fouls occurred and is unaffected. A21 is ejected even though the yardage penalties cancel.

tags: instant replay;second forward pass;lateral vs pass distinction;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 1/10 at the B-40. Shotgun QB A1 catches the snap at the B-45 and flips it forward to receiver A80 at the B-44, who was coming in motion toward him. A80 attempts to hand the ball from the B-44 to A88, who is moving in the opposite direction at the B-43. A88 gets the ball and runs for a TD. There are no flags on the play. Replays show the ball clearly leave A80's hand and go into the air before A88 catches it. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A stands. Since there were no flags on the field, replay cannot create a foul that wasn't called live.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-44, Snap. The play is treated as a legal backward pass exchange between A80 and A88.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-43, Snap. The illegal second forward pass results in loss of possession rather than loss of down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/19 @ B-49, Snap. Replay can create a foul for a second forward pass during the down, and since A80 did not hand the ball to A88, it is illegal. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the second pass with a loss of down.

tags: instant replay;fumble at goal line;possession time in end zone;touchback overturn;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week14;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 1/10 at the 50. B3 intercepts A1's pass at the B-40 and runs down the sideline with an unobstructed path to the end zone. The ball comes loose as he crosses the goal line, and he attempts to regain control in the end zone while upright. The ball eventually hits the ground and bounces over the end line. The officials signal TD. Replays show the ball clearly come loose before it crosses the goal line and that B3 regained control in the end zone with his right foot down, then immediately lost it. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B stands, since B3 got a foot down with control before losing the ball in the end zone.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. B3's momentary control in the end zone is enough for a touchback in Team B's favor.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-40, Snap. Since the ball came loose before the goal line, the spot reverts to where B3 lost control.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-20, Snap. The ball in relation to the goal line and recovery of the loose ball in the end zone are both reviewable. Since the ball came loose short of the goal line it is a fumble, and B3 must regain possession in the end zone to score a TD. He got control and a body part down but did not have the ball long enough to perform an act common to the game, so there is no possession and the result is a touchback.

question_text: 3/Goal at the B-6. At the snap, A88 is on the end of the line of scrimmage 10 yards from the tackle position and A84 is in the slot between the tackle and A88. Just before the Team A passer releases the ball, A88 contacts B21 three yards beyond the neutral zone. The pass is thrown to A84, who has moved in front of and outside the spot where A88 had contacted B21. A84 catches the pass at the B-1 and turns up into the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands, since the contact by A88 occurred away from the eventual receiver A84.
choice_b: A, 3/Goal, B-6, Snap. The touchdown is nullified but no penalty yardage is assessed since it is a judgment foul.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-6, Snap. Offensive pass interference on a scoring play results in loss of possession for Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-16, Snap. A88 has committed a foul for Offensive Pass Interference. The penalty is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap as the play results in a touchdown.

tags: nose wipe signal;automatic unsportsmanlike conduct;2026 rule change;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/20 at the 50. QB A12 rolls right and spots receiver A88, who has beaten cornerback B21 with a double move and makes a diving catch for a first down at the B-20. After the play, A88 gets up and gives a demonstrative "nose wipe" just after getting up from the ground. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready. The nose wipe is a bench warning only for the first occurrence and carries no penalty.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The unsportsmanlike conduct penalty is enforced as a live ball foul instead of a dead ball foul.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The unsportsmanlike conduct foul is severe enough to cost Team A the first down and possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. Rule 9-2-1-a-4-b now lists the nose wipe signal with the throat slash as an automatic unsportsmanlike conduct foul, so the action by A88 is a foul. The play made the line to gain, and the unsportsmanlike conduct is penalized as a dead ball foul, so it is 1st and 10 after enforcement. A88 will have a "counter" for unsportsmanlike conduct, and if he gets a second one, he will be ejected.

tags: kicking formation;2x2 numbering exception;illegal shift after snapper set;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 2 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A8 and A31 to the right. All five players are in the tackle box and A49 has his hands above his knees. A29 is in the shield five yards behind the line of scrimmage. After this initial formation, there is a shift and A29 and A31 switch positions as A49 puts his hands on the ball. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Since the kick resulted in a touchback, the shift by A29 and A31 is irrelevant to enforcement.
choice_c: A, 4/10, B-45, Snap. The shift is legal because A31 is not one of the five numbering-exception linemen.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap only. Team B has no option to instead replay 4th down at the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, 4/15, 50, Snap. Team A commits a foul for Illegal Formation when the ball is snapped. The five players in the 2 X 2 formation are locked into their positions once they line up after the snapper is in his position. Once established as a numbering exception, A31 may not shift and must remain in his position as part of the 2 X 2 formation. Team B may enforce the 5-yard penalty at the previous spot and repeat 4th down, or tack on the 5 yards to where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-35. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 3 X 1 formation with A33, A27, and A8 to the left of snapper A49, and A31 to the right. All five players are in the tackle box with no other teammates on the line of scrimmage within the tackle box. A49 snaps the ball to A94, who kicks the ball, and it goes out of bounds at the B-25. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/10, A-35, Snap. The formation is legal since all five players are within the tackle box regardless of split.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap only. There is no option to instead replay 4th down at the previous spot.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The penalty is enforced at the spot where the kick went out of bounds rather than being a Team B option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap – or – A, 4/15, A-30, Snap. Team A commits a foul at the snap for an Illegal Formation. Team A must be in a 2 X 2 formation to meet the requirements for numbering exceptions. With a 3 X 1 set, even if all players are in the tackle box, the formation is not legal. Team B may enforce the 5-yard penalty at the previous spot and repeat 4th down, or tack on the 5 yards to where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them.

tags: kicking formation;legal scrimmage kick formation;no back in frame;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the A-45. Team A has a potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards directly behind the snapper and a three-person shield five yards deep that is not aligned within the frame of the snapper. Team A's snapper, A55, is lined up with no other linemen in the tackle box. A80 is lined up wide to the left side of the formation, and wide to the right of the snapper are A60, A65, A70, A75, and A88, who is on the end of the line. A55 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team A is flagged for illegal formation since the snapper has no linemen in the tackle box.
choice_b: A, 4/10, A-45, Snap. The formation is illegal because the shield is not aligned within the frame of the snapper.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-20, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap for a scrimmage kick down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. This is a legal scrimmage kick formation as Team A meets the requirement of having 5 linemen numbered 50-79. There is no back lined up in the frame of the snapper, and it is obvious a kick will be attempted.

question_text: Late in the 4th quarter with the receiving team behind by 2 points, receiver B21 is in position to catch A96's punt at the 50 and gives a valid fair catch signal. Just before the ball is touched by B21, A80 makes contact with B21, the ball then touches B21 at the 50, and is recovered by A80 at the B-45. After the play, the game clock displays 0:00. The receiving team coach requests a Fair Catch Kick just before the penalty is enforced. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-35, Snap. Since the game clock reads 0:00, the game ends before the penalty can be enforced.
choice_b: B, Fair-Catch Kick, B-45, Game extended for an untimed down. The kick is set up from the spot of the foul relative to Team B's own territory.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-35, Snap. Team A retains possession from its recovery, and no fair catch kick option exists.
choice_d: B, Fair-Catch Kick, A-35, Game extended for an untimed down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, Fair-Catch Kick, A-35, Game extended for an untimed down. A80 commits a foul for Kick Catching Interference, and Team B has an awarded fair catch at the A-35. The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, and Team B will set up for a Fair Catch Kick at the A-35. The game will be extended for an untimed down to allow the Fair Catch Kick. The Team B head coach must inform the Referee of this option before the ball is next ready for play.

tags: fair catch;fair catch kick option;illegal formation enforcement spot;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the A-10. Late in the 4th quarter after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team A punts and receiver B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid fair catch signal. Team A was in an illegal formation at the snap. The receiving team head coach requests a Fair Catch Kick. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, Fair-Catch Kick, A-40, Kick. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the original line of scrimmage rather than the catch spot.
choice_c: A, 4/15 @ A-5, Snap. Team A's illegal formation foul forces a repeat of 4th down instead of a fair catch kick.
choice_d: B, Fair-Catch Kick, 50, Kick. The illegal formation penalty is declined automatically once a Fair Catch Kick is requested.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, Fair-Catch Kick, A-45, Kick. The ball is dead when caught by B21 at the 50. Since the receiving team coach requests a Fair Catch Kick, the 5-yard penalty for the illegal formation is enforced from where the ball's subsequent dead ball spot belongs to the receiving team, which is the 50. The receiving team could have instead enforced the 5-yard penalty at the previous spot and repeated 4th down, but selecting the Fair Catch Kick option negates that opportunity.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-10. Late in the 4th quarter after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team A punts and receiver B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid signal. The receiving team head coach does not elect a Fair Catch Kick, and after the ready for play, the offense comes to the line with 1st and 10 at the 50. While at the line and the QB calling signals, defender B55 jumps into the neutral zone and makes contact with the snapper. After the penalty is enforced and before the next ready for play, the head coach for the offense now requests a Fair Catch Kick. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, Fair-Catch Kick, 50, Kick. The Fair Catch Kick option remains available until the next snap after any penalty.
choice_b: A, 1/5 @ B-45, Snap. No option for a Fair Catch Kick.
choice_c: A, 1/5 @ B-45, Snap. Team A may still request a Fair Catch Kick at the next dead ball since the down had not yet been snapped.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ 50, Ready. B55's neutral zone infraction is declined so Team A can pursue the Fair Catch Kick.
