question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. A12's field goal attempt is partially blocked and the ball comes to rest untouched beyond the neutral zone at the B-10. Team A was in an Illegal Formation at the snap. What is the ruling?
explanation: The unsuccessful field goal attempt was untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone, and since the previous spot was not between the B-20 and the goal line, the ball is returned to the previous spot, the B-30 (8-4-2-b-1). "Tack-on" does not apply on field goal attempts, so Team B will decline the penalty for Team A's Illegal Formation (6-3-13).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. A12's field goal attempt is partially blocked and the ball comes to rest untouched beyond the neutral zone at the B-10. During the kick, B67 committed a Personal Foul at the B-28. What is the ruling?
explanation: All conditions of postscrimmage kick enforcement are satisified (10-2-3-b), so Team B is considered to be in possession. Since the unsuccessful field goal attempt was untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone, and since the previous spot was not between the B-20 and the goal line, the basic spot is the previous spot, the B-30 (2-5-11-Special Case 1-a). Spot of the foul (B-28) by the team in possession is behind the basic spot, so enforcement is from the spot of the foul (10-2-2-c-1). B-28 → B-14.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. The kick strikes the ground at the B-16 and A52 recovers while grounded at the B-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: The kick did not cross the neutral zone, so all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). A52 could have advanced the ball (6-3-1-a), but since he did not, the ball belongs to B at that spot (5-1-1-c).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. The kick strikes the ground at the B-14 and A52 recovers while grounded at the B-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though the kick crossed the neutral zone and is dead when recovered by A52 (6-1-6-a), it was declared dead behind the neutral zone, so all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-3). The ball belongs to B at that spot (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. The kick strikes the ground at the B-16, then touches B99 at the B-17. A52 recovers while grounded at the B-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: The kick did not cross the neutral zone, so all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). B99's touching is disregarded since the ball did not cross the neutral zone (6-3-3), so the ball belongs to B at the spot of recovery (5-1-1-c).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. The kick strikes the ground at the B-14, then touches B99 at the B-16. A52 recovers while grounded at the B-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since B99 touched the kick after it crossed the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). His touching allows any player to legally recover recover the ball (6-3-3), so the ball belongs to A at the spot of recovery (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. A10's pass intended for A24 falls incomplete. B35 interfered with A24 on the far sideline in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock - Option on Lateral Placement.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock - Far Hash.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock - Near Hash.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-2 - No Clock - Far Hash.
explanation: By rule, the penalty is half the distance to the goal, B-3 → B-1½ (10-2-5-b). Since the foul was by Team B, the ball may be relocated to a different lateral position (8-3-2-c).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-15. A3's field goal attempt is partially blocked. A10's pass is complete to A24 for two points; however, Team A was in an Illegal Formation at the snap. The Team A head coach requests that the ball be relocated to the middle of the field for a one-point Try. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Middle.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Near Hash unless Team A calls a Time Out.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Near Hash even if Team A calls a Time Out.
explanation: The ball may be relocated after a Team B (not a Team A) foul or a charged charged timeout to either team, unless the timeout is preceded by a Team A foul or offsetting fouls (8-3-2-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3 in the middle of the field. A4's kick was successful; however: B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Team A may take the point off the board and go for two, and it may be A - Try @ B-1½ - Far Hash.

choice_c: The penalty is declined by rule.
explanation: Team A shall have the option of declining the score and repeating the Try after enforcement, or declining the penalty and accepting the score (8-3-3-b). Since the foul was by Team B, the ball may be relocated to a different lateral position (8-3-2-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3 in the middle of the field. A4's kick was successful; however: Team A was in an Illegal Formation and B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the ruling?
choice_a: It may be A - Try @ B-3 - Far Hash.

explanation: If both teams foul during the Try and Team B fouls before the change of possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated, even if additional fouls occur after the change of possession. Any repeat of the Try after offsetting fouls must be from the previous spot (8-3-3-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3 in the middle of the field. A4's kick was successful; however: B96 was flagged for Leaping. What is the ruling?

Since the foul was by Team B, the ball may be relocated to a different lateral position (8-3-2-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3 after a Touchdown as time expired in the 4th quarter to make the score A-20, B-21. A5's kick is successful; however, B96 is flagged for: roughing the kicker. What is the ruling?

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B43 at the B-1 and returned 99 yards for an apparent two points; however: while the pass was in flight, B96 roughed A12, and during the return, A87 targeted B56 (confirmed by RO). What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score.  A - Try @ B-18 - No Clock.
choice_b: Try over.  A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.

explanation: If both teams foul during the Try and Team B fouls before the change of possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated, even if additional fouls occur after the change of possession (8-3-3-a). A87 is still disqualified (10-1-4).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B43 at the B-1 and returned 99 yards for an apparent two points; however: during the return, B96 illegally blocked A34 below the waist, and the return, A87 targeted B56 (confirmed by RO). What is the ruling?

A87 is disqualified.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B43 at the B-1 and returned 99 yards for an apparent two points; however: at the snap, Team A was in an Illegal Formation, and during the return, a Team B assistant coach ran onto the field and knocked down the Line Judge. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score.  A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock.

choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: Each team committed a live ball foul during a down in which there was a change of possession, and the team in final possession fouled before the change of possession. So, this does not meet the criteria of 10-1-4-Exception 1, and thus the fouls offset and the down must be repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4's kickoff is fielded by B21 at the B-20 and returned to the B-35 where he is tackled. Team A was offside on the free kick. During the return, B43 clipped A86 at the B-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules is the only option.
choice_b: Both A and B are options.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap is the only option.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap is the only option.
explanation: Team B's foul occurred after change of possession, so they may accept offsetting fouls and replay down, or decline penalty for Team A's foul and have the penalty for their foul enforced. The basic spot on this return is the end of the run (10-2-2-d-2-c), and since foul (B-40) by team in possession occurred beyond basic (B-35), enforce from basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). B-35 → B-20.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-10. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the first extra period. A41 carries to the B-5 where he fumbles. B36 picks up the loose ball and carries it: to the B-10 where he fumbles. A7 recovers while grounded at the B-15. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A's possession series continues.  A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Untimed.
choice_b: A's possession series continues.  A - 4/10 @ B-15 - Untimed.
choice_c: A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 25-yard line.
explanation: The ball remains alive after a change of team possession until it is declared dead. However, Team A may not have a first down if it regains possession after a change of team possession (3-1-3-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-10. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the first extra period. A41 carries to the B-5 where he fumbles. B36 picks up the loose ball and carries it: to the A-5 where A82 brings him down by the face mask. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 25-yard line.
choice_b: A's possession series continues.  A - 3/20 @ B-25 - Untimed.
choice_c: A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 40-yard line.
explanation: Penalties against either team are declined by rule in extra periods (3-1-3-g-1) unless FUDL. A FMM is a personal foul, but not a flagrant personal foul, so penalty does not carry over.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-10. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the first extra period. A41 carries to the B-5 where he fumbles. B36 picks up the loose ball and carries it: across A's goal line. During B36's return, B52 delivered an illegal blind-side block with targeting against A84. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score.  A's possession series continues.  A - 1/G @ B-5 - Untimed.
choice_b: No score.  A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 40-yard line.
choice_c: No score.  A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 25-yard line.
explanation: Team B's foul cancels their score (3-1-3-g-2), but since TGT is a flagrant person foul, the penalty carries over to the succeeding spot (3-1-3-g-1-Exception). DQ A84.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-10. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the first extra period. A41 carries to the B-5 where he fumbles. B36 picks up the loose ball and carries it: across A's goal line. During B36's return, the Line Judge ran into a Team B assistant coach who was on the field. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score.  A's possession series continues.  A - 1/G @ B-5 - Untimed.
choice_c: No score.  A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 25-yard line.
choice_d: No score.  A's possession series is over.  B begins their possession series at the 40-yard line.
explanation: Since Team B's foul is a live-ball treated as a dead-ball, the score counts (3-1-3-g-2), and at that instant Team B wins the game (3-1-3-f). The foul will go unpenalized.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. The score is A-28, B-28 in the second possession series of the first extra period. A9's pass is intercepted by B34 and returned across A's goal line. After the pass was thrown, B75 roughed A9. During B34's return, A68 committed a flagrant personal foul against B41. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A's possession series continues.  A - 2/5 @ B-20 - Untimed.
choice_b: A's possession series is over.  The second extra period with either A or B at the 25-yard line.
choice_c: A's possession series is over.  The second extra period with A at the 40-yard line or B at the 12½-yard line.
explanation: Each team committed a live ball foul during a down with a change of possession, and the team in final possession fouled before the change of possession. Offsetting fouls (10-1-4). DQ B41

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. The score is A-28, B-28 in the second possession series of the first extra period. A9's pass is intercepted by B34 and returned across A's goal line. During B34's return, B75 illegally blocked A9 below the waist and A68 committed a flagrant personal foul against B41. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A's possession series continues.  A - 2/5 @ B-20 - Untimed.
choice_b: A's possession series is over. The second extra period begins with A at the 40-yard line or B at the 12½-yard line.
choice_c: A's possession series is over.  The second extra period begins with either A or B at the 25-yard line.
explanation: Since both teams fouled with B's foul after the COP, the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated, but the penalty for A's FUDL foul carries over (3-1-3-g-3). DQ B41.

question_text: 4/G @ B-5. The score is A-31, B-39 in the second possession series of the second extra period. A12's pass is complete to A84 in the end zone to make the score A-37, B-39. After the pass was thrown, B96 roughed A12. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Both B and C are options.
choice_b: The penalty is declined by rule.  A - Try @ B-3 - Untimed.
choice_c: The penalty may be enforced on the Try.  A - Try @ B-1½ - Untimed.
choice_d: The penalty may be enforced to begin a potential third extra period. A at the 1½-yard line or B at the 18-yard line.
explanation: Fifteen-yard penalties for personal and unsportsmanlike fouls are enforced on the Try or from the succeeding spot in extra periods (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/G @ B-5. The score is A-31, B-39 in the second possession series of the second extra period. A12's pass is complete to A84 in the end zone to make the score A-37, B-39. There was no foul on the touchdown play. On the Try, A12's pass is complete to A84 in the end zone to make the score A-39, B-39. After the pass was thrown, B96 roughed A12. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The penalty is declined by rule.  The third extra period will begin with either A or B at the 3-yard line.
choice_b: The third extra period will begin with A at the 1½-yard line or B at the 18-yard line.
explanation: Team A may accept the score with penalties for personal and unsportsmanlike fouls enforced from the succeeding spot in extra periods (8-3-3-b).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. The score is A-17, B-21 with 0:06 remaining in the 4th quarter. A8's pass is complete to A19 at the B-5. He runs to the B-2 where he is tackled as time expires. If more than one option exists, select the best for Team A. B31 brought A19 down with an illegal horse-collar tackle. What is the ruling?
explanation: The foul occurred during a run which ended beyond the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-2). Since the foul was by the team not in possession, enforcement is from the basic spot (10-2-2-c-3). B-2 → B-1. Since time expired during the down and the penalty for a live ball foul was accepted, the period is extended (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. The score is A-17, B-21 with 0:06 remaining in the 4th quarter. A8's pass is complete to A19 at the B-5. He runs to the B-2 where he is tackled as time expires. If more than one option exists, select the best for Team A. B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the ruling?
explanation: Team A would like to decline the penalty (A - 1/G @ B-2), but the period can only be extended if there is accepted penalty (3-2-3-a-1). The penalty is enforced from the previous spot (B-20 → B-15) for an untimed down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-15. The score is A-24, B-23 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A6 takes the snap, runs around outside the tackle box until time expires, and then throws a pass which lands out of bounds behind the neutral zone with no eligible receivers in the area. If more than one option exists, select the best for Team B. The pass was thrown from the A-2. What is the ruling?
choice_b: No Foul.  Game Over.  A Wins 24-23.
choice_c: Foul.  Game Over.  A Wins 24-23.  Period not extended since statement of penalty includes loss of down.
explanation: Since the ball was not legally grounded beyond the neutral zone, this is ING and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h). This brings up B - 1/G @ A-2. The period may be extended at the option of the offended team, Team B (3-2-3-a-1). Team B will opt for the untimed down and a chance to win the game with a touchdown or field goal.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-15. The score is A-24, B-23 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A6 takes the snap, runs around outside the tackle box until time expires, and then throws a pass which lands out of bounds behind the neutral zone with no eligible receivers in the area. If more than one option exists, select the best for Team B. The pass was thrown from A's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Foul.  Game Over.  A Wins 24-23.
choice_c: Foul.  B Leads 25-24.  A - F/K @ A-20 - Untimed.
explanation: Since the ball was not legally grounded beyond the neutral zone, this is ING and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h). This accepted penalty leaves the ball behind Team A's goal line, so the result is a safety (8-5-1-b). Team B now leads 25-24, and will opt not to extend the period (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-34. A31 loses his helmet at the end of the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
choice_b: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_c: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
explanation: Line to gain made, so helmet coming off not only reason for stopping clock. So, no option for runoff (3-3-9-b). Play clock to 25 seconds since helmet off offensive player (3-3-9-b-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-36. B78 is injured during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
explanation: Line to gain not made, so helmet coming off only reason for stopping clock. So, option for runoff (3-3-5-f). Play clock to 40 seconds since injury to defensive player (3-3-5-f-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-36. A31 loses his helmet at the end of the down, and B78 is injured during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
explanation: Clock stopped for A helmet and B injury, so no runoff (3-3-5-f / 3-3-9-b). Play clock to 40 seconds because any defensive reason "trumps" any offensive reason (3-3-5-f-1 / 3-3-9-b-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-36. A31 loses his helmet at the end of the down and A54 is injured during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_b: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 25 seconds.
choice_c: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock at 40 seconds.
explanation: Both helmet and injury were on A, so option for runoff (3-3-5-f / 3-3-9-b). Play clock to 25 seconds because both the helmet and the injury are on the offense (3-3-5-f-1 / 3-3-9-b-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-36. A54 holds during the down. A31 loses his helmet at the end of the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Ready or Snap at Team B's option.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Snap by rule.
choice_c: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Ready by rule.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock in accordance with runoff options.
explanation: Clock stopped to complete penalty and for helmet, so no runoff (3-3-9-b). Foul by team behind, so clock starts on Ready (3-3-2-e-4 / 3-3-2-e-16 / 3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with less than one minute remains in the 4th quarter. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is tackled by B96. A31 is marked down at the: B-36. B96 is lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. A31 loses his helmet at the end of the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Ready by rule.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Snap by rule.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock in accordance with runoff options.  or snap at Team A's option
choice_d: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Clock on the Ready or Snap at Team A's option.
explanation: Clock stopped to complete penalty and for helmet, so no runoff (3-3-9-b). Foul by team ahead, so clock at A's option (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. It is near the end of the 2nd quarter with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining, and is rushing to attempt a field goal before halftime. A78 false starts. 0:03 remain on the clock. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Half Over.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/15 @ B-30 - Ready.
explanation: FST causes clock to stop immediately, so option for runoff (3-4-4-a) which will end the half.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. It is near the end of the 2nd quarter with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining, and is rushing to attempt a field goal before halftime. A78 false starts, then B96 charges across the neutral zone and commits a personal foul. 0:03 remain on the clock. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/10 @ B-25 - Ready by rule.
choice_b: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Half Over.  The penalty for B96's foul is enforced on the 2nd half kickoff.
choice_c: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/10 @ B-25 - Snap at Team A's option.
choice_d: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready by rule.
explanation: FST causes clock to stop immediately, so option for runoff (3-4-4-a) which ends half right there. PF comes after, and penalized at succeeding spot (10-2-2-a / AR-3-4-4-V).

question_text: 2/15 @ B-30. It is near the end of the 2nd quarter with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining. A4 runs to the B-29 where he pitches forward to A35. A35 catches the pitch and is tackled at the B-25. The clock is stopped at 0:27. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/10 @ B-25 - Ready.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/19 @ B-34 - Ready.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/19 @ B-34 - Ready unless Team B declines the runoff.
explanation: IFP (7-3-2-a), but no runoff since pass complete (3-4-4-a / 3-4-4-a-3). Five yards from spot of pass. B-29 → B-34.

question_text: 2/15 @ B-30. It is near the end of the 2nd quarter with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining. A4 runs to the B-29 where he pitches forward to A35. A35 muffs the pitch and it strikes the ground. The clock is stopped at 0:27. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/19 @ B-34 - Ready.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/19 @ B-34 - Snap.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 3/19 @ B-34 - Ready unless Team B declines the runoff.
explanation: IFP (7-3-2-a) and runoff option since pass INC. (3-4-4-a / 3-4-4-a-3). Five yards from spot of pass. B-29 → B-34.

question_text: 2/15 @ B-30. It is near the end of the 2nd quarter with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining. A4 runs to the B-29 where he pitches forward to A35. B96 is offside at the snap. A35 muffs the pitch and it strikes the ground. The clock is stopped at 0:27. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 2/15 @ B-30 - Snap.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 2/15 @ B-30 - Ready.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  A - 2/15 @ B-30 - Ready unless Team B declines the runoff.
explanation: IFP (7-3-2-a) and DOF create offsetting fouls (10-1-4), so no runoff (3-4-4-e). Incomplete, so Snap (3-3-2-d-4).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A4 carries to the A-30 to bring up 2/5 @ A-30. After the play: two Team A substitutes enter, but no one leaves. Team A rushes to the line, and ten seconds after the previous play ended, the 12th and 13th Team A players leave the field, after which the ball is snapped and A4 carries to the A-35. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Live ball foul.  A - 2/10 @ A-25 - Ready.
choice_b: Dead ball foul.  A - 2/10 @ A-25 - Ready.
explanation: Team A may not keep more than 11 players in the formation for more than three seconds. This should be shut down more quickly, but certainly right after the snap for a dead ball foul (3-5-3-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A4 carries to the A-30 to bring up 2/5 @ A-30. After the play: one Team B substitute enters. Team A rushes to the line without substituting, and ten seconds after the previous play ended, the ball is snapped with the exiting 12th player one step away from the sideline. A4 is tackled at the A-30. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Dead ball foul.  A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Ready.
choice_c: Live ball foul.  A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Ready.
explanation: Team B may not have more than 11 players on the field when the ball is snapped. This is a live ball foul, and the play should be allowed to continue (3-5-3-b). Team A will decline the penalty.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A4 carries to the A-30 to bring up 2/5 @ A-30. After the play: Team does not hurry and does not substitute. One Team B substitute enters, but no one leaves. Team A comes to the line late in the play clock and, with the quarterback struggling to read the defense, allows the play clock to expire. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Delay of Game on A.  A - 2/10 @ A-30 - Ready.
choice_b: Illegal Substitution on B.  A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Ready.
explanation: In this case, when the play clock expires, officials should create a dead ball Illegal Substitution (AR-3-5-3-VII-b).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-19 where he fumbles. A31 recovers at the B-17 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball remains live when recovered by the fumbling player, and the advance is allowed (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line- to-gain is made, the clock will not stop, and the ball will remain at the dead ball spot.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-19 where he fumbles. A24 recovers at the B-17 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball is dead when recovered by the non-fumbling player, and since recovered beyond the fumble spot, it is returned to the fumble spot (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line-to-gain is made, and the clock will not stop.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-19 where he fumbles. A31 recovers at the B-21 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball remains live when recovered by the fumbling player, and the advance is allowed (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line-to-gain is made, the clock will not stop, and the ball will remain at the dead ball spot.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-19 where he fumbles. A24 recovers at the B-21 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball is dead when recovered by the non- fumbling player, and since recovered behind the fumble spot, it remains at the dead ball spot (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line-to-gain is not made, and the clock will stop (3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-21 where he fumbles. A31 recovers at the B-19 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball remains live when recovered by the fumbling player, and the advance is allowed (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line-to-gain is made, the clock will not stop, and the ball will remain at the dead ball spot.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. A31 carries to the B-21 where he fumbles. A24 recovers at the B-19 and advances to the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball is dead when recovered by the non- fumbling player, and since recovered beyond the fumble spot, it is returned to the fumble spot (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The line-to-gain is not made, and the clock will stop (3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-44. The score is A-24, B-28, and the clock is running with 4:41 remaining in the 4th quarter. Team A is set in a legal formation with 30 seconds remaining on the play clock. QB A7 is in position 5 yards behind the center and claps as if to call for the snap. When A7 claps, the three receivers that were set tight to the left side of the formation abruptly step back to shift out toward the numbers. Defensive end B90 jumps across the neutral zone and contacts TE A88 in a reaction to the shift. What is the ruling?
explanation: Any movement by one or more players that simulates action at the snap is a False Start, and 7-1-2-b-1 specifically includes "abruptly shifting a player or players that simulates the start of a play". The action by these three receivers is a foul.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The free kick is muffed at the B-10, bounces and is headed toward Team B’s goal line. At the B-4, B27 recovers the kick and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B27 is running in the end zone where A41 tackles him. During B27’s run, B50 blocks A81 low at the B-6. What is the ruling?
explanation: The Momentum Exception is in effect (8-5-1-a-Exception), so even though B27 was tackled in the end zone, the related run by definition ended at the B-4 (2-25-8-h). Since the foul occurred during B27's run, the basic spot is thus the B-4 (10-2-2-d-1-c) … and since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. B1 signals for a fair catch at the B-10, but lets the punt go over his head. After the signal, B1 blocks A88 above the waist at the B-12. The punt bounces at the B-5 and is in the air over the end zone when A9 bats the ball back into the field of play. A21 possesses the ball at the B-4. What is the ruling?
explanation: A player of Team B who has made a valid or invalid signal for a fair catch and does not touch the ball may not subsequently block an opponent (6-5-4), so B1 has committed a foul. A9's bat of the kick over B's end zone is not a foul, but is a violation for Illegal Touching (6-3-11). B1's foul satisfies all of the conditions for PSK enforcement (10-2-3- b), and whenever 6-3-11 is in effect, the PSK (basic) spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-Special Case 2). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) is behind the basic spot, enforcement of the 10-yard penalty is half-the- distance (10-2-6) from the spot of the foul, B-12 → B-6 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/13 @ A-27. QB A7 completes a pass to A87 at the A-41 where he is tackled. B20 is flagged for holding receiver A88 at the A-29 prior to the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since a legal forward pass play is the interval between the snap and when a legal forward pass is complete, incomplete or intercepted (2-30-1), B20's foul occurred during a passing play, and the basic spot would be the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). Enforcement of the 10-yard penalty, A-27 → A-37, would not be as advantageous as the result of the play, so Team A will decline the penalty.

question_text: 2/13 @ A-27. QB A7 can’t find an open receiver, but he is able to scramble to the A-41 where he is tackled. Prior to A7 leaving the tackle box, B20 is flagged for holding receiver A88 at the A-29. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A7 intended this to be a passing play, it was just a running play … and since the related run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The foul was by the team not in possession, so enforcement of the 10-yard penalty is from the basic spot, A-41 → B-49 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/12 @ A-38. QB A15 receives the snap and sets up at the A-32 for a pass. At the snap RB A22 releases to the right in an effort to set up to receive a pass. LB B55 tackles A22 outside the tackle box at the A-36 thinking he was the ball carrier. A15 is sacked at the A-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A15 intended this to be a passing play, it was just a running play … and since the related run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The foul was by the team not in possession, so enforcement of the 10-yard penalty is from the basic spot, A-38 → A-48 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. B35 signals for and completes a fair catch of A2's punt at the B-10. There were two Team A players wearing jersey number 82 during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: If penalty accepted, it will be A - 4/15 @ 50 - Snap.
choice_b: If penalty accepted, it will be B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap.
choice_c: Both A and B are options.
choice_d: Neither A nor B is an option.
explanation: No two players of the same team may participate in the same down wearing identical numbers (1-4-2-b). Even though the penalty statement accompanying this rule says only "five yards from the previous spot" and does not explicitly give another option (as is the case with many other rules -- for example, 7-1-4), rule 6-3-13 is clear that penalties for all fouls by the kicking team (other than kick catch interference) during a scrimmage kick play (except field goal attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced at the previous spot as the basic spot (here, B-45 → 50) or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (here, B-10 → B-15). The purpose of this rule (as well as corresponding rule 6-1-8 for free kicks) is to prevent re-kicks. This answer and interpretation were presented to and are supported by the Rules Editor.

question_text: 3/8 @ B-38. 0:47 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12 rolls to his right under pursuit by B99. As B99 begins to tackle A12 a pass is thrown to A86 that is caught at the B-29, where A86 goes out of bounds (0:37). There is indisputable video evidence that A12’s knee was on the ground at the B-42 (0:41) prior to his release of the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, the ball would have been declared dead at the B-42 with 0:41 remaining on the clock and the clock would have continued to run. Replay is thus creating a running clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, and so there is a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). The clock is reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:41), and then the runoff is applied (0:41 → 0:31). Either team could have prevented the runoff by calling a time out, if one had been available. Unlike runoffs for fouls, helmets, and injuries, only a time out can be used to avoid a "Replay runoff". Other runoffs may simply be declined by the offended team.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-44. The score is A-24, B-28, and the clock is running with 4:41 remaining in the 4th quarter. Team A is set in a legal formation with 30 seconds remaining on the play clock. QB A7 is in position 5 yards behind the center and claps as if to call for the snap. When A7 claps, the three receivers that were set tight to the left side of the formation abruptly step back to shift out toward the numbers. Defensive end B90 jumps across the neutral zone and contacts TE A88 in a reaction to the shift. What if this occurred with 0:41 remaining in the 4th quarter, and Team A had one time out remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/9 @ B-49 - Snap (0:31), with no option for either team to influence the clock.
choice_c: A - 3/9 @ B-49 - Ready (0:41), since Team B may not decline a runoff after the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_d: A - 3/9 @ B-49 - Ready (0:31), with no option for Team A to use their remaining timeout to avoid the runoff.
explanation: Since the foul occurred following the Two-Minute Timeout and caused the clock the stop immediately, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a). Team B may accept (Ready - 0:31) or decline (Snap - 0:41) the runoff (3-4-4-c). If Team B accepts the runoff, Team A may then use their remaining timeout (Snap - 0:41) to avoid the runoff (3-4-4-d).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The free kick is muffed at the B-10, bounces and is headed toward Team B’s goal line. At the B-4, B27 recovers the kick and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B27 is running in the end zone where A41 tackles him. During B27’s run, B50 blocks A81 low at the B-6. What if B27 recovered the kick at the B-4 and carried it into the end zone on his own (no momentum)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, since the basic spot is the end of B27's run rather than the goal line.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, since Team A retains the ball after the accepted penalty.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the Momentum Exception still applying even without contact from A41.
explanation: Unless the momentum exception is in effect (as in the original question), when a foul occurs after a change of team possession in the field of play and the related run ends in the end zone, the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-b). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). When an accepted penalty for a foul leaves the ball on or behind the offending team’s goal line, it is a safety (8-5-1-b).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The free kick is muffed at the B-10, bounces and is headed toward Team B’s goal line. At the B-4, B27 recovers the kick and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B27 is running in the end zone where A41 tackles him. During B27’s run, B50 blocks A81 low at the B-6. What if B27 recovered the kick in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, with the foul enforced from the previous spot rather than the spot of the foul.
choice_b: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, since a foul after a touchback always results in a safety kick.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-3 - Snap, since the enforcement spot favors the team not in possession.
explanation: The result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). When a foul occurs after a change of team possession in the end zone, the related run ends in the end zone, and the result of the play is a touchback, the basic spot is the succeeding spot (10-2-2-d-2-a), which in the case of a free kick is the B-25 (8-6-2). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, B-6 → B-3 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. B1 signals for a fair catch at the B-10, but lets the punt go over his head. After the signal, B1 blocks A88 above the waist at the B-12. The punt bounces at the B-5 and is in the air over the end zone when A9 bats the ball back into the field of play. A21 possesses the ball at the B-4. What if A9 got to the ball before it got to B's end zone, batting it backward at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/10 @ A-45 - Snap, since A9's backward bat is treated the same as his earlier illegal touching.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the basic spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, with the penalty enforced the full 10 yards rather than half-the-distance.
explanation: A9's bat is backward and thus legal (9-4-1-c), but B1 has still committed a foul by blocking after a fair catch signal without having touched the ball (6-5-4). B1's foul satisfies all of the conditions for PSK enforcement (10-2-3-b), and the PSK (basic) spot is the end of the kick, the B-4 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) is beyond the basic spot, enforcement of the 10-yard penalty is half-the-distance (10-2-6) from the basic spot, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. B1 signals for a fair catch at the B-10, but lets the punt go over his head. After the signal, B1 blocks A88 above the waist at the B-12. The punt bounces at the B-5 and is in the air over the end zone when A9 bats the ball back into the field of play. A21 possesses the ball at the B-4. What if -- rather than letting the punt go over his head -- B1 muffed the kick, and A9's bat was above B's end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since Team B still benefits from the illegal touching violation.
choice_c: A - 4/20 @ A-45 - Snap, with no adjustment made for B1's now-legal block.
choice_d: A - 4/10 @ A-45 - Snap, since B1's touching of the kick has no effect on A9's bat.
explanation: Since B1 touched the kick, his block after a fair catch signal is legal (6-5-4); however, the touching of the kick means that 6-3-11 no longer applies, and A9's bat in the EZ is illegal (9-4-1-c). A is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (6-3-3), so enforcement must be from the previous spot, A-45 → A-35 (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 2/13 @ A-27. QB A7 completes a pass to A87 at the A-41 where he is tackled. B20 is flagged for holding receiver A88 at the A-29 prior to the pass. What if -- in holding the receiver -- B20 twisted and turned A88's face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-49 - Ready, since B20's face mask foul is enforced the same as his holding foul.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-41 - Ready, with Team A declining the penalty as in the original question.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-39 - Ready, with the personal foul enforced only 5 yards from the end of the run.
explanation: B20's face mask foul is a personal foul (9-1-8-b), and the penalty for any Team B personal or unsportsmanlike foul during a legal forward pass is enforced from the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e), which here would be A-41 → B-44.

question_text: 2/13 @ A-27. QB A7 can’t find an open receiver, but he is able to scramble to the A-41 where he is tackled. Prior to A7 leaving the tackle box, B20 is flagged for holding receiver A88 at the A-29. What if -- in holding the receiver -- B20 twisted and turned A88's face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-41 - Ready, since a personal foul is always enforced from the previous spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-49 - Ready, treating the run the same as the passing-play scenario in question 4.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-39 - Ready, with the penalty enforced only 5 yards from the end of the run.
explanation: Even though A7 intended this to be a passing play, it was just a running play … and since the related run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The foul was by the team not in possession, so enforcement of the 15-yard penalty is from the basic spot, A-41 → B-44 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/12 @ A-38. QB A15 receives the snap and sets up at the A-32 for a pass. At the snap RB A22 releases to the right in an effort to set up to receive a pass. LB B55 tackles A22 outside the tackle box at the A-36 thinking he was the ball carrier. A15 is sacked at the A-32. What if A15 faked a handoff to A22? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-48 - Ready, since B55 still committed a foul for tackling a non-runner.
choice_b: A - 3/18 @ A-32 - Ready, with the play blown dead for an inadvertent whistle.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-46 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul.
explanation: Defensive players may not use hands and arms to tackle, hold or otherwise illegally obstruct an opponent other than a runner (9-3-4-d), and by definition, a runner is a player in possession of a live ball or simulating possession of a live ball (2-27-7-a). The fake handoff is enough to give B55 the benefit of the doubt, so there is no foul.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. B35 signals for and completes a fair catch of A2's punt at the B-10. There were two Team A players wearing jersey number 82 during the down. What if B35 muffed the kick with the subsequent loose ball recovered by A46 at the B-15? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where B35 fumbled.
choice_c: A - 4/15 @ 50 - Snap, or B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, at the option of Team B, the same options as the original question.
choice_d: A - 4/10 @ B-45 - Snap, since the penalty is declined by rule once the ball is fumbled.
explanation: Rule 6-3-13 does not apply since the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, so the only option is previous spot enforcement, B-45 → 50.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. B35 signals for and completes a fair catch of A2's punt at the B-10. There were two Team A players wearing jersey number 82 during the down. What if -- in the original question -- the duplicate jersey numbers were worn by Team B players? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/15 @ 50 - Snap, with the same tack-on option available regardless of which team commits the foul.
choice_b: A - 4/5 @ B-40 - Snap (Accept) or B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap (Decline).
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B.
choice_d: A - 4/5 @ B-40 - Snap, with no option for Team A to instead decline the penalty.
explanation: If Team A wishes to accept the penalty, their only option is previous spot enforcement, B-45 → B-40, with a re-kick. While "tack-on" enforcement was an option when the foul was on Team A, there is not a corresponding option for this foul by Team B because the foul occurred at the snap -- not during the kick -- and so postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply (10-2-3-b). Team A may likely choose to just decline the penalty and leave Team B in possession at the B-10.

question_text: 3/8 @ B-38. 0:47 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12 rolls to his right under pursuit by B99. As B99 begins to tackle A12 a pass is thrown to A86 that is caught at the B-29, where A86 goes out of bounds (0:37). There is indisputable video evidence that A12’s knee was on the ground at the B-42 (0:41) prior to his release of the pass. What if -- in making the tackle -- B99 twisted and turned A12's face mask? Assume Team B ahead in score. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-23 - Ready (0:31), with a 10-second runoff applied since Team B is ahead in score.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-23 - Snap (0:41), with no clock option since the foul is by the team ahead in score.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-38 - Option (0:41), with the personal foul enforced from the previous spot instead of the basic spot.
explanation: Properly officiated, the ball would have been declared dead at the B-42 with 0:41 remaining on the clock and the clock would have been stopped to complete the penalty. Replay is thus creating a stopped clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, and so there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock is reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:41), and since the foul is by the team ahead in score, the clock may start on either the Ready or the Snap at the option of Team A (3-4-3-b). Since the related run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The foul was by the team not in possession, so enforcement of the 15-yard penalty is from the basic spot, B-38 → B-23 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-38. 0:47 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12 rolls to his right under pursuit by B99. As B99 begins to tackle A12 a pass is thrown to A86 that is caught at the B-29, where A86 goes out of bounds (0:37). There is indisputable video evidence that A12’s knee was on the ground at the B-42 (0:41) prior to his release of the pass. What if -- in the original question -- A67 had been flagged for holding at the B-40? Assume Team B ahead in score. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/18 @ B-48 - Snap (0:41), at the option of Team B since they are ahead in score.
choice_b: A - 4/12 @ B-42 - Ready (0:31), with a 10-second runoff applied for the holding foul.
choice_c: A - 3/13 @ B-53 - Ready (0:41), with the holding foul enforced from the basic spot instead of the previous spot.
explanation: Properly officiated, the ball would have been declared dead at the B-42 with 0:41 remaining on the clock and the clock would have been stopped to complete the penalty. Replay is thus creating a stopped clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, and so there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock is reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:41), and since the foul is by the team behind in score, there is no option for Team B (3-4-3-b) and the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4). Even though this foul occurred behind the previous spot, it is one of the four fouls (illegal use of hands, holding, illegal block, and personal foul) which -- if by Team A behind the previous spot -- have their penalties enforced at the previous spot (10-2-2-b), B-38 -- > B-48.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Receiver B21 blocks A85 below the waist at the B-40. Afterward, the kick is caught at the goal line and returned to the B-30. Team A accepts the penalty. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot on legal kick plays unless the foul is governed by postscrimmage kick rules (10-2-2-d-4-a). Since this is a free kick rather than a scrimmage kick, enforcement is only possible from the previous spot, A-35 → 50, with a re-kick.

question_text: 4/G @ B-10. Team A attempts a field goal. The kick is partially blocked at the LOS by B70 and is caught by B40 three yards deep in the end zone. He returns it to the 50 where he is tackled (twist and pull) by the facemask. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap.  Replay will overturn to a Touchback, and the face mask foul is disregarded.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap.  Replay will overturn to a Touchback, but the face mask foul is treated as a dead ball foul.
explanation: Since the kick did not touch the ground in B's end zone (6-3-9), it is not a touchback and the ball continues in play (6-3-5). The foul occurred during B40's legal advance (run), and so the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-c). Since the foul was by the team not in possession, enforcement is from the basic spot, 50 → A-35 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/7 @ 50. With the clock running, Team B has 12 players in formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field. Team A snaps the ball, and the run by A34 gains 4 yards. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Dead ball foul.  A - 3/2 @ B-45 - Ready.  Officials should shut this play down.
choice_b: Live ball foul.  A - 3/2 @ B-45 - Ready.
choice_c: Live ball foul.  A - 1/10 @ B-41 - Ready.
explanation: Team B is allowed to briefly retain more than 11 players on the field to anticipate the offensive formation, but it may not have more than 11 players on the field when the ball is snapped (3-5-3-b). The infraction is treated as a live-ball foul with enforcement from the previous spot, 50 → B-45 (3-5-3-b-Penalty).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-35. A1’s punt is high and deep. B12 is in a position to field the punt at the B-25 when A15 interferes with his opportunity to catch the kick. Time in the 3rd quarter expires during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Untimed to extend 3rd quarter.
choice_b: Either A or B is possible, at the option of Team B.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap to begin 4th quarter.
explanation: A period is only extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul that occurs during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter (3-2-3-a-1). Since this foul occurred at the end of the 3rd quarter, the 15- yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-25 → B-40 (6-4-1-Penalty), and the quarter is not extended.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. Team A is in punt formation and kicks the ball to B18 who is in a position to make the catch at the B-40. A2 hits B18 prior to the ball arriving, leading with the crown of the helmet to the sternum. The ball touches A2 at the B41 and then B20 picks up the ball at the B-43 and runs to the A-40 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap.  B will accept the penalty for TGT and decline the penalty for KCI.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap.  B's best option is to decline both penalties.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap.  B will accept the penalty for KCI and decline the penalty for TGT.
explanation: A2 is guilty of both Kick Catch Interference (6-4-1-a) and Targeting with the crown of the helmet (9-1-3). The penalty for KCI may only be enforced from the spot of the foul, B-40 → A-45 (6-4-1-Penalty), but the penalty for TGT may be enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to B, A-40 → A-25 (6-4-1-g). Accordingly, Team B will accept the penalty for TGT and decline the penalty for KCI.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-40. QB A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A44 who takes a few steps forward and throws the ball back to A12 who is still in the tackle box. Avoiding rushers, A12 scrambles outside the tackle box and at the A-35 throws the ball to an area where there are no eligible receivers, and the ball lands out of bounds beyond the neutral zone. What is the ruling?
explanation: The passer may not conserve yardage by throwing the ball forward into an area where there is no eligible Team A receiver (7-3-2-h), but it is not a foul if the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended (7-3-2-h-Exception). However, this exception applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the forward pass. Since A12 controlled the snap but relinquished possession to A-44, he may not legally ground the ball. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the A-35 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. 0:15 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. Team A lines up for a field goal. At the B-27, holder A4 muffs the snap, immediately recovers it, and throws it forward into the ground (0:12). What is the ruling?
choice_c: Both B and C are options.
explanation: A4 may not throw the ball directly to the ground after the ball has already touched the ground (7-3-2-f), and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-27 (7-3-2-f-Penalty). Since this is an incomplete Illegal Forward Pass after the Two-Minute Timeout, there is the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). Team B may either accept the runoff with the clock starting on the ready or decline the runoff with the clock starting on the snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/9 @ B-41. QB A7 rolls out of the pocket. As A7 nears the sideline, he throws a forward pass to A80 at the B-30. A80 runs to the B-25 where he is tackled. The H rules A7 stepped out of bounds at the B-45 before the pass was thrown. Replays show A7 was inbounds when he released the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: If a passer is ruled out of bounds prior to throwing a pass and the replay official has indisputable video evidence that the ball was released prior to the dead ball ruling, replay can rule on the immediate continuing action. If the pass is caught by either team, they are awarded possession at that spot with no advance (12-3-3-d-3).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Receiver B21 blocks A85 below the waist at the B-40. Afterward, the kick is caught at the goal line and returned to the B-30. Team A accepts the penalty. What if A85 blocked B21 below the waist during the kick at the B-40? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of Team B's chosen option.
choice_c: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, since Team A would still choose the previous-spot option.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with the penalty enforced only 5 yards from the spot where the dead ball belongs to Team B.
explanation: Penalties for all fouls by the kicking team during a free kick play other than KCI may be enforced at the previous spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, at the option of Team B (6-1-8). Team B would surely opt for the latter, B-30 → B-45.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Receiver B21 blocks A85 below the waist at the B-40. Afterward, the kick is caught at the goal line and returned to the B-30. Team A accepts the penalty. What if the original foul by B21 occurred during a punt on 4/10 @ B-45? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot since this is a punt.
choice_c: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, since the free-kick-only enforcement rule from the original question still applies.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the PSK spot set at the goal line rather than the B-20.
explanation: Since this is a punt, the foul meets all the conditions for PSK (10-2-3-b). The kick ended when it was caught at the goal line, and when the kick ends in Team B’s end zone, the PSK spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-b). Since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) is beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/G @ B-10. Team A attempts a field goal. The kick is partially blocked at the LOS by B70 and is caught by B40 three yards deep in the end zone. He returns it to the 50 where he is tackled (twist and pull) by the facemask. What if the blocked kick rolled into the end zone where B40 recovered the loose ball and advanced? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-34 - Snap, since B40's advance and return stand despite the touchback.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the face mask foul declined since it followed a dead-ball touchback.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, since the face mask foul results in a change of possession.
explanation: The ball should have been declared dead when the scrimmage kick untouched by Team B beyond the neutral zone touched the ground in B's end zone (6-3-9). Replay will overturn to a Touchback, and even though B40's advance and the subsequent face mask foul should have never occurred, the penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls are always enforced, regardless of the outcome of a replay review (12-3-6-j). The 15-yard penalty will be enforced as a dead ball foul from the succeeding spot (10-2-2-a), B-20 → B-35.

question_text: 3/7 @ 50. With the clock running, Team B has 12 players in formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field. Team A snaps the ball, and the run by A34 gains 4 yards. What if it had been Team A with 12 players in formation, and no Team A player attempting to leave the field? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/2 @ B-45 - Ready, with the extra-player foul enforced the same way regardless of which team commits it.
choice_b: A - 3/12 @ A-45 - Snap, with the dead-ball foul's clock always starting on the snap.
choice_c: A - 3/7 @ 50 - Ready, with the penalty declined since Team A gained yardage on the play.
explanation: Team A may not break the huddle with more than 11 players nor keep more than 11 players in the huddle or in a formation for more than three seconds. Officials shall stop the action whether or not the ball has been snapped (3-5-3-a). The infraction is treated as a dead-ball foul with enforcement from the succeeding spot, 50 → A-45 (3-5-3-a-Penalty).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-35. A1’s punt is high and deep. B12 is in a position to field the punt at the B-25 when A15 interferes with his opportunity to catch the kick. Time in the 3rd quarter expires during the down. What if this foul occurred during a down in which time in the 2nd quarter expired? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Untimed or Half Over.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the period always extended once a penalty for a live-ball foul is accepted.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Untimed, since the kicking team retains the right to an untimed down.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Untimed, with no option for Team A to instead go to halftime.
explanation: The period shall be extended for an untimed down if, during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter, a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul (3-2-3-a-1). Team A may accept the penalty from the spot of the foul, B-25 → B-40 (6-4-1-Penalty) with the quarter extended, or may decline the penalty and go to halftime. The penalty may not be carried over to the second half kickoff.

question_text: 4/14 @ A-35. A1’s punt is high and deep. B12 is in a position to field the punt at the B-25 when A15 interferes with his opportunity to catch the kick. Time in the 3rd quarter expires during the down. What if this foul occurred during a down in which time in the 2nd quarter expired, and B46 also held A82 during the kick? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Half Over, since offsetting fouls never extend the period.
choice_c: A - 4/14 @ A-35 - Snap, at the option of Team A to decline the untimed down.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Untimed, with the holding foul enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: A period shall be extended for an untimed down if, during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter, there are offsetting fouls (3-2-3-a-2). There is no option, but there is nothing to prevent Team A from simply taking a knee.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. Team A is in punt formation and kicks the ball to B18 who is in a position to make the catch at the B-40. A2 hits B18 prior to the ball arriving, leading with the crown of the helmet to the sternum. The ball touches A2 at the B41 and then B20 picks up the ball at the B-43 and runs to the A-40 where he is tackled. What if B20 ran to the A-40 where he fumbled, with A80 recovering while grounded at the A-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap, with Team B instead accepting the penalty for Targeting.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with both fouls enforced together for a combined penalty.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, since the fumble negates Team B's right to the KCI penalty.
explanation: Since the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, there can be no "tack-on" enforcement of the penalty for TGT (6-4-1-g). Team B will accept the penalty for KCI, enforced from the spot of the foul, B-40 → A-45 (6-4-1-Penalty).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. Team A is in punt formation and kicks the ball to B18 who is in a position to make the catch at the B-40. A2 hits B18 prior to the ball arriving, leading with the crown of the helmet to the sternum. The ball touches A2 at the B41 and then B20 picks up the ball at the B-43 and runs to the A-40 where he is tackled. What if B20 ran to the A-40 where he fumbled, with A80 recovering while grounded at the A-35, and only Targeting had been called (no Kick-Catch Interference)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/15 @ A-10 - Snap, with a re-kick and Team B retaining the option to instead take the ball at the fumble spot.
choice_b: A - 4/5 @ A-20 - Snap, with the 15-yard penalty enforced the full distance rather than half-the-distance.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, since the Targeting foul alone is enough to overturn possession.
explanation: With no KCI called, this is simply one foul (TGT) by Team A during a scrimmage kick, and so enforcement can only be from the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a). The 15-yard penalty is enforced half-the-distance to the goal line, A-20 → A-10, with a re-kick (10-2-6).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-40. QB A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A44 who takes a few steps forward and throws the ball back to A12 who is still in the tackle box. Avoiding rushers, A12 scrambles outside the tackle box and at the A-35 throws the ball to an area where there are no eligible receivers, and the ball lands out of bounds beyond the neutral zone. What if A12's pass had landed near the sideline beyond the neutral zone with A83 in the area? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/15 @ A-35 - Snap, since A12 is still considered to have relinquished possession.
choice_c: A - 3/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with a loss of down assessed even though there is no foul.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, since an ineligible-area pass is always a turnover on downs.
explanation: With an eligible receiver in the area, A12 has not violated the original conditions of the rule (7-3-2-h), so the fact that he relinquished possession (7-3-2-h-Exception) is of no consequence. There is no foul, and this is simply an incomplete pass.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-40. QB A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A44 who takes a few steps forward and throws the ball back to A12 who is still in the tackle box. Avoiding rushers, A12 scrambles outside the tackle box and at the A-35 throws the ball to an area where there are no eligible receivers, and the ball lands out of bounds beyond the neutral zone. What if A12 had muffed the snap with A44 recovering the loose ball and grounding the ball himself as A12 did in the original question? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/15 @ A-35 - Snap, since only the original ball-carrier is covered by the grounding exception.
choice_c: A - 3/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with a loss of down for grounding despite the muffed snap.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, since a muffed snap voids any exception for legal grounding.
explanation: The ability to legally ground the ball is extended to the player who controls to snap or the resulting backward pass (7-3-2-h-Exception). The snap is a backward pass (2-23-1-a), and since A44 controlled this backward pass and did not relinquish it, he is covered by the exception. There is no foul, and this is simply an incomplete pass.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. 0:15 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. Team A lines up for a field goal. At the B-27, holder A4 muffs the snap, immediately recovers it, and throws it forward into the ground (0:12). What if Team A had one timeout remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/12 @ B-27 - Snap (0:12), with no runoff option since Team A still has a timeout remaining.
choice_b: A - 4/12 @ B-27 - Ready (0:12), since Team B must always accept the runoff when offered.
choice_d: A - 4/5 @ B-20 - Snap (0:12), since Team A's remaining timeout negates the foul entirely.
explanation: As in the original question, this foul includes the option for a 10-second runoff which Team B may either accept (Ready - 0:02) or decline (Snap - 0:12). If Team B accepts the runoff, Team A could then immediately use their time out (3-4-4-d) to avoid the runoff (Snap - 0:12), or they could allow the runoff and subsequently call a time out (Snap - 0:02) so as not to have to rush the kick.

question_text: 3/9 @ B-41. QB A7 rolls out of the pocket. As A7 nears the sideline, he throws a forward pass to A80 at the B-30. A80 runs to the B-25 where he is tackled. The H rules A7 stepped out of bounds at the B-45 before the pass was thrown. Replays show A7 was inbounds when he released the pass. What if A7's pass had fallen incomplete? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Reviewable. A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Ready, since the same replay standard applies to an incomplete pass.
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ B-45 - Ready, since the on-field ruling of out-of-bounds stands without indisputable evidence.
choice_c: A - 4/9 @ B-41 - Ready, with the down counting but the clock starting on the ready.
explanation: If a passer is ruled out of bounds prior to throwing a pass and the replay official has indisputable video evidence that the ball was released prior to the dead ball ruling, replay can rule on the immediate continuing action. If the pass is incomplete, the down counts 12-3-3-d-3). More accurately, the ball belongs to the passing team at the previous spot (7-3-7-b).

question_text: 4/12 @ B-24. A’s field goal attempt is partially blocked at the LOS and rolls out of bounds at the B-2. B91 blocked A65 in the back at the B-4. What is the ruling?
explanation: Note: Options A and B were not my best work! Since the penalty for an illegal block in the back is 10 yards (not 15 yards or half-the distance), A and B should have been presented as A - 4/2 @ B-14 - Snap and B - 1/10 @ B-14 - Snap, respectively. All of the conditions for postscrimmage kick enforcement are met (10-2-3-b), and the PSK spot is the previous spot (B-24) on unsuccessful field goals which are untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone and are declared dead beyond the neutral zone (2-25-11-Special Case 1a). Since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) occurs behind the PSK (basic) spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/12 @ B-32. A attempts a FG, and at the snap A76 grabs and twist B12's face mask. B32 blocks the FG attempt and the ball is picked up by B87 at the B-26 and returned to the A-34. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball remains in play when recovered by B87 (6-3-5), and the result of the play is B - 1/10 @ A-34 - Snap. Penalty enforcement is only possible from the previous spot because field goals are a specific exception to the "tack-on" rule (6-3-13). Team B will decline the penalty.

question_text: 4/12 @ A-40. As the punt rolls along the ground at the B-26, A44 blocks B33 into the ball which bounces away and strikes B48 in the leg at the B-19. Team A recovers at the B-19. What is the ruling?
explanation: A player blocked by an opponent into a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone shall not be deemed to have touched the kick (6-3-4-a), so since A44 blocked B33 into the ball, B33 is not considered to have touched the kick. However, B48 was not blocked, and so when this loose ball strikes him, he did touch the kick (AR-6-3-4-V). This touching allows Team A to recover and be in legal possession of the ball (6-3-3).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-32. The score is A-23, B-26 with 1:23 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts remaining for either team. QB A32 runs to the B-25 and pitches the ball back to A98. A98 muffs the pitch at the B-26 and while trying to recover the ball accidentally kicks the ball out of bounds to the B-21. B62 was offside at the snap. What is the ruling?
explanation: A32's pitch from the B-25 to the B-26 is a backward pass, and it remains a backward pass despite A98's muff and kick. So, this is just a backward pass that goes out of bound, and it remains at the dead ball spot, the B-21 (7-2-5-a). Whenever a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock starts by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2) and the 3-4-3-b option which would have been available to Team A had this been "Ready" does not apply.

question_text: 1/G @ B-9. Early in the 2nd quarter. As receiver A87 begins his route B23 grabs A87’s mouthpiece (which was attached to his facemask but not being worn) and pulls downward and rips it off the facemask. A87 has to readjust his helmet slightly while continuing his route to the end zone where he catches a pass for a TD. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock and A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock and A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: Both B and C are options.
explanation: While it is illegal to grasp and then twist, turn or pull the face mask, chin strap or any helmet opening of an opponent (9-1-8-b), there is no rule or interpretation to include the grasping of a mouthpiece as personal foul. This contact might be considered Defensive Holding, but the penalty would be declined by rule (10-2-5-a-2).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-14. The score is A-24, B-31 late in the 4th quarter. QB A12 throws a pass to receiver A87 in the end zone that is complete for a TD. After the score, A87 points at B23 as he is running by and makes an obscene gesture. Time expires in the 4th quarter on the play. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The penalty is declined by rule.
choice_b: The penalty may be enforced on either the Try or at the succeeding spot in a potential extra period.
choice_c: The penalty may only be enforced at the succeeding spot in a potential extra period.
choice_d: The penalty may only be enforced on the Try.
explanation: When a foul occurs after a touchdown and before the ball is ready for play on the Try, enforcement is on the Try, the succeeding kickoff or from the succeeding spot in extra periods at the option of the offended team (10-2-5-c). It will either be A - Try @ B-18 - No Clock or A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock and -- if A scores one point on the Try -- A's ball at the 40-yard line or B's ball at the 12½-yard line to begin the first extra period.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Lineman B90 jumps into the neutral zone and contacts A77. After the officials stops play before the snap can occur, A87 grabs B90 and forcefully throws him to the ground. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock and A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-16½ - No Clock and A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: Penalties for dead-ball fouls are administered separately and in order of occurrence (10-1-5). The penalty for B90's foul is enforced B-3 → B-1½, after which the penalty for A87's foul is enforced B-1½ → B-16½. There is no option to carry the penalty for A87's foul over to the succeeding kickoff because it did not occur prior to the Ready (10-2-5-c) or after the Try (8-3-5).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. The score is A-27, B-20 late in the 4th quarter with no time outs remaining for either team. Running back A22 runs around right end and is tackled inbounds at the A-47. During the run, A81 held B98 at the A-40. B55’s helmet comes off during the play. When the play is over there is 3:22 remaining in the 4th quarter. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 2/15 @ A-30 - Option Of Team B.
explanation: Since the run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-40 → A-30 (10-2-2-c-1). The clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in score, but since this is not after the Two-Minute Timeout, the offended team does not have a clock option (3-4-3-b). The referee does, however, have broad authority in the timing of the game, including starting the game clock on the Snap if the foul is by the team ahead in the score … and as a guideline, referees should consider invoking this rule when the game clock is under five minutes of each half (3-4-3-a). Since Team A -- ahead by 7 points -- could effectively run 40 additional seconds off the clock (3-2-4-c-14), the Referee should certainly consider this option.

question_text: 4/12 @ B-24. A’s field goal attempt is partially blocked at the LOS and rolls out of bounds at the B-2. B91 blocked A65 in the back at the B-4. What if B82 muffed the loose ball at the B-4 prior it going out of bounds at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the PSK spot unaffected by B82's touching of the loose ball.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-9 - Snap, since Team A's block in the back is enforced from the previous spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the penalty enforced the full 10 yards from the PSK spot.
explanation: Since the kick was touched by Team B beyond the neutral zone, 2-25-11-Special Case 1a no longer applies, and the PSK spot is the end of the kick, the B-2 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) occurs beyond the PSK (basic) spot, enforcement is from the PSK spot, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/12 @ B-32. A attempts a FG, and at the snap A76 grabs and twist B12's face mask. B32 blocks the FG attempt and the ball is picked up by B87 at the B-26 and returned to the A-34. What if B87 returned the kick to the A-34 where he fumbled, with A65 recovering while grounded at the A-30? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, with Team B keeping the ball at the spot of A65's fumble recovery.
choice_c: A - 4/27 @ B-47 - Snap, with the facemask foul enforced from the succeeding spot instead of the previous spot.
choice_d: A - 4/17 @ B-37 - Snap, with the penalty enforced only 5 yards from the previous spot.
explanation: Team A is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down, and so Team B will accept the penalty from the previous spot, B-32 → B-47, with Team A still in possession (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-40. As the punt rolls along the ground at the B-26, A44 blocks B33 into the ball which bounces away and strikes B48 in the leg at the B-19. Team A recovers at the B-19. What if -- to prevent A from recovering -- B48 batted the ball at the B-20 and it went out of bounds at the B-22? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, since B48's bat does not affect Team A's earlier legal touching.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-22 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the PSK spot rather than the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-26 - Snap, since B48's bat is treated as legal despite going forward.
explanation: B48's bat is forward and thus illegal (9-4-1-c). The ball belongs to Team B at the end of the down (6-3-7), and all conditions of postscrimmage kick enforcement are satisfied (10-2-3-b). The PSK spot is the end of the kick, the B-22 (2-25-11-a), and since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) occurs behind the PSK (basic) spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-40. As the punt rolls along the ground at the B-26, A44 blocks B33 into the ball which bounces away and strikes B48 in the leg at the B-19. Team A recovers at the B-19. What if -- to prevent A from recovering -- B48 batted the ball at the B-20 and it went out of bounds at the B-18? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, treating the backward bat the same as a forward bat.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where A44 blocked B33 into it.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where B48 first touched it.
explanation: B48's bat is backward and thus legal (9-4-1-c), and the ball belongs to Team B at the end of the down (6-3-7).
