question_text: 4/5 @ B-32. The score is A-23, B-26 with 1:23 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts remaining for either team. QB A32 runs to the B-25 and pitches the ball back to A98. A98 muffs the pitch at the B-26 and while trying to recover the ball accidentally kicks the ball out of bounds to the B-21. B62 was offside at the snap. What if A98 controlled the pitch, ran to the B-24, and fumbled with the ball going out of bounds at the B-21? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-21 - Ready, with the ball remaining at the out-of-bounds spot rather than the spot of the fumble.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-24 - Ready, with no clock option available since the offside foul is declined.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-24 - Snap, since Team B is awarded the ball once A98 fumbles a second time.
explanation: Since the fumble went out of bounds in advance of the spot of the fumble, the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1), and the clock would start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2). Team A would decline the penalty, but since the clock was stopped to complete a penalty (accept or decline per 10-1-1-a) for a foul by the team ahead in the score after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 1/G @ B-9. Early in the 2nd quarter. As receiver A87 begins his route B23 grabs A87’s mouthpiece (which was attached to his facemask but not being worn) and pulls downward and rips it off the facemask. A87 has to readjust his helmet slightly while continuing his route to the end zone where he catches a pass for a TD. What if B23 grabs, twists, and turns A87's chin strap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with no option to instead carry the penalty to the succeeding kickoff.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock and A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules or A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock and A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: There is no foul on the play, since grasping a chin strap alone does not violate the face mask rule.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the penalty declined by rule since it is treated as Defensive Holding.
explanation: Grasping then twisting and turning an opponent's chin strap is a personal foul (9-1-8-b), and penalties for personal fouls may be enforced on the Try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-14. The score is A-24, B-31 late in the 4th quarter. QB A12 throws a pass to receiver A87 in the end zone that is complete for a TD. After the score, A87 points at B23 as he is running by and makes an obscene gesture. Time expires in the 4th quarter on the play. What if -- in addition to the above -- A65 had been flagged for Holding at the line of scrimmage? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/23 @ B-24 - Untimed, with only the dead-ball unsportsmanlike foul enforced.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-24 - Untimed, since the live-ball and dead-ball fouls offset each other.
choice_d: A - 3/33 @ B-39 - Snap, with the period not extended since the fouls are administered together.
explanation: Team B will accept the penalty for Team A's live-ball foul, thus negating the touchdown, but the period must be extended by an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1). When a live-ball foul by one team is followed by a dead ball foul by either team, the penalties are administered separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). The penalty for A65's Holding foul will be enforced from the previous spot, B-14 → B-24 (10-2-2-d-3), after which the penalty for A87's Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul will be enforced from the succeeding spot, B-24 → B-39 (10-2-2-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Lineman B90 jumps into the neutral zone and contacts A77. After the officials stops play before the snap can occur, A87 grabs B90 and forcefully throws him to the ground. What if -- immediately after A87's action -- B65 forcefully throws A87 to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with all three fouls canceling out entirely.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-16½ - No Clock, with B90's and B65's fouls both enforced against Team B.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with A87's foul also enforced against Team A after the offset.
explanation: When dead-ball unsportsmanlike or dead-ball personal fouls by both teams are reported to the referee and none of the penalties have been completed, the yardage penalties cancel (10-1-5-Exception). So, the penalty for B90's foul is enforced B-3 → B-1½ (10-2-2-a), and the penalties for A87's and B65's fouls cancel. Team A could certainly decline the penalty for B90's foul (A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock) if they wish to kick from the same spot.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. The score is A-27, B-20 late in the 4th quarter with no time outs remaining for either team. Running back A22 runs around right end and is tackled inbounds at the A-47. During the run, A81 held B98 at the A-40. B55’s helmet comes off during the play. When the play is over there is 3:22 remaining in the 4th quarter. What if the ball was snapped with 1:22 remaining in the 4th quarter, and A81's hold occurred at the A-33? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/20 @ A-25 - Option Of Team B.
choice_b: A - 2/20 @ A-25 - Ready, with no clock option since this is not after the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_c: A - 2/15 @ A-30 - Option Of Team B, with the holding foul enforced from the basic spot instead of the previous spot.
choice_d: A - 2/20 @ A-25 - Referee's Discretion, with the Snap option unavailable since the clock stopped for the first down.
explanation: Since the Holding foul occurred behind the neutral zone, enforcement is from the previous spot, A-35 → A-25 (10-2-2-b). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because B55's helmet was not the only reason for stopping the clock after the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-10-b). The clock was also stopped because of the 1st down, and to complete the penalty. But, since the clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The ball has been spotted at the left hash at the request of the Team A head coach. After which of the following would a subsequent request from the Team A head coach to place the ball on the right hash be granted? Team A is at the line for the snap when: What is the ruling?
explanation: On a Try, the ball may be relocated after a Team B foul or a charged timeout to either team, unless the timeout is preceded by a Team A foul or offsetting fouls (8-3-2-c). Thus, the request is granted after (A) the Team A time out or (C) the Team B head coach's Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul, but not after (B) A77's False Start.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 5 points and neither team with a timeout remaining. A42 runs to the B-19 where he is tackled with 0:32 remaining on the game clock. A78 uses forcible contact to push B55 off the pile while the Umpire is getting the ball from A42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-34 - Option of Team B (0:32)
explanation: A new series is awarded to Team A since the ball was declared dead in their possession beyond the line to gain (5-1-1-b), and the penalty for the dead-ball foul that occurred after the series ended and before the ball was ready for play is completed before the line to gain is established (5-2-7-a). This results in a 1st down at the B-19, with enforcement of the penalty from the succeeding spot, B-19 → B-34 (10-2-2-a) and a new series, 1/10 @ B-34. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because making the line-to-gain -- not the foul -- caused the clock to stop (3-4-4-a), and Team B does not have an option for Snap because the foul was not by the team ahead in score (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-45. A7's punt is over B's end zone when A82 bats it back into the field of play. B46 picks up the loose ball at the B-4 and returns it to the B-15 where he fumbles with A19 recovering while grounded at the B-20. During the kick, B37 blocked A15 in the back at the B-10. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A82 batted the punt over B's end zone, it was not a foul but rather a violation for Illegal Touching (6-3-11) which would have given Team B the right to take the ball at the B-20 had it not been for B37's foul (6-3-2-b). PSK does not apply since Team B will not next put the ball into play (10-2-3-b-4), so enforcement of the penalty is from the previous spot, B-45 → B-35 (10-2-2-d-4-a). Team A would like to decline the penalty and take the ball at the spot of their recovery, the B-20, but if they do, Team B would then accept the Illegal Touching and retain the ball (6-3-2-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the half, QB A12 -- who has not been outside the tackle box -- is under heavy duress and throws a pass from the B-35. The pass falls incomplete and ineligible A58 touches the ball at the B-31 in an attempt to catch it. There was no eligible receiver in the area and the clock stopped at 0:31 after the play. It will be A - 3/15 @ B-35 with the clock starting on the: What is the ruling?
choice_d: Ready (0:21) or Snap (0:31) -- ILT and ING.
explanation: Because A12 had not been outside the tackle box and threw the ball into an area where there was not an eligible receiver, he is guilty of Intentional Grounding, which is an illegal forward pass (7-3-2-h). Even though ineligible A58 touched the pass, this cannot be Illegal Touching because this foul applies only to legal forward passes (7-3-11). An incomplete illegal forward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout qualifies for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3), which Team B may accept with the clock starting on the Ready or decline with the clock starting on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ A-40. A12's pass is complete to A86 at the B-45. He runs to the B-40 where he is hit and fumbles. B17 muffs the loose ball several times and it finally rolls out of bounds at the 50. B99 is flagged for roughing A12. What is the ruling?
explanation: Roughing the Passer is a personal foul (9-1-9-a), and the penalties for Team B personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls are enforced at the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e). There was never a change of team possession as the ball remained in Team A's possession while it was loose (2-4-1-b-3), and the last run ended at the spot of A86's fumble, the B-40 (2-25-8-b). So, enforcement of the 15-yard penalty is from that spot, B-40 → B-25.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B27 muffs A9's kick into the air at the B-4, but B18 catches the loose ball at the B-6. He carries to the B-15 where he is tackled. Video shows that while A9's kick was in flight, B18 gave a waving signal at waist level. What is the ruling?
explanation: The receiving team advancing after giving a fair catch signal (valid or invalid) is reviewable (12-3-4-g), and if during a free kick, a Team B receiver gives any waving signal that does not meet all of the requirements of a valid fair catch signal, and subsequently catches the ball behind the B-25 yard line, the ball belongs to Team B at its own 25-yard line (6-5-3-a-Exception).

question_text: 2/20 @ A-18. A14 completes a pass to A82 at the A-28 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass, B6 holds A82 at the A-22. What is the ruling?
explanation: B6 is guilty of Defensive Holding (9-3-4-d), and since a legal forward pass play includes all action from the snap until the pass is complete, incomplete, or intercepted (2-30-1), this foul occurred during a passing play. The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, A-18 → A-28 (10-2-2-c-3) and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. During A9's kick, B55 holds A22 at the B-40. When B21 catches the kick, he is straddling the sideline with the left foot out of bounds and the right foot inbounds at the B-10. What is the ruling?
choice_c: Both A and C are Options.
choice_d: Both B and D are Options.
explanation: When B21 who is out of bounds first touches the kick, the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). This is then a free kick out of bounds untouched by an inbounds player of Team B and is a foul on Team A (6-2-1). Since B55 also fouled during the kick, there is a live-ball foul by each team and the fouls will offset (10-1-4).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The ball has been spotted at the left hash at the request of the Team A head coach. After which of the following would a subsequent request from the Team A head coach to place the ball on the right hash be granted? Team A is at the line for the snap when: What if the Try is successful, but there are live ball fouls on each team, and after a charged time out the Team A head coach then asks that the ball be placed on the right hash? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock - Right Hash, with the request granted since offsetting fouls occurred before the timeout.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock - Left Hash.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock - Left Hash, with the offsetting fouls enforced half-the-distance.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock - Right Hash, since a charged timeout always allows relocation regardless of preceding fouls.
explanation: If live-ball fouls by both teams are reported to the Referee, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4). On a Try, the ball may be relocated after a Team B foul or a charged timeout to either team, unless the timeout is preceded by a Team A foul or offsetting fouls (8-3-2-c). The request is denied.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 5 points and neither team with a timeout remaining. A42 runs to the B-19 where he is tackled with 0:32 remaining on the game clock. A78 uses forcible contact to push B55 off the pile while the Umpire is getting the ball from A42. What if A42 ran to the B-21 where he was tackled with 0:32 remaining, and A78 was flagged with 0:29 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-21 - Ready (0:29), with the new series awarded despite the dead-ball foul.
choice_b: A - 3/16 @ B-36 - Snap (0:29), with no runoff option since the clock had already stopped for the first down.
choice_d: A - 3/1 @ B-21 - Ready (0:29), with the dead-ball foul declined by rule.
explanation: The result of the live-ball action is 3/1 @ B-21 with the clock running, and A78's foul then causes the clock to stop at 0:29. This qualifies for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a), which Team may accept with the clock starting on the Ready or decline with the clock starting on the Snap (3-4-4-c). The penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-21 → B-36 (10-2-2-a).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-45. A7's punt is over B's end zone when A82 bats it back into the field of play. B46 picks up the loose ball at the B-4 and returns it to the B-15 where he fumbles with A19 recovering while grounded at the B-20. During the kick, B37 blocked A15 in the back at the B-10. What if B46's fumble had rolled out of bounds at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the forward fumble out of bounds belonging to Team A at the recovery spot.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the PSK spot set at the goal line since the ball crossed into the end zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot rather than the PSK spot.
explanation: B46's forward fumble out of bounds is returned to the spot of the fumble and belongs to Team B (7-2-4-b-1). All of the conditions for PSK enforcement are met (10-2-3-b), and because A82's bat of the punt over B's end zone triggered Rule 6-3-11, the PSK spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-Special Case 2). Since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred behind the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the spot of the foul, B-10 → B-5 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the half, QB A12 -- who has not been outside the tackle box -- is under heavy duress and throws a pass from the B-35. The pass falls incomplete and ineligible A58 touches the ball at the B-31 in an attempt to catch it. There was no eligible receiver in the area and the clock stopped at 0:31 after the play. It will be A - 3/15 @ B-35 with the clock starting on the: What if eligible A37 had been in the vicinity when ineligible A58 touched the pass? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/23 @ B-35 - Snap (0:31), with a loss of down assessed for Illegal Touching.
choice_c: A - 3/15 @ B-35 - Ready (0:21), with a 10-second runoff applied for the Illegal Touching foul.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-30 - Snap (0:31), since Illegal Touching is declined once there is an eligible receiver nearby.
explanation: With A37 in the vicinity, this is a legal forward pass which cannot be Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h), but is it Illegal Touching since an originally ineligible player touched this legal forward pass before it was touched by an opponent or an official (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-30 → B-35, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). Since the foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately, there is no option for a 10- second runoff (3-4-4-a), and the clock will start by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-d-4).

question_text: 3/15 @ A-40. A12's pass is complete to A86 at the B-45. He runs to the B-40 where he is hit and fumbles. B17 muffs the loose ball several times and it finally rolls out of bounds at the 50. B99 is flagged for roughing A12. What if B31 recovered the loose ball while grounded just before it rolled out of bounds at the 50? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - Ready, treating the play the same as the original question since Team A never lost possession.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, with the 15-yard penalty enforced from the end of A86's run instead of the previous spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, since B31's recovery of a loose ball inbounds does not change team possession.
explanation: When B31 recovers the loose ball inbounds, he is in player possession (2-4-1-a), which means that team possession has then changed (2-4-1-b-1). Since there was a change of team possession during the down, 10-2-2-e does not apply and enforcement of the 15-yard penalty is from the previous spot, A-40 → B-45 (10-2-2-d-3).

question_text: 3/15 @ A-40. A12's pass is complete to A86 at the B-45. He runs to the B-40 where he is hit and fumbles. B17 muffs the loose ball several times and it finally rolls out of bounds at the 50. B99 is flagged for roughing A12. What if A24 recovered the loose ball while grounded just before it rolled out of bounds at the 50? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, treating A24's recovery the same as a change of team possession.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, with enforcement from the spot of A86's original fumble.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - Ready, with the 15-yard penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: When A24 recovers the loose ball inbounds, it constitutes a new "run" which essentially begins and ends at the spot of recovery (2-25-8-a). Since the ball remained in Team A's possession while it was loose (2-4-1-b-3) and is still in Team A's possession when A24 recovers (2-4-1-a / 2-4-1-b-1), there has been no change of team possession during the down. Enforcement of the 15-yard penalty is from the end of the last run, 50 → B-35 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B27 muffs A9's kick into the air at the B-4, but B18 catches the loose ball at the B-6. He carries to the B-15 where he is tackled. Video shows that while A9's kick was in flight, B18 gave a waving signal at waist level. What if all of the above happened during a punt on 4/10 @ B-45? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where B27 first muffed the kick.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the advance after the signal allowed to stand since this is a punt, not a free kick.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, since an invalid fair catch signal always forfeits Team B's rights to the ball.
explanation: The receiving team advancing after giving a fair catch signal (valid or invalid) is reviewable (12-3-4-g). While a catch after an invalid signal is not a fair catch, the ball is dead where caught (6-5-3-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B27 muffs A9's kick into the air at the B-4, but B18 catches the loose ball at the B-6. He carries to the B-15 where he is tackled. Video shows that while A9's kick was in flight, B18 gave a waving signal at waist level. What if all of the above happened during a punt on 4/10 @ B-45, but B27 also gave a valid fair catch signal? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, with the ball placed where B18 completed the catch rather than where B27 signaled.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the advance allowed to stand since B18's catch was not itself signaled.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, since two different players being involved voids the fair catch protection.
explanation: The receiving team advancing after giving a fair catch signal (valid or invalid) is reviewable (12-3-4-g). If a Team B player makes a valid fair catch signal, muffs the kick, and he (or another Team B player) subsequently catches the kick, the ball belongs to Team B where the player making the signal first touched it (6-5-1-b).

question_text: 2/20 @ A-18. A14 completes a pass to A82 at the A-28 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass, B6 holds A82 at the A-22. What if B6 held A82 as above, but A14 kept the ball, carrying to the A-28 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-28 - Ready, with the previous spot used instead of the basic spot for the run ending beyond the neutral zone.
choice_c: A - 2/10 @ A-28 - Ready, with the automatic first down declined since this was a running play.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-33 - Ready, with the penalty enforced only 5 yards from the end of the run.
explanation: This foul occurs during a running play which ends beyond the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, A-28 → A-38 (10-2-2-c-3) and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: 2/20 @ A-18. A14 completes a pass to A82 at the A-28 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass, B6 holds A82 at the A-22. What if B6 held A82 as above, and A14 was sacked at the A-12? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-38 - Ready, with the end of the run used as the basic spot despite ending behind the neutral zone.
choice_b: A - 2/10 @ A-18 - Ready, with the automatic first down declined for a sack.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-33 - Ready, with the penalty enforced only 5 yards from the previous spot.
explanation: This foul occurs during a running play which ends behind the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, A-18 → A-28 (10-2-2-c-3) and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. During A9's kick, B55 holds A22 at the B-40. When B21 catches the kick, he is straddling the sideline with the left foot out of bounds and the right foot inbounds at the B-10. What if there was no foul by B55? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, since a re-kick is Team B's only enforcement option here.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, since Team B would choose the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them.
choice_c: A - F/K @ B-35 - F/K Rules, with Team B awarded the ball 30 yards beyond Team A's restraining line.
explanation: When B21 who is out of bounds first touches the kick, the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). This is then a free kick out of bounds untouched by an inbounds player of Team B and is a foul on Team A (6-2-1). By rule, there are three penalty options: (1) five yards from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, with a re- kick; (2) five yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-10 → B-15; or (3) Team B's ball 30 yards beyond Team A’s restraining line, A-35 → B-35. Option (2) would not be considered, but Option (1) might be.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. During A9's kick, B55 holds A22 at the B-40. When B21 catches the kick, he is straddling the sideline with the left foot out of bounds and the right foot inbounds at the B-10. What if B55 fouled as above, but B21 touched the kick while inbounds then stepped out of bounds at the B-10? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, since B21 touching the ball inbounds still results in a kick out of bounds.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with Team A required to accept B55's holding penalty and re-kick.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the previous-spot re-kick as Team A's only available option.
explanation: Since B21 touched to ball while inbounds, it is not a free kick out of bounds (6-2-1), and the ball belongs to Team B at the out of bounds spot (6-2-2). The basic spot for the enforcement of the penalty for B55's foul would be the previous spot, A-35 → A-45 (10-2-2-d-4-a), so while a re-kick is an option, Team A will most likely decline the penalty.

question_text: 2/8 @ B-48. QB A10 completes a pass to A24 at the B-20 and he runs to the B-10 where he fumbles when hit by B44. The ball is recovered in the EZ by B23 near the end line. B66 is flagged for Roughing the Passer and B44 is flagged for Targeting. Replay doesn’t confirm the Targeting, but during the review also sees that B23 was touching the end line when he first touched the loose ball. What is the ruling?
explanation: Enforcement of penalties for Team B personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls during a legal forward pass play is at the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e). At no time during the down did Team B gain possession, and the last run ended at the spot of A24's fumble, the B-10 (2-25-8-b), so enforcement is B-10 → B-5. A24's fumble provided the impetus which caused the ball to go into B's end zone (8-7-1) where it became dead, so the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a). The clock is stopped for a touchback, and restarts on the snap (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A97 punts the ball and B1 gives a valid fair catch signal. The ball bounces at the B-8 and rolls towards Team B’s end zone. While the ball is rolling, B1 blocks A4 above the waist and in the chest at the B-6. The ball is downed by A24 at the B-4. What is the ruling?
explanation: A player of Team B who has made a valid or invalid signal for a fair catch and does not touch the ball shall not block or foul an opponent during that down (6-5-4). B1 has thus committed a foul, and it meets all the criteria for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b). The PSK spot is the end of the kick, the B-4 (2-25-11-a), and since the foul occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. QB A1 is in the pocket under heavy pressure. A1 throws the ball at the A-12 just as defender B99 hits him from behind. The Referee rules fumble even though the passer’s hand came forward with the football. B98 recovers at the A-18. The Replay Official stops the game to review a pass or fumble. The Referee announces, “There is no foul for intentional grounding. The ruling on the field is a fumble recovered by the defense. The previous play is under further review.” What is the ruling?
explanation: A loose ball by a potential passer ruled a fumble is reviewable (12-3-3-a), and the Referee's announcement establishes that if the fumble is overturned to an incomplete forward pass, there will be a foul for Intentional Grounding (2024 Replay Case Book, Play #68). Since the passer's hand came forward with the football, Replay will reverse the ruling on the field to an incomplete forward pass (2-19-2-b) and add the foul. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the A-12 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. From a scrimmage kick formation, A17 punts the ball to the B-10 where the ball hits B18 on the leg. As the ball rolls on the ground at the B-4, B18 kicks the ball to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B’s end zone and over the end line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap if the penalty is accepted and B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules if the penalty is declined.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap if the penalty is accepted and B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap if the penalty is declined.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap if the penalty is accepted and B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap if the penalty is declined.
choice_d: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules regardless of whether the penalty is accepted or declined.
explanation: B18 illegally kicking the ball provided the impetus which caused the ball to go into his end zone (8-7-1) where it became dead, so the result of the play is a safety (8-5-1-a). The foul meets all the criteria for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), and since the kick ended in Team B's end zone, the PSK spot is the B-20 (2-2-5-11-b). The foul occurred behind the basic (PSK) spot, so enforcement of the penalty is from the spot of the foul, B-4 → B-2. (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. The score is A-14, B-21 with 0:07 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A2 throws a low pass to A18 who is at the B-10 in the middle of the field. The ruling on the field is an incomplete pass and the clock reads 0:00. Video shows that A18 completed the catch with 0:02 left on the clock. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over if Team A has no timeout, but A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap (0:02) if Team A has a timeout.
choice_b: Game Over regardless of whether or not Team has a timeout remaining,
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Ready (0:02) if Team A has no timeout, but A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap (0:02) if Team has a timeout.
explanation: Properly officiated -- and with an immediate signal by the covering official and an immediate reaction by the game clock operator -- the clock would have stopped at 0:02 for the first down and restarted on the Ready (3-3-2-e-1). If time expires in a half, and the clock would start on the Referee’s signal after review, there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the ball should have been declared dead to restore time to the clock. With 2 seconds or 1 second remaining on the clock, the half is over unless Team A has a time out remaining (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Back A31 is stationary and lines up in the tackle box. Just after the snap, A31 immediately releases outside the tackle box. QB A19 is in the pocket to pass and A31 sees LB 14 blitzing. A31 returns to the tackle box and before A19 throws a pass, blocks LB14 below the waist from the front at the A-20. A19’s pass is complete for a 20-yard gain to the 50. What is the ruling?
explanation: Stationary Backs lined up with any part of their body inside the tackle box may block below the waist within the tackle box until the ball leaves the tackle box, provided the force of the initial contact is directed from the front (9-1-6-a-2); however, once a player leaves the tackle box during the play, that player is considered outside the tackle box for the remainder of the down (AR-9-1-6-X). So, A31's block is considered illegal. The penalty for any Team A personal foul behind the neutral zone is enforced from the previous spot, so A-30 → A-15 (10-2-2-b).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. The score is A-20, B-20 with 0:03 left in the 4th quarter. B11 intercepts A12’s pass in the end zone and returns it to the A-4 where he is tackled by the facemask. During B11’s run, B82 blocked A85 low in Team B’s end zone. What is the ruling?
explanation: If live-ball fouls by both teams are reported to the referee, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4), and since there were offsetting fouls during a down in which time expired in the 2nd or 4th quarter, the period is extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-2). Team B undoubtedly does not want to give Team A another shot at the end zone, and since Team B did not foul before gaining possession, they might wish to decline offsetting fouls and retain possession after completion of the penalty for their foul (10-1-4-Exception 1). But, since their foul occurred in their end zone, the result of the play would be a safety (8-5-1-b), and Team B's only rational choice would be to accept offsetting fouls.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. First possession of the first extra period. B12 intercepts A10’s pass at the B-10 and returns it for a touchdown. During the return, B12 taunts A’s bench at the A-15 before scoring. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Score.  B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock.
choice_b: Game Over.  Team B wins by 6 points.
choice_c: No Score.  B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
explanation: In extra periods, a score by a team committing a foul during the down is canceled (3-1-3-g-2), and penalties for fouls by either team after a change of possession are declined by rule (3-1-3-g-1), unless the foul is "FUDL" (a Flagrant personal foul, an Unsportsmanlike conduct foul, a Dead-ball personal foul, or a Live-ball foul treated as a dead-ball foul. Since B12's taunting is Unsportsmanlike (9-2-1-a-1-b), the penalty is enforced at the succeeding spot in the second possession of the first extra period, A-25 → A-40 (3-1-3-g-1-Exception).

question_text: 2/8 @ B-48. QB A10 completes a pass to A24 at the B-20 and he runs to the B-10 where he fumbles when hit by B44. The ball is recovered in the EZ by B23 near the end line. B66 is flagged for Roughing the Passer and B44 is flagged for Targeting. Replay doesn’t confirm the Targeting, but during the review also sees that B23 was touching the end line when he first touched the loose ball. What if B23 possessed the ball in the end zone before touching the end line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready, with the 15-yard penalty enforced from the end of A24's run since there is no change of possession.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-33 - Snap, since B23's possession before touching the end line still results in a touchback for Team A.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-33 - Ready, with the clock starting on the ready since this is a personal-foul enforcement.
explanation: Because B23 recovered the loose ball, there was a change of possession during the down and "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (10-2-2-e); therefore, enforcement is from the previous spot, B-48 → B-33 (10-2-2-d-3). A24's fumble provided the impetus which caused the ball to go into B's end zone (8-7-1) where it became dead, so the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a). The clock is stopped for a touchback, and restarts on the snap (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A97 punts the ball and B1 gives a valid fair catch signal. The ball bounces at the B-8 and rolls towards Team B’s end zone. While the ball is rolling, B1 blocks A4 above the waist and in the chest at the B-6. The ball is downed by A24 at the B-4. What if the ball rolled into B's end zone where A24 recovered it? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, since A24's recovery of a kick in the end zone gives Team A possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-8 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the PSK spot set at the end of the kick rather than the end zone.
explanation: If a scrimmage kick untouched by Team B beyond the neutral zone touches the ground on or behind Team B’s goal line, the ball becomes dead and belongs to Team B (6-3-9), so A24 has recovered a dead ball. A player of Team B who has made a valid or invalid signal for a fair catch and does not touch the ball shall not block or foul an opponent during that down (6-5-4). B1 has thus committed a foul, and it meets all the criteria for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b). The kick ended in Team B's end zone, so the PSK spot is the B-20 (2-2-5-11-b), and since the foul occurred behind the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the spot of the foul, B-6 → B-3. (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. QB A1 is in the pocket under heavy pressure. A1 throws the ball at the A-12 just as defender B99 hits him from behind. The Referee rules fumble even though the passer’s hand came forward with the football. B98 recovers at the A-18. The Replay Official stops the game to review a pass or fumble. The Referee announces, “There is no foul for intentional grounding. The ruling on the field is a fumble recovered by the defense. The previous play is under further review.” What if -- after B98 recovered the loose ball -- he jumped up from the ground and taunted A1? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/18 @ A-12 - Snap, since B98's taunting is a dead-ball foul that does not affect Team A's loss of down.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-22 - Snap, with the dead-ball foul enforced only 5 yards from the previous spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-12 - Snap, since B98's taunting cancels his own team's recovery of the fumble.
explanation: It is important to take care of all live-ball action first, and then deal with dead-ball action, because live-ball fouls do not offset dead-ball fouls (10-1-6-a). As explained in the answer to the original question, the result of the live ball action is loss of down at the spot of the pass, A - 4/18 @ A-12. When a live-ball foul by one team is followed by one or more dead-ball fouls by an opponent or by the same team, the penalties are administered separately and in the order of occurrence. So, the penalty for B98's dead-ball UNS will be enforced from the succeeding spot, A-12 → A-27 (10-2-2-a) and carries with it an automatic first down (9-2-1-a-1-b-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. From a scrimmage kick formation, A17 punts the ball to the B-10 where the ball hits B18 on the leg. As the ball rolls on the ground at the B-4, B18 kicks the ball to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B’s end zone and over the end line. What if B18's kick had gone out of bounds at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap (Accept) or B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap (Decline).
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, since the kick going out of bounds always returns possession to the kicking team.
choice_c: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with the safety no longer available once the ball goes out of bounds.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap (Accept) or B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap (Decline), reversing the accept/decline spots from the correct ruling.
explanation: This loose ball retains its status as a scrimmage kick throughout the down (8-7-2-c), so when it goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7). B18's foul meets all the criteria for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), and the PSK spot is the end of the kick, the B-2 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. The score is A-14, B-21 with 0:07 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A2 throws a low pass to A18 who is at the B-10 in the middle of the field. The ruling on the field is an incomplete pass and the clock reads 0:00. Video shows that A18 completed the catch with 0:02 left on the clock. What if the ruling on the field had been a catch, but video showed the pass was incomplete with 0:02 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over, since the 3-second restoration rule applies the same way whether the clock starts on the Ready or the Snap.
choice_b: 3/10 @ B-25 - Snap (0:02), regardless of whether or not Team A has a time out remaining.
choice_c: 3/10 @ B-25 - Ready (0:02), with the clock starting on the Ready since the ruling on the field is being overturned.
choice_d: 3/10 @ B-25 - Snap (0:02) only if Team A has a time out remaining, otherwise the half is over.
explanation: Properly officiated -- and with an immediate signal by the covering official and an immediate reaction by the game clock operator -- the clock would have stopped at 0:02 for the incomplete pass and restarted on the Snap (3-3-2-d-4). The prohibition of 12-3-6-d-2 that there must there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the ball should have been declared dead to restore time to the clock applies only when the clock will start on the Ready. This rule does not impact situations when the clock is stopped and will remain stopped until the Snap such as an incomplete pass or a ball carrier out of bounds.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Back A31 is stationary and lines up in the tackle box. Just after the snap, A31 immediately releases outside the tackle box. QB A19 is in the pocket to pass and A31 sees LB 14 blitzing. A31 returns to the tackle box and before A19 throws a pass, blocks LB14 below the waist from the front at the A-20. A19’s pass is complete for a 20-yard gain to the 50. What if A31 immediately steps up and blocks blitzing B14 at the A-31? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/25 @ A-15 - Ready, with a personal foul assessed since A31's block occurs outside the tackle box.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Ready, with the clock stopped despite the block being ruled legal.
choice_d: A - 3/20 @ A-40 - Ready, with a 5-yard penalty assessed for an illegal block below the waist.
explanation: Stationary Backs lined up with any part of their body inside the tackle box may block below the waist within the tackle box until the ball leaves the tackle box, provided the force of the initial contact is directed from the front (9-1-6-a-2). Though A31's block is technically not within the tackle box (it does not extend beyond the neutral zone, a block initiated 1-yard beyond the neutral zone is considered within the tackle box for both a lineman and back (AR-9-1-6-XII). Since A31's block otherwise meets the criteria, this block is legal.

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. The score is A-20, B-20 with 0:03 left in the 4th quarter. B11 intercepts A12’s pass in the end zone and returns it to the A-4 where he is tackled by the facemask. During B11’s run, B82 blocked A85 low in Team B’s end zone. What if B82 blocked A85 below the waist at the A-10? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Untimed, since Team A would always accept an offsetting-fouls untimed down.
choice_b: Game Over. A wins 22-20, since Team B's foul negates their own interception return.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Untimed, since accepting the penalty is Team B's only available option.
explanation: Team B undoubtedly does not want to give Team A another shot at the end zone, and since Team B did not foul before gaining possession, they might wish to decline offsetting fouls and retain possession after completion of the penalty for their foul (10-1-4-Exception 1). If accepted, this would bring up B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Untimed, as the period would have to be extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1). Team A, likewise, would not want to give Team B another play, so since any penalty may be declined (10-1-1-b), this would be Team A's best option. The period would not be extended since no penalty was accepted.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. First possession of the first extra period. B12 intercepts A10’s pass at the B-10 and returns it for a touchdown. During the return, B12 taunts A’s bench at the A-15 before scoring. What if B12 returned the interception to the A-10 where A34 tackled him by the face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_b: No Score. B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock, with the face mask foul carried over regardless of whether it is flagrant.
choice_c: Game Over. Team B wins by 6 points, since any personal foul during a change-of-possession return cancels the score.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with A34 automatically disqualified whether or not the contact is ruled flagrant.
explanation: Penalties for fouls by either team after a change of possession are declined by rule (3-1-3-g-1), unless the foul is "FUDL" (a Flagrant personal foul, an Unsportsmanlike conduct foul, a Dead-ball personal foul, or a Live-ball foul treated as a dead-ball foul. Since A34's face mask foul is just a personal foul (not a flagrant personal foul), there is no carry-over to the second possession of the first extra period (3-1-3-g-1-Exception). If officials determine that A34's action is flagrant, he would be disqualified, and the penalty would be enforced at the succeeding spot, A-25 → A-12½.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. A5’s kick is blocked by B33 and does not cross the neutral zone. The ball is recovered by B22 at the A-35 and he advances to the A-20 where he runs out of bounds. At the snap, Team A was in an illegal formation. What is Team B's best available option? What is the ruling?
explanation: Penalties for all fouls by the kicking team (other than KCI) during a scrimmage kick play (except FG attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced by rule either at the previous spot as the basic spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-3-13). In this play, the ball did not cross the neutral zone, so "tack-on" enforcement does not apply and the penalty could only be enforced from the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a). Team B would decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B22 signals for a fair catch at the B-10 but muffs the ball into the air as he attempts to catch the kick. The still airborne ball is subsequently caught by B33 at the B-5. What is the ruling?
explanation: If a Team B player makes a valid fair catch signal, muffs the kick, and that player or another Team B player subsequently catches the kick, the ball belongs to Team B where the player making the signal first touched it (6-5-1-b). So, when B33 catches the ball at the B-5, it is returned to the spot where B22 first touched it, the B-10. Note that this is not a fair catch since B33 did not give a valid fair catch signal (2-8-1-a).

question_text: 4/8 @ B-25. A5’s field goal attempt is low and B99 is able to deflect it at the B-23. The loose ball hits the ground at the B-15 and rolls to the B-5 where B22 muffs it into the end zone and then recovers it there while grounded. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap.  The ball is dead when it contacts the ground in the end zone.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap.  The ball is dead when it contacts the ground in the end zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap.  The ball remains alive until recovered by B22 in the end zone.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap.  The ball remains alive until recovered by B22 in the end zone.
explanation: The blocking of a scrimmage kick by an opponent of the kicking team who is not more than three yards beyond the neutral zone is considered to have occurred within or behind that zone (6-3-1-b), so B99 is not considered to have touched the ball beyond the neutral zone. But when B22 touched the ball well downfield on this unsuccessful field goal attempt, it meant that all rules of scrimmage kicks would apply (8-4-2-b-2). This means that the ball remained alive when it touched the ground in the end zone (6-3-9), and that the result of the play was simply a touchback (8-6-1-b).

question_text: 2/G @ B-8. A17 rolls to his right and throws a pass toward the end zone. A88 leaps, controlling the ball and being hit by B22 over the end zone which drives him back into the field of play. A88 lands on his feet at the B-2, runs 5 yards to his left and is tackled at the B-1. What is the ruling?
explanation: When an airborne pass receiver of either team completes a catch inbounds after an opponent has driven the receiver backward and the ball is declared dead at the spot of the catch, the forward progress is where the player gained and maintained firm control of the ball (5-1-3-a-Exception 2). By interpretation, when the firm control and contact occurs beyond B's goal line and the catch is subsequently completed, the ball is dead and it is a touchdown (AR-5-1-3-I-b).

question_text: 2/8 @ A-32. A17 drops back to pass and under pressure rolls to his right. B99 grabs A17’s face mask at the A-25 causing him to fumble the ball. The ball rolls forward to the A-34 where A67 recovers it while grounded. What is the ruling?
explanation: A17's run ended at the spot of his fumble, the A-25 (2-25-8-b), and the basic spot is the previous spot when the related run ends behind the neutral zone (10-2-2-d-1-a). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement is from the basic spot, A-32 → A-47 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. A17 throws a pass to A88 who catches it at the B-30. A88 runs to the B-20 where he fumbles the ball after a hard hit from B22. The ball rolls backwards and goes out of bounds at the B-24. B99 was flagged for roughing the passer after the pass was in flight. What is the ruling?
explanation: Enforcement of penalties for Team B personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls during a legal forward pass play is at the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e). The last run ended at the spot of A88's fumble, the B-20 (2-25-8-b), so that would be B-20 → B-10.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A17’s pass is intercepted by B33 in the end zone and returned for a touchdown. At the snap, Team A had five players in the backfield. During the interception return, B33 blocked A33 below the waist and B44 targeted A22, which Replay confirms. The score is canceled, and the next play will be: What is the ruling?
explanation: If both teams foul during a Try and Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, the fouls cancel, the down is not repeated, and the Try is over (8-3-4-c). However, penalties for "FUDL" fouls (a Flagrant personal foul, an Unsportsmanlike conduct foul, a Dead-ball personal foul, or a Live-ball foul treated as a dead-ball foul) are enforced on the succeeding kickoff (8-3-4-c-Exception). Targeting is a flagrant foul, so enforcement will be A-35 → 50.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-43. The score is A-17, B-23 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A34 runs to the B-6 and is tackled inbounds by the face mask with 0:02 remaining on the clock. What is Team A's best available option? What is the ruling?
explanation: Under normal circumstances, the clock would start here on the Ready; however, if the game clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) anytime after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd or 4th quarters and the clock would start by rule on the referee’s signal, it will start on the snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b). Team A would surely elect to have the clock started on the Snap.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. A5’s kick is blocked by B33 and does not cross the neutral zone. The ball is recovered by B22 at the A-35 and he advances to the A-20 where he runs out of bounds. At the snap, Team A was in an illegal formation. What is Team B's best available option? What if the kick struck the ground at the A-42 then rebounded to the A-35 where B22 recovered and advanced? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-15 - Snap, since a re-kick is always available whenever a kick fails to cross the neutral zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-20 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the succeeding spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, since Team B may not choose tack-on enforcement once the ball has been touched.
explanation: Penalties for all fouls by the kicking team (other than KCI) during a scrimmage kick play (except FG attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced by rule either at the previous spot as the basic spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-3-13). While the kick did not remain beyond the neutral zone, it did cross … and so "tack-on" enforcement applies, A-20 → A-15.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B22 signals for a fair catch at the B-10 but muffs the ball into the air as he attempts to catch the kick. The still airborne ball is subsequently caught by B33 at the B-5. What if B33 also gave a valid fair catch signal? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where B33 completed the catch.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where B22 first touched it.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, since a second player's fair catch signal voids the first player's protection.
explanation: If a Team B player makes a fair catch of a free kick behind Team B’s 25-yard line, the ball belongs to Team B at its own 25-yard line (6-5-1-a-Exception). Note that this is a fair catch since B33 gave a valid fair catch signal (2-8-1-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B22 signals for a fair catch at the B-10 but muffs the ball into the air as he attempts to catch the kick. The still airborne ball is subsequently caught by B33 at the B-5. What if B33 also gave an invalid fair catch signal? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, treating an invalid signal the same as a completed fair catch.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where B33 completed the catch.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, since an invalid signal forfeits Team B's right to the ball.
explanation: During a free kick, if a Team B receiver gives any waving signal that does not meet all of the requirements of a valid fair catch signal, and subsequently catches the ball behind the B 25-yard line, the ball belongs to Team B at its own 25-yard line (6-5-3-a-Exception). Note that this is not a fair catch since B33 did not give a valid fair catch signal (2-8-1-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B22 signals for a fair catch at the B-10 but muffs the ball into the air as he attempts to catch the kick. The still airborne ball is subsequently caught by B33 at the B-5. What if B22's muff struck the ground and bounced into B33's arms? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, treating the recovery the same as a completed catch under 6-5-1-b.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where B22 first touched it.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap, since the muff without a completed catch results in a change of possession to Team A.
explanation: A valid or invalid fair catch signal deprives the receiving team of the opportunity to advance the ball. The ball is declared dead at the spot of recovery, the B-5 (2-8-1-c). This is different from the original question (and 6-5-1-b does not apply) because B33 recovered rather than caught the muff.

question_text: 4/8 @ B-25. A5’s field goal attempt is low and B99 is able to deflect it at the B-23. The loose ball hits the ground at the B-15 and rolls to the B-5 where B22 muffs it into the end zone and then recovers it there while grounded. What if B99 deflected the ball as above, but B22 never touched the ball? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since the ball is dead the moment it strikes the ground in the end zone regardless of where Team B's touching occurred.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the ball spotted where it first struck the ground rather than at the previous spot.
choice_d: A - 4/8 @ B-25 - Snap, since an unsuccessful field goal attempt untouched by Team B beyond the neutral zone must be re-kicked.
explanation: The blocking of a scrimmage kick by an opponent of the kicking team who is not more than three yards beyond the neutral zone is considered to have occurred within or behind that zone (6-3-1-b), so B99 is not considered to have touched the ball beyond the neutral zone. If an unsuccessful field goal attempt untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone is declared dead, it belongs to Team B at the previous spot unless the previous spot was between its 20-yard line and its goal line (8-4-2-b-1). Also note that when this scrimmage kick untouched by Team B beyond the neutral zone touched the ground in the end zone, the ball became dead (6-3-9), and B22 simply recovered a dead ball.

question_text: 2/G @ B-8. A17 rolls to his right and throws a pass toward the end zone. A88 leaps, controlling the ball and being hit by B22 over the end zone which drives him back into the field of play. A88 lands on his feet at the B-2, runs 5 yards to his left and is tackled at the B-1. What if this were 2/6 @ B-8 with A88 leaping vertically at the B-1, controlling the ball and being driven back to the B-4, where he goes to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, treating the eventual grounding the same as a dead-ball catch in the end zone.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-4 - Running, with progress marked at the spot the receiver was driven back to rather than firm control.
choice_d: A - 2/G @ B-1 - Ready, with the down not advancing since the receiver went to the ground.
explanation: When an airborne pass receiver of either team completes a catch inbounds after an opponent has driven the receiver backward and the ball is declared dead at the spot of the catch, the forward progress is where the player gained and maintained firm control of the ball (5-1-3-a-Exception 2). Since the ball was declared dead (receiver went to ground) where the catch was completed (B-4), progress is given at the point of firm control (B-1).

question_text: 2/G @ B-8. A17 rolls to his right and throws a pass toward the end zone. A88 leaps, controlling the ball and being hit by B22 over the end zone which drives him back into the field of play. A88 lands on his feet at the B-2, runs 5 yards to his left and is tackled at the B-1. What if this were 2/6 @ B-8 with A88 leaping vertically at the B-1, controlling the ball and being driven back to the B-4, where he lands on his feet and attempts to gain additional yardage, only to be tackled at the B-3? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Running, with progress marked at the point of firm control since the catch was completed in the air.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, treating a catch followed by an advance the same as a dead-ball catch.
choice_d: A - 3/1 @ B-4 - Running, with the down marked at the spot the receiver was driven back to.
explanation: When an airborne pass receiver of either team completes a catch inbounds after an opponent has driven the receiver backward and the ball is declared dead at the spot of the catch, the forward progress is where the player gained and maintained firm control of the ball (5-1-3-a-Exception 2). Since the ball was not declared dead where the catch was completed (B-4), progress is not given at the point of firm control (B-1). The receiver-turned-runner advanced at his own peril to the B-3 where the ball became dead.

question_text: 2/8 @ A-32. A17 drops back to pass and under pressure rolls to his right. B99 grabs A17’s face mask at the A-25 causing him to fumble the ball. The ball rolls forward to the A-34 where A67 recovers it while grounded. What if A67 recovered at the A-34 and carried it to the 50 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-47 - Ready, with the penalty accepted and enforced from the basic spot instead of declined by Team A.
choice_b: A - 2/8 @ A-32 - Ready, with the play blown dead at the spot of B99's face mask foul instead of allowed to continue.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, since A67 recovering the fumble while grounded awards Team B the choice of enforcement.
explanation: There are two running plays during this down: A17's run, which includes all of the loose ball action following his fumble (2-30-4-c), and A67's run. B99's foul occurred during A17's run, so enforcement of the penalty for that foul is dictated by where A17's related run ended, which is the spot of the fumble, the A-25 (2-25-8-b). The basic spot is the previous spot when the related run ends behind the neutral zone (10-2-2-d-1-a), and since the foul was by the team not in possession, enforcement would be from the basic spot, A-32 → A-47 (10-2-2-c-3). Team A would elect to decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 2/8 @ A-32. A17 drops back to pass and under pressure rolls to his right. B99 grabs A17’s face mask at the A-25 causing him to fumble the ball. The ball rolls forward to the A-34 where A67 recovers it while grounded. What if -- rather than fumbling -- A17 got off a wobbling pass which was caught by A89 at the A-34 and advanced to the 50 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the catch instead of the end of the run.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, with the personal foul enforced only 5 yards from the end of the run.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, since the passing-play foul results in a change of possession.
explanation: A legal forward pass play is the interval between the snap and when a legal forward pass is complete, incomplete or intercepted (2-30-1), so B99's foul occurred during a passing play. Enforcement of penalties for Team B personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls during a legal forward pass play is at the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e), so here that would be 50 → B-35.

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. A17 throws a pass to A88 who catches it at the B-30. A88 runs to the B-20 where he fumbles the ball after a hard hit from B22. The ball rolls backwards and goes out of bounds at the B-24. B99 was flagged for roughing the passer after the pass was in flight. What if B46 recovered the loose ball at the B-24 just prior to it rolling out of bounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-12 - Ready, treating B46's recovery of the loose ball as a new running play.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-24 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot where the ball went out of bounds.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-24 - Snap, since B46's recovery of the fumble gives Team B possession at the end of the down.
explanation: Since there was a change of team possession during the down, 10-2-2-e does not apply and enforcement is from the previous spot, B-37 → B-22 (10-2-2-d-3).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. A17 throws a pass to A88 who catches it at the B-30. A88 runs to the B-20 where he fumbles the ball after a hard hit from B22. The ball rolls backwards and goes out of bounds at the B-24. B99 was flagged for roughing the passer after the pass was in flight. What if A46 recovered the loose ball at the B-24 just prior to it rolling out of bounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-22 - Ready, treating A46's recovery the same as no change in the run's ending spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-24 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the out-of-bounds spot rather than the spot of recovery.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-37 - Ready, with the personal foul enforced from the previous spot instead of the end of the run.
explanation: Since there was no change of team possession during the down, enforcement is from the end of the last run (10-2-2-e). A46's recovery of the loose ball constitutes a brief "run" (2-25-8-a), so enforcement is from that spot, B-24 → B-12.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A17’s pass is intercepted by B33 in the end zone and returned for a touchdown. At the snap, Team A had five players in the backfield. During the interception return, B33 blocked A33 below the waist and B44 targeted A22, which Replay confirms. The score is canceled, and the next play will be: What if -- in place of Team A having five players in the backfield -- B96 was lined up in the neutral zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with all of Team B's live-ball fouls enforced together for a combined penalty.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with B44 not disqualified since only one penalty may be enforced.
choice_c: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, since Team A must choose the flagrant-foul enforcement over the offside foul.
explanation: When two or more live-ball fouls by the same team are reported to the referee, the offended team may elect only one of these penalties (10-1-3). Team A would choose the only one which would allow them to repeat the Try, the one for B96's offside foul. All other penalties are declined, though B44 will still be disqualified.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A17’s pass is intercepted by B33 in the end zone and returned for a touchdown. At the snap, Team A had five players in the backfield. During the interception return, B33 blocked A33 below the waist and B44 targeted A22, which Replay confirms. The score is canceled, and the next play will be: What if -- in addition to all of the fouls in the original question -- B96 was lined up in the neutral zone? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with B96's offside foul enforced in addition to the offsetting fouls.
choice_c: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, since the additional pre-snap foul removes the option to repeat the Try.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with B44 not disqualified since the fouls offset.
explanation: If both teams foul during a Try and Team B fouls before the change of possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated, even if additional fouls occur after the change of possession (8-3-3-a). B44 will still be disqualified (10-1-4).

question_text: 2/7 @ A-43. The score is A-17, B-23 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A34 runs to the B-6 and is tackled inbounds by the face mask with 0:02 remaining on the clock. What is Team A's best available option? What if -- rather than a face mask foul -- Team B was offside at the snap and the clock stopped at 0:02? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Ready, since only a personal foul allows the offended team a clock option.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Option, with Team A given the choice between Ready and Snap.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-38 - Snap, with the offside foul enforced from the previous spot instead of declined.
explanation: Under normal circumstances, the clock would start here on the Ready; however, if the game clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) anytime after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd or 4th quarters and the clock would start by rule on the referee’s signal, it will start on the snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b). A penalty is completed when it is accepted, declined or canceled according to rule (10-1-1-a), and Team A would surely decline the penalty and elect to have the clock started on the Snap.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-43. The score is A-17, B-23 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A34 runs to the B-6 and is tackled inbounds by the face mask with 0:02 remaining on the clock. What is Team A's best available option? What if -- rather than a face mask foul -- Team B was offside at the snap and time expired (confirmed by Replay)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Untimed, treating the offside foul the same as the original face mask foul's spot.
choice_b: Game Over, since Team A cannot extend the period once time has expired on a Team B foul.
choice_d: A - 2/2 @ A-48 - Snap, with the period extended but the clock never actually stopping.
explanation: A period shall be extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul (3-2-3-a-1). Team A would rather decline the penalty, but if they do, the game is over. Their only option -- if they wish to have another play -- is to accept the penalty from the previous spot, A-43 → A-48.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. The score is A-7, B-10 late in the 2nd quarter. B42 is offside at the snap. A7 completes a pass to A9 who runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds with 0:23 remaining. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready.  Since Team A declined the penalty, the clock starts by rule on the Ready.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap.  Since the foul was by the team ahead in score, the clock starts by rule on the Snap.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Option.  Since the foul was by the team ahead in score, Team A has an option of Ready or Snap.
explanation: Team A will decline the penalty, and a penalty is considered completed whether it is accepted or declined (10-1-1-a). The clock would normally start on the Ready (3-2-3-e-4), but if the clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score anytime after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-15. Punter A49’s kick is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A89 recovers the bounding ball at the A-4 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.  The momentum exception does not apply.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap.  The momentum exception applies.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap.  The ball is dead as soon as A89 recovers it.
explanation: The momentum rule applies only to the catch or recovery of an opponent's pass, fumble, or kick (8-5-1-a-Exception a). So, the ball became dead in A89's possession behind his own goal line, and Team A was responsible for the ball being there, thus resulting in a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-35. A5 runs to the B-29 where a defender strips the ball and B35 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-27. B0 was offside at the snap. What is the ruling?
explanation: Although there is a change of possession, the clock will start on the Ready because Team B will not next snap the ball (3-2-3-d-3). The only reason for stopping the clock was to complete the penalty (3-2-3-e-4).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-45. A15's punt is blocked by B22 at the A-30. As the ball is rolling on the ground, A44 intentionally kicks the ball at the A-32 to prevent B55 from recovering. The ball rolls out of bounds at the A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: Illegally kicking the ball is a foul for which the penalty is 10 yards and a loss of down if by Team A, unless a legal scrimmage kick is beyond the neutral zone (9-4-4-Penalty). Since Illegally Kicking the Ball is not among the fouls listed in 10-2-2-b (Illegal Use of Hands, Holding, Illegal Block, and Personal Foul), enforcement is according to the Three- and-One principle. The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a), and since the foul by the team in possession is behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-32 → A-22 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-17. A’s field goal attempt is blocked. B18 muffs the ball at the B-13. The ball rolls out of bounds at the B-15. What is the ruling?
explanation: On an unsuccessful field goal, if Team B touches the ball after it crosses the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). If a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-17. A’s field goal attempt is blocked. The ball lands at the B-15, rebounds to the B-18 where it is muffed by A89, and subsequently rolls out of bounds at the B-19. What is the ruling?
explanation: On an unsuccessful field goal, if the ball crosses the neutral zone, is untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone, and is declared dead behind the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-3). If a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/15 @ A-45. B2 is deep to receive the kick. During the kick, gunner A3 hits B2 in the head/neck area with his forearm before the ball arrives at the B-5 and is flagged for Kick Catch Interference with Targeting. The ball is recovered by B1 at the B-10 and returned to the B-15. It will be: What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 regardless of whether or not Replay confirms the Targeting.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 regardless of whether of not Replay confirms the Targeting.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-30 if Replay confirms the Targeting, and B - 1/10 @ B-20 if Replay does not confirm the Targeting.
explanation: The penalty for KCI is 15 yards from the spot of the foul, B-5 → B-20 (6-4-1-Penalty), so if the Targeting is not confirmed, it will be B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap. If the Targeting is confirmed, the penalty for any foul by the kicking team (other than KCI) during a scrimmage kick play (except FG attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-15 → B-30 (6-3-13). So, if the Targeting is confirmed, it will be B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20, Near Hash. QB A8 throws a pass to A88 very close to the far sideline at the B-4. The LJ and FJ rule an incomplete pass, but the FJ has a foul for Defensive Pass Interference. The CJ also has a foul for Hands To The Face by the defense. The DPI is enforced, after which Replay initiates a review of the play and determines that A88 completed the catch at the B-4. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Far Hash.  After review, A - 1/G @ B-4 - Ready - Far Hash.
choice_b: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Far Hash.  After review, A - 1/G @ B-2 - Ready - Far Hash.
choice_c: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Near Hash.  After review, A - 1/G @ B-2 - Ready - Far Hash.
choice_d: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Near Hash.  After review, A - 1/G @ B-4 - Ready - Far Hash.
explanation: Before review, Team A would accept the penalty for DPI (15 yards from the previous spot, 7-3-8-c-Penalty) rather than HTF (half-the-distance from the previous spot, 10-1-6). Note that enforcement from the previous spot means both the yard line and the lateral position, so this would leave the ball on the Near Hash. After review, the catch would leave the ball at the B-4, so Team A would decline the penalty for DPI and accept the one for HTF, which as a personal foul can be "tacked-on" to the end of the last run, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. The score is A-7, B-10 late in the 2nd quarter. B42 is offside at the snap. A7 completes a pass to A9 who runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds with 0:23 remaining. What if time expired during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/5 @ A-40 - Untimed, with no option for Team A to instead go to halftime.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Untimed, treating the completed pass as if it still counted despite time expiring.
choice_c: A - 2/5 @ A-40 - Snap, with the period extended but the down replayed instead of advanced.
choice_d: A - 2/5 @ A-40 - Untimed or Half Over.
explanation: The period shall be extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul that occurred during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter (3-2-3-a-1). Unlike the original situation -- which requires only that the penalty be completed (accepted or declined) -- the period can not be extended if the penalty is declined.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-15. Punter A49’s kick is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A89 recovers the bounding ball at the A-4 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled. What if B89 picked up the ball at the A-8, then fumbled at the A-4 with A89 recovering at the A-2 and his momentum carrying him into the end zone where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, since the momentum exception does not apply to a recovered fumble.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, since Team B's ball carrier's momentum has no bearing on Team A's recovery.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-4 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where the fumble first occurred instead of where A89 gained possession.
explanation: Whenever a player, between his own team’s five-yard line and goal line, (a) recovers an opponent’s fumble, (b) his original momentum carries him into his own end zone, and (c) the ball remains behind his team’s goal line and is declared dead in his team’s possession there, the ball belongs to this player’s team at the spot where he gained possession (8-5-1-a-Exception).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-35. A5 runs to the B-29 where a defender strips the ball and B35 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-27. B0 was offside at the snap. What if this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, and B35 recovered the loose ball and ran out of bounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/2 @ B-30 - Snap, since a ball carrier going out of bounds always starts the clock on the snap after the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-27 - Snap, since B35 going out of bounds forfeits Team A's recovery rights.
choice_d: A - 3/7 @ B-35 - Ready, with the penalty declined since Team A gained no advantage from the strip.
explanation: The rule for starting the clock on the Snap when the ball goes out of bounds applies only to a Team A ball carrier (3-2-3-d-2). The only reason for stopping the clock was to complete the penalty, so the clock will start on the Ready. (3-2-3-e-4).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-45. A15's punt is blocked by B22 at the A-30. As the ball is rolling on the ground, A44 intentionally kicks the ball at the A-32 to prevent B55 from recovering. The ball rolls out of bounds at the A-40. What if A44 had batted the ball at the A-32 and it rolled out of bounds at the A-30? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, since a backward bat by the kicking team always returns possession to Team A.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-32 - Snap, with the ball placed where A44's bat occurred instead of the dead-ball spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with a penalty assessed for A44's bat even though it traveled backward.
explanation: A44's bat is backward (A-32 → A-30), and there is no foul for batting a ball backward in the field of play (9-4-1-c). If a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-17. A’s field goal attempt is blocked. B18 muffs the ball at the B-13. The ball rolls out of bounds at the B-15. What if A18 -- rather than B18 -- muffed the ball at the B-13? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-17 - Snap, with the ball spotted at the previous spot regardless of where it falls relative to the 20.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot of A18's muff.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, since Team A retains possession once its own player muffs the ball.
explanation: On an unsuccessful field goal, if the ball untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone is declared dead [beyond the neutral zone], it belongs to Team B at the previous spot unless the previous spot was between its 20-yard line and its goal line. In that case Team B will next snap the ball at its 20-yard line (8-4-2-b-1). The snap shall be from midway between the hash marks unless Team B selects a different location on or between the hash marks before the ready-for-play signal (8-4-2-b-1-a).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-17. A’s field goal attempt is blocked. The ball lands at the B-15, rebounds to the B-18 where it is muffed by A89, and subsequently rolls out of bounds at the B-19.
