What if B89 -- rather than A89 -- muffed the ball at the B-18, and A89 recovered the loose ball at the B-19? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, since Team B's prior touching always results in Team B retaining the ball.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-18 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot of B89's muff instead of A89's recovery.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-17 - Snap, treating this the same as an untouched field goal attempt beyond the neutral zone.
explanation: On an unsuccessful field goal, if Team B touches the ball after it crosses the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). When a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone touches a player of the receiving team who is inbounds, any player may catch or recover the ball (6-3-3). When a player of the kicking team catches or recovers a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone, the ball becomes dead, and because of Team B's prior touching, Team A is in legal possession of the ball (6-3-6-a).

question_text: 4/15 @ A-45. B2 is deep to receive the kick. During the kick, gunner A3 hits B2 in the head/neck area with his forearm before the ball arrives at the B-5 and is flagged for Kick Catch Interference with Targeting. The ball is recovered by B1 at the B-10 and returned to the B-15. It will be: What if the flag is for Targeting only (confirmed), and the ball is muffed by B2 and recovered by A1 at the B-10? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, since Team B's recovery of the muffed kick gives them possession regardless of enforcement.
choice_c: A - 4/15 @ A-45 - Snap, since there is no foul once Targeting is the only penalty confirmed.
choice_d: A - 4/30 @ A-30 - Snap, with Team B given the option to instead take the ball at the spot of the muff.
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot on legal kick plays unless the foul is governed by postscrimmage kick rules (10-2-2-d-4-a), and since there is no foul for KCI (6-4-1-Penalty) and the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B (6-3-13), previous spot enforcement, A-45 → A-30, is the only option.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20, Near Hash. QB A8 throws a pass to A88 very close to the far sideline at the B-4. The LJ and FJ rule an incomplete pass, but the FJ has a foul for Defensive Pass Interference. The CJ also has a foul for Hands To The Face by the defense. The DPI is enforced, after which Replay initiates a review of the play and determines that A88 completed the catch at the B-4. What if the ball had been snapped on 1/10 @ B-12, Far Hash, and that the interference had occurred very close to the near sideline in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap - Near Hash. After review, Touchdown for A with the penalty for HTF declined by rule.
choice_b: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-6 - Snap - Far Hash. After review, Touchdown for A with no option to enforce either foul.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap - Far Hash (Before Review) and Touchdown for A (After Review) with the penalty for HTF enforced on the Try or the succeeding kickoff.
choice_d: Before review, A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap - Far Hash. After review, the touchdown is negated by the HTF foul.
explanation: Before review, Team A would accept the penalty for DPI (to the 2-yard line from the previous spot, 7-3-8-c-Penalty) rather than HTF (half-the-distance from the previous spot, 10-1-6). Note that enforcement from the previous spot means both the yard line and the lateral position, so this would leave the ball on the Far Hash. After review, the catch would result in a Touchdown, and penalties for personal fouls by the non-scoring team on a down ending in a touchdown may be enforced either on the Try or the succeeding kickoff (10-2-5-a-1). So Team A would decline the penalty for DPI and accept the penalty for HTF on either the Try or kickoff.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A’s kick is blocked. B21 recovers the ball at the B-5 and advances across Team A’s goal line. During B21’s run, B44 clips A88 at the B-7. The score is canceled and the ball will next be put into play: What is the ruling?
explanation: For fouls during a Try after a change of team possession, penalties against either team are declined by rule, with the exception that penalties for flagrant personal fouls, unsportsmanlike conduct fouls, dead-ball personal fouls and live- ball fouls treated as dead-ball fouls are enforced on the succeeding kickoff or at the succeeding spot in extra periods. (8-3-4-a). While Clipping is a personal foul, it is not a flagrant personal foul, and so the penalty is declined by rule.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B88 gives a valid fair catch signal while standing at the B-43. As the ball sails over his head and into and out of the end zone, B88 blocks A33 at the B-38. Which of these would be an option for Team A? What is the ruling?
explanation: A player of Team B who has made a valid or invalid signal for a fair catch and does not touch the ball shall not block or foul an opponent during that down (6-5-4), so B88 has committed a foul. When this occurs during a free kick, the penalty is 10 yards from the spot of the foul, B-38 → B-28 (6-5-4-Penalty). It will be more advantageous for Team A to simply decline the penalty, bringing up B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap (8-6-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-45. A12 receives the shotgun snap and throws a forward pass toward A82. The ball is deflected high in the air by B92 and A12 catches the pass at the 50. After catching the ball, A12 rolls toward the sideline and from the 50 throws the ball into the sideline where it lands at the B-35. What is the ruling?
explanation: A forward pass is illegal if it is the second forward pass during the same down (7-3-2-d). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, 50 → A-45, with a loss of down (7-3-2-d-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. A33 takes a handoff from A11 and runs to the B-15 where he is hit and fumbles. B22 recovers the ball and runs to the A-5 where he is tackled. During B22's run, B56 blocked A34 in the back at the A-20. At the snap, Team A had 5 players in the backfield. What is the ruling?
explanation: In extra periods, if both teams foul during the down and Team B did not foul before the change of possession, the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated, with the exception that penalties for flagrant personal fouls, unsportsmanlike conduct fouls, dead-ball personal fouls and live-ball fouls treated as dead-ball fouls are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-3). Since an Illegal Block in the Back is neither a personal foul nor flagrant, B will begin their possession series at the A-25.

question_text: 4/7 @ B-43. A5’s punt hits the ground at the B-2 and is across the goal line and over the end zone when A88 bats the ball back into the field of play. The kick is recovered by B33 at the B-5 and is returned to the B-25 where he is hit and fumbles. A44 recovers the loose ball and runs across Team B’s goal line. During the kick, B44 held A66 at the B-28. What is the ruling?
explanation: If a scrimmage kick untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone is batted in Team B’s end zone by a player of Team A, it is a violation for illegal touching, with the spot of the violation the B-20 (6-3-11). Five- and 10-yard penalties for fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down. Although postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply since Team B will not next put the ball into play (10-2-3-b-4), enforcement of the 10-yard penalty is possible from the previous spot, B-43 → B-33 (10-2-2-d-4-a). Enforcement of the penalty cancels the illegal touching privilege (6-3-2-b).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. Team B has 12 players in the formation and no one is attempting to leave the field. The ball is ready for play, both teams are in formation, and the snap is imminent. QB A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense and Team A’s head coach calls a time out. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/5 @ B-35 - Ready.  Warning to Team B.  Team A is offered their timeout back.
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ B-35 - Snap.  No foul.  Team A is charged a timeout.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Ready.  Dead-ball foul.  Team A is offered their timeout back.
explanation: When the deep officials count 12 Team B players, both teams are in formation, no Team B player is attempting to leave the field, the snap is imminent, and Team A calls a time out, the crew will offer Team A their time out back and penalize Team B for a dead-ball Illegal Substitution foul (AR-3-5-3-VII-a).

question_text: 3/6 @ B-28. The score is A-33, B-35 with 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter. Team A attempts a field goal that is blocked by B4. The ball is rolling toward the sideline and is muffed out of bounds by B88 at the B-35 as the clock runs to 0:00. Video shows that 0:01 remained when the ball went out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: In the fourth quarter, to restore time, the score differential must be eight points or less, which is the case here (12-3-6-d-3). Since the clock should have stopped by rule when the ball went out of bounds at 0:01 and would not start until the snap because of the legal kick down (3-3-2-d-8), Replay will reset the clock to 0:01 (12-3-6-d-2). If an unsuccessful field goal attempt does not cross the neutral zone, all rules of scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2), and when a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. The score is A-17, B-21 late in the 4th quarter, and Team A has no time outs remaining. QBA1 scrambles out of the pocket and is hit as he throws at the B-45. The pass hits the ground at the B-37 and is ruled incomplete (0:36). Video shows that A1’s left knee was down before he released the pass (0:40). A76’s helmet came off during the down. What is the ruling?
explanation: Replay will overturn the ruling on the field to the quarterback down at the B-45 (12-3-3-c). Without a helmet off, this would trigger a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c) … and properly officiated (quarterback ruled down) with a helmet off, there would also be a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b). But, if there is an injury or helmet off at any point during a play which results in a replay reversal to a running clock, there is no runoff and also no clock adjustment (2023 Instant Replay Memo #1).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A’s kick is blocked. B21 recovers the ball at the B-5 and advances across Team A’s goal line. During B21’s run, B44 clips A88 at the B-7. The score is canceled and the ball will next be put into play: What if B44's foul occurred before B21 gained possession of the loose ball? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, since a foul before the change of possession is still declined by rule on a Try.
choice_b: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, since a live-ball personal foul during a Try is always carried to the succeeding kickoff.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with PSK enforcement applied since the ball crossed the neutral zone.
explanation: Since B44's foul occurred before a change of team possession, the penalty will be enforced. Postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply since this is a Try (10-2-3-b-1), and so enforcement is from the previous spot, B-3 → B-1½ (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B88 gives a valid fair catch signal while standing at the B-43. As the ball sails over his head and into and out of the end zone, B88 blocks A33 at the B-38. Which of these would be an option for Team A? What if this occurred during a punt on 4/10 @ 50 with B88 signaling at the B-28 and then blocking at the B-23? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-23 - Snap, with the penalty enforced the full 10 yards from the spot of the foul instead of the PSK spot.
choice_c: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, treating a punt the same as a free kick for enforcement purposes.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-33 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot rather than the PSK spot.
explanation: A player of Team B who has made a valid or invalid signal for a fair catch and does not touch the ball shall not block or foul an opponent during that down (6-5-4), so B88 has committed a foul. When this occurs during a scrimmage kick, the penalty is 10 yards administered according to postscrimmage kick enforcement. The PSK spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-b), and since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) is beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-45. A12 receives the shotgun snap and throws a forward pass toward A82. The ball is deflected high in the air by B92 and A12 catches the pass at the 50. After catching the ball, A12 rolls toward the sideline and from the 50 throws the ball into the sideline where it lands at the B-35. What if -- clearly after A12 releases the second pass -- B96 charges into him, knocking him to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with the Illegal Forward Pass foul enforced instead of the offsetting fouls rule.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, since Roughing the Passer after an illegal pass results in a turnover on downs.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap since this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout.
explanation: A passer is a player who throws a forward pass (2-27-5), and no defensive player shall unnecessarily rough a passer when it is obvious the ball has been thrown (9-1-9-a). Note that there is no requirement that the forward pass be legal in order for there to be Roughing the Passer. When there are live-ball fouls by both teams, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. A33 takes a handoff from A11 and runs to the B-15 where he is hit and fumbles. B22 recovers the ball and runs to the A-5 where he is tackled. During B22's run, B56 blocked A34 in the back at the A-20. At the snap, Team A had 5 players in the backfield. What if B22's foul had been an Illegal Blind Side Block with Targeting, confirmed by Replay? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, since a blindside block with confirmed Targeting is treated the same as any other foul in extra periods.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with Team A retaining the ball since Team B never gained legal possession.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with B22 not disqualified since the block occurred before the change of possession.
explanation: In extra periods, if both teams foul during the down and Team B did not foul before the change of possession, the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated, with the exception that penalties for flagrant personal fouls, unsportsmanlike conduct fouls, dead-ball personal fouls and live-ball fouls treated as dead-ball fouls are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-3). Since Targeting is a flagrant foul (2-10-3), the penalty is enforced on the succeeding play, A-25 → A-40.

question_text: 4/7 @ B-43. A5’s punt hits the ground at the B-2 and is across the goal line and over the end zone when A88 bats the ball back into the field of play. The kick is recovered by B33 at the B-5 and is returned to the B-25 where he is hit and fumbles. A44 recovers the loose ball and runs across Team B’s goal line. During the kick, B44 held A66 at the B-28. What if B44's foul had been a block below the waist? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-33 - Snap, treating a block below the waist the same as the holding foul in the original question.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with no option for Team A to instead take a re-kick.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since Team B may still claim the illegal-touching spot despite the personal foul.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock or A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
explanation: If a scrimmage kick untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone is batted in Team B’s end zone by a player of Team A, it is a violation for illegal touching, with the spot of the violation the B-20 (6-3-11). Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls and for unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are enforced on the try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1). Enforcement of the penalty cancels the illegal touching privilege (6-3-2-b).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. Team B has 12 players in the formation and no one is attempting to leave the field. The ball is ready for play, both teams are in formation, and the snap is imminent. QB A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense and Team A’s head coach calls a time out. What if the play clock had expired? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ B-35 - Ready, with only a warning issued once the play clock expires.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the illegal substitution foul enforced with the clock on the snap.
choice_d: A - 3/5 @ B-35 - Snap, with no foul recognized once the play clock has expired.
explanation: When the deep officials count 12 Team B players, both teams are in formation, no Team B player is attempting to leave the field, the snap is imminent, and the play clock expires, there is no foul for Delay of Game. Team B will be penalized for a dead-ball Illegal Substituion foul (AR-3-5-3-VII-b).

question_text: 3/6 @ B-28. The score is A-33, B-35 with 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter. Team A attempts a field goal that is blocked by B4. The ball is rolling toward the sideline and is muffed out of bounds by B88 at the B-35 as the clock runs to 0:00. Video shows that 0:01 remained when the ball went out of bounds. What if the kick was partially blocked, the ball was muffed by B88 at the B-22 and rolled out of bounds at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over, since a muffed loose ball going out of bounds cannot restore time to the clock.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-22 - Snap (0:01), with the ball placed at the spot of the muff instead of the out-of-bounds spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Ready (0:01), with the clock starting on the ready instead of the snap.
explanation: In the fourth quarter, to restore time, the score differential must be eight points or less, which is the case here (12-3-6-d-3). Since the clock should have stopped by rule when the ball went out of bounds at 0:01 and would not start until the snap because of the legal kick down (3-3-2-d-8), Replay will reset the clock to 0:01 (12-3-6-d-2). If an unsuccessful field goal attempt is touched by Team B after it crosses the neutral zone, all rules of scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2), and when a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball becomes dead and belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. The score is A-17, B-21 late in the 4th quarter, and Team A has no time outs remaining. QBA1 scrambles out of the pocket and is hit as he throws at the B-45. The pass hits the ground at the B-37 and is ruled incomplete (0:36). Video shows that A1’s left knee was down before he released the pass (0:40). A76’s helmet came off during the down. What if, in addition to the above, Team A had been in an illegal formation at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/20 @ B-45 - Ready (0:40), with Team A retaining the ball despite the replay reversal.
choice_b: B - 4/20 @ B-45 - Ready (0:36), with no runoff adjustment made for the helmet coming off.
choice_d: B - 4/20 @ B-45 - Snap (0:40), with Team B required to accept the illegal formation penalty.
explanation: Replay will overturn the ruling on the field to the quarterback down at the B-45 (12-3-3-c). This creates a stopped clock to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4), so the clock will be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule, 0:40 (12-3-6-b). There is no 10-second runoff for the helmet off because this was not the only reason for the clock stopping (3-3-10-b). Team B will decline the penalty for the Illegal Formation.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. The score is tied with 0:18 remaining in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. QB A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-40 that falls incomplete in an area where there are no eligible receivers -- only ineligible A58 who touches the ball trying to catch it. There are 0:12 remaining at the end of the play. What is the ruling?
explanation: A10's pass is an Illegal Forward Pass because he was not outside the tackle box when he threw this pass into an area with no eligible offensive receivers (7-3-2-h-Exception). A58 is not guilty of Illegal Touching since this was not a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty for the Illegal Forward Pass is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-40 (7-3-2-h-Penalty), and since this is an incomplete illegal forward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout, there is also an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). The clock starts on the Ready if Team B accepts the runoff (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Early in the 4th quarter, A22 takes a handoff and runs for a 35-yard gain and is down inbounds. During the play, A80 is guilty of holding at the A-35 and B74’s helmet comes completely off (not as a direct result of a foul). What is the ruling?
explanation: A80's foul did not occur behind the neutral zone, so enforcement is not automatically from the previous spot (10-2-2-b). The foul occurred during a running play which ended beyond the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-25 (10-2-2-d-1-b). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, A-35 → A-25 (10-2-2-c-1). The play clock is set to 40 seconds if the helmet comes completely off a player of the defensive team (3-2-4-c-14, 3-3-10-b-1). There is some ambiguity in 3-2-4-c, and there is no parallel statement to 3-3-2-f (Snap supersedes Ready) for the play clock. But, the fact that the a 40-second play clock for a defensive helmet / injury takes precedence over a 25-second play clock for an offensive helmet / injury implies that the 40-second play clock would also "trump" the 25-second play clock for any other administrative stop, and there have been informal interpretations to this effect.

question_text: 4/8 @ B-40. Team A’s field goal attempt is blocked by B99 at the B-41. B4 jumps at the B-38 and grabs the ball in mid- air. Just as B4 is returning to the ground, the ball is hit by the helmet of a teammate and becomes loose. A42 scoops up the loose ball at the B-39 and runs to the B-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: B4's action is not that of blocking a scrimmage kick (6-3-1-b), so when he touches the ball beyond the neutral zone, all players become eligible to catch or recover (6-3-3). Since B4 did not complete the catch (2-4-3), this is a muff, and A42's recovery at the B-39 caused the ball to become dead (6-3-6-a) and a new series is awarded to Team A (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: 4/13 @ B-28. The score is A-14, B-23 with 1:58 remaining in the 4th quarter and Team A with two timeouts remaining. At the snap, B98 grabs tackle A70 and pulls him to the left allowing B55 to run through the gap. B55 tries to block the field goal attempt, but the kicked ball travels through the uprights. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Both B and D are Options.
choice_b: Both A and C are Options.
choice_c: Both B and E are Options.
explanation: B98's "pull-and-shoot" action is Defensive Holding (9-3-4-d), for which the penalty is 10 yards and an automatic first down (9-3-4-Penalty). When the field goal is successful and Team B commits a foul for which the penalty is 5 or 10 yards, Team A has the option of canceling the score with the penalty enforced from the previous spot or declining the penalty and accepting the score (10-2-5-d).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A4’s pass is intercepted by B16 at the B-3, and his momentum carries him into B’s end zone. B16 runs around in the end zone, then is hit and fumbles. The ball rolls out of bounds at the B-2. There are no fouls on the play. What is the ruling?
explanation: When a player between his team’s five-yard line and goal line (a) intercepts a pass, (b) his momentum him into his own end zone, and (c) the ball remains behind the goal line, the ball belongs to his team at the spot where he gained possession (8-5-1-a-Exception). Condition (c) includes a fumble that goes from the end zone into the field of play and out of bounds, since the ball would be returned by rule to the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1) and it would be "as if" the ball never left the end zone.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-41. Punter A99 muffs the snap and the ball is rolling on the ground at the A-32. To keep Team B from recovering, he kicks the ball at the A-32 while the ball is on the ground, and it subsequently goes out of bounds at the A-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: A99 is guilty of Illegally Kicking a Loose Ball (9-4-4) for which the penalty is 10 yards and a loss of down. This foul occurred behind the neutral zone, but is not Illegal Use of Hands, Holding, an Illegal Block, or a Personal Foul (10-2-2-b), so Three-And-One Enforcement applies. The foul occurred during a running play (there was no kick) with the related run ending where the snap was released (the backward pass was "thrown" -- 2-25-8-e), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-32 → A-22 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 3/12 @ B-22. Ball on the right hash. Slot receiver A87 makes a move towards the left sideline and is grabbed at the waist by B32 at the B-16 before the ball is thrown. The pass toward A87 falls incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: B32 did not commit Defensive Pass Interference because his restriction occurred prior to the pass (7-3-8-c), but he is guilty of Defensive Holding (9-3-4-d). The foul occurred during a legal forward pass, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-22 → B-12 (10-2-2-c-3), and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-Penalty). Note that the "previous spot" means both the yard line and the lateral position, so the ball will remain on the right hash.

question_text: 3/G @ B-2. 1:25 remaining in the 4th quarter and both teams have a time out remaining. QB A9 scrambles and as he approaches the goal line, leaps in the air to attempt to score. He subsequently gets flipped in the air and lands in the end zone. The ruling on the field is a touchdown. After the score, QB A9 momentarily lays on the field injured, and officials call an injury timeout. Thereafter, Replay reviews the scoring play, where video shows the runner was down short of the goal line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: There is an option for a 10-second runoff which either team may avoid by calling a timeout.
choice_b: There is an option for a 10-second runoff which Team B may decline or Team A may avoid by calling a timeout.
choice_c: There is no option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: Replay will overturn the ruling on the field to A9 down at the B-½ (12-3-3-c). Without an injury, this would trigger a 10- second runoff (12-3-6-c) … and properly officiated (quarterback ruled short) with an injury, there would also be a 10- second runoff (3-3-6-f). But, if there is an injury or helmet off at any point during a play which results in a replay reversal to a running clock, there is no runoff and also no clock adjustment (2023 Instant Replay Memo #1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. The score is tied with 0:18 remaining in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. QB A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-40 that falls incomplete in an area where there are no eligible receivers -- only ineligible A58 who touches the ball trying to catch it. There are 0:12 remaining at the end of the play. What if A10 had been outside the tackle box and the pass landed beyond the neutral zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/20 @ B-40 - Ready (0:02), treating the legal pass the same as the original illegal forward pass ruling.
choice_b: A - 4/15 @ B-35 - Ready (0:02), with a runoff applied since the pass is now a legal forward pass.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-31 - Snap (0:12), since A58's touching of a legal forward pass is a turnover on downs.
explanation: There is no foul for an Illegal Forward Pass because the passer was outside the tackle box and threw the ball so that it landed beyond the neutral zone (7-3-2-h-Exception). Since this pass is now legal, though, A58 has now committed Illegal Touching (7-3-11), for which the penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-30 → B-35, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Early in the 4th quarter, A22 takes a handoff and runs for a 35-yard gain and is down inbounds. During the play, A80 is guilty of holding at the A-35 and B74’s helmet comes completely off (not as a direct result of a foul). What if A80's foul occurred at the A-28 and A56 was injured during the play? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/15 @ A-25 - Ready / 40, with the play clock set for a defensive-player helmet coming off.
choice_c: A - 1/20 @ A-20 - Ready / 40, treating an offensive-player injury the same as a defensive-player helmet coming off.
choice_d: A - 1/15 @ A-25 - Ready / 25, with the basic spot set at the end of the run instead of the previous spot.
explanation: A80's foul occurred behind the neutral zone and is one of the specified fouls (Illegal Use of Hands, Holding, Illegal Block, Personal Foul), so enforcement is from the previous spot, A-30 → A-20 (10-2-2-b). The play clock is set to 25 seconds if the injury is to an offensive player (3-2-4-c-5, 3-3-6-f-1).

question_text: 4/8 @ B-40. Team A’s field goal attempt is blocked by B99 at the B-41. B4 jumps at the B-38 and grabs the ball in mid- air. Just as B4 is returning to the ground, the ball is hit by the helmet of a teammate and becomes loose. A42 scoops up the loose ball at the B-39 and runs to the B-32. What if B4 returned to the ground, then advanced to the B-40 before being hit and the ball coming loose? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-39 - Snap, treating B4's advance and fumble the same as a muff of the kick.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, since B4's fumble after the catch returns the ball to Team B by rule.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where B4 originally caught it.
explanation: Since B4 completed the catch (2-4-3), this is no longer a kick. B4 has fumbled the ball (2-11-1), and a fumble which is caught of recovered by any player continues in play (7-2-2-a).

question_text: 4/13 @ B-28. The score is A-14, B-23 with 1:58 remaining in the 4th quarter and Team A with two timeouts remaining. At the snap, B98 grabs tackle A70 and pulls him to the left allowing B55 to run through the gap. B55 tries to block the field goal attempt, but the kicked ball travels through the uprights. What if B98 moves forward to block the kick, leaving his feet and jumping into the plane directly above A70's frame? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-18 - Snap or A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, with the personal foul enforced the full 15 yards instead of half-the-distance.
choice_b: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, with no option for Team A to instead cancel the score and keep the ball.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-14 - Snap or A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-28 - Snap, since Leaping during a scoring kick always results in a change of possession.
explanation: B98 is guilty of Leaping (9-1-11-b), for which the penalty is 15 yards (here, half-the-distance, 10-2-6) and an automatic first down (9-1-11-Penalty). When the field goal is successful and Team B commits a personal foul or unsportsmanlike conduct foul, Team A has the option of canceling the score with the penalty enforced from the previous spot or accepting the score with the penalty enforced on the succeeding kickoff or from the succeeding spot in extra periods (10-2-5-d).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A4’s pass is intercepted by B16 at the B-3, and his momentum carries him into B’s end zone. B16 runs around in the end zone, then is hit and fumbles. The ball rolls out of bounds at the B-2. There are no fouls on the play. What if B16's fumble was muffed by A25 at the B-2 and recovered by B16 while grounded in his end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the momentum exception still applying since B16 recovered his own fumble.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, since A25's muff of the loose ball gives Team A possession.
choice_c: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with no safety recognized since B16's fumble was accidental.
explanation: The "momentum exception" no longer applies because the ball did not remain behind the goal line, and was not fumbled forward and out bounds (8-5-1-a-Exception c). B16's fumble is the impetus which is responsible for the ball’s progress in any direction, even though its course is reversed after being muffed by A25 (8-7-2-a). So, since the ball became dead in the B16's possession behind his goal line, and he was responsible for the ball being there, it is a safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 4/9 @ A-41. Punter A99 muffs the snap and the ball is rolling on the ground at the A-32. To keep Team B from recovering, he kicks the ball at the A-32 while the ball is on the ground, and it subsequently goes out of bounds at the A-24. What if A99 was able to punt the ball, but it was blocked behind the neutral zone and then illegally kicked as above? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-24 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement applied since the original kick was blocked beyond the neutral zone.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-32 - Snap, since Team A retains the ball once B99 blocks the punt.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-32 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the block instead of the spot of the illegal kick.
explanation: "Tack-on" enforcement from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, the A-24, is not possible because the scrimmage kick did not cross the neutral zone (6-3-13). A99 is guilty of Illegally Kicking a Loose Ball (9-4-4) for which the penalty is 10 yards and a loss of down, unless it occurs when a legal scrimmage kick is beyond the neutral zone, which was not the case here. This foul occurred behind the neutral zone, but is not Illegal Use of Hands, Holding, an Illegal Block, or a Personal Foul (10-2-2-b), so Three-And-One Enforcement applies. The foul occurred during a legal kick play, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-32 → A-22 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 3/12 @ B-22. Ball on the right hash. Slot receiver A87 makes a move towards the left sideline and is grabbed at the waist by B32 at the B-16 before the ball is thrown. The pass toward A87 falls incomplete. What if A87's grab did not occur until just prior to the pass arriving? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-16 - Snap - Right Hash, with the lateral position unaffected by the spot of the foul.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-22 - Snap - Left Hash, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-22 - Snap, since incidental contact before the pass arrives is never a foul.
explanation: B32 has committed Defensive Pass Interference (7-3-8-c). The penalty is Team A's ball at the spot of the foul if the foul occurred fewer than 15 yards from the previous spot (7-3-8-c-Penalty). Note that "spot of the foul" means both the yard line and the lateral position, so the ball will be placed on the left hash.

question_text: 3/G @ B-2. 1:25 remaining in the 4th quarter and both teams have a time out remaining. QB A9 scrambles and as he approaches the goal line, leaps in the air to attempt to score. He subsequently gets flipped in the air and lands in the end zone. The ruling on the field is a touchdown. After the score, QB A9 momentarily lays on the field injured, and officials call an injury timeout. Thereafter, Replay reviews the scoring play, where video shows the runner was down short of the goal line. What if officials ruled A9 short of the goal line, but video shows the ball was beyond the plane of the goal line in his possession prior to him being down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with a 10-second runoff applied since the injury stopped the clock.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-½ - No Clock, with the ball spotted at the goal line instead of a touchdown being awarded.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with the clock reset to the time of the injury timeout instead of the touchdown.
explanation: Replay will overturn the ruling on the field to a touchdown (12-3-1-a) and reset the clock to the time that the ball was dead by rule, when the ball in A9's possession crossed the goal line (12-3-6-b). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the injury would not have been the only reason for stopping the clock (3-3-6-f). It would also have been stopped for the touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-45. QB A12 receives the snap from a shotgun formation and immediately hands off to A42 who runs towards the right side of the formation. He then throws a backwards pass from the B-49 to A12 who throws a high pass from the A-45 that just crosses the NZ and lands in the back of the team bench area. The nearest eligible receiver was A89 who was at the hash. What is the ruling?
explanation: A12 has thrown an Illegal Forward Pass because he threw the ball into an area where is no eligible Team A receiver (7-3-2-h), and he is not exempt from Intentional Grounding because even though he controlled the snap, he relinquished possession to A42 (7-3-2-h-Exception). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the A-45 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: 4/16 @ A-7. Team A’s punt is blocked, recovered by B89 at the A-10, and returned to the A-4 where he fumbles. The ball rolls out of bounds at the A-6. While the ball was loose during B89's fumble, A89 clipped B66 in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: A89's foul occurred during B89's run, which ended at the spot of his fumble (2-25-8-b). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-4 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, A-4 → A-2 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 1/G @ B-8. The score is A-12, B-14 with 1:01 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A12 runs up the middle to the B-6. B66 is flagged for Illegal Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: The foul occurred during a running play which ended beyond the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-6 (10-2-2-d-1-b). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-6 → B-3 (10-2-2-c-3). The clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score after the Two-Minute Timeout. Since the clock would start by rule on the Ready, Team A has the option for the clock to start on the Snap.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The ball is kicked deep and out of bounds at the B-1. B35 and B66 were lined up shoulder to shoulder at the B-38 and blocked A16 while the ball was in the air. What is the ruling?
choice_d: Both A and C are options.
explanation: This is a Free Kick Out of Bounds (6-2-1), but not a foul for an Illegal Wedge Formation. B35 and B66 formed a wedge (6-1-10-a) -- and normally it is illegal for two or more members of the receiving team to intentionally form a wedge for the purpose of blocking for the ball carrier, whether or not there is contact (6-1-10-b) -- but there is no foul if the kick is from an obvious onside kick formation (6-1-10-c) or if the play results in a touchback, a foul for free kick out of bounds, or fair catch (6-1-10-d). The three options available to Team B for Team A's Free Kick Out of Bounds are five yards from the previous spot (A - F/K @ A-30), five yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (B - 1/10 @ B-6), or 30 yards beyond Team A’s restraining line (B - 1/10 @ B-35). Only the first and third options are worthy of consideration.

question_text: 4/9 @ 50. Punter A1 is lined up on the left edge of the tackle box to receive the snap. The rugby style punter takes three steps to the left where he B55 takes aim to attack him with forcible contact to the head / neck area after the kick is away. B16 completes a fair catch at the B-20. What is the ruling?
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap.  Roughing with Targeting.
explanation: There is no foul for Roughing the Kicker because the A1's protection ends when he carries the ball outside the tackle box before kicking (9-1-16-a-4-b). A kicker in the act of or just after kicking a ball, or during the kick or the return is a defenseless player (2-27-14-c), so since B55 took aim to attack A1 with forcible contact to the head / neck area, this is a foul for Targeting (9-1-4). The penalty for any personal foul against the kicker who is in the act or just after kicking the ball is 15 yards from the previous spot and an automatic first down (9-1-16-a-Penalty).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The score is A-31, B-31 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. With the clock running, Team A is hurrying to the line to attempt a game-winning field goal. Just before the snap, A72 false starts, after which B96 charges across the neutral zone and is flagged for a personal foul. The clock stops at 0:03. Team B will accept the 10-second runoff, if applicable. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Ready (0:03).  Both fouls are penalized separately and in order of occurrence.
choice_b: 10-second runoff.  B96's foul will be penalized on the first possession of the first extra period.
choice_c: A - 2/5 @ B-15 - Ready (0:03). The yardage penalties cancel and the down established before the fouls is unaffected.
choice_d: 10-second runoff.  Neither foul will be penalized on the first possession of the first extra period.
explanation: Penalties for dead-ball fouls are administered separately and in order of occurrence (10-1-5). The instant that A72 commits a false start, it triggers not only a five-yard penalty (7-1-2-b-Penalty) but also a 10-second runoff since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a-1). Team B will accept the runoff, which will end the half and send the game to extra periods. The penalty for B96's foul is then enforced from the succeeding spot on the first possession of the first extra period (10-2-2-a). A similar situation is presented in AR-3-4-4-V.

question_text: 3/8 @ B-38. QB A12 throws a pass that is simultaneously controlled by A89 and B19 while both are off the ground. Both return to the ground with B19 touching first inbounds in the back corner of the end zone, after which A89 touches the end line pylon. Both players maintain control as they go the ground out of bounds. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock.  Not a simultaneous catch.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap.  Not a simultaneous catch.
explanation: In order for this to be a simultaneous catch, A89 and B19 would have to return to the ground inbounds at the same time (AR-7-3-6-I). That did not occur here. Rather, B19 first touched the ground inbounds after which A89 touched the ground out of bounds. A player who touches a pylon is out of bounds (4-2-1-c). This is not a simultaneous catch, and the pass is completed or intercepted by the player who first returned to the ground (AR-7-3-6-II), which in this case means an interception by B19. The result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a).

question_text: 2/8 @ B-48. The score is tied with 1:36 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A12 throws a completed pass to A84 at the B-39. The FJ has a hat down and a flag for A84 stepping out of bounds on his own then re-establishing inbounds and catching the pass. The CJ has a flag on B69 for Illegal Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. Replays shows that A84 jumped from out of bounds and never reestablished inbounds prior to touching the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A84 touched the pass before returning inbounds, it was an incomplete pass and not a foul for illegal touching (7-3-4, 7-3-7-a), and the clock starts by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-d-4). The only foul that remains is B69’s Illegal Use of Hands, and since it occurred during a legal forward pass play, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-48 → B-33 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-45. QB A12 receives the snap from a shotgun formation and immediately hands off to A42 who runs towards the right side of the formation. He then throws a backwards pass from the B-49 to A12 who throws a high pass from the A-45 that just crosses the NZ and lands in the back of the team bench area. The nearest eligible receiver was A89 who was at the hash. What if A12 never handed off, taking the ball outside the tackle box and throwing it as above, but with A67 at the B-40 when the pass was thrown? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 2/20 @ A-45 - Snap, treating the throwaway the same as an Illegal Forward Pass since A67 was five yards beyond the neutral zone.
choice_c: A - 2/25 @ A-40 - Snap, with loss of down assessed for Ineligible Receiver Downfield.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with no foul recognized but the down not advancing.
explanation: It is not a foul for an Illegal Forward Pass if the passer -- who controls the snap or resulting pass and does not relinquish possession to another player -- is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended (7-3-2-h-Exception). Also, even though A65 was five yards beyond the neutral zone when the pass was thrown, there is no foul for Ineligible Receiver Downfield since the passer was legally throwing the ball away and the ball landed near or beyond the sideline (7-3-10-Exception).

question_text: 4/16 @ A-7. Team A’s punt is blocked, recovered by B89 at the A-10, and returned to the A-4 where he fumbles. The ball rolls out of bounds at the A-6. While the ball was loose during B89's fumble, A89 clipped B66 in the end zone. What if A89's clip occurred while the ball was loose following the blocked punt and prior to B89's recovery? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-6 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement as the only available option once the ball is loose beyond the neutral zone.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ A-3 - Snap.
choice_c: Safety. 2 Points for B, with no free-kick option since the foul occurred before B89's recovery.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with no option for Team B to instead accept tack-on enforcement.
explanation: A89's foul occurred during a scrimmage kick play (2-30-3), and penalties for all fouls by the kicking team other than KCI during a scrimmage kick play (except FG attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced by rule either at the previous spot as the basic spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, at the option of Team B (6-3-13). A specific exception to this rule is that if the foul occurs in Team A's end zone, previous spot enforcement is replaced by a Safety (6-3-13-Exception). So, Team B may choose 2 points and the ball via a free kick, or may accept "tack-on" enforcement, A-6 → A-3. The subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B at the A-6 because when a fumble goes out of bounds behind the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the out-of-bounds spot (7-2-4-b-2).

question_text: 1/G @ B-8. The score is A-12, B-14 with 1:01 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A12 runs up the middle to the B-6. B66 is flagged for Illegal Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What if A12 has been tackled at the B-10 and the ball had been snapped with 2:01 remaining in the 4th quarter? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-4 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready since the Two-Minute Timeout had not yet occurred.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-6 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the basic spot.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap, with the penalty enforced the full 10 yards rather than half-the-distance.
explanation: The foul occurred during a running play which ended behind the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the previous spot, the B-8 (10-2-2-d-1-a). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-8 → B-4 (10-2-2-c-3). Since the ball was live when the game clock reached 2:00, play continued with the covering official stopping the clock when the ball was dead for the Two-Minute Timeout, and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-5-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The ball is kicked deep and out of bounds at the B-1. B35 and B66 were lined up shoulder to shoulder at the B-38 and blocked A16 while the ball was in the air. What if B17 touched the kick just prior to it rolling out of bounds at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the ball spotted at the location B17 touched the kick rather than the dead-ball spot.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, since B17's touching does not affect the Free Kick Out of Bounds enforcement options.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the Illegal Wedge Formation penalty enforced only 5 yards instead of 15.
explanation: Because B17 touched the kick, this is not a Free Kick Out of Bounds (6-2-1), and the ball belongs to Team B at the B-2 (6-2-2). And, since this is no longer a Free Kick Out of Bounds, Team B is guilty of an Illegal Wedge Formation (6-1-10-b). The penalty is 15 yards from the spot of the foul, or 15 yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B if this is behind the spot of the foul (6-1-10-Penalty). So here, enforcement will be half-the-distance, B-2 → B-1.

question_text: 4/9 @ 50. Punter A1 is lined up on the left edge of the tackle box to receive the snap. The rugby style punter takes three steps to the left where he B55 takes aim to attack him with forcible contact to the head / neck area after the kick is away. B16 completes a fair catch at the B-20. What if B55's contact against A1 occurred well after the ball had been kicked but prior to the fair catch? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, treating the delayed contact the same as Roughing the Kicker since A1 remains defenseless.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the PSK spot rather than half-the-distance to the goal.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with the previous-spot enforcement still available since the foul is for Targeting.
explanation: A1 remains defenseless throughout the down (2-27-14-c), so B55's actions still constitute Targeting (9-1-4), but since the foul did not occur just after the kick, the penalty is not enforced from the previous spot (9-1-16-a-Penalty). This foul now meets all of the conditions for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b) with a PSK spot at the end of the kick, the B-20 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The score is A-31, B-31 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. With the clock running, Team A is hurrying to the line to attempt a game-winning field goal. Just before the snap, A72 false starts, after which B96 charges across the neutral zone and is flagged for a personal foul. The clock stops at 0:03. Team B will accept the 10-second runoff, if applicable. What if, rather than an A72 false start and a B96 personal foul, there had been a B96 offside with contact? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Ready (0:03), with no option for Team A to decline the runoff.
choice_c: 10-second runoff. A - 2/5 @ B-15 - Snap (0:03), with the yardage penalty for the offside foul also applied.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap (0:03), with the personal-foul yardage enforced instead of the offside foul's five yards.
explanation: The instant that B96 jumps into the neutral zone with contact, it triggers not only a five- yard penalty (7-1-5-a-1-Penalty) but also a 10-second runoff since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a-1). Team A will then decline the runoff, which causes the clock to start on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-38. QB A12 throws a pass that is simultaneously controlled by A89 and B19 while both are off the ground. Both return to the ground with B19 touching first inbounds in the back corner of the end zone, after which A89 touches the end line pylon. Both players maintain control as they go the ground out of bounds. What if A89 first touched the end line plyon, after which B19 touched inbounds in the back corner of the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, treating the reversed touch order the same as the original simultaneous-catch ruling.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since B19's later touch inbounds is still considered the controlling event.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, ruling this a simultaneous catch since both players touched a boundary marker.
explanation: It is an incomplete pass when a player leaves his feet and receives the pass but first lands on or outside a boundary line. A player who touches a pylon is out of bounds (4-2-1-c). Since A89 had control of the ball and first touched the pylon, this pass is incomplete.

question_text: 2/8 @ B-48. The score is tied with 1:36 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A12 throws a completed pass to A84 at the B-39. The FJ has a hat down and a flag for A84 stepping out of bounds on his own then re-establishing inbounds and catching the pass. The CJ has a flag on B69 for Illegal Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. Replays shows that A84 jumped from out of bounds and never reestablished inbounds prior to touching the pass. What if the CJ had no foul for Illegal Hands to the Face, and the FJ's ruling was confirmed without stoppage by Replay? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/8 @ B-48 - Ready, since a confirmed ruling without stoppage still allows the down to be replayed.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-33 - Snap, with the Illegal Touching penalty still enforced despite the confirmed ruling.
choice_c: A - 2/8 @ B-48 - Snap, with the down counting since the FJ's original flag is no longer relevant.
explanation: A84 has committed Illegal Touching because he went out of bounds on his own and was the first to touch the pass inbounds (7-3-4). The penalty is loss of down at the previous spot (7-3-4-Penalty) -- not "an incomplete pass". This pass was complete, and as such the clock would start by rule on the Ready because the only reason for stopping was clock was to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4). Whenever the clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) anytime after the Two-Minute Timeout, and the clock would start by rule on the referee’s signal, it will start on the snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b)

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. A18 is in his own end zone and throws a forward pass which is blocked high into the air by B99. A39, on the end of his line of scrimmage at the snap, jumps and bats the ball at the A-4 across his end line. What is the ruling?
explanation: While a pass is in flight, any player who is eligible to touch the ball may bat it in any direction (9-4-1-a), except that a backward pass in flight may not be batted forward by the passing team (9-4-2). A39 is eligible, and therefore his bat is legal and this is simply an incomplete forward pass.

question_text: 2/G @ B-9. The score is tied with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A9 runs outside the pocket and fumbles. The ball bounces right back into his hands, and from the B-15 he throws the ball beyond the line of scrimmage and into the stands. 0:10 remain when the pass lands out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: It is not a foul for the passer to conserve yardage by throwing the ball forward into an area where there is no eligible Team A receiver, provided the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended, and provided the passer controlled the snap or the resulting backward pass and did not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the pass (7-3-2-h-Exception). A9 satisfied all of these requirements, and so this is simply an incomplete forward pass.

question_text: 2/12 @ B-18. The score is tied with 1:50 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A31 sweeps around the end with A57 leading the way. B24 blocks A57 at the knees at the B-18. A31 is tackled inbounds at the B-16. What is the ruling?
explanation: With the exception of players aligned in a stationary position within 1-yard of the line of scrimmage and within the tackle box on their initial line charge -- or when blocking or tackling the runner -- blocking below the waist by Team B players is illegal (9-1-6-b). B24's foul occurred during a running play which ended beyond the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-16 (10-2-2-d-1-b). Enforcement of the penalty for a foul by the team not in possession is from the basic spot, B-16 → B-8 (10-2-2-c-3). When the game clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. The score is tied with 1:21 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. B99 forcibly contacts A77’s facemask at the line of scrimmage, pushing his helmet off. A9 muffs the snap, and A77 chases the bouncing ball, recovering it while grounded at the A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: B99's contact to A77's facemask need not be continuous to be a foul (9-1-8-a) because this is more of a striking blow to the facemask (9-1-2-a). But when A77's helmet comes completely off, he is prohibited from continuing to participate beyond the immediate action in which he is engaged (9-1-17). So, both B99 and A77 have committed live ball fouls, which will offset (10-1-4). The down is replayed from the previous spot (5-2-8), and the since there were fouls by both teams, there will be no 3-4-3-b option and the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4). Since A77's helmet coming off was the direct result of a foul by an opponent, he may remain in the game (3-3-10-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. The score is tied with 0:09 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A19 throws a deep pass. B31 contacts A88 at the B-7 before the ball arrives, but A88 makes the catch falling at the B-2. The clock is at 0:00, but video evidence shows A88 down at 0:01. What is the best option available to Team A? What is the ruling?
explanation: Under normal circumstances, the clock would start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4), and when time expires in a half and the clock would start on the Ready after review, there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the ball should have been declared dead in order for Replay to restore time (12-3-6-d-2). But here, since the game clock should have stopped at 0:01 to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b). So, Team A would exercise this option, and Replay could then restore the one second. Team A need not accept the penalty as "to complete a penalty" means the penalty can be accepted or declined (10-1-1-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is tied in the second possession of the second extra period. Team A have five players in the backfield at the snap. A19 throws an interception to B11 who returns it for a touchdown. Video evidence shows B99 launching and forcibly contacting the head of A19 from the blind side at the B-35 during the return. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The second extra period continues with A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock.
choice_b: The third extra period begins with A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock or B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock.
choice_c: The third extra period begins with A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock or B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
choice_d: The third extra period begins with A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock or B - 1/10 @ A-3 - No Clock.
explanation: In extra periods, a score by a team committing a foul during the down is canceled (3-1-3-g-2), and if both teams foul during the down and Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated (3-1-3-g-3). However, penalties for flagrant personal fouls, unsportsmanlike conduct fouls, dead-ball personal fouls and live-ball fouls treated as dead-ball fouls are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-3-Exception), and B99's Targeting (9-1-4) is a flagrant foul (2-10-3). Beginning with the third extra period, a team’s possession series will be one play for a two-point try from the three-yard line, unless relocated by penalty (3-1-3-e). So here, depending on the option, the penalty will be enforced B-3 → B-1½ for a try by Team A, or A-3 → A-18 for a try by Team B.

question_text: 3/G @ B-4. The score is tied with 0:57 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A33 fumbles at the B-6, and A80 recovers for a TD (0:50). Video evidence shows the ball glance off B33 with a foot on the sideline at the B-1 (0:52) prior to the recovery by A80. What is the ruling?
explanation: When the loose ball touches out of bounds B33 (4-2-1-a), then the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). This creates a "forward fumble out of bounds" which is returned to the spot of the fumble, the B-6 (7-2-4-b-1), and for which the clock is stopped and will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2). Properly officiated, the clock would have stopped when the loose ball touched B33. So, Replay is creating a stopped clock for which the clock is reset, but there is no runoff (12-3-6-b).

question_text: 3/9 @ A-40. The score is tied with 0:55 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A19 completes a pass to A87 at the B-40, who then fumbles at the B-35. B1 recovers while grounded at the B-30. Video evidence shows B99 targeted QB A19 right after the pass was thrown. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since there was a change of possession during the down, "tack-on" enforcement for a personal or unsportsmanlike foul during a legal forward pass play does not apply (10-2-2-e), and so the basic spot for enforcement is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). Enforcement of the penalty for a foul by the team not in possession is from the basic spot, A-40 → B-45 (10-2-2-c-3). Since the clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. A18 is in his own end zone and throws a forward pass which is blocked high into the air by B99. A39, on the end of his line of scrimmage at the snap, jumps and bats the ball at the A-4 across his end line. What if tackle A75 jumped and batted the ball at the A-4 across his end line? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ A-3 - Snap, since a bat by an ineligible player is treated the same as a completed forward pass.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with the ball declared dead the instant it crosses the end line.
choice_d: A - 4/14 @ A-2 - Snap, with a loss of down assessed for the ineligible player's bat.
explanation: While A75 is ineligible by position at the snap (7-3-3-c-1), all players become eligible when a Team B player or an official touches a legal forward pass. So, A75's bat is legal (9-4-1-a), and there is also no foul for illegal touching (7-3-11) since B99 touched the pass prior to A75. Again, this is simply an incomplete forward pass.

question_text: 2/G @ B-9. The score is tied with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A9 runs outside the pocket and fumbles. The ball bounces right back into his hands, and from the B-15 he throws the ball beyond the line of scrimmage and into the stands. 0:10 remain when the pass lands out of bounds. What if A9 muffed the snap and the ball hit the ground, but he immediately re-secured the ball at the B-11 and threw it directly to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/G @ B-11 - Snap, with the loss-of-down penalty enforced but no runoff option available.
choice_c: A - 3/G @ B-11 - Ready, since re-securing the ball erases the earlier muff for runoff purposes.
choice_d: 10-Second Runoff. A - 3/G @ B-11 - Snap, with Team B required to accept both the penalty and the runoff.
explanation: Half Over. It is a foul for the passer to conserve time by throwing the ball directly to the ground after the ball has already touched the ground (7-3-2-f-1). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, which would bring up A - 3/G @ B-11 (7-3-2-f-Penalty), but since this is an incomplete illegal forward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout, it also qualifies for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3), which Team B will surely accept.

question_text: 2/12 @ B-18. The score is tied with 1:50 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A31 sweeps around the end with A57 leading the way. B24 blocks A57 at the knees at the B-18. A31 is tackled inbounds at the B-16. What if A31 had been tackled at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-9 - Snap, with no clock option available once the ball is tackled short of the original spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot rather than the end of the run.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-4 - Option, with the basic spot unaffected by the shorter run.
explanation: B24's foul for blocking below the waist (9-1-6-b) occurred during a running play which ended behind the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Enforcement of the penalty for a foul by the team not in possession is from the basic spot, B-18 → B-9 (10-2-2-c-3). When the game clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. The score is tied with 1:21 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. B99 forcibly contacts A77’s facemask at the line of scrimmage, pushing his helmet off. A9 muffs the snap, and A77 chases the bouncing ball, recovering it while grounded at the A-40. What if A77 had not chased after the loose ball, allowing B68 to recover it while grounded at the A-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with no option for Team A since both players committed live-ball fouls.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Option, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the basic spot.
choice_c: A - 2/10 @ A-45 - Option, treating B68's recovery the same as A77's original recovery for enforcement purposes.
explanation: Only B99 has fouled (9-1-2-a) since A77 did not continue to participate after losing his helmet (9-1-17). The related run ended behind the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a), and the penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-45 → B-40 (10-2-2-c-3). Because the game clock is stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b). Since A77's helmet coming off was the direct result of a foul by an opponent, he may remain in the game (3-3-10-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. The score is tied with 0:09 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A19 throws a deep pass. B31 contacts A88 at the B-7 before the ball arrives, but A88 makes the catch falling at the B-2. The clock is at 0:00, but video evidence shows A88 down at 0:01. What is the best option available to Team A? What is the best option available to Team A if video evidence confirms that time expired? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-40 - Untimed, with the ball spotted at the end of the run rather than the enforcement spot.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, since Team A may choose to decline the penalty and still get an untimed down.
choice_d: Game Over, since a period may not be extended by penalty once the clock has reached 0:00.
explanation: A period shall be extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter. The penalty is 15 yards from the previous spot, A-45 → B-40 (7-3-8-c-Penalty). In this situation, even though Team A would prefer to decline the penalty, they may only have an untimed down through acceptance of the penalty.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is tied in the second possession of the second extra period. Team A have five players in the backfield at the snap. A19 throws an interception to B11 who returns it for a touchdown. Video evidence shows B99 launching and forcibly contacting the head of A19 from the blind side at the B-35 during the return. What if -- in addition to the fouls above -- B78 was guilty of Roughing the Passer? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - No Clock, with the enforcement spot set by Team A's Illegal Formation foul instead of B78's foul.
choice_c: The third extra period begins with A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock or B - 1/10 @ A-3 - No Clock, with B99 not disqualified since the fouls offset.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock, with both B78's and B99's fouls enforced together for a combined penalty.
explanation: The second possession of the second extra period continues. Since B78 fouled before the change of possession, Team A's Illegal Formation foul and Team B's Roughing the Passer foul offset (10-1-4). Since Team A may only elect one of the two live-ball fouls by Team B for penalty enforcement (10-1-3), their acceptance of B78's foul for the offset mandate that the penalty for B99's foul will be declined. B99 would, however, still be disqualified (10-1-4).

question_text: 3/G @ B-4. The score is tied with 0:57 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A33 fumbles at the B-6, and A80 recovers for a TD (0:50). Video evidence shows the ball glance off B33 with a foot on the sideline at the B-1 (0:52) prior to the recovery by A80. What if video evidence showed that A33's knee was down at the B-6 (0:54) prior to the fumble? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/G @ B-6 - Snap (0:44), with the clock starting on the snap since Replay is creating the stoppage.
choice_b: A - 4/G @ B-6 - Ready (0:54), with no runoff applied since the ball was already inside the ten-yard line.
choice_d: A - 4/G @ B-1 - Ready (0:44), with the spot corrected to the location the ball was ruled down before review.
explanation: Properly officiated, the ball would have been declared dead when A33's knee was down at the B-6, and the clock would have continued to run. Thus, Replay is creating a running clock for which there will be a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). The clock is reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:54), and the runoff is applied, 0:54 → 0:44. Either team could have avoided the runoff by using a timeout, if available. Unlike runoffs for injuries (3-3-6-f), helmets (3-3-10-b-2), or fouls that cause the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a), this runoff cannot simply be "declined". A timeout is required.

question_text: 3/9 @ A-40. The score is tied with 0:55 remaining in the 2nd quarter and neither team with a timeout remaining. A19 completes a pass to A87 at the B-40, who then fumbles at the B-35. B1 recovers while grounded at the B-30. Video evidence shows B99 targeted QB A19 right after the pass was thrown. What if B99's foul had been for targeting A87 with the crown of the helmet, causing him to fumble at the B-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with no clock option since Targeting is a flagrant personal foul.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Option, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the basic spot.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Option, with the basic spot set at the recovery spot rather than the end of the related run.
explanation: B99's foul occurred during a running play, and the end of A87's related run was the spot of his fumble, the B-35 (2-25-8-b). The basic spot is the end of the related run when it ends beyond the neutral zone (10-2-2-d-1-b), and enforcement of the penalty for a foul by the team not in possession is from the basic spot, B-35 → B-20 (10-2-2-c-3). Since the clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 4/7 @ A-6. Team A’s punt is low and hits B54 in the helmet at the A-10. The ball rebounds into Team A’s end zone where punter A97 recovers and carries the ball to the A-12. What is the ruling?
explanation: When a player of the kicking team catches or recovers a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone, the ball becomes dead (6-3-6-a). So, A97 is in possession of a dead ball in his own end zone, and the impetus imparted by a player who kicks the ball is responsible for the ball’s progress in any direction even if its course is deflected after touching a player of either team (8-7-2-a). Since the ball became dead behind Team A's goal line, and Team A is responsible for the ball being there, it is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A’s punt bounces towards the end zone at the B-8. A45 bats the ball at the B-2 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-5. The ball deflects into the end zone where it is recovered by A11. During the kick, B17 is flagged for holding at the B-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: A45's bat is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but an inbounds player touched by a ball that is batted by an opponent is not deemed to have touched the ball (6-3-4-b). So, B88 is not considered to have touched the ball, and when the kick untouched by Team B beyond the neutral zone touches the ground in Team B's end zone, it is a touchback (6-3-9). B17's foul is governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b) with PSK spot the B-20 (2-25-11-b). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A's high punt is first touched in flight by A23 at the B-4, and he then propels the ball backwards from the B-1. B88 picks up the loose ball at the B-3 and advances to the B-20 where A45 tackles him by the facemask. Video shows that A23 gained possession of the ball at the B-2. What is the ruling?
explanation: A23 commits Illegal Touching at the B-4 when he is the first to touch the scrimmage kick beyond the neutral zone (6-3-2-a), and then when he completes the catch of the scrimmage kick at the B-2, the ball becomes dead (6-3-6-a). So, A45's foul is now a dead-ball foul, but penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls are always enforced, regardless of the outcome of a replay review (12-3-6-j). The Illegal Touching privilege is canceled if there is an accepted penalty for a live-ball foul (6-3-2-b), but here Team B may both accept the Illegal Touching spot and have the penalty for the dead-ball foul enforced from the succeeding spot, B-4 → B-19 (10-2-2-a).

question_text: 4/1 @ B-36. QB A1 under center receives the snap and pitches the ball from the B-38 to A22 who muffs the pitch at the B-40. The loose ball hits A22’s foot and rolls forward to the B-34 where it is recovered by A79. What is the ruling?
explanation: This was a pitch (backward pass) and a muff -- not a fumble -- so there is no restriction on who may recover the loose ball (7-2-2-a). A79 recovers the ball beyond the line to gain, but the clock does not stop for the first down (3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-22. The score is A-14, B-17 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. QB A18’s pass is intercepted and returned to the B-30. Just after releasing the pass, B94 hits A18 with force below the knees at the B-28. After the play, A64 is flagged for a late hit at the B-30. What is the ruling?
explanation: When an offensive player is in a passing posture with one or both feet on the ground, no defensive player rushing unabated shall hit the player forcibly at the knee area or below, so B94 has committed a live-ball foul for Roughing the Passer (9-1-9-b), after which A64 committed a dead-ball foul for his late hit (9-1-7-a). When a live-ball foul by one team is followed by one or more dead-ball fouls, the penalties are administered separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). Enforcement of the penalty for B94's foul is from the previous spot, B-22 → B-11 (10-2-2-d-3, 10-2-2-c-3) with an automatic first down, after which the penalty for A64's foul is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-11 → B-26 (10-2-2-a). Because the clock was stopped to complete a penalty (3-3-2-e-4) and Team B will not next snap the ball (3-3-2-d-3), the clock would start by rule on the Ready. Since the game clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it may start on the Snap at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/3 @ A-30. QB A1 under center receives the snap, retreats backwards, and gives the ball to A2 at the A-27 who runs one yard to A-28 and tosses it back to A1 at the A-23 (flea flicker play). Unable to find any open receivers and under duress, QB A1 rolls to the right outside the tackle box at the A-23 and throws the ball into an area with no eligible offensive receivers. The ball lands beyond the neutral zone near a hash mark. A79 was at B-48 when the pass was released. What is the ruling?
