choice_a: A - 3/8 @ A-25 or 4/3 @ A-30 - Snap.  IDP Only.
choice_c: A - 4/10 @ A-23 - Snap.  Both IDP and ING.
choice_d: A - 4/3 @ A-30 - Snap.  Neither IDP nor ING.
explanation: It is not a foul if the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended, but this applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the pass (7-3-2-h-Exception). So, A1 is guilty of Intentional Grounding (ING) which is an illegal forward pass, but there is no foul for an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (IDP) because these restrictions are only in effect during legal forward passes (7-3-10). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass, the A-23 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. Team A’s punt hits the ground and appears to graze A88 in the leg @ B-43, but no Illegal Touching is ruled. B6 dives for the ball at the B-40 where the covering official declares the ball dead. Video shows that B6 did not control the ball while grounded, and that in the immediate continuing action A23 recovered the loose ball at the B-45. Video cannot establish whether or not the kick touched A88. What is the ruling?
explanation: Listed among reviewable plays is a loose ball ruled dead when the clear recovery of that loose ball occurs in the immediate continuing football action. If the replay official rules that the ball was not dead, it belongs to the recovering team at the spot of the recovery (12-3-3-d-2). Since B6 did not control the ball while grounded, the ball was not dead, and Replay will award possession to A23 at the B-45. Note that 12-3-3-d is a specific exception to Rule 4-1-2-b-3 which deals with inadvertent whistles.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-6. 2:07 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A4 drops back to pass in the end zone. Under duress and beginning to be tackled in the end zone, the QB reaches the ball out across the goal line. Officials rule that when A4 was down, the ball was the A-½ (1:59). Video shows that when A4 was down, only part of the ball was breaking the plane of the goal line (2:01). What is the ruling?
explanation: Unless the entire ball is out of A's end zone when the ball is declared dead, it is a Safety (AR-8-5-1-I). Replay will overturn the ruling on the field (12-3-1-a), but there will be no clock adjustment since the snap occurred before the Two- Minute Timeout (12-3-6-b).

question_text: 4/7 @ A-6. Team A’s punt is low and hits B54 in the helmet at the A-10. The ball rebounds into Team A’s end zone where punter A97 recovers and carries the ball to the A-12. What if A79 recovered the ball at the A-2 and carried it to the A-12? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/17 @ A-6 - Snap, since A79's recovery beyond the neutral zone does not itself earn a new series.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-10 - Snap, since B54's touching more than three yards beyond the neutral zone forfeits Team A's recovery rights.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-12 - Snap, with the new series awarded at the dead-ball spot instead of the recovery spot.
explanation: When a player of the kicking team catches or recovers a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone, the ball becomes dead (6-3-6-a), but since B54 touched the kick more than three yards beyond the neutral zone (6-3-1-b), A79 is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (6-3-3). A new series is always awarded to the team in legal possession when the ball is declared dead if a scrimmage kick crosses the neutral zone (5-1-1-e-2), so a first down is awarded to Team A.

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A’s punt bounces towards the end zone at the B-8. A45 bats the ball at the B-2 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-5. The ball deflects into the end zone where it is recovered by A11. During the kick, B17 is flagged for holding at the B-24. What if, rather than a bat, A45 and B88 were actively engaged in blocking when the ball hit B88 in the leg? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, since active blocking between opponents is treated the same as forced touching.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with B17's Holding foul enforced instead of declined by rule.
choice_d: Touchback. B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since B88's touch during blocking still creates a dead ball short of the goal line.
explanation: If A45 had blocked B88 into the ball, B88 would not be deemed to have touched the kick and the result would be the same as in the original question (6-3-4-a). But when opponents are actively engaged in blocking, this is not considered forced touching (AR-6-3-4-IV) and B88 is considered to have touched the ball. The ball remains alive when it enters B's end zone (6-3-9) and A11 legally recovers the ball (6-3-3) for a Touchdown. The penalty for B17's Holding foul is declined by rule (10-2-5-a-2).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A's high punt is first touched in flight by A23 at the B-4, and he then propels the ball backwards from the B-1. B88 picks up the loose ball at the B-3 and advances to the B-20 where A45 tackles him by the facemask. Video shows that A23 gained possession of the ball at the B-2. What if -- in addition to all of the above -- Team A had been in an Illegal Formation at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/9 @ B-9 - Snap, with the Illegal Formation penalty tacked directly onto the Illegal Touching spot.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-7 - Snap, with only the Illegal Formation foul enforced and the face mask foul declined by rule.
choice_c: A - 4/24 @ A-40 - Snap or B -1/10 @ B-22 - Snap.
choice_d: A - 4/19 @ A-45 - Snap, with the two live-ball fouls offsetting instead of being administered separately.
explanation: As in the original question, there is Illegal Touching by A23 at the B-4, a dead ball that belongs to Team B at the B-2, and what will now be a dead-ball face mask foul on A45 … but we now add to these a live-ball Illegal Formation foul on Team A. When a live-ball foul by one team is followed by a dead- ball by an opponent or by the same team, the penalties are administered separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). The penalty for Team A's Illegal Formation foul may be enforced from the previous spot, B-40 → B-45, or from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-2 → B-7, at the option of Team B (6-3-13). From there, the penalty for Team A's now-dead-ball Face Mask would be enforced, B-45 → A-40 or B-7 → B-22 (10-2-2-a). The penalty for the Illegal Formation cannot be "tacked on" to the illegal touching spot, B-4 → B-9, because acceptance of the penalty for a live-ball foul cancels the illegal touching privilege (6-3-2-b).

question_text: 4/1 @ B-36. QB A1 under center receives the snap and pitches the ball from the B-38 to A22 who muffs the pitch at the B-40. The loose ball hits A22’s foot and rolls forward to the B-34 where it is recovered by A79. What if A22 controlled the pitch at the B-40, then lost control of the ball at the B-38? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-38 - Snap, since a Team A player other than the fumbler recovering keeps the ball with Team A.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot of the pitch instead of the spot of the fumble.
choice_c: A - 4/2 @ B-38 - Snap, with the down replayed since the ball did not reach the line to gain.
explanation: Since A22 controlled the pitch at the B-40 and then lost control at the B-38, he has fumbled. On fourth down before a change of team possession, when a Team A fumble is caught or recovered by a Team A player other than the fumbler, the ball is dead... and if the catch or recovery is beyond the spot of the fumble, the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). So, the ball is returned to the B-38 which is short of the line to gain, and the ball goes over on downs (5-1-1-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-22. The score is A-14, B-17 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. QB A18’s pass is intercepted and returned to the B-30. Just after releasing the pass, B94 hits A18 with force below the knees at the B-28. After the play, A64 is flagged for a late hit at the B-30. What if -- rather than a late hit -- A64 tackled the runner by the face mask at the B-30? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-11 - Ready, with both live-ball fouls enforced together instead of offsetting.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-11 - Snap, with the down advanced despite the offsetting fouls.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-22 - Snap, with the offended team given the Snap-clock option despite the offsetting fouls.
explanation: As above, B94 is guilty of Roughing the Passer (9-1-9-a), and now A64 is guilty of a live-ball face mask foul (9-1-8-b). If live-ball fouls by both teams are reported to the referee, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4). Since there were fouls by both teams, there will be no 3-4-3-b option and the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 3/3 @ A-30. QB A1 under center receives the snap, retreats backwards, and gives the ball to A2 at the A-27 who runs one yard to A-28 and tosses it back to A1 at the A-23 (flea flicker play). Unable to find any open receivers and under duress, QB A1 rolls to the right outside the tackle box at the A-23 and throws the ball into an area with no eligible offensive receivers. The ball lands beyond the neutral zone near a hash mark. A79 was at B-48 when the pass was released. What if A1 never relinquished possession of the ball? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/10 @ A-23 - Snap, treating this pass the same as Intentional Grounding since possession was retained.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, since a downfield ineligible receiver always results in a turnover on downs.
choice_d: A - 3/8 @ A-25 - Snap, with no option for Team A to instead decline the penalty and keep the original down.
explanation: Since A1 controlled the snap and did not relinquish possession, it is not a foul when he goes outside the tackle box and throws the ball beyond the neutral zone (7-3-2-h-Exception)... and there would be no foul for A79 being downfield if the ball had landed "at or near the sideline" (7-3-10-Exception) … but since the ball landed near a hash mark, this is still an Ineligible Receiver Downfield. The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, or the penalty may be declined (7-3-10-Penalty, 10-1-1-b).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. Team A’s punt hits the ground and appears to graze A88 in the leg @ B-43, but no Illegal Touching is ruled. B6 dives for the ball at the B-40 where the covering official declares the ball dead. Video shows that B6 did not control the ball while grounded, and that in the immediate continuing action A23 recovered the loose ball at the B-45. Video cannot establish whether or not the kick touched A88. What if Replay could also determine that the kick touched A88? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/10 @ A-35 - Snap, since the recovery must still be awarded to Team A regardless of the touching.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the ball spotted at A23's original recovery point rather than the touching spot.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, treating A23's recovery as valid despite the confirmed touching by A88.
explanation: Touching of a kick is always reviewable (12-3-4-a), and when Replay determines that the kick touched A88, they may create Illegal Touching (6-3-2-a) and Team B will exercise their option to accept the ball at that spot.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-6. 2:07 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A4 drops back to pass in the end zone. Under duress and beginning to be tackled in the end zone, the QB reaches the ball out across the goal line. Officials rule that when A4 was down, the ball was the A-½ (1:59). Video shows that when A4 was down, only part of the ball was breaking the plane of the goal line (2:01). What if 1:07 remained in the 4th quarter … onfield officials ruled Safety (0:59) … but video showed that when A4 was down, the ball was at the A-½ (1:01)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/10½ @ A-½ - Ready (1:01), with no runoff applied since the original ruling was already a stoppage.
choice_b: Safety. 2 Points for B, with the on-field ruling standing since Replay only reviews scoring plays involving Team A.
choice_c: A - 4/10½ @ A-½ - Snap (0:51), with Team A required to accept the runoff rather than choosing between clock options.
explanation: Replay will overturn the ruling of a Safety to runner down in the field of play (12-3-1-a), and properly officiated the clock would not have stopped. Replay is thus creating a running clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, and this qualifies for a 10-second runoff. The clock will be reset to the time it was dead by rule (1:01), after which 10 seconds will be subtracted (1:01 → 0:51) and the clock will start on the Ready (12-3-6-c). Either team may use an available timeout to avoid the runoff, in which case the clock will start on the Snap.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. The score is A-21, B-20 with 0:54 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout. QB A7 runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds. The clock is stopped (0:49) because B33 was offside at the snap. After penalty enforcement but before the Ready, B99 goes to the ground with a cramp. What is the ruling?
explanation: There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock was not stopped for B99's injury (3-3-6-f). It was already stopped to complete the penalty, for which the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4). Since the clock was stopped to complete a penalty for a foul by the team behind in the score after the Two-Minute Timeout, the offended team does not have an option for Snap (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. The score is A-21, B-20 with 0:54 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout. QB A7 runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds. After the play, A7 is flagged for showing up B33 by shoving the ball in his stomach, and the clock is stopped (0:49). What is the ruling?
choice_b: Both B and C are options.
choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: The clock is stopped immediately when A7 commits his Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul in order to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4) … and if after the Two-Minute Timeout a team commits a foul that causes the clock to stop immediately and the penalty for that foul is accepted, there is the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a). Team B may accept the runoff, 0:49 → 0:39, in which case the clock will start on the Ready … or may decline the runoff, in which the clock will start on the Snap (3-4-4-a-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. QB A7 is in a passing posture at the A-25 when unabated B33 hits him forcibly in the knee area. Simultaneous with the contact, A7 completes a pass to A88 behind the neutral zone at the A-27. A88 carries to the A-35 where he fumbles, with A86 recovering the loose ball while grounded at the A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: When an offensive player is in a passing posture with one or both feet on the ground, no defensive player rushing unabated shall hit the player forcibly at the knee area or below (9-1-9-b). Note that the contact need not be "late" for this to be a foul (AR-9-1-9-II). As a matter of fact, there need not be a pass at all (AR-9-1-9-III). Penalty enforcement for Team B personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls during a legal forward pass play is at the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e). It matters not that the pass was caught behind the neutral zone … only that the last run ended beyond the neutral zone. The last run ended at the spot of A86's recovery, the A-40 (2-25-8-a), and enforcement is from that spot, A-40 → B-45.

question_text: 4/14 @ A-6. After the snap, punter A11 rocks back and his right heel touches the end line, but both feet are inbounds when he receives the snap. A11's kick rolls out of bounds at the B-45. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Foul.  Both B and C are options.
choice_d: Foul.  Both A and C are options.
explanation: No Team A player who goes out of bounds during a scrimmage kick play may return inbounds during the down (6-3- 12), and the five-yard penalty may be enforced from the previous spot, A-6 → A-3 (6-3-12-Penalty), or from the spot when the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-45 → 50 (6-3-13).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. In a scrimmage kick formation, punter A18 is lined up on the edge of the tackle box with A66 and A88 in front of him. A18 receives the snap and takes a step outside the tackle box. From inside the tackle box, B99 leaves his feet, goes directly above the frame of A88, and blocks the punt, which rolls out of bounds at the A-20. What is the ruling?
explanation: It is a foul if a defensive player who is inside the tackle box tries to block a punt or an apparent punt by leaving their feet and leaping into the plane directly above the frame of the body of an opponent (9-1-11-c). The punter need not be in the tackle box as the rule is written in terms of the "leaper's" position at the snap. The penalty is 15 yards from the previous spot and an automatic first down (9-1-11-Penalty). The clock starts on the snap because this is a legal kick down (3-3-2-d-8).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. A5's pass is intercepted by B37 at the B-20 and returned to the A-10. During the return, the L has to run around, but does not contact, Team B personnel who are "in the white" at the A-30. Team B received a Sideline Warning earlier in the game. What is the ruling?
explanation: Physical interference with an official is a foul charged to the team for unsportsmanlike conduct (9-2-5-b), and it is considered interference if the official runs into or has to run around team personnel "in the white" (AR-9-2-5-I and II). The penalty is 15 yards, administered as a dead ball foul (9-2-5-b-Penalty), so from the succeeding spot, A-10 → A-25 (10-2-2-a). Sideline warnings are reserved for team personnel who are beyond the coaching line while the ball is alive (or in the continuing action after the ball has been declared dead), but do not physically interfere with officials (9-2-5-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. A33 takes the handoff and runs to his right. Lead blocker A88 is at the B-41 when DB B22 contacts him from the front at the knees trying to disrupt the play. A33 carries for a touchdown. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Foul.  Penalty on Try or KO.
explanation: Team B players aligned in a stationary position within 1-yard of the line of scrimmage within the tackle box may legally block below the waist in the tackle box on their initial line charge (9-1-6-b-1), but otherwise Team B players are not allowed to block below the waist except against a runner (9-1-6-b-2). B22's block did not occur in the tackle box as the tackle box does not extend beyond the neutral zone (2-34-1-a), so this is a foul. Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are enforced on the Try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/G @ B-1. The score is Team A-24, B-21 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A33 takes the handoff and scores, and the clock runs to 0:00. Video shows that the A33 crossed the goal line at 0:02. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No time is restored, and the game is over.
choice_b: Reset the clock to 0:02.  Team A must attempt the Try and kick off to Team B.
choice_c: No time is restored, but Team A must attempt the Try.
explanation: With the touchdown, Team A now lead 30-21, and in the fourth quarter -- in order the restore time -- the score differential must be eight points or less … and after a touchdown, all potential results of the try down must be considered. (12-3-6-d-3). One of the potential results is that -- on Team A's Try attempt -- B ends up with the ball and scores two points. This would make the score A-30, B-23 and within the differential. Thus, the clock is reset to 0:02, and Team A must attempt the Try and then kick off to Team B. Obviously, all Team A needs to do is successfully take a knee, but this possibility must be considered when applying this rule.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. The score is A-21, B-20 with 0:54 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout. QB A7 runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds. The clock is stopped (0:49) because B33 was offside at the snap. After penalty enforcement but before the Ready, B99 goes to the ground with a cramp. What if the clock is restarted after penalty enforcement, after which B99 goes to the ground with a cramp (0:44)? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ B-40 - Ready (0:49), with no runoff option since the injury occurred after penalty enforcement.
choice_c: A - 3/5 @ B-40 - Snap (0:39), with the offended team given the option for the clock to start on the snap.
choice_d: Game Over, since the play clock reset for the injury exceeds the time remaining regardless of Team A's runoff choice.
explanation: Because the clock was restarted after penalty enforcement, B99's injury now causes the clock to stop, and Team A has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). Team A will surely accept the runoff, 0:49 → 0:39, and have the clock start on the Ready (3-3-6-f-2), and because the play clock is set to 40 seconds for the defensive injury (3-3-6-f-1), Team A will not have the snap the ball again and the game will be over.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. The score is A-21, B-20 with 0:54 remaining in the 4th quarter and neither team with a timeout. QB A7 runs to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds. After the play, A7 is flagged for showing up B33 by shoving the ball in his stomach, and the clock is stopped (0:49). What if -- after A7 shoved the ball in B33's stomach -- B33 retaliated by throwing the ball at A7's head? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/20 @ A-45 - Ready (0:39), with both fouls administered together instead of having their yardage cancel.
choice_c: A - 4/5 @ B-40 - Ready (0:39), with no option for Team B to instead decline the runoff.
choice_d: 10-Second Runoff not available. A - 4/20 @ A-45 - Snap (0:49), since these fouls are treated as offsetting live-ball fouls.
explanation: The clock is stopped the instant A7 commits his foul, and thus creates the 10-second runoff option for Team B as above. B33 subsequently commits another Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul. When dead-ball unsportsmanlike or dead-ball personal fouls by both teams are reported to the referee and none of the penalties have been completed, the yardage penalties cancel and the number or type of down established before the fouls occurred is unaffected (10-1-5-Exception). While it is true that there is no option for a 10-second runoff when there are offsetting fouls, it is important to note that these are not offsetting fouls, which are always live-ball (10-1-4). They are fouls whose penalties cancel.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. QB A7 is in a passing posture at the A-25 when unabated B33 hits him forcibly in the knee area. Simultaneous with the contact, A7 completes a pass to A88 behind the neutral zone at the A-27. A88 carries to the A-35 where he fumbles, with A86 recovering the loose ball while grounded at the A-40. What if B36 recovered the loose ball at the A-40? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Ready, with enforcement from the previous spot since B36's recovery changed team possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, since B36 recovering the loose ball awards Team B possession at the recovery spot.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, treating B36's recovery the same as the original play's enforcement.
explanation: The enforcement is exactly the same as in the original question. This is a running play for which the related run ended at the spot of the fumble, and since the related run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of this related run, the A-35, and enforcement is from this spot. Note that if this had been a forward pass, enforcement would have been from the previous spot because there was a change of possession during the down (10-2-2-e, 10-2-2-d-3).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-6. After the snap, punter A11 rocks back and his right heel touches the end line, but both feet are inbounds when he receives the snap. A11's kick rolls out of bounds at the B-45. What if -- with his right heel on the end line -- A11 leaps to control the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, since A11 controlling the snap with his heel on the end line is not out of bounds.
choice_b: A - 4/17 @ A-3 - Snap, with the five-yard penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of a safety.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with no safety recognized since A11 never actually possessed the ball while airborne.
explanation: A player is out of bounds when any part of their body touches anything other than another player or a game official on or outside a boundary line (2-27-15-a-1), so A11 is out of bounds … and since an out-of- bounds player who becomes airborne remains out of bounds until they touch the ground inbounds without simultaneously being out of bounds (2-27-15-a-2), A11 is still out of bounds when he touched the loose ball. So, the ball becomes dead (4-2-3-a), and it is a safety when the ball becomes dead out of bounds behind a goal line and the defending team is responsible for the ball being there (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. In a scrimmage kick formation, punter A18 is lined up on the edge of the tackle box with A66 and A88 in front of him. A18 receives the snap and takes a step outside the tackle box. From inside the tackle box, B99 leaves his feet, goes directly above the frame of A88, and blocks the punt, which rolls out of bounds at the A-20. What if B99 leaves his feet and goes through the gap between A66 and A88? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with the fifteen-yard penalty enforced from the previous spot for an illegal leap.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-20 - Snap, with B99 also disqualified for leaping through the gap between players.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, treating a legal leap through the gap the same as no play at all.
explanation: While it is a foul if a defensive player who is inside the tackle box tries to block a punt or an apparent punt by leaving their feet and leaping into the plane directly above the frame of the body of an opponent (9-1-11-c), it is not a foul if a player leaps through or over the gap between players (9-1-11-c-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. A5's pass is intercepted by B37 at the B-20 and returned to the A-10. During the return, the L has to run around, but does not contact, Team B personnel who are "in the white" at the A-30. Team B received a Sideline Warning earlier in the game. What if B37 returned the interception across A's goal line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock, with no option for Team B to instead take a free kick.
choice_b: B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock or B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap, since interference with an official during a return always negates the touchdown.
choice_d: B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced on the interception return instead of the succeeding play.
explanation: Since the penalty is administered as a dead ball foul (9-2-5-b-Penalty), the touchdown counts and the penalty may be enforced on either the Try or the succeeding kickoff (10-2-5-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. A33 takes the handoff and runs to his right. Lead blocker A88 is at the B-41 when DB B22 contacts him from the front at the knees trying to disrupt the play. A33 carries for a touchdown. What if this occurred on the last timed down of the 4th quarter, and the touchdown made the score A-27, B-28? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with no option for Team A to instead carry the foul over to a potential extra period.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with the touchdown negated by B22's below-the-waist block.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock or carry over to potential extra period (A - 1/10 @ B-12½ - No Clock or B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock).
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the personal foul enforced on the succeeding kickoff instead of the Try.
explanation: Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are enforced on the Try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods (10-2-5-a-1). So, Team A may choose to take the penalty now in order to go or two and win the game in regulation, or play for overtime.

question_text: 4/G @ B-1. The score is Team A-24, B-21 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A33 takes the handoff and scores, and the clock runs to 0:00. Video shows that the A33 crossed the goal line at 0:02. What if the score is A-26, B-21 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, since Team A must still attempt the Try whenever a penalty affects the score differential.
choice_b: No time is restored, but Team A must attempt the Try, since the differential still allows a two-point return.
choice_c: Reset the clock to 0:05. Team A must attempt the Try and kick off to Team B, since any nonzero differential requires it.
explanation: With the touchdown, Team A now leads 32-21 (11-point differential) … and even if Team A's Try attempt resulted in 2 points for Team B, the score differential would still be 9 points. So, even with a Touchdown and a successful 2-point Try, Team B could still not win the game. Thus, no time is restored (12-3-6-d-3), and Team A is not required to attempt the Try since it would not affect the outcome of the game (8-3-2-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. A99 punts the ball and B88 gives a fair catch signal at the B-10. B88 muffs the kick at the B-10 and runs forward to catch the ball when A22 steps in-front of him and catches it at the B-13. A22 did not make contact with B88. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since B88 gave a valid fair signal, his unimpeded opportunity to catch the scrimmage kick continued when he muffed the kick and still had an opportunity to complete the catch (6-5-1-b). So, when A22 stepped in front of B88, he impeded his opportunity to complete the catch, and this is Kick-Catch Interference (6-4-1-a). The penalty is 15 yards from the spot of the foul, B-13 → B-28 (6-4-1-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. The score is tied with 0:55 remaining in the 2nd quarter. B99 runs onto the field, realizes he is the 12th player, and retreats. He is just outside of the numbers when A11 receives the snap and passes it to A89 who catches it and is downed inbounds at the A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: Team B may not have more than 11 players on the field when the ball is snapped, so this is a live-ball foul for Illegal Substitution (3-5-3-b). If accepted, the penalty is five yards from the previous spot (3-5-3-b-Penalty), so Team A will decline the penalty (10-1-1-b). Whenever the clock is stopped to complete a penalty (accept or decline, per 10-1-1-a) for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it will start on the Snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A11 throws a pass from the A-17 to A88 who is at A-16. B22 is attempting to intercept the pass, but before he can touch the ball, A88 bats it at the A-16 and the ball rolls out of bounds at the A-15. What is the ruling?
explanation: While a pass is in flight, any player who is eligible to touch the ball may bat it in any direction (9-4-1-a), with the exception that a backward pass in flight may not be batted forward by the passing team (9-4-2). A11's pass is backward (A-17 → A-16) … eligibility rules apply only to forward passes (7-3-3-a) … and A88's bat is backward (A-16 → A-15) … so there is no foul. When a backward pass goes out of bounds, the ball belongs to the passing team at the out-of-bounds spot. (7-2-4-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. The score is A-21, B-17 with 1:00 remaining in the 4th quarter and Team B out of timeouts. A11 carries to the B-35 where he is tackled inbounds. After the play is whistled dead, B8 grabs A11 by the facemask and pulls it off his head with 0:54 remaining on the clock. What is the ruling?
explanation: With the game clock running after the Two- Minute Timeout in the 2nd or 4th quarters, and before a change of team possession, if either team commits a foul that causes the clock to stop immediately and the penalty for that foul is accepted, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a). B8's foul will cause the clock to stop immediately, and Team A will undoubtedly accept the runoff. The penalty for this dead-ball foul is enforced 15 yards from the succeeding spot, B-35 → B-20 (10-2-2-a), and the runoff will be applied, 0:54 → 0:44. The game clock starts by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c), and the play clock is set to:25 for a helmet off the offensive team (3-2-4-c-14). A11 may remain in the game because his helmet came off as the direct result of a foul (3-3-10-a).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-30. The score is A-14, B-14 with 0:02 remaining in the 4th quarter. A7's field goal attempt is short and is caught by B16 in his end zone. He returns the ball the length of the field for the apparent game-winning touchdown. While the kick was in flight, B96 held A82 at the B-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: B96's foul meets all of the conditions for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), and since the kick ended in B's end zone, the PSK (basic) spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-b). [Note: 2-25-11-Special Case 1b, which would make the PSK spot the B-30, does not apply since the unsuccessful field goal attempt was touched by B.] Since the foul occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement of the penalty by the team considered in possession at the time (10-2-3-a) is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2). The period must be extended for an untimed down since the penalty was accepted for a live-ball foul during the last timed down of the 4th quarter (3-2-3-a-1). Note that if B16 had been tackled anywhere short of the goal line, Team A would simply decline the penalty and the game would move to extra periods.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. During A6's kick, A44 runs out of bounds to avoid B32’s block, but returns inbounds immediately to tackle B22 at the B-15. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Foul. A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
choice_b: Foul.  Both B and D are options.
explanation: A Team A player who goes out of bounds during a free kick play may not return inbounds during the down (6-1-11), so A44 has fouled. The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, or five yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-15 → B-20 (6-1-11-Penalty).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. The score is A-24, B-24 with 0:57 remaining in the 4th quarter and both teams out of timeouts. A11 is pressured and runs downfield. About to be hit, he pitches the ball to A69, who then runs out of bounds at the 50 (0:49). Video show that A11 pitched the ball from the A-45 and that A69 first touched the ball at the A-46. What is the ruling?
explanation: A11's pitch (pass) from the A-45 to the A-46 was forward and occurred beyond the neutral zone, so this is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-a). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-45 → A-40, and will not include a loss of down here since the ball is at the line to gain after enforcement (7-3-2-a-Penalty). [Note: The words "loss of down" essentially mean "loss of the right to replay the down". But here, Team A would not be replaying 3rd down anyway because they are beyond the line to gain even after enforcement, and have thus earned a 1st down.] There is no option for a 10-second runoff because this was not an incomplete illegal forward pass (3-4-4-a-3). And, even though the pass was touched by an ineligible receiver, it is not Illegal Touching because that can occur only during a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The clock starts by rule on the Snap because A69 ran out of bounds (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-44. A11 runs an option play to the right of the formation and keeps the ball. As he approaches the line to gain at the B-41, he attempts to pitch the ball back to A22, but B99 hits A11 causing him to fumble. The ball rolls to the B-38 where B58 recovers. He advances it to the B-45 where he fumbles. A22 recovers the loose ball at the B-47 and is tackled at the B-42. What is the ruling?
explanation: On fourth down before a change of team possession, when a Team A fumble is caught or recovered by a Team A player other than the fumbler, the ball is dead (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). But here, once team possession changes, the "4th Down Fumble Rule" no longer applies, so A22's recovery and advance are legal. A new series is awarded to the team in legal possession when the ball is declared dead if a change of team possession occurs during the down, so Team A is awarded a new series at the dead ball spot. There is no rules-based reason for the clock to ever stop, though it is likely an official's time out may be needed to discuss all that has happened … in which case the clock starts on the Ready.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. A99 punts the ball and B88 gives a fair catch signal at the B-10. B88 muffs the kick at the B-10 and runs forward to catch the ball when A22 steps in-front of him and catches it at the B-13. A22 did not make contact with B88. What if B88's muff strikes the ground, and as he runs forward to recover the ball, A22 steps in front of him and recovers it at the B-13? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, since A22 stepping in front of a muffed kick before it strikes the ground is still legal.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-28 - Snap, treating the muff striking the ground the same as a completed fair catch.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, with Kick-Catch Interference not recognized once the ball has already been muffed.
explanation: When B88, who gave a valid fair catch signal, muffed the kick, he was entitled to the unimpeded opportunity to complete the catch … but when the loose ball struck the ground, B88's protection ended as he could no longer catch the ball (6-5-1-b). Since B88 touched the kick, A22 could legally recover the loose ball (6-3-3), and the ball belongs to Team A at the spot of his recovery (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. The score is tied with 0:55 remaining in the 2nd quarter. B99 runs onto the field, realizes he is the 12th player, and retreats. He is just outside of the numbers when A11 receives the snap and passes it to A89 who catches it and is downed inbounds at the A-40. What if A11 kept the ball and carried to the A-28 where he was tackled inbounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Option, treating the completed pass the same as the original scenario regardless of the tackle spot.
choice_b: A - 1/5 @ A-25 - Option or A - 2/2 @ A-28 - Option.
choice_c: A - 1/5 @ A-25 - Snap, with no option for Team A to instead decline the penalty and keep the ball at the A-28.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-28 - Snap, since Team B's Illegal Substitution results in a turnover once the ball is snapped.
explanation: The penalty for Team B's Illegal Substitution may be accepted, five yards from the previous spot, A-20 → A-25 (3-5-3-b) or declined with the ball remaining at the A-28 (10-1-1-b). In either case, since the clock was to complete a penalty (accept or decline, per 10-1-1-a) for a foul by the team ahead in the score (or either team if the score is tied) after the Two-Minute Timeout and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, it will start on the Snap, at the option of the offended team (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A11 throws a pass from the A-17 to A88 who is at A-16. B22 is attempting to intercept the pass, but before he can touch the ball, A88 bats it at the A-16 and the ball rolls out of bounds at the A-15. What if A88 batted the ball at the A-16 and it rolled out of bounds at the A-17? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/17 @ A-13 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul rather than half-the-distance.
choice_b: No foul. A - 2/15 @ A-17 - Ready, treating A88's forward bat as legal since he did not control the ball.
choice_d: A - 1/22 @ A-8 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap since the enforcement follows a running play.
explanation: A backward pass in flight may not be batted forward by the passing team (9-4-2). A11's pass is backward (A-17 → A-16) and A88's bat is forward (A-16 → A-17), so this is a foul. The penalty is 10 yards with no loss of down (9-4-2-Penalty). Since the enforcement spot is not specified in the statement of the penalty, the enforcement spot is determined by the Three-and-One Principle (10-2-1-a). Because this is a backward pass, this is considered a running play (2-30-4-a), and the end of the related run is the spot where the backward pass was thrown, the A-17 (2-25-8-e). Since the related run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a) … and because the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is half- the-distance (10-2-6) from the spot of the foul, the A-16 → A-8 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. The score is A-21, B-17 with 1:00 remaining in the 4th quarter and Team B out of timeouts. A11 carries to the B-35 where he is tackled inbounds. After the play is whistled dead, B8 grabs A11 by the facemask and pulls it off his head with 0:54 remaining on the clock. What if B8 grabbed and twisted A11's facemask while making the tackle at the B-35, but the helmet remained on? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Ready (0:44), with a 10-second runoff applied since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Ready (0:54), with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the end of the run.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap (0:54), with the offended team given the option for the clock to start on the snap.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-30. The score is A-14, B-14 with 0:02 remaining in the 4th quarter. A7's field goal attempt is short and is caught by B16 in his end zone. He returns the ball the length of the field for the apparent game-winning touchdown. While the kick was in flight, B96 held A82 at the B-24. What if, in addition to the above, Team A was in an Illegal Formation at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Untimed, with Team B required to accept offsetting fouls once Team A commits an Illegal Formation.

choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Untimed, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the PSK spot.
explanation: Team B could, but will not, accept offsetting fouls (10-1-4) which would give Team A an untimed down (3-2-3-a-2) to try again to win the game in regulation. Since B96's foul has postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), Team B may refuse offsetting fouls and have the penalty for their foul enforced as above before playing an untimed down. Note that if B16 had been tackled anywhere short of the goal line, Team A could then decline the penalty for B96's foul and the game would move to extra periods.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. During A6's kick, A44 runs out of bounds to avoid B32’s block, but returns inbounds immediately to tackle B22 at the B-15. What if B22 fumbled at the B-15, and A44 returned inbounds immediately to recover the loose ball at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with the ball placed at the previous spot rather than five yards beyond it.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since B22's fumble transfers possession to Team B at the spot of A44's recovery.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, with A44 also disqualified for returning inbounds during the down.
explanation: A44 fouled as above by simply returning inbounds, but since B22 fumbled the ball, A44 was in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (7-2-2-a). Since the subsequent dead ball did not belong to Team B, the only enforcement option is from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30 (6-1-11-Penalty).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. The score is A-24, B-24 with 0:57 remaining in the 4th quarter and both teams out of timeouts. A11 is pressured and runs downfield. About to be hit, he pitches the ball to A69, who then runs out of bounds at the 50 (0:49). Video show that A11 pitched the ball from the A-45 and that A69 first touched the ball at the A-46. What if A69 muffed the pitch at the A-46 (0:51), it struck the ground at the A-48 (0:50), and it rolled out of bounds at the 50 (0:49)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready (0:49), with no runoff applied since the ball never left Team A's possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap (0:49), since A69's muff hitting the ground and going out of bounds is a change of possession.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap (0:40), with no option for Team B to instead have the clock start on the Ready.
explanation: When A69's muff of A11's pitch hit the ground, it became an incomplete illegal forward pass (7-3-2-a), and is one of the fouls specifically listed as triggering a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). The clock will be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:50), and Team B may either accept the runoff, 0:50 → 0:40, in which case the clock will start on the Ready … or decline the runoff, in which case the clock will remain at 0:50 and will start on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-44. A11 runs an option play to the right of the formation and keeps the ball. As he approaches the line to gain at the B-41, he attempts to pitch the ball back to A22, but B99 hits A11 causing him to fumble. The ball rolls to the B-38 where B58 recovers. He advances it to the B-45 where he fumbles. A22 recovers the loose ball at the B-47 and is tackled at the B-42. What if B58 muffed A11's fumble at the B-38 with A22 recovering the loose ball at the B-42 and carrying it to the B-40 where he is tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-42 - Snap, since A22's recovery of the loose ball keeps possession with Team A regardless of the fumble rule.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-38 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot of B58's muff instead of the dead-ball spot.
choice_c: A - 4/6 @ B-42 - Snap, treating A22's advance as continuing Team A's original possession beyond the fumble.
explanation: Since B58 muffed -- rather than recovered and subsequently fumbled -- A11's fumble, there was never a change of team possession during the down. So, when A22 recovers the loose ball (which was still considered A11's fumble), the "4th Down Fumble Rule" still applies (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). The ball is dead when recovered by A2 at B-42, and since this spot is behind the spot of the fumble, the B-41, the ball remains at the dead ball spot. Team A is short of the line to gain and possession never changed, so the ball goes over to Team B (5-1-1-c).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. A7's punt is first touched by A82 at the B-15. The ball is picked up by B36 at the B-10 and carried to the B-35 where he fumbles. question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A7's punt is muffed by B36 at the B-20. The loose ball continues into B's end zone where A19 recovers.
choice_a: TD. A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock.
explanation: When a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone touches a player of the receiving team, any player may catch or recover the ball (6-3-3). The ball remains alive when it touches the ground in B's end zone (6-3-9), and the result of the play is a touchdown.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A7's punt is first touched by A82 at the B-25, then muffed by B36 at the B-20. The loose ball continues into B's end zone where A19 recovers.
choice_a: TD. A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock.
explanation: When a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone touches a player of the receiving team, any player may catch or recover the ball (6-3-3). The ball remains alive when it touches the ground in B's end zone (6-3-9), and the result of the play is a touchdown. However, no inbounds player of the kicking team shall touch a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone before it touches an opponent. This is illegal touching, a violation that, when the ball becomes dead, gives the receiving team the privilege of taking the ball at the spot of the violation (6-3-2-a).
