question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. Just over two minutes remaining in the 4th quarter. B1 is in the neutral zone at the snap. A2 throws a pass to A88 that is ruled complete at the B-15 (1:59). The referee announces that the penalty is declined, and the Two- Minute Timeout begins. During the break, Replay identifies video that shows that the pass was incomplete (2:01). What is the ruling?
explanation: The play is reviewable until the ball is next legally put in play (12-5-2-a), so Replay will overturn this to an incomplete pass. There is no clock adjustment since the ball was snapped prior to the Two-Minute Timeout (12-3-6-b). Because the ruling on the field was overturned, Team A may change their penalty option and will now accept the penalty.

question_text: 4/8 @ B-13. Team A lines up to attempt a field goal. The kick is blocked by B99 at the B-16, and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-10. What is the ruling?
explanation: Because the unsuccessful field goal attempt was declared dead beyond the neutral zone and was untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone, Team B is entitled to a "special spot" (8-4-2-b-1). Since the ball was snapped from inside the B-20, the ball will be placed at the B-20, and Team B may choose the lateral position (8-4-2-b-1-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A6's high, short punt is almost at rest at the B-30 when A82 bats the loose ball and it goes out of bounds at the B-15. If Team B does not desire a re-kick, which of the following is their best option?
explanation: A82's bat of the grounded loose ball is forward and therefore a foul (9-4-1-c). If accepted, the penalty would be enforced from the previous spot, 50 → A-40 (10-2-2-d-4-a / 10-2-2-c-2) without a loss of down since the foul occurred when a legal scrimmage kick was beyond the neutral zone (9-4-1-c-Penalty). The penalty could also be enforced from where the subsequent dead ball belonged to Team B, B-15 → B-25 (6-3-13), but since A82's bat also constitutes Illegal Touching, Team B's best option is to take the ball at that spot, the B-30 (6-3-2-a). Enforcement from there, B-30 → B-40, is not an option because acceptance of a penalty cancels the Illegal Touching privilege (6-3-2-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is A-18, B-20 late in the 4th quarter, and Team A has no timeouts remaining. QB A2 muffs the snap, immediately picks the ball up, and throws a forward pass into the ground at the B-27 to stop the clock (0:12). What is the ruling?
explanation: A forward pass is illegal if the passer to conserve time throws the ball directly to the ground after the ball has already touched the ground (7-3-2-f-1). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-27 (7-3-2-f-Penalty). An incomplete illegal forward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout carries the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3) which Team B will likely accept. When a 10-second runoff is accepted, the clock starts by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and Team A has no timeouts remaining. QB A2 is in pistol formation when he muffs the snap. The ball bounces off the ground and A2 is able to immediately gather the ball then rolls to his right. Outside the tackle box at the B-40, A2 throws the ball out of bounds beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers (1:32). What is the ruling?
choice_d: Both B and C are options.
explanation: It is not a foul if the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended (7-3-2-h-Exception). This applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the forward pass. While A2 muffed the snap, a snap becomes a backward pass when the snapper releases the ball (2-19-2-d), so A2 controlled the resulting backward pass and there is no foul.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. With the clock running, Team B attempts to substitute players while Team A made no changes from the prior play. Team A hurries to the line in an effort to catch Team B with more than 11 players, but snaps the ball prior to WR A88 ever getting set. QB A12 muffs the snap, and falls on the loose ball at the A-30. At the time of the snap, Team B had 12 players clearly on the field. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Ready.  Dead-ball foul on A and B.
choice_b: A - 1/15 @ A-30 - Ready.  Dead-ball foul on A only.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Ready.  Live-ball foul on A and B.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ A-40 - Ready.  Dead-ball foul on B only.
explanation: It is a dead-ball foul on Team A for a False Start if the offensive team never comes to a one-second stop prior to the snap after the ball is ready for play (7-1-2-b-5). It is only a foul if Team B has more than 11 players on the field when the ball is snapped (3-5-3-b), so since the ball never actually became live, there is no foul on Team B.

question_text: 2/4 @ B-34. QB A15 throws a forward pass to eligible WR A8 who is at the B-29. A8 jumps in the air, controls the ball, and attempts to pass the ball backward to A10 all before contacting the ground. A10 muffs the backward pass and B90 recovers the loose ball on the ground at the B-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since A8 never completed the process of catching the forward pass by contacting the ground (2-4-3-a-2) and then maintaining control long enough to perform an act common to the game (2-4-3-a-3), the status of the ball remains a forward pass and it is incomplete when the loose ball touches the ground (7-3-7).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A7 is under pressure so he steps up in the pocket. At the A-37, he releases a pass that is complete to A88 at midfield, where he steps out of bounds. Officials announce and enforce the Illegal Forward Pass and mark the ball ready for play, A - 2/8 @ A-32 - Ready. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The enforcement is correct, so Replay should not intervene.
choice_b: The enforcement is incorrect, and this is reviewable.  If impossible to correct via O2O, Replay may stop the game.
choice_c: The enforcement is incorrect, but this is not reviewable.  Replay may not stop the game, but may attempt to correct via O2O.
explanation: Replay may review and correct the number of a down, including the result of a penalty enforcement that includes an automatic first down or loss of down (12-3-6-f-1). While this may be accomplished through a formal stoppage, Replay should in the interest of efficiency seek to correct the error via O2O (2025 Replay Case Book, Play 157).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. Just over two minutes remaining in the 4th quarter. B1 is in the neutral zone at the snap. A2 throws a pass to A88 that is ruled complete at the B-15 (1:59). The referee announces that the penalty is declined, and the Two- Minute Timeout begins. During the break, Replay identifies video that shows that the pass was incomplete (2:01). What if Replay could not identify video to overturn the ruling during the break … then Team A committed a False Start to bring up A - 1/15 @ B-20 … and then TV showed another view clearly indicating the pass was incomplete? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (1:59), with the completed-pass ruling standing since the False Start already brought up a new down.
choice_c: A - 1/15 @ B-20 - Snap (1:59), with the False Start penalty enforced and the incomplete-pass reversal declined.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-35 - Snap (2:01), with the clock reset to the time the incomplete pass was identified.
explanation: It is still not too late to review the previous down because the ball has not been legally put into play (12-5-1-a). Replay will overturn to an incomplete pass... Team A may then accept the penalty for Team B's Offside foul … and Team A's False Start foul is then disregarded because it was not a Personal Foul or Unsportsmanlike Conduct (12-3-6-i / 12-3-6-j).

question_text: 4/8 @ B-13. Team A lines up to attempt a field goal. The kick is blocked by B99 at the B-16, and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-10. What if the loose ball was touched by B24 prior to rolling out of bounds at the B-10 What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the special-spot rule still applying despite B24's touch.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, with the ball returned to the previous spot after Team B's touch.
choice_d: A - 4/8 @ B-13 - Snap, since Team B touching the blocked kick gives the ball back to Team A.
explanation: If Team B touches an unsuccessful field goal after it crosses the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-2). When a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/8 @ B-13. Team A lines up to attempt a field goal. The kick is blocked by B99 at the B-16, and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-10. What if the loose ball stuck the ground at the B-12, then rolled out of bounds untouched at the B-14? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the special spot applied since the kick was untouched by Team B.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-12 - Snap, with the ball spotted where it first struck the ground.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-13 - Snap, with the ball returned to the previous spot.
explanation: If an unsuccessful field goal crosses the neutral zone, is untouched by Team B after crossing the neutral zone, and is declared dead behind the neutral zone, all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (8-4-2-b-3). When a scrimmage kick goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball belongs to the receiving team at the dead-ball spot (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A6's high, short punt is almost at rest at the B-30 when A82 bats the loose ball and it goes out of bounds at the B-15. If Team B does not desire a re-kick, which of the following is their best option? What if the loose ball rolled out of bounds at the B-25, and Team B does not desire a re-kick? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with Team B taking the ball at the spot where the dead ball belonged to them.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with Team B taking the ball at the spot of Illegal Touching.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead.
explanation: A82's bat of the grounded loose ball is forward and therefore a foul (9-4-1-c). If accepted, the penalty would be enforced from the previous spot, 50 → A-40 (10-2-2-d-4-a / 10-2-2-c-2) without a loss of down since the foul occurred when a legal scrimmage kick was beyond the neutral zone (9-4-1-c-Penalty). Since A82's bat also constitutes Illegal Touching, Team B could take the ball at that spot, the B-30 (6-3-2-a), but that is not as good as the option to enforce the penalty from where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-25 → B-35 (6-3-13), Enforcement from the spot of Illegal Touching, B-30 → B-40, is not an option because acceptance of a penalty cancels the Illegal Touching privilege (6-3-2-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is A-18, B-20 late in the 4th quarter, and Team A has no timeouts remaining. QB A2 muffs the snap, immediately picks the ball up, and throws a forward pass into the ground at the B-27 to stop the clock (0:12). What if A2 controlled the snap, but paused for a couple of seconds before spiking the ball? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 2/12 @ B-27 - Ready (0:12), with no runoff applied since the passer paused before spiking.
choice_c: A - 2/10 @ B-25 - Snap (0:12), with the pause making the spike a legal clock-stopping play.
choice_d: A - 2/17 @ B-32 - Ready (0:02), with the penalty enforced 15 yards instead of loss of down at the spot.
explanation: A forward pass is illegal if the passer to conserve time throws the ball directly to the ground not immediately after controlling the ball (7-3-2-f-2). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-27 (7-3-2-f-Penalty). An incomplete illegal forward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout carries the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3) which Team B will likely accept. When a 10-second runoff is accepted, the clock starts by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and Team A has no timeouts remaining. QB A2 is in pistol formation when he muffs the snap. The ball bounces off the ground and A2 is able to immediately gather the ball then rolls to his right. Outside the tackle box at the B-40, A2 throws the ball out of bounds beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers (1:32). What if A2 picked up the loose ball as above, then handed off to A34 who carried it outside the tackle box and from the B-40 threw the ball out of bounds beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Snap (1:32), with no foul since A2 controlled the resulting backward pass.
choice_c: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Ready (1:22) only, with no option for Team B to decline the runoff.
choice_d: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Snap (1:32) only, with no 10-second runoff option available on Intentional Grounding.
explanation: It is not a foul if the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended (7-3-2-h-Exception). This applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the forward pass. While A2 controlled the resulting backward pass, he relinquished possession to A34, so this is a foul for Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-40 (7-3-2-h-Penalty). Intentional Grounding after the Two-Minute Timeout carries the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-2). The clock starts on the Ready If Team B accepts the runoff and the Snap if they decline (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and Team A has no timeouts remaining. QB A2 is in pistol formation when he muffs the snap. The ball bounces off the ground and A2 is able to immediately gather the ball then rolls to his right. Outside the tackle box at the B-40, A2 throws the ball out of bounds beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers (1:32). What if A34 picked up the loose ball, carried it outside the tackle box, and from the B-40 threw the ball out of bounds beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Ready (1:22), treating A34's throwaway as Intentional Grounding.
choice_b: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Snap (1:32), with a loss-of-down penalty for A34 relinquishing possession.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Ready (1:22), with a 10-second runoff applied to the incomplete pass.
explanation: It is not a foul if the passer is or has been outside the tackle box and throws the ball so that it crosses or lands beyond the neutral zone or neutral zone extended (7-3-2-h-Exception). This applies only to the player who controls the snap or the resulting backward pass and does not relinquish possession to another player before throwing the forward pass. While A2 muffed the snap, a snap becomes a backward pass when the snapper releases the ball (2-19-2-d), so A34 controlled the resulting backward pass and there is no foul.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. With the clock running, Team B attempts to substitute players while Team A made no changes from the prior play. Team A hurries to the line in an effort to catch Team B with more than 11 players, but snaps the ball prior to WR A88 ever getting set. QB A12 muffs the snap, and falls on the loose ball at the A-30. At the time of the snap, Team B had 12 players clearly on the field. What if all Team A players came to a one-second stop, and then both A88 and A24 were shifting to new positions when the ball was snapped? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/15 @ A-30 - Ready, with only Team A's Illegal Shift enforced and no offset.
choice_b: A - 1/5 @ A-40 - Ready, with only Team B's Illegal Substitution enforced.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, with the offended team given the option for the clock to start on the snap.
explanation: After all Team A players come to a one-second stop, two or more players may shift (2-21-1-a), but only if they are once again motionless for one second before the snap (2-21-1-b). When one or more do not, it is a live-ball foul on Team A for an Illegal Shift (7-1-2-a). When the ball became live, Team B was guilty of an Illegal Substitution because they had more than 11 players on the field (3-5-3-b). Since both teams committed live-ball fouls, the fouls offset and the down is repeated.

question_text: 2/4 @ B-34. QB A15 throws a forward pass to eligible WR A8 who is at the B-29. A8 jumps in the air, controls the ball, and attempts to pass the ball backward to A10 all before contacting the ground. A10 muffs the backward pass and B90 recovers the loose ball on the ground at the B-32. What if A8 controlled the ball in flight at the B-29 and propelled it forward to A10, who caught the ball at the B-24 and carried it across B's goal line? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/4 @ B-34 - Snap, with the forward pass ruled incomplete when A8 batted it forward.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with A8's forward bat ruled an Illegal Bat resulting in a change of possession.
choice_d: A - 3/4 @ B-34 - Snap, since only the original passer may legally advance an incomplete forward pass.
explanation: Since A8 never completed the process of catching the forward pass by contacting the ground (2-4-3-a-2) and then maintaining control long enough to perform an act common to the game (2-4-3-a-3), the status of the ball remains a forward pass. When A8 propels it forward, it is batting (2-11-3), but it is legal for an eligible receiver to bat a forward pass in any direction (9-4-1-a). This is a legal play resulting in a touchdown (AR 7-3-6 VIII).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A7 is under pressure so he steps up in the pocket. At the A-37, he releases a pass that is complete to A88 at midfield, where he steps out of bounds. Officials announce and enforce the Illegal Forward Pass and mark the ball ready for play, A - 2/8 @ A-32 - Ready. What if officials announce and enforce the penalty as loss of down at the spot of the foul, A - 3/3 @ A-37 - Ready? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/3 @ A-37 - Ready, with the announced loss-of-down enforcement standing since penalty enforcement is not reviewable.
choice_c: A - 3/8 @ B-37 - Snap, with the offended team given the snap-clock option.
choice_d: A - 2/8 @ A-32 - Ready, with the original announced enforcement left uncorrected.
explanation: While the location of the passer (12-3-2-c) and the number of the down (12-3-6-f) are both reviewable, the penalty enforcement itself is not (12-3-7). Replay should use O2O to see that the five yards are marked off (2025 Replay Case Book, p. 31, 3rd bullet from the bottom).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4’s kick lands at the B-3 and bounces high into the air. B10 gives a fair catch signal after the ball hits the ground, and possesses the ball inbounds at the B-1. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap.  The ball is dead when possessed by B10.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap.  The ball is dead when possessed by B10.
choice_c: The ball remains alive when possessed by B10.
explanation: Because B10 signaled after the ball struck the ground, his signal is invalid (2-8-3-c) … and because this is a recovery (after the ball hit the ground) rather than a catch (ball in flight), he does not get the B-25 (6-5-3-a-Exception). The ball is declared dead at the spot of recovery, the B-1 (2-8-1-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. The score is tied with 1:40 left in the 4th quarter. QB A5 completes a pass to receiver A80 at the B-28 where he is tackled. Immediately after the play, B68 goes down with an injury. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock :25.
choice_b: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock :25.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock :40.
choice_d: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock :40.
explanation: The clock was stopped for two reasons: (1) to award a first down to Team A, and (2) because of the injury to B68. Since B68's injury is not the only reason for stopping the clock, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The play clock is set to 40 seconds whenever there is an injury to a Team B player, unless it is followed by a charged timeout or the Two-Minute Timeout (3-2-4-c-5). B68 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-35. A84 is lined up at the TE position on the left side of the formation with receivers A88 and A3 split wide to his left. Slot receiver A3 is on the line covering A84. A84 goes downfield five yards and blocks linebacker B50. Receiver A3 runs a crossing route behind this action and catches A7's pass at the A-38. A3 runs to the 50 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A84 is ineligible because he is neither on the end of the line of scrimmage (7-3-3-c-1) nor a back (7-3-3-c-2), and when he goes more than three yards downfield before a pass which crossed the neutral zone is in flight, he is an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10). He also commits Offensive Pass Interference by blocking downfield (7-3-8-b). Team B will decline the five-yard penalty for IDP and accept the 15-yard penalty for OPI, A-35 → A-20. Neither penalty includes a loss of down (7-3-10-Penalty / 7-3-8-b-Penalty).

question_text: 2/G @ B-4. The clock is running late in the 2nd quarter, and Team A has one timeout remaining. A56 false starts with 0:38 remaining. After enforcement of the penalty, which of the following is not possible?
explanation: Because A56's False Start causes the clock to stop immediately, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-1). If Team B accepts the runoff, the clock will start on the Ready from 0:28 (3-4-4-c, Option C) unless Team A uses their last timeout to avoid the runoff (3-4-4-d, Option B). Team B could also decline the runoff, in which case the clock would start on the Snap (3-4-4-c, Option B). Option A is not possible under any of the conditions of 3-4-4.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A’s kick is blocked and B55 recovers at the B-2. He attempts to advance but fumbles, and the ball rolls into B's end zone where B60 recovers while grounded. What is the ruling?
explanation: B55's fumble provides the impetus which puts the ball into B's end zone (8-7-2-a) where it becomes dead by rule when B60 recovers it while grounded. This is a Safety (8-5-1-a), and since this is a Try, Team A receives one point (8-1-1).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A3's kick is muffed by B24 at the B-10. The ball rolls into B's end zone where B24 chases it down and bats it across the end line. What is the ruling?
choice_c: Both A and D are options.
choice_d: Both A and C are options.
explanation: Although B24's bat is backward, it is illegal to bat a loose ball in any direction if it is in the end zone (9-4-1-c). Since this foul occurred during a free kick, the penalty would be from the previous spot, A-35 → A-45, with a re-kick (10-2-2-d-4-a). B24's muff of the kick does not change its impetus (8-7-2-a), so Team A is responsible for the ball going into B's end zone where it subsequently became dead. This results in a Touchback (8-6-1-b), so Team A might chose to simply decline the penalty and let Team B have the ball at the B-25 (8-6-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. 1:20 left in the 4th quarter. QB A18 hands off to A22 who runs to the right side and is tackled by B95 at the B-37. A22 is injured on the play, and B95’s helmet comes off during normal play. It will be A - 3/7 @ B-37 and:
choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock 0:40.
choice_b: No option for a 10-second runoff.  Play clock 0:25.
choice_c: Team A option for a 10-second runoff. Play clock 0:40.
choice_d: Team B option for a 10-second runoff. Play clock 0:40.
explanation: The clock was stopped for two reasons: (1) A22's injury, and (2) B95's helmet. Since neither was the only reason, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f / 3-3-10-b-2). The play clock is set to 40 seconds whenever there is a helmet off a Team B player, unless it is followed by a charged timeout or the Two-Minute Timeout (3-2-4-c-14). A22 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1). B95 must also leave the game for at least one down, but he could remain in the game if Team B uses a charged time out (3-3-10-a). In such case, the play clock would be set to 25 seconds.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A10 throws a low pass that appears to be intercepted by B45 at the A-33, but the pass is ruled incomplete. While lining up for the next play, A76 false starts. After the penalty is enforced, video shows that B45 established firm control of the ball before it touched the ground. What is the ruling?
explanation: It is not too late to review the previous down because the ball has not been legally put into play (12-5-1-a). Replay will overturn to an interception, and A76's False Start foul is disregarded because it was neither a Personal Foul nor Unsportsmanlike Conduct (12-3-6-i / 12-3-6-j).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4’s kick lands at the B-3 and bounces high into the air. B10 gives a fair catch signal after the ball hits the ground, and possesses the ball inbounds at the B-1. What if B10 possessed the ball in B's end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball dead at the spot of recovery in the end zone.
choice_b: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, treating the end-zone recovery as a safety kick.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick since B10's signal was invalid.
explanation: The ball is dead at the spot of recovery (2-8-1-c), and since this is a kick which Team A put into B's end zone where it subsequently became dead, this is a touchback (8-6-1-b). Because this is a free kick, the ball is placed at the B-25 (8-6-2).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4’s kick lands at the B-3 and bounces high into the air. B10 gives a fair catch signal after the ball hits the ground, and possesses the ball inbounds at the B-1. What if B10 was touching the sideline at the B-1 when he first touched the ball? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, treating the out-of-bounds kick as a touchback.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules only, with no option for Team B to take the 30-yard spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball placed where B10 first touched it out of bounds.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap.
explanation: B10 is out of bounds (4-2-1-a), and when he touches the loose ball, it is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). This, then, is a free kick that goes out of bounds untouched by an inbounds player of Team B, and is thus a free kick out of bounds (6-2-1). Team B may elect a re-kick after a five-yard penalty from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, or may take the ball 30 yards from the previous spot, A-35 → B-35 (6-2-1-Penalty).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. The score is tied with 1:40 left in the 4th quarter. QB A5 completes a pass to receiver A80 at the B-28 where he is tackled. Immediately after the play, B68 goes down with an injury. What if B68 went down with an injury after the ball had been spotted but before the clock had been restarted, and Team B had no timeouts remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-23 - Ready, with no snap-clock option since the clock was not running.
choice_c: A - 1/5 @ B-23 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff, with a runoff applied to the Delay of Game foul.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-33 - Option, with the Delay of Game penalty enforced after the line to gain is established.
explanation: Because B68 presented as injured after the ball had been spotted and Team B had no time outs remaining, Team B is penalized for Delay of Game. Since the ball was not yet ready for play, the five-yard penalty, B-28 → B-23, is enforced before the line to gain is established (5-2-7-a). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock was not running (3-3-6-f), but since the score is tied and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, Team A has the option for the clock to start on the Snap (3-4-3-b). The play clock is set to 40 seconds (3-2-4-c-5), and B68 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. The score is tied with 1:40 left in the 4th quarter. QB A5 completes a pass to receiver A80 at the B-28 where he is tackled. Immediately after the play, B68 goes down with an injury. What if B68 went down with an injury after the ball had been spotted and after the clock had been restarted, and Team B had no timeouts remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-23 - Option, with no runoff since the clock was not running when the injury occurred.
choice_b: A - 1/5 @ B-23 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff.
choice_c: A - 1/5 @ B-23 - Ready, with no snap-clock option available on the runoff.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-33 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff, with the penalty enforced before the line to gain is established.
explanation: Because B68 presented as injured after the ball had been spotted and Team B had no time outs remaining, Team B is penalized for Delay of Game. Since the ball had already been made ready for play, the five-yard penalty, B-28 → B-23, is enforced after the line to gain is established (5-2-7-b). Because the foul caused the clock to stop immediately, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a). The clock will start on the Ready if Team A accepts the runoff, and on the Snap if they decline the runoff (3-4-4-c). The play clock is set to 40 seconds (3-2-4-c-5), and B68 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-35. A84 is lined up at the TE position on the left side of the formation with receivers A88 and A3 split wide to his left. Slot receiver A3 is on the line covering A84. A84 goes downfield five yards and blocks linebacker B50. Receiver A3 runs a crossing route behind this action and catches A7's pass at the A-38. A3 runs to the 50 where he is tackled. What if A3 caught the pass at the A-34? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/20 @ A-20 - Ready, with A84 still guilty of Offensive Pass Interference on the block.
choice_b: A - 3/10 @ A-30 - Ready, with A84 penalized as an Ineligible Receiver Downfield.
choice_d: A - 4/10 @ A-30 - Ready, with the completed pass negated by A84's downfield block.
explanation: A84 is guilty of neither being an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10) nor committing Offensive Pass Interference (7-3-8-b) because both of these fouls require that a legal forward pass cross the neutral zone. This is a legal play.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-35. A84 is lined up at the TE position on the left side of the formation with receivers A88 and A3 split wide to his left. Slot receiver A3 is on the line covering A84. A84 goes downfield five yards and blocks linebacker B50. Receiver A3 runs a crossing route behind this action and catches A7's pass at the A-38. A3 runs to the 50 where he is tackled. What if A84 caught the pass at the A-45 with A3 in the area, and ran to the 50 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/20 @ A-20 - Ready only, with no loss-of-down option for the Illegal Touching.
choice_b: A - 4/10 @ A-30 - Ready only, with the OPI penalty declined in favor of the Illegal Touching loss of down.
choice_c: A - 3/10 @ A-30 - Ready, with only the Ineligible Receiver Downfield penalty enforced.
choice_d: A - 3/20 @ A-20 - Ready or A - 4/10 @ A-30 - Ready.
explanation: In addition to being an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10) and committing Offensive Pass Interference (7-3-8-b), A84 is guilty of Illegal Touching (7-3-11). As an originally ineligible receiver (7-3-3-c-1 / 7-3-3-c-2), the penalty for ILT is five-yards from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). This is likely a better option than the 15-yard penalty for OPI which includes no loss of down.

question_text: 2/G @ B-4. The clock is running late in the 2nd quarter, and Team A has one timeout remaining. A56 false starts with 0:38 remaining. After enforcement of the penalty, which of the following is not possible? What if B78 charged into the neutral zone, causing A56 to react, and Team B had no timeouts remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 2/G @ B-2 - Ready (0:28) only, with no option for Team A to decline the runoff.
choice_c: A - 2/G @ B-2 - Snap (0:38) only, with no 10-second runoff option on Defensive Offside.
choice_d: A - 2/G @ B-4 - Ready (0:28), with the offside penalty enforced the full five yards from the previous spot.
explanation: Because B78's Defensive Offside causes the clock to stop immediately, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-1). Team A may accept the runoff with the clock starting on the Ready, or decline the runoff with the clock starting on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A’s kick is blocked and B55 recovers at the B-2. He attempts to advance but fumbles, and the ball rolls into B's end zone where B60 recovers while grounded. What if B55 muffed the kick at the B-2, with the ball rolling into B's end zone where B60 recovers while grounded? What is the ruling?
choice_a: 1 point for Team A, treating the end-zone recovery as a Try safety.
choice_c: 2 points for Team A, since B55's muff sends the ball into the end zone for a touchdown.
choice_d: No Score, with the ball next put in play by a snap rather than a kickoff.
explanation: B55's muff of the kick does not change its impetus (8-7-2-a), so Team A is responsible for the ball going into B's end zone. The ball remains alive when it enters the end zone because B55 touched it (7-3-7-a), but when recovered by B60 while grounded, the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). By rule, the ball is next put in play by a kickoff (8-3-6).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A’s kick is blocked and B55 recovers at the B-2. He attempts to advance but fumbles, and the ball rolls into B's end zone where B60 recovers while grounded. What if B55 muffed the kick at the B-2, with the ball rolling into B's end zone where A79 recovers while grounded? What is the ruling?
choice_a: 1 point for Team A, treating A79's end-zone recovery as a safety on the Try.
choice_b: No Score, since the ball became dead when B55 muffed the kick.
choice_d: 2 Points For Team A, with the score counting only if A79 recovered while airborne.
explanation: The ball remains alive when it enters the end zone because B55 touched it (7-3-7-a), and when recovered by A79, this is a Touchdown (8-2-1-d). When this occurs on a Try, Team A receives 2 points (8-1-1).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A3's kick is muffed by B24 at the B-10. The ball rolls into B's end zone where B24 chases it down and bats it across the end line. What if, after muffing the kick at the B-10, B24 batted the ball at the B-6 and it went across the end line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick since B24's bat was in the end zone.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, treating the end-zone bat as a touchback.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, since Team A remains responsible for the ball's impetus.
explanation: B24's bat in the field of play is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but this bat of a grounded loose ball changes its impetus (8-7-2-b-1). So, Team B is responsible for the ball going into B's end zone where it subsequently became dead, and the result is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. 1:20 left in the 4th quarter. QB A18 hands off to A22 who runs to the right side and is tackled by B95 at the B-37. A22 is injured on the play, and B95’s helmet comes off during normal play. It will be A - 3/7 @ B-37 and: What if A22 was injured on the play, but B95's helmet did not come off? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff.
choice_b: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Ready, with no runoff option since the injury stopped the clock for a Team A player.
choice_c: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff, with the play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Snap, with Team A given the snap-clock option instead of a runoff.
explanation: Because A22's injury is the only reason for stopping the clock, Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The clock will start on the Ready if Team B accepts the runoff, and on the Snap if they decline the runoff (3-3-6-f-2). The play clock is set to 25 seconds (3-3-6-f-1), and A22 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. 1:20 left in the 4th quarter. QB A18 hands off to A22 who runs to the right side and is tackled by B95 at the B-37. A22 is injured on the play, and B95’s helmet comes off during normal play. It will be A - 3/7 @ B-37 and: What if A22 was injured on the play and B95's helmet did not come off, but A78 was flagged for holding at the B-40? Assume the score is tied. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/20 @ 50 - Option For A 10-Second Runoff, with a runoff applied since A78's foul stopped the clock.
choice_c: A - 3/7 @ B-37 - Ready, with A78's holding penalty declined and no snap-clock option.
choice_d: A - 2/20 @ 50 - Ready, with no snap-clock option since the score is tied.
explanation: The clock was stopped for two reasons: (1) A22's injury, and (2) to complete the penalty for A78's foul. So, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f), but since the score is tied and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, Team B has the option for the clock to start on the Snap (3-4-3-b). The play clock is set to 25 seconds (3-2-4-c-5), and A22 must leave the game for at least one down (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A10 throws a low pass that appears to be intercepted by B45 at the A-33, but the pass is ruled incomplete. While lining up for the next play, A76 false starts. After the penalty is enforced, video shows that B45 established firm control of the ball before it touched the ground. What if, after A76 committed a False Start, B98 came across the line and was flagged for Unnecessary Roughness? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-33 - Snap, with B98's personal foul declined since the down is being replayed.
choice_b: A - 2/15 @ A-15 - Snap, with A76's False Start enforced instead of disregarded.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-18 - Snap, with the personal foul enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: After Replay overturns to an interception, A76's False Start foul is disregarded (12-3-6-i), but the penalty for B98's personal foul is enforced from the succeeding spot as a dead ball foul, A-33 → A-48 (12-3-6-j).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. A10 throws a low pass that appears to be intercepted by B45 at the A-33, but the pass is ruled incomplete. While lining up for the next play, A76 false starts. After the penalty is enforced, video shows that B45 established firm control of the ball before it touched the ground. What if B98 charged into the neutral zone first, causing A76 to react in way that rose to Unnecessary Roughness? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-33 - Snap, with A76's personal foul offsetting B98's Defensive Offside.
choice_b: A - 2/15 @ A-15 - Snap, with B98's offside enforced against Team B instead of disregarded.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-48 - Snap, with the personal foul enforced from the succeeding spot toward Team A's goal.
explanation: After Replay overturns to an interception, B98's Defensive Offside is disregarded (12-3-6-i), but the penalty for A67's personal foul is enforced from the succeeding spot as a dead ball foul, A-33 → A-18 (12-3-6-j).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. A1 drops back for a screen pass. Running back A5 gets free, catches the pass at the B-39, and carries to the B-26 where he is tackled. B99 held A5 at the B-42 trying to prevent him from releasing out of the backfield. If there is a foul on the play, assume the penalty is accepted. What is the ruling?
explanation: B99's foul occurred during a legal forward pass play (2-30-1), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession (10-2-2-c-3) is enforced 10 yards from the basic spot, B-40 → B-30, and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A9 punts the ball to the B-25 where it is fielded and returned to the 50. During the kick, B55 hits A33 from the blindside with forcible contact to the head / neck area at the A-40. B55 had a counter on him from a UNS foul earlier in the game. Replay upholds the targeting foul. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap.  B55 is disqualified.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-12½ - Snap.  B55 is disqualified.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap.  B55 is disqualified.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-12½ - Snap.  B55 is ejected.
explanation: The foul meets all the conditions for PSK enforcement (10-2-3-b), and the PSK (basic) spot is the end of the kick, the B-25 (2-25-11-a). The foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic spot, so enforcement is half-the-distance (10-2-6) from the basic spot, B-25 → B-12½ (10-2-2-c-2). B55 is disqualified for this flagrant personal foul (9-1-4-Penalty), but his earlier Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul does not factor in to upgrade this to an ejection. He may remain in the team area (2-27-12-a-1).

question_text: 3/G @ B-3. A34 takes the pitch and runs toward the right goal line pylon. A34 dives with the ball in his outside hand and the ball crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. The ball is beyond the goal line extended when A34’s foot touches B55 who is standing in the end zone. A34 comes to the ground out of bounds with 2:02 remaining in the 2nd quarter. What is the ruling?
explanation: The goal line plane extends beyond the pylon only for a player who touches the pylon or the ground in the end zone (8-2-1-a). A34 touched a player in the end zone -- not the ground -- and so he does not get goal line extended. Since the ball did not pass inside or over the pylon, it is not a touchdown (AR 8-2-1 V and AR 8-2-1 IX). The ball is placed where it crossed the sideline, the B-1 (4-2-4-d-Exception). Because the ball carrier was ruled out of bounds before the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-3).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Team A lines up for an onside kick. The kick is driven immediately into the ground and bounces high in the air. B88 is in position to catch the kick at the A-45 when A17 jumps into the air directly in front of B88 at the A-44 and bats the ball to the 50 where B3 recovers. B88 did not give a fair catch signal. Which of the following is the best option available to Team B?
explanation: A17 has committed two fouls: Kick Catch Interference for being within one yard directly in front of B88 (6-4-1-b) and Illegal Batting for batting this loose ball forward (9-4-1-c). KCI is still in play even though the ball touched the ground because it was immediately driven to the ground (6-4-1-f), and B88 need not give a signal to enjoy this protection (6-4-1-a). The Illegal Batting penalty could be enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belonged to Team B, 50 → A-40 (6-1-8), but this is not as advantageous as taking the KCI penalty, A-45 → A-30 (6-4-1-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. A9 punts the ball to B81 who gives a fair catch signal and then muffs the kick at the B-10. While the ball is loose, B81 blocks A35 at the B-6 to prevent him from recovering the kick. B55 recovers the ball in the end zone and advances it to the B-18 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: There is no foul on B81 for an Illegal Block because he touched the kick (6-5-4), but because he gave a fair catch signal, the ball is dead when recovered by B55 in the end zone (2-8-1-c). B81's muff does not affect the impetus of A9's kick, which put the ball into B's end zone (8-7-2-a). The result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. The score is A-21, B-28 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 takes the snap, carries the ball outside of the tackle box, and from a passing posture throws a pass into the end zone where it is caught by A88. Unabated defensive end B99 hit A1 forcibly in the knee area as he dove to try to make a tackle as A1 was releasing the pass. Time expires during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Foul.  If accepted, the penalty may be enforced on the Try or from the succeeding spot in a possible extra period.
choice_b: Foul.  If accepted, the penalty must be enforced on the Try.
explanation: B99's hit was not late, but it was low, and this is a foul regardless on the timing (9-1-9-b). Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown may be enforced on the Try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-25. The score is A-28, B-21 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter. Punter A9 takes the snap and under pressure carries the ball outside of the tackle box, gets his feet set, and kicks the ball down to the B-40 where B81 catches and returns the kick for a touchdown. Unabated defensive end B99 hit A9 in the plant leg just after the kick, knocking him to the ground. Time expires during the down. What is the ruling?
explanation: There is no foul for B99's contact as A9's protection under the Roughing / Running Into the Kick rule ends when he carries or possesses the ball outside the tackle box before kicking (9-1-16-a-4-b). The result of the play is a touchdown.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-29. QB A12 throws a pass that is intercepted by B17 at the B-2, and his momentum carries him into the end zone. While in the end zone, B17 fumbles the ball forward out of the end zone, and A77 recovers at the B-6 with a foot touching out of bounds. While B17 still had the ball in the end zone, B81 blocked A47 in the back at the B-8. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A77 first touches the loose ball with a foot on the sideline, the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). This creates a forward fumble out of bounds which is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1), "as if" the ball never left the end zone. So, the Momentum Exception is still in effect (8-5-1-a-Exception), and the basic spot is the momentum spot, the B-2 (10-2-2-d-2-b-Exception). The foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, so enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. A1 drops back for a screen pass. Running back A5 gets free, catches the pass at the B-39, and carries to the B-26 where he is tackled. B99 held A5 at the B-42 trying to prevent him from releasing out of the backfield. If there is a foul on the play, assume the penalty is accepted. What if, after B99 was held, A1 tucked the ball and ran to the B-26 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot since it was a passing play.
choice_c: A - 3/5 @ B-30 - Ready, with the holding penalty declined and the down replayed.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-16 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the tackle.
explanation: B99's foul occurred during a running play (2-30-4-a), and since the run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession (10-2-2-c-3) is enforced 10 yards from the basic spot, B-26 → B-16.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. A1 drops back for a screen pass. Running back A5 gets free, catches the pass at the B-39, and carries to the B-26 where he is tackled. B99 held A5 at the B-42 trying to prevent him from releasing out of the backfield. If there is a foul on the play, assume the penalty is accepted. What if, after B99 was held, A1 was sacked at the B-45? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-16 - Ready, with the basic spot set at the end of the run instead of the previous spot.
choice_c: A - 3/25 @ B-45 - Ready, with the holding penalty declined after the sack.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap.
explanation: B99's foul occurred during a running play (2-30-4-a), and since the run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession (10-2-2-c-3) is enforced 10 yards from the basic spot, B-40 → B-30, and includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A9 punts the ball to the B-25 where it is fielded and returned to the 50. During the kick, B55 hits A33 from the blindside with forcible contact to the head / neck area at the A-40. B55 had a counter on him from a UNS foul earlier in the game. Replay upholds the targeting foul. What if B55 did not have an earlier UNS, but was flagged for one after his disqualification was announced? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-12½ - Snap, with no additional enforcement since the UNS was B55's first.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-49½ - Snap, with the dead-ball UNS enforced the full 15 yards from the succeeding spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-6¼ - Snap, with B55 disqualified but not ejected for a first Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul.
explanation: Eject B55. The penalty for B55's dead-ball foul is enforced half-the-distance (10-2-6) from the succeeding spot, 12½ → 6¼ (10-2-2-a). Even though this in only his first Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul, any player who has been disqualified and is subsequently penalized for Unsportsmanlike Conduct is ejected (AR 2-27-12 III). B55 must leave the playing enclosure (2-27-12-b-1).

question_text: 3/G @ B-3. A34 takes the pitch and runs toward the right goal line pylon. A34 dives with the ball in his outside hand and the ball crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. The ball is beyond the goal line extended when A34’s foot touches B55 who is standing in the end zone. A34 comes to the ground out of bounds with 2:02 remaining in the 2nd quarter. What if A34's foot touched the goal line after the ball broke the plane of the goal line extended? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/G @ B-1 - Ready, since touching the goal line does not grant goal line extended.
choice_b: A - 4/G @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball spotted where it crossed the sideline.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with a 10-second runoff since the runner went out of bounds.
explanation: The goal line is in the end zone (2-12-2), so A34 has touched the ground in the end zone. This means that he gets goal line extended, and so therefore this is a touchdown (8-2-1-a).

question_text: 3/G @ B-3. A34 takes the pitch and runs toward the right goal line pylon. A34 dives with the ball in his outside hand and the ball crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. The ball is beyond the goal line extended when A34’s foot touches B55 who is standing in the end zone. A34 comes to the ground out of bounds with 2:02 remaining in the 2nd quarter. What if A34's foot touched the sideline in the end zone after the ball broke the plane of the goal line extended? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, since touching the sideline in the end zone grants goal line extended.
choice_c: A - 4/G @ B-1 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the ball went out of bounds.
choice_d: A - 4/G @ B-½ - Ready, with the ball spotted at the goal line rather than where it crossed the sideline.
explanation: The sideline is out of bounds and is not part of the end zone (2-12-1), so A34 has not touched the ground in the end zone and he does not get goal line extended. Since the ball did not pass inside or over the pylon, it is not a touchdown (AR 8-2-1 V and AR 8-2-1 IX). The ball is placed where it crossed the sideline, the B-1 (4-2-4-d-Exception). Because the ball carrier was ruled out of bounds before the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-3).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Team A lines up for an onside kick. The kick is driven immediately into the ground and bounces high in the air. B88 is in position to catch the kick at the A-45 when A17 jumps into the air directly in front of B88 at the A-44 and bats the ball to the 50 where B3 recovers. B88 did not give a fair catch signal. Which of the following is the best option available to Team B? What if B3 recovered the ball at the 50 and carried it to the A-35 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, with the KCI penalty enforced instead of the Illegal Batting penalty.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, with the ball placed where B3 recovered the batted kick.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, with the ball spotted where B3 was tackled.
explanation: As before, A17 committed two fouls: KCI (6-4-1-b) and BAT (9-4-1-c). The KCI penalty could be enforced from the spot of the interference, A-45 → A-30 (6-4-1-Penalty), but this is not as advantageous as enforcing the BAT penalty from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, A-35 → A-25 (6-1-8).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. A9 punts the ball to B81 who gives a fair catch signal and then muffs the kick at the B-10. While the ball is loose, B81 blocks A35 at the B-6 to prevent him from recovering the kick. B55 recovers the ball in the end zone and advances it to the B-18 where he is tackled. What if, after muffing the kick, B81 batted the grounded ball at the B-6 into the end zone where B55 recovered? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, treating the end-zone recovery as a touchback.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick since B81's bat was in the field of play.
choice_d: No Score. B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, with the ball spotted where B81 batted it.
explanation: B81's bat is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but the bat changes the impetus (8-7-2-b-1). Team B is therefore responsible for the ball going into B's end zone, and when B55 recovers after a fair catch signal has been given, the ball is dead (2-8-1-c). The result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. The score is A-21, B-28 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 takes the snap, carries the ball outside of the tackle box, and from a passing posture throws a pass into the end zone where it is caught by A88. Unabated defensive end B99 hit A1 forcibly in the knee area as he dove to try to make a tackle as A1 was releasing the pass. Time expires during the down. What if the pass had been caught by A88 at the B-2, and time expired during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-1 - No Clock, with the period ending since time expired during the down.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-2 - Untimed, with the personal foul enforced the full 15 yards from the end of the run.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, treating the catch in the end zone as a touchdown.
explanation: The penalty for B99's personal foul during a legal forward pass play (2-30-1) is enforced half- the-distance (10-2-6) from the end of the last run, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-e). Because time expired during the down and the penalty for a live-ball foul was accepted, the period is extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. The score is A-21, B-28 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 takes the snap, carries the ball outside of the tackle box, and from a passing posture throws a pass into the end zone where it is caught by A88. Unabated defensive end B99 hit A1 forcibly in the knee area as he dove to try to make a tackle as A1 was releasing the pass. Time expires during the down. What if B99's contact sent A1 to the ground at the B-45, and time expired during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Untimed, with enforcement from the end of the run rather than the previous spot.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock, with the period ending since A1 never released the ball.
choice_d: A - 2/25 @ B-45 - Untimed, with no foul since A1 was not technically a passer.
explanation: While A1 is not technically a passer because he did not release the ball, the action by B99 is a foul under 9-1-9-b because of the vulnerability of A1 as a potential passer in a passing posture (AR 9-1-9 III). B99's foul occurred during a running play (2-30-4-a), and since the run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession (10-2-2-c-3) is enforced 15 yards from the basic spot, B-40 → B-25, (10-2-2-c-3). Because time expired during the down and the penalty for a live-ball foul was accepted, the period is extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-25. The score is A-28, B-21 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter. Punter A9 takes the snap and under pressure carries the ball outside of the tackle box, gets his feet set, and kicks the ball down to the B-40 where B81 catches and returns the kick for a touchdown. Unabated defensive end B99 hit A9 in the plant leg just after the kick, knocking him to the ground. Time expires during the down. What if, with A9 standing at the A-15, the ball had been snapped over his head … and after recovering the ball inside the tackle box at the A-5 and getting the kick away, B99 hit him as above? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/5 @ A-30 - Untimed, with Roughing the Kicker enforced since A9 was still protected.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Untimed, with an automatic first down for Running Into the Kicker.
choice_d: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with a 10-second runoff since time expired during the down.
explanation: There is no foul for B99's contact as A9's protection under the Roughing / Running Into the Kick rule ends when he carries or possesses the ball more than five yards behind his initial position at the snap (9-1-16-a-4-c). The result of the play is a touchdown.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-29. QB A12 throws a pass that is intercepted by B17 at the B-2, and his momentum carries him into the end zone. While in the end zone, B17 fumbles the ball forward out of the end zone, and A77 recovers at the B-6 with a foot touching out of bounds. While B17 still had the ball in the end zone, B81 blocked A47 in the back at the B-8. What if A77 recovered the loose ball inbounds at the B-6 and carried it into B's end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with A81's Illegal Block in the Back enforced on the Try.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-6 - Ready, with the touchdown negated by A81's block.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-13 - No Clock, with the block-in-the-back penalty enforced on the succeeding spot.
explanation: The fumble remains in play (7-2-2-a), and A77 legally advances for a touchdown (8-2-1-a). Five- and ten-yard penalties for fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are not enforced on the Try or the succeeding kickoff. Such penalties are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down (10-2-5-a-2), so the penalty for A81's Illegal Block in the Back is declined by rule. Had A81's foul been a personal foul or unsportsmanlike conduct foul, that penalty could have been enforced on the Try or succeeding kickoff (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-29. QB A12 throws a pass that is intercepted by B17 at the B-2, and his momentum carries him into the end zone. While in the end zone, B17 fumbles the ball forward out of the end zone, and A77 recovers at the B-6 with a foot touching out of bounds. While B17 still had the ball in the end zone, B81 blocked A47 in the back at the B-8. What if B88 recovered the loose ball while grounded at the B-6? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready, with the Momentum Exception returning the ball to the B-1.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball spotted where B88 recovered it.
choice_c: No Score. B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, treating the recovery as ending the down without a safety.
explanation: Although B17's momentum took him into the end zone, the ball did not become dead there (and was not returned there as a forward fumble out of bounds). So, the Momentum Exception no longer applies (8-5-1-a-Exception c), and the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-b). The foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, so enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot (10-2-2-c). The result is a Safety (8-5-1-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. A41 takes the handoff but is tackled at the A-16. During A41's run: A86 illegally blocks B97 below the waist at the A-22. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-22) is beyond basic spot (A-20), so enforcement is from basic spot (A-20) (10-2-2-c-2). A-20 → A-10.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. A41 takes the handoff but is tackled at the A-16. During A41's run: B97 grasps A86's face mask at the A-22. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (A-20) (10-2-2-c-3). A-20 → A-35.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. A41 takes the handoff but is tackled at the A-16. During A41's run: A86 holds B97 at the A-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul by A behind NZ, and Holding is among fouls listed in 10-2-2-b, so enforcement is from previous spot (A-20). A-20 → A-10.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. A41 takes the handoff but is tackled at the A-16. During A41's run: B97 strikes A86's helmet at the A-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (A-20) (10-2-2-c-3). A-20 → A-35.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. A21 carries to the B-35 where he fumbles with A43 recovering while grounded at the B-30. During A21's run, A86 illegally blocks B97 in the back at the B-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (B-40) is behind basic spot (B-35), so enforcement is from spot of foul (10-2-2-c-1). B-40 → 50.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. A21 carries to the B-35 where he fumbles with A43 recovering while grounded at the B-30. While the ball is loose, A86 clips B97 at the B-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (B-32) is beyond basic spot (B-35), so enforcement is from basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). B-35 → 50.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. A21 carries to the B-35 where he fumbles with A43 recovering while grounded at the B-30. B97 grasps A21's face mask at the B-37, causing him to fumble at the B-35. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (B-35) (10-2-2-c-3). B-35 → B-20.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. From the B-24, A14 pitches to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-26, with A41 recovering while grounded at the B-28. Prior to A14's pitch, A68 holds B79 at the B-22. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul by A behind NZ, and Holding is among fouls listed in 10-2-2-b, so enforcement is from previous spot (B-20). B-20 → B-30.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. From the B-24, A14 pitches to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-26, with A41 recovering while grounded at the B-28. Prior to A14's pitch, A68 illegally blocks B79 in the back at the B-18. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (B-18) is beyond basic spot (B-20), so enforcement is from basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). B-20 → B-30.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. From the B-24, A14 pitches to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-26, with A41 recovering while grounded at the B-28. While the ball is loose, B97 illegally blocks A41 below the waist at the B-26, causing him to muff the pitch. What is the ruling?
explanation: Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (B-20) (10-2-2-c-3). B-20 → B-10.

question_text: 2/15 @ A-40. A36 carries to the A-45 where he fumbles. A42 recovers at the 50 and runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. During A36's run, A86 illegally blocks B97 in the back at the A-47. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul was during 1st run, which ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-47) is beyond basic spot (A-45), so enforcement is from basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). A-45 → A-35.

question_text: 2/15 @ A-40. A36 carries to the A-45 where he fumbles. A42 recovers at the 50 and runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. During A42's run, A86 holds B97 at the A-47. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul was during 2nd run, which ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-47) is behind basic spot (B-45), so enforcement is from spot of foul (10-2-2-c-1). A-47 → A-37.

question_text: 2/15 @ A-40. A36 carries to the A-45 where he fumbles. A42 recovers at the 50 and runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. While the ball is loose, B97 grasps A86's face mask at the A-47. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul considered part of 1st run, which ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (A-45) (10-2-2-c-3). A-45 → B-40.

explanation: Foul was during 1st run, which ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-42) is beyond basic spot (A-40), so enforcement is from basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). A-40 → A-25.

explanation: Foul was during 2nd run, which ended beyond NZ, so basic spot is end of related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-42) is behind basic spot (B-45), so enforcement is from spot of foul (10-2-2-c-1). A-42 → A-27.

explanation: Foul considered part of 1st run, which ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (A-40) (10-2-2-c-3). A-40 → B-45.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A41 fumbles at the B-26 and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: Fumble from B-26 goes out of bounds beyond spot of fumble (B-24) so the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble (B-26) (7-2-4-b-1). The clock starts on the Ready throughout the game when a "forward fumble" goes out of bounds (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A41 fumbles at the B-26 and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-28. What is the ruling?
explanation: Fumble from B-26 goes out of bounds behind spot of fumble (B-28), so the ball remains at the out of bounds spot (B-28) (7-2-4-b-2). The clock starts on the Snap when a "backward fumble" goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. From the B-32, A14 pitches to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-35, and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-37. What is the ruling?
explanation: The pitch from the B-32 to the B-35 is a backward pass (and it's still a backward pass, even though muffed), so the ball remains at the out of bounds spot (B-37) (7-2-4-a). The clock starts on the Snap when a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. From the B-32, A14 pitches to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-35, and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-33. What is the ruling?
explanation: The pitch from the B-32 to the B-35 is a backward pass (and it's still a backward pass, even though muffed), so the ball remains at the out of bounds spot (B-33) (7-2-4-a). The clock starts on the Snap when a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A14 fumbles near the sideline at the B-30. A32 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-28 and it goes out of bounds at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: A32 batted the ball from the B-28 to the B-25, which from A's perspective is forward, so this is a foul (9-4-1-c). Foul by A behind NZ, but Illegal Batting is not among fouls listed in 10-2-2-b. Run ended behind NZ, so basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (B-28) is behind basic spot (B-25), so enforcement is from spot of foul (10-2-2-c-1). B-28 → B-38. The fumble (and it's still a fumble, even though batted) from the B-30 goes out of bounds at the B-25 (beyond spot of fumble), and the clock starts on the Ready throughout the game when a "forward fumble" goes out of bounds (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A14 fumbles near the sideline at the B-30. A32 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-28 and it goes out of bounds at the B-31. What is the ruling?
explanation: A32 batted the ball from the B-27 to the B-29, which from A's perspective is backward, so, this is not a foul (9-4-1-c). The fumble (and it's still a fumble, even though batted) from the B-30 goes out of bounds at the B-31 (behind spot of fumble), so the ball remains at the out of bounds spot (7-2-4-b-2). The clock starts on the Snap whenever a "backward fumble" goes out of bounds after the Two- Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. From the B-21, A14 pitches to A32 who muffs the ball at the B-23. B96 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-22 and it goes out of bounds at the B-20. What is the ruling?
explanation: B96 batted the ball from the B-22 to the B-20, which from B's perspective is backward; so this is not a foul (9-4-1-c). A14's pitch from the B-21 to the B-23 is a backward pass (and it's still a backward pass, even though muffed and batted), so the ball remains at the out-of- bounds spot (B-20) (7-2-4-a). The clock starts on the Snap whenever a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. From the B-21, A14 pitches to A32 who muffs the ball at the B-23. B96 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-22 and it goes out of bounds at the B-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: B96 batted the ball from the B-22 to the B-24, which from B's perspective is forward; so this is a foul (9-4-1-c). A14's pitch from the B-21 to the B-23 is a backward pass (and it's still a backward pass, even though muffed and batted). The end of the related run is the spot of the backward pass (B-21) (2-30-4-c), and since the run ended beyond NZ, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Foul by team not in possession, so enforcement is from basic spot (B-21) (10-2-2-c-3). B-21 → B-11. The clock starts on the Snap whenever a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. A4's pass is complete to A86 at the A-35, who then runs to the A-40 where he is tackled. During the pass, A56 taunts B98 at the A-28. Assume that the current rule remains in force for 2025. What is the ruling?
explanation: Foul by A behind NZ, but Taunting is Unsportsmanlike Conduct, which is not among fouls listed in 10-2-2-b. Basic spot is previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). Foul by team in possession, and spot of foul (A-28) is behind basic spot (A-30), so enforcement is from spot of foul (10-2-2-c-1). A-28 → A-14. If UNS is added to 10-2-2-b in 2025, enforcement from previous spot. A-30 → A-15.

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. A89 is on the end of the line, covering up A17 who is also on the line. At the snap, both receivers proceed well downfield immediately. A4's pass is complete to back A36 at the A-28, and he runs to the A-45 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A17 is ineligible because he is neither a back nor on the end of the line (7-3-3-c), and he is more than three yards downfield when the pass is in flight, but this pass did not cross the neutral zone, so there is no foul for Ineligible Downfield (7-3-10).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. As he is running his route, eligible receiver A84 steps on the sideline at the B-20. A84 is not contacted by a defender, but returns inbounds to secure A6's pass at the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A84 went out of bounds, then re-established inbounds and was the first to touch the pass. This is a foul for Illegal Touching (7-3-4), and while the penalty is loss of down at the previous spot, the pass was complete, so clock on Ready.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. As he is running his route, eligible receiver A84 steps on the sideline at the B-20. A84 is forced out of bounds by B37, but immediately returns inbounds to secure A6's pass at the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since A84 was forced out of bounds and returned immediately, there is no foul for Illegal Touching (7-3-4-Exception), and since A84 re-established inbounds, this is a catch and the clock continues to run.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. As he is running his route, eligible receiver A84 steps on the sideline at the B-20. A84 is not contacted by a defender, and leaps from out of bounds to secure A6's pass at the B-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A84 went out of bounds, but did not re-establish inbounds prior to touching the ball, so this is not Illegal Touching (7-3-4). Rather, he was still out of bounds when he touched the loose ball. So, the pass is incomplete with clock on the Snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. A8 runs to the A-46 then retreats to the A-44. From there, he completes a pass to A30 at the B-45 where he falls out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: While the passer was behind the neutral zone when the ball was released, he had been beyond. So, this is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-e). A-44 → A-39 + LOD.

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. With Team A trailing by 14 points and 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter, A1 throws an incomplete pass near the sideline and time expires on the play. Video shows that, when the ball hit the ground, there was 0:01 remaining. What is the ruling?
explanation: In the 4th quarter, to restore time, the score differential must be eight points or less (12-3-6-d-3), although after a touchdown, all potential results of the Try down must be considered. Since Team A trails by 14 points, Replay will not restore the one second, and the game is over.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. A1 advances to the B-40 where he fumbles. B1 recovers at the B-35 and returns the ball across Team A's goal line. While the ball was loose, B2 committed a personal foul at the 50. What is the ruling?
explanation: A1's running play include his run and the loose ball action which followed before B1 recovered (2-30-4-c), so B2's foul occurred during this running play. The end of the related run is the spot of the fumble, the B-40 (2-25-8-b), and since this is beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-40 → B-25 (10-2-2-c-3). The clock starts on the snap because the result of the play was a Touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A32 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B’s end zone and over the end line. What is the ruling?
choice_c: Both B and C are options for Team A.
explanation: B25 has illegally kicked a loose ball (9-4-4), and this action provides the impetus which puts the ball into B's end zone (8-7-2-b-1). Because the ball then became dead behind the goal line, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). Since Team B would next put the ball into play via a free kick (8-5-2), all conditions of postscrimmage kick enforcement are satisfied (10-2-3-b), should Team A wish to accept the penalty. The PSK (basic) spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-b), and since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred behind the basic spot, the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-1). Thus, both B and C are options for Team A.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Defender B31 is in press coverage, and at the snap strikes wideout A89 in the face mask. A89 is initially disrupted from running the route. A11 is sacked for a 7-yard loss. What is the ruling?
explanation: B31's striking blow to the face mask is a Personal Foul (9-1-2-a). Since the related run ended behind the neutral zone, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced 15 yards from the previous spot, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Defensive back B24 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. While still in the end zone, he fumbles the ball into the field of play, and in the scramble A23 intentionally kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line. What is the best option available to Team B?
explanation: While B24's fumble provided the initial impetus (8-7-2-a), responsibility shifted to A23 when he illegally kicked the ball (8-7-2-b-1). Since Team A is responsible for putting the ball into Team B's end zone where it became dead behind the goal line, the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a). Because A23's foul (9-4-4) occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, and the result of the play is a touchback, the basic spot is the B-20 (10-2-2-d-2-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-20 → B-30 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. The score is tied late in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Team A completes a pass over the middle, and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 with 0:05 remaining on the clock. B27 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official’s timeout for injury prior to the ball being spotted. What is the most likely result?
explanation: If a player injury occurs on a play where there is a temporary stoppage of the clock for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f-Exception). Team A will certainly decline the runoff, so the clock will start by rule on the Snap (3-3-6-f-2). There is no Delay of Game foul (options C and D) because B27 presented as injured immediately after the play (3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards where he is tackled. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54’s helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. What is the ruling?
