choice_a: No option for a 10-second runoff , Play Clock :40.
choice_b: Option for a 10-second runoff , Play Clock :25.
choice_c: No option for a 10-second runoff , Play Clock :25.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff , Play Clock :40.
explanation: Since A22 ran for 8 yards with the play ending at 1:58, we may infer that the ball was snapped before the Two-Minute Timeout. So, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b-2). While a defensive helmet would normally create a 40-second play clock, it will be 25 seconds here because we will now go to the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-10-b-1).

question_text: 2/7 @ A-5. Late in the 2nd quarter, A11 drops back to pass and is scrambling in his end zone as he tries to find an open receiver. About to be tackled in the end zone, A11 throws the ball forward to the ground in an area where there are no eligible receivers. The referee throws a flag for Intentional Grounding. When the ball is dead, the game clock shows 0:18. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Both A and B are options for Team B.
choice_c: Neither A nor B is an option for Team B.
explanation: The penalty for A11's foul is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h), resulting in a Safety (8-5-1-b). This foul also triggers a 10-second runoff option (3-4-4-a-2). If Team B declines the runoff, the clock will start when the ensuing free kick is legally touched in the field of play (AR 3-4-4 VIII). If Team B accepts the runoff, the clock will start on the Ready (3-4-4-c), meaning that Team A would not be required to kick the ball and the half could end.

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. With Team A trailing by 14 points and 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter, A1 throws an incomplete pass near the sideline and time expires on the play. Video shows that, when the ball hit the ground, there was 0:01 remaining. What if Team A trails by 8 points on 2/5 @ B-10 … the pass is complete inbounds at the B-1 as time expires … Team A has no time outs remaining … and video shows that when the receiver was down, there was 0:01 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Snap (0:01), with the one second restored since the differential is eight points or less.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready (0:01), with time restored on the Referee's signal.
choice_d: A - 3/G @ B-10 - Snap (0:01), with the incomplete-pass ruling standing and time restored.
explanation: Even though the score differential is 8 points or less, the clock would start by rule on the Ready (3-3-2-e-1) … and if time expires in a half and the clock would start on the Referee’s signal, there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the ball should have been declared dead to restore time to the clock (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. With Team A trailing by 14 points and 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter, A1 throws an incomplete pass near the sideline and time expires on the play. Video shows that, when the ball hit the ground, there was 0:01 remaining. What if Team A trails by 8 points on 2/5 @ B-10 … the pass is complete inbounds at the B-1 as time expires … Team A has one time out remaining … and video shows that when the receiver was down, there was 0:01 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over, since fewer than three seconds cannot be restored even with a timeout.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready (0:01), with the clock starting on the Ready despite the timeout.
choice_d: A - 3/G @ B-10 - Snap (0:01), with the pass ruled incomplete and no time restored.
explanation: Team A will be allowed to use their remaining time out, which of course means the clock will then start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-5). This allows Replay to restore fewer than 3 seconds to the clock (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. With Team A trailing by 14 points and 0:08 remaining in the 4th quarter, A1 throws an incomplete pass near the sideline and time expires on the play. Video shows that, when the ball hit the ground, there was 0:01 remaining. What if Team A trails by 8 points on 2/5 @ B-10 … the pass is complete inbounds at the B-1 as time expires … Team A has no time outs remaining … B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap … and video shows that when the receiver was down, there was 0:01 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over, since the offside foul does not create a snap-clock option.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready (0:01), with the clock starting on the Ready after the accepted penalty.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-5 - Snap (0:01), with the offside penalty enforced five yards from the previous spot.
explanation: Since the clock was stopped to complete the penalty for a foul by the team ahead in score, Team A has the option to start the clock on the Snap (3-4-3-b). This allows Replay to restore fewer than 3 seconds to the clock (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. A1 advances to the B-40 where he fumbles. B1 recovers at the B-35 and returns the ball across Team A's goal line. While the ball was loose, B2 committed a personal foul at the 50. What if B1 recovered at the B-35, but then fumbled at the 50 with A2 recovering while grounded at the A-45 … and there was no foul by B2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Ready, with the clock stopped for the change of possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, since Team B's fumble recovery gives them the ball at the fumble spot.
choice_c: A - 4/5 @ A-40 - Running, with no new series since Team A recovered short of the line to gain.
explanation: Even though Team A has possession short of the original line to gain, a new series is awarded to them because there was a change of team possession during the down (5-1-1-e-1). The clock should continue to run because there is no rules-based reason for the clock to have stopped (3-3-2-d / 3-3-2-e).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A32 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B’s end zone and over the end line. What if B25 batted the loose ball at the B-4, and it rolled out of bounds at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot where B25 batted it.
choice_c: Safety. 2 Points for A, since B25's bat sends the ball out of bounds behind the goal line.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-42 - Snap, since B25's bat gives Team A the ball at the previous kick spot.
explanation: B25's bat is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), so the ball belongs to Team B at the out- of-bounds spot, the B-2 (6-3-7).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A32 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B’s end zone and over the end line. What if B25 batted the loose ball at the B-4, and it struck the goal line pylon? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, treating the pylon contact as a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball spotted where B25 batted it in the field of play.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-42 - Snap, with Team A awarded the ball since B25's bat was illegal.
explanation: B25's bat is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but this action provides the impetus which caused the ball to strike the pylon (8-7-2-b-1). Because the pylon is out of bounds behind the goal line (4-2-3-b), the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Defender B31 is in press coverage, and at the snap strikes wideout A89 in the face mask. A89 is initially disrupted from running the route. A11 is sacked for a 7-yard loss. What if A11 eluded the rush and carried the ball to the A-30 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot instead of the end of the run.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the tackle.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with the personal foul enforced as a passing-play foul from the end of the run.
explanation: Since the related run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced 15 yards from the basic spot, A-30 → A-45 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Defender B31 is in press coverage, and at the snap strikes wideout A89 in the face mask. A89 is initially disrupted from running the route. A11 is sacked for a 7-yard loss. What if A11's pass to A27 was complete at the A-40 where he ran out of bounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot since the pass was caught behind the neutral zone.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap since A27 went out of bounds.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, with the personal foul enforced only from the spot of the catch.
explanation: This Personal Foul occurred during a legal forward pass play (2-30-1), so the penalty is enforced from the end of the last run, A-40 → B-45 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Defensive back B24 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. While still in the end zone, he fumbles the ball into the field of play, and in the scramble A23 intentionally kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line. What is the best option available to Team B? What if A23's intentional kick was recovered by B24 while grounded at the B-1? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the basic spot the B-20 since the result was a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball placed where B24 recovered it in the end zone.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since A23's kick sent the ball into the end zone where B recovered.
explanation: Since A23's foul occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, the related run ended in the end zone, and the result of the play is not a touchback, then the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-c). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, GL → B-10 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Defensive back B24 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. While still in the end zone, he fumbles the ball into the field of play, and in the scramble A23 intentionally kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line. What is the best option available to Team B? What if A23's intentional kick went out of bounds at the B-1? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the basic spot the goal line since the run ended in the end zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where A23's kick went out of bounds.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since A23's illegal kick provides new impetus into the end zone.
explanation: A loose ball retains its original status when there is new impetus (8-7-2-c), so A23's intentional kick does not change the fact this is still a fumble. Since the fumble went out of bounds in the field of play in advance that of the spot of the fumble, it is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1) "as if" it never left the end zone. So, the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a). Because A23's foul (9-4-4) occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, and the result of the play is a touchback, the basic spot is the B-20 (10-2-2-d-2-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-20 → B-30 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. The score is tied late in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Team A completes a pass over the middle, and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 with 0:05 remaining on the clock. B27 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official’s timeout for injury prior to the ball being spotted. What is the most likely result? What if A88 was tackled inbounds at the B-15 with 0:05 remaining, and the injury was to A88 rather than B27? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:05), with Team B declining the runoff since the injury was to Team A.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:05), with no runoff option since the injury did not stop the clock.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap (0:05), with a Delay of Game foul on Team A for the injury timeout.
explanation: If a player injury occurs on a play where there is a temporary stoppage of the clock for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f-Exception). Team B would undoubtedly accept the runoff.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. The score is tied late in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Team A completes a pass over the middle, and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 with 0:05 remaining on the clock. B27 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official’s timeout for injury prior to the ball being spotted. What is the most likely result? What if A88 was tackled inbounds at the B-15 with 0:12 remaining, and the injury was to A88 rather than B27? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:12), with the clock starting on the snap after the injury timeout.
choice_b: End Of Regulation, with Team B accepting a 10-second runoff to end the game.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:02), with a 10-second runoff applied for the injury.
explanation: The clock is stopped for two reasons -- to award a new series to Team A, and because of the injury to A88 -- and since more than 10 seconds remain, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards where he is tackled. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54’s helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. What if, on the next play (A - 3/2 @ B-32), A22 is tackled legally for no gain and loses his helmet (1:54)? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/2 @ B-32 - Ready (1:44) only, with no option for Team A to avoid the runoff.
choice_c: A - 4/2 @ B-32 - Snap (1:54) only, with no runoff option since the helmet came off during normal play.
choice_d: A - 4/2 @ B-32 - Ready (1:44), with the play clock set to 40 seconds for the defensive helmet.
explanation: Because the helmet coming off was the reason for stopping the clock, Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b-2). The clock will start on the Ready if Team B accepts the runoff, and on the Snap if they decline it (3-3-10-b-3). Team A may also avoid the runoff by using an available timeout (3-3-10-b-4), in which case the clock would start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-5).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards where he is tackled. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54’s helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. What if, on the next play (A - 3/2 @ B-32), A22 is tackled by the face mask for no gain (1:54) and loses his helmet? Assume that Team A is trailing. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-17 - Ready, with a 10-second runoff applied for the helmet coming off.
choice_c: A - 4/2 @ B-32 - Option, with the face mask penalty declined and only the helmet addressed.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-17 - Snap, with no Ready option since the foul was by the team ahead in score.
explanation: Because the helmet came off as the direct result of a foul by the opponent, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b-2). The 15-yard penalty will be enforced from the end of the run, B-32 → B-17, and because the foul was by the team ahead in score, Team A has a Ready / Snap option (3-4-3-b). A22 may remain in the game because he lost his helmet due to the foul (3-3-10-a).

question_text: 2/7 @ A-5. Late in the 2nd quarter, A11 drops back to pass and is scrambling in his end zone as he tries to find an open receiver. About to be tackled in the end zone, A11 throws the ball forward to the ground in an area where there are no eligible receivers. The referee throws a flag for Intentional Grounding. When the ball is dead, the game clock shows 0:18. What if, in addition to A11's grounding foul, B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/7 @ A-5 - Ready (0:08), with a 10-second runoff applied to the offsetting fouls.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules (0:18), with the grounding safety standing despite the offside foul.
choice_d: A - 2/7 @ A-5 - Snap (0:08), with the runoff applied since Team A's grounding stopped the clock.
explanation: A11's ING and B96's DOF combine to make offsetting fouls (10-1-4) and the down is repeated. There is no option for a 10-second runoff when there are offsetting fouls (3-4-4-e).

question_text: 2/7 @ A-5. Late in the 2nd quarter, A11 drops back to pass and is scrambling in his end zone as he tries to find an open receiver. About to be tackled in the end zone, A11 throws the ball forward to the ground in an area where there are no eligible receivers. The referee throws a flag for Intentional Grounding. When the ball is dead, the game clock shows 0:18. What if, in the original play, A11 was outside the tackle box and threw the ball into the ground beyond the neutral zone at the numbers, but A56 was at the A-10 when the pass was thrown? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/9½ @ A-2½ - Snap (0:08), with a 10-second runoff applied for A56's foul.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules (0:18), treating the throwaway as Intentional Grounding for a safety.
choice_c: A - 3/7 @ A-5 - Snap (0:18), with no Ineligible Downfield foul since the ball was thrown away.
explanation: A11 has legally grounded the ball (7-3-2-h-Exception), but A56 has fouled by being more than three yards beyond the neutral zone before a legal forward pass is in flight (7-3-10). Note that there would have been no foul on A56 if the ball had been legally grounded at or near the sideline (7-3-10-Exception). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because A56's foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Ball at the near hash. A1 throws a pass toward A88. While the ball is in flight, B82 grabs and twists A88’s facemask at the A-35 in the far side zone. The pass falls incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: B82 has committed two fouls: DPI and FMM. Enforcement of the penalty for the DPI would place the ball at the spot of the foul, the A-35 (7-3-8-c-Penalty), but the penalty for the FMM would be enforced 15 yards from the previous spot, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-d-3). So, Team A would decline the DPI penalty and accept the penalty for the FMM. "Previous spot" means both the yard line and the lateral position, so the ball would remain at the near hash.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A87 kicks the ball into the end zone, untouched by B, for a touchback. Team A has two players wearing #4 during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Both A and B are options.
explanation: Penalties for all fouls by the kicking team during a free kick play, other than KCI, may be enforced at the previous spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-1-8). So, Team B may elect previous spot enforcement, A-35 → A-30, or "tack-on" enforcement, B-25 → B-30.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-4. Team A’s untouched punt goes out of bounds at the A-40. A81, in the end zone, grabs and twists B52’s face mask before the ball is kicked. What is the ruling?
choice_c: Both A and B are options.
explanation: The action prior to the kick is still part of a scrimmage kick play (2-30-3), and penalties for all fouls by the kicking team other than KCI during a scrimmage kick play (except FG attempts) in which the ball crosses the neutral zone may be enforced by rule either at the previous spot as the basic spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-3-13). However, if the foul occurs in A’s end zone, the "previous spot" option becomes a Safety (6-3-13-Exception). So, Team B may choose between a Safety and the ball via a free kick, or "tack-on" enforcement, A-40 → A-25.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is A-17, B-21 with 0:12 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and runs to the B-15 where he is tackled inbounds with 0:06 remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. What is the best option available to Team A?
explanation: After the Two-Minute Timeout in either half, if the defense commits a substitution foul and 12 or more players are on the field and participate in a down, officials will penalize the defense for the foul and at the option of the offended team, reset the game clock back to the time displayed at the snap. The game clock will then restart on the next snap (3-5-3-Penalty). The offense may decline the yardage penalty and still receive the timing benefit (AR 3-5-3-X).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. 1:00 remains in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a time out remaining. After the ball is ready for play, the Center Judge is in his regular position and the clock restarted. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The Center Judge is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. This is the second such instance of this scenario by the current Team A. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/15 @ B-35 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff.
choice_d: A - 1/25 @ B-45 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: When Team A sends in substitutes, the officials will not allow the ball to be snapped until Team B has been given an opportunity to substitute (3-5-2-e). By interpretation, if the Center Judge is unable to prevent the snap, the play is shut down, the game clock is stopped and the defense is allowed to substitute in response to Team A’s late substitutions. (AR 3-5-2-V). The penalty for the second and subsequent violations is 15 yards for a team Unsportsmanlike Conduct (3-5-2-Penalty). Since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately after the Two-Minute Timeout, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A’s punt bounces toward B's end zone. A85 bats the ball at the B-2 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-5. The ball deflects into the end zone where it is recovered by A81. During the kick, B87 is flagged for holding at the B-24. What is the ruling?
explanation: A85's bat is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but B88 is deemed not to have touched the ball since it was batted into him (6-3-4-b). So, when the kick untouched by Team B touches the ground in B's end zone, the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). All conditions for postscrimmage kick enforcement apply (10-2-3-b) with the PSK spot the B-20 (2-25-11-b). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B81 intercepts A1’s pass at the B-5 and returns it to the B-13 where he is tackled. During the return, B63 clips in his endzone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Score.  B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock.
choice_b: Two Points for Team A.  Game Over.
choice_c: No Score.  B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
choice_d: Two Points for Team A.  B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
explanation: Penalties for fouls by either team after a change of team possession in extra periods are declined by rule (3-1-3-g-1) with the exception of FUDL (Flagrant, Unsportsmanlike, Dead-ball, and Live-ball treated as dead-ball) fouls … and Clipping is simply a personal foul. There is no score, and Team B will begin their possession series at the A-25.

question_text: 2/15 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with 0:10 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for Team A. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-34 and runs toward the sideline. He is tackled at the B-30 and ruled out of bounds with 0:02 on the game clock. Replays show A80’s left knee hit the ground inbounds at the B-30 with 0:03 remaining just prior to his right knee hitting out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: Whether a runner was down inbounds or out of bounds at the same spot is not reviewable unless a correct ruling of down inbounds would have ended the half (2025 Replay Case Book, Play #155). We consider this to be the case if there are 10 or fewer seconds remaining because this would create a reversal to a running clock and a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). So, this situation is reviewable and the game will end on a runoff.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Ball at the near hash. A1 throws a pass toward A88. While the ball is in flight, B82 grabs and twists A88’s facemask at the A-35 in the far side zone. The pass falls incomplete. What if this occurred on 1/10 @ B-20, Near Hash, and the fouls occurred at the B-8 in the far side zone? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap - Far Hash, with the FMM penalty enforced instead of the DPI.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-8 - Snap - Near Hash, with the ball kept on the near hash despite the far-side foul.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap - Near Hash, with the FMM half-the-distance enforcement on the near hash.
explanation: Enforcement of the penalty for the FMM would be half-the-distance from the previous spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-d-3), but the penalty for DPI would place the ball at the spot of the foul, the B-8 (7-3-8-c-Penalty). So, Team A would decline the FMM penalty and accept the penalty for the DPI. "Spot of the foul" means both the yard line and the lateral position, so the ball would be placed at the far hash.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Ball at the near hash. A1 throws a pass toward A88. While the ball is in flight, B82 grabs and twists A88’s facemask at the A-35 in the far side zone. The pass falls incomplete. What if this occurred on 1/G @ B-10, Near Hash, and the fouls occurred along the far sideline in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Near Hash, with the FMM half-the-distance penalty enforced instead of the DPI.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap - Far Hash, with the ball relocated to the far hash where the foul occurred.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-5 - Snap - Far Hash, with the FMM penalty enforced on the far hash.
explanation: Enforcement of the penalty for the FMM would be half-the-distance from the previous spot, B-10 → B-5 (10-2-2-d-3), but the penalty for DPI would be from the previous spot to the B-2 (7-3-8-c-Penalty). So, Team A would decline the FMM penalty and accept the penalty for the DPI. "Previous spot" means both the yard line and the lateral position, so the ball would remain on the near hash.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A87 kicks the ball into the end zone, untouched by B, for a touchback. Team A has two players wearing #4 during the down. What if B23 fielded the kick at the B-5, returned it to the B-20 where he fumbled, and A46 recovered the loose ball while grounded at the B-25? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement also available.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the penalty tacked on to the dead-ball spot.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick from the previous spot and no yardage penalty.
explanation: Since the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (6-1-8), and so the only enforcement option is from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30 (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A87 kicks the ball into the end zone, untouched by B, for a touchback. Team A has two players wearing #4 during the down. What if B23 fielded the kick at the B-5, returned it to the B-35 where he fumbled, then A46 recovered the loose ball at the B-40 and advanced it to the B-30 where he fumbled, and B23 recovered while grounded at the B-25? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules only, with no tack-on option after the change of possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap only, with the previous-spot re-kick unavailable.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the tack-on from the fumble spot rather than the dead-ball spot.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap.
explanation: Despite the change of possession, the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, so "tack-on" enforcement is still an option. The penalty may be enforced from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, or from the dead-ball spot, B-25 → B-30 (6-1-8).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-4. Team A’s untouched punt goes out of bounds at the A-40. A81, in the end zone, grabs and twists B52’s face mask before the ball is kicked. What if the face mask foul was by A79 at the A-6? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/12 @ A-2 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap.
choice_b: A - 4/12 @ A-2 - Snap only, with no tack-on option for Team B.
choice_c: Safety. 2 Points for B, since A79's face mask foul occurred in Team A's end zone.
choice_d: A - 4/6 @ A-8 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap, with the foul enforced half-the-distance from the previous spot.
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, A-4 → A-2 (10-2-2-c-2). Team B could also elect to have the penalty enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them, A-40 → A-25 (6-3-13).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-4. Team A’s untouched punt goes out of bounds at the A-40. A81, in the end zone, grabs and twists B52’s face mask before the ball is kicked. What if the face mask foul was by A79 at the A-2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety. 2 Points for B, since the face mask foul at the A-2 is in Team A's end zone.
choice_b: A - 4/12 @ A-2 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap.
choice_c: A - 4/12 @ A-2 - Snap only, with tack-on enforcement unavailable behind the neutral zone.
choice_d: A - 4/14 @ A-1 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-25 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul.
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a), but even though the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the previous spot, A-4 → A-2, because this is personal foul by Team A behind the neutral zone (10-2-2-b). Team B could also elect to have the penalty enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them, A-40 → A-25 (6-3-13).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is A-17, B-21 with 0:12 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and runs to the B-15 where he is tackled inbounds with 0:06 remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. What is the best option available to Team A? What if the 12th player was attempting to leave, but was still on the field at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/5 @ B-20 - Snap (0:06), with the clock reset to the snap and the penalty accepted.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:12), with the game clock reset to the time of the snap.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:06), with no snap-clock option for the offense.
explanation: If the 12th defender is attempting to exit but was still on the field at the snap and had no influence on the play, then the normal substitution penalty is enforced with no clock adjustment (3-5-3-Penalty). So, the clock remains at 0:06, but Team A has the option to start the clock on the snap since the foul was by the team ahead in score (3-4-3-b). This option exists whether the penalty is accepted or declined.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. The score is A-17, B-21 with 0:12 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A1 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and runs to the B-15 where he is tackled inbounds with 0:06 remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. What is the best option available to Team A? What if the 12th player was attempting to leave, but was still on the field at the snap and time expired? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Untimed, with Team A declining the penalty but still getting the untimed down.
choice_b: Game Over, since the period cannot be extended on a defensive substitution foul.
choice_c: A - 1/5 @ B-20 - Snap (0:00), with the penalty enforced but no untimed down.
explanation: If the 12th defender is attempting to exit but was still on the field at the snap and had no influence on the play, then the normal substitution penalty is enforced with no clock adjustment (3-5-3-Penalty). The period is extended for an untimed down if the penalty is accepted for a foul that occurs during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter. Team A will have to give up the additional yardage because if they decline the penalty, the game will be over.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. 1:00 remains in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a time out remaining. After the ball is ready for play, the Center Judge is in his regular position and the clock restarted. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The Center Judge is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. This is the second such instance of this scenario by the current Team A. What if this was the first time during this game that Team A had rushed to the line as above? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/15 @ B-35 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff, since a first Delay of Game does not create a runoff.
choice_c: A - 1/25 @ B-45 - Option For 10-Second Runoff, with the 15-yard Unsportsmanlike penalty on the first violation.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-20 - Option For 10-Second Runoff, with the ball moved back only five yards toward the goal.
explanation: The penalty for the first violation is 5 yards for Delay of Game (3-5-2-Penalty), and since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately after the Two-Minute Timeout, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. 1:00 remains in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a time out remaining. After the ball is ready for play, the Center Judge is in his regular position and the clock restarted. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The Center Judge is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. This is the second such instance of this scenario by the current Team A. What if the original situation occurred when the clock was stopped and would not restart until the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/25 @ B-45 - Option For 10-Second Runoff, since any foul after the Two-Minute Timeout allows a runoff.
choice_b: A - 1/25 @ B-45 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff.
choice_c: A - 1/15 @ B-35 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff, with a five-yard Delay of Game penalty on the second violation.
choice_d: A - 1/25 @ B-45 - No Option For 10-Second Runoff, with the clock starting on the Ready regardless.
explanation: The penalty for the second and subsequent violations is 15 yards for a team Unsportsmanlike Conduct (3-5-2-Penalty). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the game clock was not running when the foul occurred (3-4-4-b).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A’s punt bounces toward B's end zone. A85 bats the ball at the B-2 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-5. The ball deflects into the end zone where it is recovered by A81. During the kick, B87 is flagged for holding at the B-24. What if B87's holding foul occurred at the B-10? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the PSK spot instead of the spot of the foul.
choice_b: A - 4/4 @ B-40 - Snap, with a re-kick since the holding occurred during the kick.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the penalty enforced half-the-distance to the goal.
explanation: As before, A85's bat is legal (9-4-1-c), B88 is deemed not to have touched the ball (6-3-4-b), and the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). All conditions for postscrimmage kick enforcement apply (10-2-3-b) with the PSK spot the B-20 (2-25-11-b). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred behind the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the spot of the foul, B-10 → B-5 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-40. Team A’s punt bounces toward B's end zone. A85 bats the ball at the B-2 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-5. The ball deflects into the end zone where it is recovered by A81. During the kick, B87 is flagged for holding at the B-24. What if, in the original question, A85 batted the ball at the B-5 directly into the leg of B88 at the B-2? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap only, with no option to replay the down after offsetting fouls.
choice_b: A - 4/4 @ B-40 - Snap only, since offsetting fouls always result in a replay of the down.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the recovery in the end zone counting as a touchdown.
choice_d: A - 4/4 @ B-40 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap.
explanation: As previously, B88 is deemed not to have touched the ball (6-3-4-b) and the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b), but here A85's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c). So, there are fouls by both teams. Team B may accept offsetting fouls (10-1-4), or since B's foul is governed by PSK kick rules, Team B may refuse offsetting fouls and retain possession after PSK enforcement. With the PSK spot the B-20 (2-25- 11-b) and the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurring beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty under this option would be from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B81 intercepts A1’s pass at the B-5 and returns it to the B-13 where he is tackled. During the return, B63 clips in his endzone. What if B63's foul was an Illegal Blind Side Block with Targeting, confirmed by Replay? What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Score. B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock.
choice_b: Two Points for Team A. B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with the score standing despite the Targeting foul.
choice_c: No Score. B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the interception.
choice_d: No Score. Game Over, since a confirmed Targeting foul ends the extra-period possession.
explanation: Penalties for FUDL fouls by either team after a change of team possession in extra periods are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-1-Exception) … and Targeting is a Flagrant foul (2-10-3). There is no score, and Team B will begin their possession series after enforcement of the penalty, A-25 → A-40.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B81 intercepts A1’s pass at the B-5 and returns it to the B-13 where he is tackled. During the return, B63 clips in his endzone. What if -- rather than an interception -- A1 fumbled the ball at the B-10 with B81 recovering at the B-5 and advancing as above … and B63's Clipping foul occurring while the ball was loose following the fumble? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-10 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot rather than the end of the run.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - No Clock, since B81's recovery gives Team B possession at the spot of recovery.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-5 - No Clock, with a 10-second runoff applied in the extra period.
explanation: The running play includes A1's run and the loose-ball action before B81 recovers (2-30-4-c), so this a running play with the related run ending at the spot of the fumble (2-25-8-b) and the basic spot being the end of that related run, the B-10 (10-2-2-d-1-b). The foul occurred prior to the change of possession, so A's possession series continues with the penalty enforced from the basic spot, B-10 → B-5 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/15 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with 0:10 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for Team A. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-34 and runs toward the sideline. He is tackled at the B-30 and ruled out of bounds with 0:02 on the game clock. Replays show A80’s left knee hit the ground inbounds at the B-30 with 0:03 remaining just prior to his right knee hitting out of bounds. What if A80 was ruled out of bounds at the B-30 (0:12) when in fact he was down inbounds at the B-30 (0:13)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/5 @ B-30 - Ready (0:13), with the clock starting on the Ready after review.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Ready (0:03), treating this the same as the reviewable line-to-gain scenario.
choice_d: Game Over Via 10-Second Runoff, since fewer than ten seconds remain.
explanation: Not reviewable. Whether a runner was down inbounds or out of bounds at the same spot is not reviewable unless there are 10 or fewer seconds remaining (2025 Replay Case Book, Play #155).

question_text: 2/15 @ B-40. The score is A-28, B-31 with 0:10 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for Team A. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-34 and runs toward the sideline. He is tackled at the B-30 and ruled out of bounds with 0:02 on the game clock. Replays show A80’s left knee hit the ground inbounds at the B-30 with 0:03 remaining just prior to his right knee hitting out of bounds. What if A80 was ruled out of bounds at the B-25 (0:12) when in fact he was down inbounds at the B-26 (0:13)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Snap (0:13), with the clock starting on the snap and no runoff.
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ B-30 - Snap (0:12), with the out-of-bounds ruling standing as not reviewable.
choice_c: Game Over Via 10-Second Runoff, with the runoff exhausting the remaining time.
explanation: Reviewable for the line-to-gain. Properly officiated, the ball would have been dead at the B-26 and the clock would have continued to run. Replay is therefore creating a running clock, and a 10-second runoff applies (12-3-6-c). Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule, and implement the runoff, 0:13 → 0:03.

question_text: 2/2 @ B-27. The score is A-24, B-28 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter, the clock running, and Team A with no timeouts. Team A hurries to the line of scrimmage. A55 short-hops the shotgun snap to A19 at the B-32. A19 fields the snap and immediately throws the ball to the ground with 0:12 remaining. What is the most likely result?
explanation: A forward pass is illegal if the passer to conserve time throws the ball directly to the ground after the ball has already touched the ground (7-3-2-f-1). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-32 (7-3-2-f-Penalty). An incomplete illegal forward pass triggers a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3) which Team B will accept and Team A -- with no timeout remaining -- cannot avoid. The clock will then start on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/25 @ 50. Late in the 3rd quarter. B33 is flagged for making early contact on receiver A81 at the B-25, and B27 intercepts the pass at the B-20 where he is tackled immediately. As he gets up, B27 taunts receiver A80. Video shows the clock at 0:00 before the ball was snapped. What is the ruling?
explanation: When there is video evidence that time expired prior to the ball being put into play, Replay will void that play regardless of the outcome; however, penalties for personal or unsportsmanlike fouls must be enforced (12-3-6-e). So, the DPI foul will be disregarded, but the penalty of the UNS / TAU will be enforced from the succeeding spot (10-2-2-a), 50 → B-35, with an automatic first down (9-2-1-b-Penalty).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-44. The score is A-14, B-17 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for either team. A12 is pressured in the pocket, and from the 50 throws the ball incomplete into an area with no eligible receivers. During the down, B99 lost his helmet (not due to foul). The clock shows 0:08 at the end of the play. What is the most likely result?
explanation: A12 is guilty of Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h), and this foul includes an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-2) which Team B will accept to end the game since Team A has no timeout to avoid it. B99's lost helmet does not trigger a runoff possibility because it was not the only reason for stopping the clock (3-3-10-b), and it does not negate the runoff for A12's foul (2025 CFO Interpretation Bulletin #1, Play #6).

question_text: 4/G @ B-9. Airborne receiver A81 has firm control of the ball one yard deep in B’s endzone when B33 drives him back into the field of play. He lands at the B-2 maintaining firm control, then spins out and is brought to the ground at the B-1. What is the ruling?
explanation: When an airborne pass receiver completes a catch after an opponent has driven the receiver backward and the ball is declared dead at the spot of the catch, the forward progress is where the player gained and maintained firm control of the ball (5-1-3-Exception 2). By interpretation, the catch is completed and the ball is dead when A81 lands at the B-2 and maintains control, so this is a touchdown (AR 5-1-3-I-b).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-44. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield, and the punt is partially blocked. The ball hits at the A-48 and then bounces backwards and out of bounds at the A-42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Both A and C are options.
explanation: Because this scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone and the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-3-7), the penalty for this foul by the kicking team during the scrimmage kick play (2-30-3) may be enforced either at the previous spot, A-44 → A-39, with a re-kick, or at spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, A-42 → A-37 (6-3-13).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. A17 drops back in the pocket in the end zone. RT A79 remains engaged with B99, but B99 gets past him. Still engaged with B99 as he is about to make the sack, A79 pushes B99 in the back and to the ground. A17 completes a pass to A82 for a TD. What is the ruling?
explanation: A block in the back (other than against the ball carrier) is illegal (9-3-5); however, offensive linemen within the blocking zone at the snap may legally block in the back in the blocking zone, subject to certain conditions (9-3-5-Exception 1). The continuing action described here is within the spirit of the exception to Rule 9-3-5 (AR 9-3-3-IX), and so there is no foul. The touchdown counts.

question_text: 3/13 @ B-48. A10's pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-4, and his momentum takes him into his end zone where he is hit and fumbles. The ball rolls to the B-1 where A88 attempts to recover it. The ball then rolls back into the end zone where B20 falls on the ball. The ruling on the field is a safety. Video shows A88's feet were out of bounds when he touched the ball. What is the ruling?
explanation: When out of bounds A88 (4-2-1-a) touches the loose ball, then the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). So, B19's fumble from the end zone went out of bounds in the field of play, and is thus returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1) "as if" it never left the end zone. Therefore, momentum holds (8-5-1-a-Exception), and the ball is placed at the momentum spot, the B-4.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield. Quarterback A19 drops back and throws an interception to B11 who returns it for a touchdown. Video shows B99 launching and forcibly contacting A19's head at the B-35 during the return. What is the ruling?
explanation: The quarterback is defenseless anytime after a change of possession (2-27-14-i), so B99's contact is Targeting (9-1-4). In extra periods, if both teams foul during the down and Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated (3-1-3-g-3); however, penalties for FUDL (Flagrant, Unsportsmanlike, Dead-ball, and Live-ball treated as dead-ball) fouls are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-Exception), and Targeting is a flagrant foul (2-10-3). The score is canceled (3-1-3-g-2), and Team B will begin their possession series after enforcement of the penalty, A-25 → A-40. B99 will be disqualified.

question_text: 2/2 @ B-27. The score is A-24, B-28 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter, the clock running, and Team A with no timeouts. Team A hurries to the line of scrimmage. A55 short-hops the shotgun snap to A19 at the B-32. A19 fields the snap and immediately throws the ball to the ground with 0:12 remaining. What is the most likely result? What if this play occurs on 3/2 @ B-27 with the score tied? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/7 @ B-32 - Ready (0:02) only, with no option for the runoff to be declined.
choice_c: A - 4/7 @ B-32 - Snap (0:12) only, with no runoff option when the score is tied.
choice_d: A - 3/2 @ B-27 - Snap (0:12), with the spike ruled a legal clock-stopping play.
explanation: This is still a foul (7-3-2-f-1) for which the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-32 (7-3-2-f-Penalty), and for which there is the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). The option is not quite as clear here, but the clock will start on the Ready if the runoff is accepted and on the Snap if the runoff is declined (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/2 @ B-27. The score is A-24, B-28 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter, the clock running, and Team A with no timeouts. Team A hurries to the line of scrimmage. A55 short-hops the shotgun snap to A19 at the B-32. A19 fields the snap and immediately throws the ball to the ground with 0:12 remaining. What is the most likely result? What if -- in the original play -- B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/7 @ B-32 - Ready (0:02), with the grounding penalty enforced and a runoff applied.
choice_c: A - 2/2 @ B-27 - Ready (0:02), with a 10-second runoff despite the offsetting fouls.
choice_d: A - 2/7 @ B-32 - Snap (0:12), with only the Illegal Forward Pass penalty enforced.
explanation: A19's Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-f-1) and B96's Defensive Offside (7-1-5-b-1) create offsetting fouls (10-1-4), and the 10-second runoff does not apply when there are offsetting fouls (3-4-4-e). The clock will start by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-d-4).

question_text: 3/25 @ 50. Late in the 3rd quarter. B33 is flagged for making early contact on receiver A81 at the B-25, and B27 intercepts the pass at the B-20 where he is tackled immediately. As he gets up, B27 taunts receiver A80. Video shows the clock at 0:00 before the ball was snapped. What if -- after B27 taunted A80 -- A80 shoved B27 to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/25 @ 50 - Snap, with the DPI penalty also enforced from the succeeding spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with A80's shove declined and the DPI enforced.
choice_d: A - 3/40 @ A-35 - Snap, with the two unsportsmanlike fouls enforced against Team A only.
explanation: Replay will void the play with penalties for personal and unsportsmanlike fouls enforced (12-3-6-e). DPI is neither a personal nor unsportsmanlike foul, and since both B27 and A80 committed unsportsmanlike fouls before the penalty for either was completed, the yardage penalties cancel and the number or type of down established before the fouls occurred is unaffected (10-1-5-Exception).

question_text: 3/25 @ 50. Late in the 3rd quarter. B33 is flagged for making early contact on receiver A81 at the B-25, and B27 intercepts the pass at the B-20 where he is tackled immediately. As he gets up, B27 taunts receiver A80. Video shows the clock at 0:00 before the ball was snapped. What if -- in the original play -- B33's contact with A80 was deemed to be Targeting, confirmed by Replay? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with only the Targeting penalty enforced and the Taunting disregarded.
choice_b: A - 3/25 @ 50 - Snap, with both personal and unsportsmanlike fouls canceling since the play is voided.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with B33 not disqualified despite the confirmed Targeting.
explanation: Replay will void the play with penalties for personal and unsportsmanlike fouls enforced (12-3-6-e). The penalties for both B33's live-ball personal foul for Targeting and B27's dead-ball unsportsmanlike foul for Taunting will be enforced separately and in the order of occurrence, 50 → B-35 → B-20 (10-1-6-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-44. The score is A-14, B-17 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for either team. A12 is pressured in the pocket, and from the 50 throws the ball incomplete into an area with no eligible receivers. During the down, B99 lost his helmet (not due to foul). The clock shows 0:08 at the end of the play. What is the most likely result? What if A12 was able to locate a receiver, and completed a pass to A84 at the B-35 where he was tackled inbounds (0:08), and B99 lost his helmet (not due to foul) during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-35 - Ready (0:08), with Team A accepting the runoff to preserve time.
choice_c: Game Over, with Team B accepting a 10-second runoff to end the game.
choice_d: A - 4/1 @ B-35 - Snap (0:00), with the runoff exhausting the remaining time.
explanation: Because B99's helmet off was the only reason for stopping the clock, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b-2). Team A will decline the runoff, and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-10-b-3).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-44. The score is A-14, B-17 with 0:15 remaining in the 4th quarter and no timeouts for either team. A12 is pressured in the pocket, and from the 50 throws the ball incomplete into an area with no eligible receivers. During the down, B99 lost his helmet (not due to foul). The clock shows 0:08 at the end of the play. What is the most likely result? What if A12 was able to escape outside the tackle box and throw the ball incomplete beyond the neutral zone into an area with no eligible receivers (0:08), and B99 lost his helmet (not due to foul) during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Game Over, with Team B accepting a 10-second runoff for the Intentional Grounding.
choice_c: A - 4/10 @ B-44 - Ready (0:08), with the clock starting on the Ready after the grounding.
choice_d: A - 4/16 @ 50 - Snap (0:08), with the grounding penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: A12 has legally grounded the ball (7-3-2-h), and since B99's helmet off was not the only reason for stopping the clock, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-10-b-2).

question_text: 4/G @ B-9. Airborne receiver A81 has firm control of the ball one yard deep in B’s endzone when B33 drives him back into the field of play. He lands at the B-2 maintaining firm control, then spins out and is brought to the ground at the B-1. What if this occurred on 4/4 @ B-9 with airborne receiver A81 having firm control of the ball at the B-4 when B33 drives him back to the B-7 where he lands on his feet, then spins out and is brought to the ground at the B-6? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, treating the airborne control as a completed catch in the end zone.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-7 - Running, with progress marked where A81 landed rather than firm control.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-6 - Running, with progress at the spot A81 was brought to the ground.
explanation: Since A81 went to the ground at the B-7 and maintained control, the ball was declared dead there at the spot of the catch. So, A81's progress is at the spot where he gained firm control, the B-4 (AR 5-1-3-III).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-44. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield, and the punt is partially blocked. The ball hits at the A-48 and then bounces backwards and out of bounds at the A-42. What if the ball hit at A-43 and then bounced backwards and out of bounds at the A-42? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/17 @ A-39 - Snap only, with no option for Team B to take the out-of-bounds spot.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-42 - Snap only, with the re-kick option unavailable.
choice_c: A - 4/17 @ A-39 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-37 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement from the dead-ball spot.
choice_d: A - 4/17 @ A-39 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-42 - Snap.
explanation: Even though the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, "tack- on" enforcement does not apply since the scrimmage kick did not cross the neutral zone (6-3-13). Team B may either accept the penalty from the previous spot, A-44 → A-39, with a re-kick (10-2-2-d-4-a), or decline the penalty and take the ball at the out of bounds spot, the A-42 (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-44. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield, and the punt is partially blocked. The ball hits at the A-48 and then bounces backwards and out of bounds at the A-42. What if ball hit at the A-48 where it then touched B97 and was recovered by A65 while grounded at the A-42? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/17 @ A-39 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ A-42 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement also available to Team B.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-42 - Snap, since Team B may take the ball at the spot where it was recovered.
choice_d: A - 4/12 @ A-37 - Snap, with the penalty enforced only five yards from the previous spot.
explanation: Team A is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down because it was touched by Team B beyond the neutral zone (6-3-3). So, even though the scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone, "tack-on" enforcement does not apply since the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B (6-3-13). Their only option is to accept the penalty from the previous spot, A-44 → A-39, with a re-kick (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. A17 drops back in the pocket in the end zone. RT A79 remains engaged with B99, but B99 gets past him. Still engaged with B99 as he is about to make the sack, A79 pushes B99 in the back and to the ground. A17 completes a pass to A82 for a TD. What if A79 disengages from B99, and in a separate action at the A-2, pushes B99 in the back to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/13 @ A-3 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready after the block.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, since the illegal block occurred in Team A's end zone.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with no foul since A79's action was within the blocking exception.
explanation: The blocking zone only extends three yards behind the line of scrimmage, so because A79's action is totally separate and a new block outside the zone, it is a foul for a block in the back (AR 9-3-3-IX). Because this is an illegal block by the offense behind the neutral zone, the penalty is enforced half-the-distance from the previous spot, A-6 → A-3 (10-2-2-b). The clock starts on the Snap because the result of the play was a touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. A17 drops back in the pocket in the end zone. RT A79 remains engaged with B99, but B99 gets past him. Still engaged with B99 as he is about to make the sack, A79 pushes B99 in the back and to the ground. A17 completes a pass to A82 for a TD. What if A79 disengages from B99, and in a separate action in the end zone, pushes B99 in the back to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/13 @ A-3 - Snap, with the illegal block enforced half-the-distance rather than a safety.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the touchdown counting since the block was within the exception.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with A79 also disqualified for the block in the end zone.
explanation: The blocking zone only extends three yards behind the line of scrimmage, so because A79's action is totally separate and a new block outside the zone, it is a foul for a block in the back (AR 9-3-3-IX). Even though this is an illegal block by the offense behind the neutral zone, if the foul occurs in Team A’s end zone, the penalty is a safety (10-2-2-b-Exception).

question_text: 3/13 @ B-48. A10's pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-4, and his momentum takes him into his end zone where he is hit and fumbles. The ball rolls to the B-1 where A88 attempts to recover it. The ball then rolls back into the end zone where B20 falls on the ball. The ruling on the field is a safety. Video shows A88's feet were out of bounds when he touched the ball. What if A88 was not out of bounds, batted the ball at the B-1, and it went out of bounds in the field of play just in front of the pylon? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball placed at the momentum spot without penalty enforcement.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, treating the out-of-bounds bat as a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-24 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the bat rather than the momentum spot.
explanation: A88's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c), but the loose ball retains its status as a fumble (8-7-2-c) … and when it goes out of bounds in the field of play, it is returned to the spot of the fumble "as if" it never left the end zone (7-2-4-b-1). Thus, momentum holds (8-5-1-a-Exception), and the basic spot is the momentum spot, the B-4 (10-2-2-d-2-b-Exception). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-4 → B-14 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/13 @ B-48. A10's pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-4, and his momentum takes him into his end zone where he is hit and fumbles. The ball rolls to the B-1 where A88 attempts to recover it. The ball then rolls back into the end zone where B20 falls on the ball. The ruling on the field is a safety. Video shows A88's feet were out of bounds when he touched the ball. What if A88 was not out of bounds, batted the ball at the B-1, and it went out of bounds in the end zone just behind the pylon? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the penalty accepted from the goal-line basic spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball returned to the momentum spot rather than a touchback.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since A88's illegal bat sent the ball out of the end zone.
explanation: A88's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c), and also adds a new impetus to the ball (8-7-2-b-1). So, the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-a). The basic spot would be the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-b), so rather than accepting the penalty from there, GL → B-10, Team B will decline the penalty and take the result of the play. Even though the result of the play was a touchback, the basic spot is not the B-20 because the change of possession occurred in the field of play (10-2-2-d-2-b) rather than in the end zone (10-2-2-d-2-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield. Quarterback A19 drops back and throws an interception to B11 who returns it for a touchdown. Video shows B99 launching and forcibly contacting A19's head at the B-35 during the return. What if B99 launched and forcibly contact A19's head just after he released the pass? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with B99's Targeting enforced on the succeeding play as a flagrant foul.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock, with B99 remaining in the game since the fouls offset.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-20 - No Clock, with the five-in-the-backfield foul enforced against Team A.
explanation: A player in the act of or just after throwing a pass is defenseless (2-27-14-a), so B99's contact is Targeting (9-1-4). But since Team B fouled prior to the change of possession, the fouls now offset (10-1-4). The "FUDL" concept only applies to fouls which occur after a change of possession (3-1-3-g), so even though B99's foul is flagrant (2-10-3), it will not be penalized. B99 will, however, still be disqualified (10-1-4).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. A snaps the ball with five players in the backfield. Quarterback A19 drops back and throws an interception to B11 who returns it for a touchdown. Video shows B99 launching and forcibly contacting A19's head at the B-35 during the return. What if -- in the original question -- Team A had only four players in the backfield, but B96 was offside at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-25 - No Clock, with Team A required to accept the Targeting penalty instead of the offside.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with B99's Targeting enforced and the interception standing.
choice_d: A - 1/5 @ B-20 - No Clock, with B99 not disqualified since only the offside was accepted.
explanation: The quarterback is defenseless anytime after a change of possession (2-27-14-i), so B99's contact is Targeting (9-1-4). So, Team B has committed two live-ball fouls (DOF and TGT), but Team A may only accept the penalty for one of them (10-1-3). In order for their possession series to continue, they must accept the penalty for the DOF, B-25 → B-20, and decline the penalty for the TGT. B99 will, however, still be disqualified (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. Team A is in an Illegal Formation at the snap. A3's punt strikes the ground at the B-10 and rolls toward B's end zone. A82 bats the untouched punt above B's end zone back into the field of play. B18 picks up the ball at the B-2 and returns it to the: B-17 where he runs out of bounds. If Team B does not want a re-kick, which of these is their best available option?
explanation: While not during the kick, the foul still occurred during a scrimmage kick play (2-30-3), so Team B may have this penalty enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them (B-17 → B-22). This is better than taking the ball at the B-20 as a result of Illegal Touching (6-3-11).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. Team A is in an Illegal Formation at the snap. A3's punt strikes the ground at the B-10 and rolls toward B's end zone. A82 bats the untouched punt above B's end zone back into the field of play. B18 picks up the ball at the B-2 and returns it to the: B-13 where he runs out of bounds. If Team B does not want a re-kick, which of these is their best available option?
explanation: While not during the kick, the foul still occurred during a scrimmage kick play (2-30-3), so Team B may have this penalty enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them (B-13 → B-18). This is not as good, though, as taking the ball at the B-20 as a result of Illegal Touching (6-3-11).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B43 at the B-1 and returned 99 yards for an apparent two points; however: during the return, B96 illegally blocked A34 below the waist, and during the return, A87 targeted B56 (confirmed by RO). What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score. A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock.
choice_b: No score. A - Try @ B-18 - No Clock.
choice_c: Try over. A- F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: Try over. A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
explanation: If both teams foul during the Try and Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, the fouls cancel and the Try is over, but penalties for flagrant personal fouls, unsportsmanlike fouls, dead-ball personal fouls and live-ball fouls treated as dead ball fouls are enforced on the succeeding kickoff (8-3-5-c). A87 is disqualified.

question_text: 3/G @ B-8. A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A23. He runs a sweep, dives toward the goal line, and lands in the end zone, but is ruled out of bounds at the B-3. Video shows that A23 did not step out of bounds, and while in the air lost control of the ball at the B-1 with the loose ball going into a pile of players in the end zone. There is no video evidence of clear recovery, but the Umpire verifies that Team A recovered the loose ball. What is the ruling?
explanation: If a ball carrier is ruled out of bounds, the play is not reviewable (12-3-3-g) unless a potential scoring play (12-3-1-a) or a fumble (12-3-3-d) is involved. When there is no video evidence of clear recovery, Replay may rely on confirmation of the recovery by the officials on the field (12-3-3-d-1). Since the Umpire verified that A recovered the loose ball in B's end zone, it is a touchdown (8-2-1-c).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-34. The score is A-23, B-24 with the clock stopped at 0:08 in the 4th quarter and no time outs remaining for either team. A12 throws a pass to A11, the pass is complete, and he is tackled at the B-9 as time expires. Team B had 12 players participating during the play. What is the most likely result?
explanation: After the Two-Minute Timeout in either half, if the defense commits a substitution foul and 12 or more players are on the field and participate in a down, officials will penalize the defense for the foul and at the option of the offended team, reset the game clock back to the time displayed at the snap, with the clock restarting on the Snap (3-5-3-b-Penalty). There is no requirement to accept the penalty to have the clock reset. The offense may decline the penalty and keep the option to reset the game clock and have the game clock start on the next snap (AR 3-5-3-X).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-40. A43 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up slot receiver A21. At the snap, both A43 and A21 drop back to potentially receive A1’s pass. The ball leaves A1’s hand at the A-37 and first touches A21’s hand at the A-38. After the catch, A21 rushes to the 50 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A21 is an originally ineligible receiver (7-3-3-c), and when he intentionally touches a legal forward pass before it has been touched by an opponent or an official, he commits Illegal Touching (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-40 → A-35, with a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 2/G @ B-16. A11 is hit in the backfield at the B-20 by B24, whose helmet comes off. B24 puts the helmet back on, pursues A11, and assists in the tackle at the B-2. What is the ruling?
explanation: A player whose helmet comes off completely during a down may not continue to participate beyond the immediate continuing action in which they are engaged (9-1-17). This is a personal foul, and the basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-2 (10-2-2-d-1-b). The 15-yard penalty for the foul by the team not in possession will be enforced half-the- distance from the basic spot, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B23 catches A4's kick at the B-10 and runs to the B-40 where he is downed. During the run, B32 blocked A37 in the back at the B-30. Video shows that B43 gave an invalid fair catch signal while the kick was in flight. What is the ruling?
explanation: The receiving team advancing after a fair catch signal is reviewable (12-3-4-g), and Replay will bring the ball back to the spot of the catch, the B-10 (6-5-3-a). Fouls that carry 5-yard and 10-yard penalties do not have those penalties enforced if the ruling is overturned and they become dead ball fouls (12-3-6-i).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A90 executes an onside kick toward the sideline. A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. He leaps from out of bounds, and while airborne at the A-47, bats the ball, and it rolls forward with A88 recovering at the A-49. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A33 leaps from out of bounds, he remains out of bounds while airborne (2-27-15-a-2), and when he touches the kick while out of bounds, the loose ball is then out of bounds (4-2-3-a) and this becomes a Free Kick Out of Bounds (6-2-1). The ball belongs to the Team B at that spot, the A-47 (6-2-2), and they will elect to have the five-yard penalty enforced from that spot, A-47 → A-42 (6-2-1-Penalty). There is no foul for a Kicking Team Player Out of Bounds (6-1-11) because A33 never returned inbounds, and there is no foul for Illegal Batting (9-4-1-c) because the ball was dead as soon as it was touched by A33.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B10 intercepts A12’s legal forward pass at the B-6 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he downs the ball. What is the ruling?
choice_a: 2 Points For A.  B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
choice_b: 0 Points For A.  B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
explanation: The momentum exception does not apply because the pass was not intercepted between the B-5 and the goal line (8-5-1-a-Exception), so the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). Team A's possession series is then over, and Team B begins their possession series at the 25-yard line on the same end of the field (3-1-3-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-25. A12 is in shotgun formation with the game clock running. Center B58 legally starts the backward motion of the snap with a quick and continuous motion. During this backward motion, the ball slips from his hands, and B54 recovers the loose ball at the B-29. What is the ruling?
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-29 - Snap.  Live-Ball Foul / Penalty Declined.
explanation: Because A58 began with a quick and continuous motion and the ball left his hand, this is a legal snap (2-23-1-a), but since the ball slipped from his hand, it became a backward pass and is in play (2-23-1-c). So, there is no foul, and B54 has legally recovered a backward pass (AR-2-23-1-II-a).

question_text: 3/G @ B-8. A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A23. He runs a sweep, dives toward the goal line, and lands in the end zone, but is ruled out of bounds at the B-3. Video shows that A23 did not step out of bounds, and while in the air lost control of the ball at the B-1 with the loose ball going into a pile of players in the end zone. There is no video evidence of clear recovery, but the Umpire verifies that Team A recovered the loose ball. What if there is no video evidence of clear recovery, but the Umpire verifies that Team B recovered the loose ball? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the ball placed where B recovered it in the end zone.
choice_c: A - 4/G @ B-1 - Ready, since without clear recovery the ball stays with Team A.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the ball advanced to the 25 as on a free-kick touchback.
explanation: As in the original question, this is reviewable (12-3-3-d / 12-3-1-a), and Replay may rely on confirmation of the recovery by the officials on the field (12-3-3-d-1). Since the Umpire verified that B recovered the loose ball in their end zone and A23's fumble put it there, it is a touchback (8-6-1-a).

question_text: 3/G @ B-8. A12 takes the snap and hands the ball to A23. He runs a sweep, dives toward the goal line, and lands in the end zone, but is ruled out of bounds at the B-3. Video shows that A23 did not step out of bounds, and while in the air lost control of the ball at the B-1 with the loose ball going into a pile of players in the end zone. There is no video evidence of clear recovery, but the Umpire verifies that Team A recovered the loose ball. What if there is no video evidence of clear recovery, and the Umpire can also not verify clear recovery? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the touchdown awarded since Team A last had the ball.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with a touchback awarded since recovery cannot be confirmed.
choice_d: A - 4/G @ B-3 - Ready, with the ball placed at the spot A23 was ruled out of bounds.
explanation: As in the original question, this is reviewable (12-3-3-d / 12-3-1-a), but if there is neither video evidence nor confirmation from the field of clear recovery, the ball will be placed at the spot of the fumble with Team A retaining possession (Extension of 2025 Replay Case Book Play #123, verified with National Replay Coordinator).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-34. The score is A-23, B-24 with the clock stopped at 0:08 in the 4th quarter and no time outs remaining for either team. A12 throws a pass to A11, the pass is complete, and he is tackled at the B-9 as time expires. Team B had 12 players participating during the play. What is the most likely result? What if the pass had fallen incomplete as time expired? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-29 - Ready (0:08), with the clock starting on the Ready after the reset.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-29 - Untimed, with the period extended for an untimed down.
choice_d: A - 3/4 @ B-34 - Snap (0:08), with no clock reset since the pass fell incomplete.
explanation: After the Two-Minute Timeout in either half, if the defense commits a substitution foul and 12 or more players are on the field and participate in a down, officials will penalize the defense for the foul and at the option of the offended team, reset the game clock back to the time displayed at the snap, with the clock restarting on the Snap (3-5-3-b-Penalty).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-34. The score is A-23, B-24 with the clock stopped at 0:08 in the 4th quarter and no time outs remaining for either team. A12 throws a pass to A11, the pass is complete, and he is tackled at the B-9 as time expires. Team B had 12 players participating during the play. What is the most likely result? What if, in the original question, the 12th B player was attempting to leave the field? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-29 - Snap (0:08), with the clock reset but no untimed down.
choice_b: Half Over, since the substitution foul does not extend the period.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-9 - Untimed, with the ball left at the end of the run.
explanation: If the 12th defender is attempting to exit but was still on the field at the snap and had no influence on the play, then the normal substitution penalty of five yards from the previous spot is enforced with no clock adjustment (3-5-3-b-Penalty). The period will be extended for an untimed down if the penalty is accepted for a live ball foul that occurs during a down in which time expires in the 2nd or 4th quarter. Team A may not decline the penalty, and thus must give up 20 yards in order to have one more play.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-40. A43 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up slot receiver A21. At the snap, both A43 and A21 drop back to potentially receive A1’s pass. The ball leaves A1’s hand at the A-37 and first touches A21’s hand at the A-38. After the catch, A21 rushes to the 50 where he is tackled. What if the ball left A1's hand at the A-38 and first touched A21's hand at the A-37? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/10 @ A-35 - Ready, with A21 penalized as an Ineligible Receiver Downfield.
choice_c: A - 4/10 @ A-35 - Ready, with Illegal Touching enforced on the backward pass.
choice_d: A - 3/12 @ A-33 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the touch.
explanation: While A2 is ineligible (7-3-3-c), A1's pass is backward, and any player may legally catch or recover a backward pass and advance.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-40. A43 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up slot receiver A21. At the snap, both A43 and A21 drop back to potentially receive A1’s pass. The ball leaves A1’s hand at the A-37 and first touches A21’s hand at the A-38. After the catch, A21 rushes to the 50 where he is tackled. What if, at the snap, both A43 and A21 went 10 yards downfield with A43 catching the pass at the 50 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/10 @ A-35 - Ready, with a loss of down for Illegal Touching added.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Running, with no foul since both receivers were downfield.
choice_d: A - 3/12 @ A-33 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul.
