explanation: As before, A21 is ineligible (7-3-3-c), and when he goes more than three yards beyond the neutral zone before a legal forward pass which crosses the neutral zone is in flight, he is an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-40 → A-35, with no loss of down (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 2/G @ B-16. A11 is hit in the backfield at the B-20 by B24, whose helmet comes off. B24 puts the helmet back on, pursues A11, and assists in the tackle at the B-2. What if B24's helmet came off when he first contacted A11 at the B-20, and B24 then immediately took A11 to the ground at the B-22? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready, with a personal foul on B24 for continuing after losing his helmet.
choice_b: A - 3/G @ B-22 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the play.
choice_d: A - 3/G @ B-20 - Ready, with the ball spotted where B24 first contacted A11.
explanation: Even after B24 loses his helmet, he is allowed to participate in the immediate continuing action in which he is engaged (9-1-17). There is no foul, but the game clock will be stopped, the play clock set to 40 seconds (3-2-4-c-13), and both will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-16).

question_text: 2/G @ B-16. A11 is hit in the backfield at the B-20 by B24, whose helmet comes off. B24 puts the helmet back on, pursues A11, and assists in the tackle at the B-2. What if, when B24 hit A11 at the B-20, A11's helmet came off? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/G @ B-20 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the helmet came off.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-16 - Ready, with the down replayed from the previous spot.
choice_c: A - 3/G @ B-22 - Ready, with the ball placed where A11 was contacted rather than the dead-ball spot.
explanation: The ball becomes dead when a ball carrier's helmet comes completely off (4-1-3-q). The game clock will be stopped, the play clock set to 25 seconds (3-2-4-c-13), and both will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-16).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B23 catches A4's kick at the B-10 and runs to the B-40 where he is downed. During the run, B32 blocked A37 in the back at the B-30. Video shows that B43 gave an invalid fair catch signal while the kick was in flight. What if B32's foul had been an Illegal Block Below the waist? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the block-below-waist penalty declined as a dead-ball foul.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the ball advanced to the 25 as on a fair catch.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the end of B23's run.
explanation: The receiving team advancing after a fair catch signal is reviewable (12-3-4-g), and Replay will bring the ball back to the spot of the catch, the B-10 (6-5-3-a). The penalties for personal fouls and unsportsmanlike conduct fouls are always enforced, regardless of the outcome of a replay review (12-3-6-j), so B32's foul becomes a dead ball foul and the penalty is enforced half-the-distance from the succeeding spot, B-10 → B-5 (10-2-2-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B23 catches A4's kick at the B-10 and runs to the B-40 where he is downed. During the run, B32 blocked A37 in the back at the B-30. Video shows that B43 gave an invalid fair catch signal while the kick was in flight. What if, in the original question, it had been B23 who gave the invalid fair catch signal? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot of the catch.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap, with a block-in-the-back penalty enforced from the catch spot.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the return allowed to stand since the signal was invalid.
explanation: The receiving team advancing after a fair catch signal is reviewable (12-3-4-g), and since the receiver who gave the invalid fair catch signal also caught the kick behind the B-25, Replay will bring the ball back to the B-25 (6-5-3-a-Exception). Fouls that carry 5-yard and 10-yard penalties do not have those penalties enforced if the ruling is overturned and they become dead ball fouls (12-3-6-i).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A90 executes an onside kick toward the sideline. A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. He leaps from out of bounds, and while airborne at the A-47, bats the ball, and it rolls forward with A88 recovering at the A-49. What if A33 re-established inbounds, then batted the ball at the A-47 with A88 recovering at the A-49? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, with only the five-yard out-of-bounds penalty enforced.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-49 - Snap, with Team B taking the ball at the spot of A88's recovery.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-25 - F/K Rules, with A33 also penalized for Illegal Touching.
explanation: There is no Illegal Touching because the ball had already gone 10 yards (6-1-3-a-2), and Team A is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (6-1-6-a). Still, A33 committed two fouls: Kicking Team Player Out of Bounds (6-1-11) and Illegal Batting (9-4-1-c). Team B will decline the five-yard penalty for the former and accept the 10-yard penalty for the latter, which must be enforced from the previous spot, A-35 → A-25 (10-2-2-d-4-a) since the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B (6-1-8).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A90 executes an onside kick toward the sideline. A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. He leaps from out of bounds, and while airborne at the A-47, bats the ball, and it rolls forward with A88 recovering at the A-49. What if A33 re-established inbounds, then batted the ball at the A-47 with B21 recovering at the A-49? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-25 - F/K Rules, with the Illegal Batting penalty enforced from the previous spot and a re-kick.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-44 - Snap, with the ball placed where A33 batted it.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-49 - Snap, with the ball left at the spot of B21's recovery.
explanation: A33 committed two fouls: Kicking Team Player Out of Bounds (6-1-11) and Illegal Batting (9-4-1-c). Team B will decline the five-yard penalty for the former and accept the 10-yard penalty for the latter. The penalty will be enforced from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them, A-49 → A-39 (6-1-8).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B10 intercepts A12’s legal forward pass at the B-6 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he downs the ball. What if B10 was attempting to run out of the end zone, but A82 brought him down by the face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_a: 0 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
choice_b: 0 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock, with the Face Mask penalty enforced on the succeeding play.
choice_c: 2 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with the safety standing despite the foul.
choice_d: 0 Points For A. Game Over, since a foul during a safety ends Team A's possession.
explanation: As in the original question, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a), but a score by a team committing a foul during the down is canceled (3-1-3-g-2). Since a Face Mask foul is simply a personal foul and not a FUDL foul, the penalty for A82's foul is declined by rule (3-1-3-g-1), and B will begin their possession at the 25-yard line on the same end of the field (3-1-3-e).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series of the first extra period. B10 intercepts A12’s legal forward pass at the B-6 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he downs the ball. What if B10 was attempting to run out of the end zone, but A82 brought him down with a crown of the helmet hit? What is the ruling?
choice_a: 0 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with the Targeting penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: 0 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock.
choice_c: 2 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock, with the safety standing since the foul was flagrant.
choice_d: 0 Points For A. B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with A82 disqualified but the penalty not carried over.
explanation: As in the original question, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a), but a score by a team committing a foul during the down is canceled (3-1-3-g-2). Since Targeting is a Flagrant foul (one of the FUDL fouls), the penalty for A82's foul is enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-1-Exception), and B will begin their possession at the 25 → 12½-yard line on the same end of the field (3-1-3-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-25. A12 is in shotgun formation with the game clock running. Center B58 legally starts the backward motion of the snap with a quick and continuous motion. During this backward motion, the ball slips from his hands, and B54 recovers the loose ball at the B-29. What if A67 recovered the loose ball at the B-29? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-29 - Snap, with Team B awarded the ball at the recovery spot.
choice_b: A - 3/10 @ B-30 - Ready, with an Illegal Snap penalty enforced.
choice_d: A - 4/9 @ B-29 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the recovery.
explanation: Because A58 began with a quick and continuous motion and the ball left his hand, this is a legal snap (2-23-1-a), but since the ball slipped from his hand, it became a backward pass and is in play (2-23-1-c). So, there is no foul, and A67 has legally recovered a backward pass (AR-2-23-1-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-25. A12 is in shotgun formation with the game clock running. Center B58 legally starts the backward motion of the snap with a quick and continuous motion. During this backward motion, the ball slips from his hands, and B54 recovers the loose ball at the B-29. What if A58 clutches and pauses, after which the ball slips from his hand and is recovered by B54? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-29 - Snap, with B54's recovery of the loose ball allowed to stand.
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ B-25 - Ready, with no foul since the ball left A58's hand.
choice_c: A - 3/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the illegal snap.
explanation: A58's snap is illegal because it did not leave his hand in a quick and continuous motion (2-23-1-a), and this a dead-ball foul with a five-yard penalty (7-1-1-a-Penalty). Any action or motion by the snapper such as not releasing the ball, pausing, restarting, or breaking the continuous motion of the ball is considered an Illegal Snap (AR 2-23-1-II-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A11 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and catches the pass and is downed after having advanced to the A-2. What is the best option available to Team B?
explanation: A70 is guilty of Illegal Touching because he touched a legal forward pass before it touched an opponent or an official (7-3-11), and the penalty for this foul is five yards (half-the-distance) from the previous spot, A-6 → A-3, with a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). Team B would elect to decline the penalty (10-1-1-b) and leave the ball at the A-2.

question_text: Try @ B-3. B21 intercepts A11's legal forward pass in the end zone and runs it back to midfield where B21 is tackled. During the return of the interception, B50 blocks an opponent below the waist in Team B's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Points For Team A.  A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock.
choice_b: No Points For Team A.  A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: No Points For Team A.  A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: Two Points For Team A.  A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
explanation: Penalties for fouls by either team during a Try after a change of team possession are declined by rule (8-3-4-a). So, there can be no Safety by penalty (8-5-1-b), and the penalty will not carry over to the succeeding kickoff because an Illegal Block Below the Waist is a not a FUDL foul (8-3-4-a-Exception); i.e., not a Flagrant personal foul.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. Time expires in the first half on a play in which QB A11 is at the B-13 when he throws and completes a pass to A88 in Team B's end zone. What is the best option available to Team B?
explanation: A11's pass is illegal because it was thrown by a player whose entire body is beyond the neutral zone when the ball is released (7-3-2-a). The penalty is five yards from the spot of foul, B-13 → B-18, and a loss of down which would bring up A - 3/13 @ B-18 (7-3-2-a-Penalty). Time expired during the down, but at Team B's option, the period will not be extended since the statement of includes loss of down (3-2-3-a-1-Exception). The half will be over.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. QB A11 takes the snap and rolls to the right. Finding no receiver open, A11 turns upfield and runs. After making the line to gain, A11 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet-first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown. What is the ruling?
explanation: Any time a ball carrier simulates or fakes a feet-first slide, the ball should be declared dead by the on-field officials at that point (4-1-3-r). The officials here allowed the play to continue, but after consultation should bring the ball back to the B-27. There is no foul, and properly officiated the ball would have declared dead immediately and the clock would have continued to run. In this case, the clock should start on the Ready after the officials time out (3-3-2-e-12) because there never really was a Touchdown.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B25 catches the kick in the end zone and attempts to advance. B25 is hit and fumbles at the B-7, and the ball rolls to the B-4 where B34 recovers. His momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled by the facemask. What is the ruling?
explanation: Momentum applies only to the recovery of an opponent's fumble (8-5-1-a-Exception a), so the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). Since the run ended in the end zone after a change of team possession in the end zone and the result of the play was not a touchback, the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-c). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, GL → B-15 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-28. QB A11 completes a pass to A88 at the B-4. As A88 is trying to get into the end zone, he is hit at the B-2 and fumbles the ball into the end zone where B33 recovers while grounded. B99 is called for Roughing the Passer. What is the ruling?
explanation: "Tack-on" enforcement does not apply because there was a change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). The penalty is enforced half-the-distance from the basic spot, B-28 → B-14, and the clock will start on the snap because the result of the play was a Touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: 2/9 @ A-30. A25 runs to the B-34 where he fumbles. B53 recovers and advances for a touchdown. Prior to the fumble and during A25’s run, A63 blocked B52 below the waist at the A-36. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The penalty for A63's foul is declined by rule.
choice_b: The penalty for A63's foul may be enforced on the Try.
choice_c: Both B and C are options.
choice_d: The penalty for A63's foul may be enforced on the KO.
explanation: Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown may be enforced on the Try or the succeeding kickoff, at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A24 carries to the B-11, where the ruling on the field is that he fumbled with B31 recovering near the sideline at the B-8 (0:42). Video shows that B31 had a toe on the sideline when he first touched the loose ball (0:44). What is the ruling?
explanation: When the loose ball touched B31 who was out of bounds, the ball was out of bounds (4-2-3-a) and the clock should have stopped at 0:44. Replay is therefore reversing to a stopped clock, and the clock will be reset to 0:44 (12-3-6-b) and there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). This reversal creates a forward fumble out of bounds, so the ball will be placed at the spot of the fumble, the B-11 (7-2-4-b-1), and the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A11 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and catches the pass and is downed after having advanced to the A-2. What is the best option available to Team B? What if there had been no eligible Team A receiver near A70 when he caught A11's pass from the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ A-3 - Ready, with a loss-of-down Illegal Touching penalty instead of a safety.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-2 - Running, with the pass ruled complete to A70.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with A70 also penalized for Illegal Touching.
explanation: This is a foul for an Illegal Forward Pass because A11 has thrown into an area where there is no eligible Team A receiver (7-3-2-h). A70 is not guilty of Illegal Touching because this is not a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty for the Illegal Forward Pass is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h), which results in a Safety (8-5-1-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A11 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and catches the pass and is downed after having advanced to the A-2. What is the best option available to Team B? What if, in the original question, B96 deflected the ball into A70's hands, and he was downed at the A-16? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/13 @ A-3 - Ready, with an Illegal Touching penalty on A70.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since A70 touched the pass in his end zone.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-16 - Snap, with the clock stopped after the completion.
explanation: There is no foul for Illegal Touching because the legal forward pass was touched by an opponent before it was touched by A70 (7-3-11), making all players eligible (7-3-5). The clock would continue to run.

question_text: Try @ B-3. B21 intercepts A11's legal forward pass in the end zone and runs it back to midfield where B21 is tackled. During the return of the interception, B50 blocks an opponent below the waist in Team B's end zone. What if B50's foul had been an Illegal Blind Side Block with Targeting, confirmed by Replay. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Points For Team A. A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_b: Two Points For Team A. A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, with the score standing despite the Targeting.
choice_c: No Points For Team A. A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: No Points For Team A. A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with the down replayed after the flagrant foul.
explanation: Penalties for FUDL fouls by either team during a Try after a change of possession are enforced on the succeeding kickoff. Since Targeting is a Flagrant personal foul (2-10-3), the penalty for B50's foul will be enforced, A-35 → 50.

question_text: Try @ B-3. B21 intercepts A11's legal forward pass in the end zone and runs it back to midfield where B21 is tackled. During the return of the interception, B50 blocks an opponent below the waist in Team B's end zone. What if, in addition to B50's Blind Side Block with Targeting, B96 had been lined up in the neutral zone at the Snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Points For Team A. A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, with the Targeting penalty enforced on the succeeding kickoff.
choice_b: Two Points For Team A. A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the score counting and the Targeting carried over.
choice_c: No Points For Team A. A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with both penalties enforced together.
choice_d: No Points For Team A. A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock.
explanation: When there are two or more live-ball fouls by the same team, the offended team may elect only one of these penalties (10-1-3). Team A will accept the penalty for B96's offside foul, B-3 → B-1½, in order to have a repeat of the down, and Team A may designate the lateral position (8-3-3-b-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. Time expires in the first half on a play in which QB A11 is at the B-13 when he throws and completes a pass to A88 in Team B's end zone. What is the best option available to Team B? What if A11's pass was complete to A88 in Team B's end zone with 0:02 remaining on the clock? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/13 @ B-18 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready after the penalty.
choice_c: Half Over, since the illegal forward pass carries a loss of down and the period is not extended.
choice_d: A - 3/8 @ B-13 - Snap, with the completion counting and no penalty enforced.
explanation: The penalty for A11's Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-a) is enforced five yards from the spot of the foul, B-13 → B-18, with a loss of down (7-3-2-a-Penalty). The clock will start on the Snap because the result of the play was a Touchdown (3-3-2-c). There is no runoff option because the illegal forward pass was complete (3-4-4-a-3).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. Time expires in the first half on a play in which QB A11 is at the B-13 when he throws and completes a pass to A88 in Team B's end zone. What is the best option available to Team B? What if A11 ran to the B-13, then retreated to the B-16, and his pass to diving A88 was complete at the B-1 (0:02)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/16 @ B-21 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the completion.
choice_c: Half Over, since the period is not extended when the penalty includes a loss of down.
choice_d: A - 3/8 @ B-13 - Ready, with no foul since A11 threw from behind the neutral zone.
explanation: A11's pass is illegal because it was thrown from behind the neutral zone after his entire body and the ball had been beyond the neutral zone (7-3-2-e). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, B-16 → B-21, plus a loss of down (7-3-2-e-Penalty). The clock will start on the Ready because it was stopped to complete a penalty (3-3-2-e-4). There is no runoff option because the illegal forward pass was complete (3-4-4-a-3). Team A may not spike the ball because there are less than three seconds and the clock will start on the Ready (3-2-5).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. QB A11 takes the snap and rolls to the right. Finding no receiver open, A11 turns upfield and runs. After making the line to gain, A11 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet-first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown. What if A11 broke stride as if he were going to slide feet-first at the B-36, but stayed upright until tackled at the B-27? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the touchdown awarded since A11 stayed upright.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-36 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the fake slide.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-27 - Ready, with the ball spotted where A11 was tackled.
explanation: After consultation, the officials should bring the ball back to the point of the fake slide, the B-36 (4-1-3-r), which is short of the line to gain. The clock should start on the Ready after the officials time out (3-3-2-e-12) because there never really was a Touchdown.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. QB A11 takes the snap and rolls to the right. Finding no receiver open, A11 turns upfield and runs. After making the line to gain, A11 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet-first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown. What if, in the original question, the on-field officials never reacted to the fake slide, but Replay had video evidence? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-36 - Ready, with Replay bringing the ball back to the fake-slide spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-27 - Ready, with the ball spotted where A11 was tackled.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with a 10-second runoff applied for the fake slide.
explanation: Although Replay might be called upon via O2O in (4) and (4a) to help identify the dead-ball spot, a fake-slide is not reviewable under Rule 12-3-3 via a replay stoppage (AR 4-1-3-IV).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B25 catches the kick in the end zone and attempts to advance. B25 is hit and fumbles at the B-7, and the ball rolls to the B-4 where B34 recovers. His momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled by the facemask. What if B25 caught the kick in the EZ, never leaving the EZ or fumbling, before being tackled by the face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the basic spot at the succeeding spot but no penalty added.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, with the basic spot the momentum spot at the B-4.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since B25 was tackled in the end zone.
explanation: The result of the play was a Touchback (8-6-1-b), and since the run ended in the end zone after a change of team possession in the end zone and the result of the play was a touchback, the basic spot is the succeeding spot (10-2-2-d-2-a), which on this free kick would be the B-25 (8-6-2). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-25 → B-40 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. B25 catches the kick in the end zone and attempts to advance. B25 is hit and fumbles at the B-7, and the ball rolls to the B-4 where B34 recovers. His momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled by the facemask. What if B25 caught the kick at the B-4 and his momentum carried him into the EZ to be tackled by the face mask? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the basic spot the succeeding spot since it was a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the basic spot the goal line rather than the momentum spot.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since the momentum exception does not apply on a free kick.
explanation: The Momentum Exception applies (8-5-1-a), and since the run ended in the end zone after a change of team possession in the end zone and Momentum applies, the basic spot is the momentum spot, the B-4 (10-2-2-d-2-b-Exception). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-4 → B-19 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-28. QB A11 completes a pass to A88 at the B-4. As A88 is trying to get into the end zone, he is hit at the B-2 and fumbles the ball into the end zone where B33 recovers while grounded. B99 is called for Roughing the Passer. What if A88's fumble rolled into the end zone and out of bounds? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready after the touchback.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with a touchback and no tack-on since possession changed.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap, with the penalty enforced the full distance from the fumble spot.
explanation: While the result of the play is a Touchback (8-6-1-b), "tack-on" enforcement still applies because there was no change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e). The end of the last run is the spot of A88's fumble, the B-2 (2-25-8-b), and the penalty is enforced half-the-distance from there, B-2 → B-1. The clock will start on the Snap because the result of the play was a Touchback (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-28. QB A11 completes a pass to A88 at the B-4. As A88 is trying to get into the end zone, he is hit at the B-2 and fumbles the ball into the end zone where B33 recovers while grounded. B99 is called for Roughing the Passer. What if the hit caused A88's fumble to go backwards where A42 recovered at the B-6? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the recovery.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-1 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the fumble spot rather than the recovery spot.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-14 - Ready, with tack-on enforcement unavailable after the fumble.
explanation: "Tack-on" enforcement applies because there was no change of possession during the down (10-2-2-e). The end of the last run is the spot A42's recovery, the B-6 (2-25-8-a), and the penalty is enforced half-the- distance from there, B-6 → B-3. The clock will start on the Ready because the clock was stopped only to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 2/9 @ A-30. A25 runs to the B-34 where he fumbles. B53 recovers and advances for a touchdown. Prior to the fumble and during A25’s run, A63 blocked B52 below the waist at the A-36. What if, prior to the fumble and during A25's run, A63 blocked B52 in the back above the waist at the A-36. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with the penalty for A63's block enforced on Team B's Try.
choice_c: A - 2/9 @ A-30 - Snap, with the touchdown negated and Team A retaining the ball.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, with A63's block declined and no score.
explanation: Five- and 10-yard penalties for fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are not enforced on the Try or succeeding kickoff. Such penalties are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down (10-2-5-a-2).

question_text: 2/9 @ A-30. A25 runs to the B-34 where he fumbles. B53 recovers and advances for a touchdown. Prior to the fumble and during A25’s run, A63 blocked B52 below the waist at the A-36. What if, in the original question, B53 recovered and advance to the A-30 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/9 @ A-30 - Snap, with Team A retaining the ball after the accepted penalty.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with A63's below-the-waist penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_d: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with B53's touchdown standing.
explanation: Team B will decline the penalty (10-1-1-b) because if the penalty is accepted for a foul that occurs before a change of possession (with some exceptions -- notably Rule 10-2-5-a-1 from question 7), the ball will still belong to Team A after enforcement (5-2-3-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A24 carries to the B-11, where the ruling on the field is that he fumbled with B31 recovering near the sideline at the B-8 (0:42). Video shows that B31 had a toe on the sideline when he first touched the loose ball (0:44). What if video showed that B31 had a toe on the sideline when he first touched the loose ball at the B-14 (0:44)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/4 @ B-14 - Ready (0:44), with the clock starting on the Ready after review.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-11 - Snap (0:44), with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-14 - Snap (0:44), with Team B awarded the ball at the recovery spot.
explanation: The loose ball was dead when it touched B31 who was out of bounds (4-2-3-a), and the clock should have stopped at 0:44. Replay is therefore reversing to a stopped clock, and the clock will be reset to 0:44 (12-3-6-b) and there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). This reversal creates a backward fumble out of bounds, so the ball remains at the out-of-bounds spot, the B-14 (7-2-4-b-2), and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A24 carries to the B-11, where the ruling on the field is that he fumbled with B31 recovering near the sideline at the B-8 (0:42). Video shows that B31 had a toe on the sideline when he first touched the loose ball (0:44). What if video showed that A24 was down at the B-11 (0:46) prior to the ball coming loose? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-11 - Snap (0:44), with the clock starting on the snap and no runoff.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-11 - Ready (0:46), with the clock reset but no runoff applied.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-8 - Snap (0:44), with the ruling on the field of a fumble standing.
explanation: Properly officiated the ball would have been declared dead when A24 was down at the B-11, and the clock would have continued to run. Replay is therefore reversing to a running clock, and the there is a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). Reset the clock to time the ball was dead by rule (0:46) and implement the runoff, 0:46 → 0:36. The clock will start on the Ready (12-3-6-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and Team B has no timeouts remaining. A1 is tackled for no gain. B96 was in the neutral zone at the snap, and B78 was injured on the play. What is the ruling?
explanation: There is no option for a 10-second runoff for B78's injury because the clock was also stopped to complete the penalty for B96's foul (3-3-6-f). The play clock is set to 40 seconds for an injury to a player of the defensive team (3-3-6-f-1) except after a charged team timeout or when the game is stopped for the Two-Minute Timeout (3-2-4-c-5).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the third extra period. A34 receives the snap, runs to his right, and as he dives for the goal line he fumbles the ball (short of the goal line) into the end zone. B76 recovers the loose ball and advances to the B-13 where he fumbles. A48 recovers and carries into B's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No points for Team A.  The ball is dead when recovered by B76.
choice_c: No points for Team A.  The ball is dead when recovered by A48.
explanation: The Try ends when a Team A player fumbles and the ball is caught or recovered by any Team A player other than the fumbler (8-3-2-d-5), but a Team B fumble may be recovered and advanced by any player (7-2-2-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. B19 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. From there, he fumbles the ball forward into the ground and into the field of play. At the B-5, A55 bats the ball back into the end zone to prevent B42 from recovering. The ball rolls over the end line. What is the ruling?
explanation: A55's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c). The initial impetus of B19's fumble is considered expended and the responsibility for the ball's progress charged to A55 when he bats the ball after it strikes the ground (8-7-2-b-1). So, since Team A put the ball into B's end zone where it became dead, the result of the play is a Touchback (8-6-1-a). Because the related run ended in the end zone, the foul occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, and the result of the play was a Touchback, the basic spot is the succeeding spot (10-2-2-d-2-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-20 → B-30 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. A punts, and during the kick, B1 holds at the B-25. B2 catches the punt at the B-4 and his momentum takes him into the EZ where he fumbles the ball forward. It is recovered by B44 at the B-8. What is the ruling?
explanation: B1's foul is governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), and the PSK spot is the end of the kick, the B-4 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, the penalty is enforced half-the-distance from the basic spot, B-4 → B-2 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 3/12 @ B-21. The score is A-17, B-24 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. QB A12 drops back to pass and while inside the tackle box throws a pass from the B-27 which A58 touches before it lands in an area where there are no eligible receivers (0:14). What is the most likely result?
explanation: A12 has thrown an Illegal Forward Pass from inside the tackle box into an area where there is no eligible Team A receiver (7-3-2-h). Though A58 is ineligible (7-3-3), he is not guilty of Illegal Touching because this is not a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty for A12's foul is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-27 (7-3-2-h-Penalty). This incomplete illegal forward pass carries with it an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3), which Team B will accept, 0:14 → 0:04, with the clock starting on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/3 @ B-30. QB A12’s forward pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-1. B19 returns the interception to the B-4 where he fumbles. B35 recovers the fumble at the B-2 and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B35 runs around in his end zone where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The momentum exception applies only to the recovery of an opponent's fumble -- not a teammate's fumble (8-5-1-a-Exception a) -- so Team B is responsible for the ball being in their end zone where it was declared dead. The result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The score is A-9, B-14 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter and no time outs for Team A. QB A11 scrambles outside the tackle box. Finding no receiver open, to conserve time, he throws a backwards pass from the B-16 which goes out of bounds at the B-18 (0:11). What is the most likely result?
explanation: It is a foul to intentionally thrown a backward pass out of bounds to conserve time (7-2-1), and the penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, B-16 → B-21, and a loss of down (7-2-1-Penalty). This foul also carries with it an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-4), which Team B will accept, 0:11 → 0:01, with the clock starting on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-5. Punter A88 punts the ball and it is low, hits B45 at the A-9 and the ball bounces back into A’s end zone where A32 recovers the loose ball and carries it out of bounds at the A-10. What is the ruling?
explanation: If a player of the kicking team catches or recovers a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone, the ball becomes dead (6-3-6-a). Thus, Team A is in possession of a dead ball in their end zone, and their kick provided the impetus which put it there (8-7-2-a). The result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and Team B has no timeouts remaining. A1 is tackled for no gain. B96 was in the neutral zone at the snap, and B78 was injured on the play. What if there was no foul by B96, but his helmet came off during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_b: No 10-second runoff option, since the injury was not the only reason for stopping the clock.
choice_c: Option for a 10-second runoff, with a 25-second play clock.
choice_d: No 10-second runoff option, with a 40-second play clock.
explanation: 40-second play clock. If the player injury is the only reason for stopping the clock -- other than that player's helmet or a teammate's helmet coming off -- the opponent has an option of a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The play clock is set to 40 seconds for an injury to a player of the defensive team (3-3-6-f-1).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and Team B has no timeouts remaining. A1 is tackled for no gain. B96 was in the neutral zone at the snap, and B78 was injured on the play. What if there was no foul by B96, but his helmet came off during the down, and Team B called a time out? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Option for a 10-second runoff, since the injury stopped the clock.
choice_c: No 10-second runoff, with a 40-second play clock.
choice_d: Option for a 10-second runoff, with a 25-second play clock following the timeout.
explanation: 25-second play clock. If the player injury is the only reason for stopping the clock -- other than that player's helmet or a teammate's helmet coming off -- the opponent has an option of a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f), but Team B will avoid the runoff through their use of a timeout (3-3-6-f-3). The play clock is set to 25 seconds because of the timeout (3-2-4-c-2 / 3-2-4-c-5). The time out allows B96 to remain in the game (3-3-10-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the third extra period. A34 receives the snap, runs to his right, and as he dives for the goal line he fumbles the ball (short of the goal line) into the end zone. B76 recovers the loose ball and advances to the B-13 where he fumbles. A48 recovers and carries into B's end zone. What if -- during the last run -- A48 fumbles at the B-1 with A21 recovering in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Two points for Team A, since A21 recovered the fumble in the end zone.
choice_b: Six points for Team A, with the recovery ruled a touchdown.
choice_d: No points for Team A, with Team B awarded the ball at the spot of recovery.
explanation: The Try end and there is no Team A score whenever a Team A player fumbles and the ball is recovered by any Team A player other than the fumbler (8-3-2-d-5).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-14, B-14 in the first possession series of the third extra period. A34 receives the snap, runs to his right, and as he dives for the goal line he fumbles the ball (short of the goal line) into the end zone. B76 recovers the loose ball and advances to the B-13 where he fumbles. A48 recovers and carries into B's end zone. What if the situation described in (2a) occurred on 4/G @ B-3 during regulation, rather than a Try in extra periods? What is the ruling?
choice_a: No points for Team A, since a Team A player other than the fumbler recovered.
choice_b: Two points for Team A, with the recovery counting only as a Try.
choice_c: Six points for Team A, only if A48 recovered behind the fumble spot.
explanation: On fourth down before a change of team possession, when a Team A fumble is caught or recovered by a Team A player other than the fumbler, the ball is dead (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). Since this fumble occurs after a change of team possession, the fourth down fumble rule does not apply, and any player may recover (7-2-2-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. B19 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. From there, he fumbles the ball forward into the ground and into the field of play. At the B-5, A55 bats the ball back into the end zone to prevent B42 from recovering. The ball rolls over the end line. What if B19's fumble was in flight when A55 batted it? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the basic spot the succeeding spot for a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the ball placed at the previous spot.
choice_d: Touchback. B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, since A55's bat sends the ball out of the end zone.
explanation: A55's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c). The initial impetus of B19's fumble is still responsible for the ball's progress in any direction because a bat cannot add new impetus to a loose ball in flight (8-7-2-b-1). So, since Team B put the ball into their own end zone where it became dead, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). Because the related run ended in the end zone, the foul occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, and the result of the play was not a Touchback, the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-c). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, GL → B-10 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. B19 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. From there, he fumbles the ball forward into the ground and into the field of play. At the B-5, A55 bats the ball back into the end zone to prevent B42 from recovering. The ball rolls over the end line. What if A55's bat of the grounded fumble went out of bounds at the B-1? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the basic spot the goal line since the result was a safety.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the ball placed at the previous spot with no penalty.
choice_d: Safety. 2 Points for A, since A55's bat provides new impetus into the end zone.
explanation: A55's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c). The loose ball retains its status as a fumble despite the bat (8-7-2-c), so when the loose ball goes out of bounds in the field of play beyond the spot of the fumble, it is returned to the spot of the fumble "as if" it never left the end zone (7-2-4-b-1). So, the result of the play is a Touchback (8-6-1-a). Because the related run ended in the end zone, the foul occurred after a change of team possession in the end zone, and the result of the play was a Touchback, the basic spot is the succeeding spot (10-2-2-d-2-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-20 → B-30 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. A punts, and during the kick, B1 holds at the B-25. B2 catches the punt at the B-4 and his momentum takes him into the EZ where he fumbles the ball forward. It is recovered by B44 at the B-8. What if A22 recovered the fumble at the B-8? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the PSK penalty enforced against Team B.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-2 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the PSK spot instead of declined.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-8 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the recovery.
explanation: The penalty -- if accepted -- would be enforced from the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a) since Team A would next put the ball into play (10-2-3-b-4). Team A will decline the penalty and accept the ball at the spot of their recovery, the B-8 (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. A punts, and during the kick, B1 holds at the B-25. B2 catches the punt at the B-4 and his momentum takes him into the EZ where he fumbles the ball forward. It is recovered by B44 at the B-8. What if B2 caught the punt in the end zone, then fumbled with B44 recovering at the B-8? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the PSK spot rather than declined.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the half-the-distance penalty accepted.
choice_c: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since B2's momentum carried into the end zone.
explanation: B1's foul is governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b), and since the kick ended in B's end zone, the PSK spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-b). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, the penalty -- if accepted -- would be enforced from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-1). It would be more advantageous for Team A to decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 3/12 @ B-21. The score is A-17, B-24 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. QB A12 drops back to pass and while inside the tackle box throws a pass from the B-27 which A58 touches before it lands in an area where there are no eligible receivers (0:14). What is the most likely result? What if A58 caught the ball with no eligible in the area and went to the ground (0:14)? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/18 @ B-27 - Snap (0:14), with the clock starting on the snap after the completion.
choice_c: A - 4/17 @ B-26 - Ready (0:04), with a 10-second runoff applied.
choice_d: A - 4/18 @ B-27 - Ready (0:04), with a runoff since the pass was incomplete.
explanation: A12 has thrown an Illegal Forward Pass from inside the tackle box into an area where there is no eligible Team A receiver (7-3-2-h). Though A58 is ineligible (7-3-3), he is not guilty of Illegal Touching because this is not a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty for A12's foul is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-27 (7-3-2-h-Penalty). Since this complete illegal forward pass did not cause clock to stop immediately, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a).

question_text: 3/12 @ B-21. The score is A-17, B-24 late in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. QB A12 drops back to pass and while inside the tackle box throws a pass from the B-27 which A58 touches before it lands in an area where there are no eligible receivers (0:14). What is the most likely result? What if A58 caught the ball with eligible A19 nearby and went to the ground (0:14)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/18 @ B-27 - Ready (0:14), with the illegal-forward-pass loss of down at the spot of the foul.
choice_c: A - 4/17 @ B-26 - Snap (0:14), with the clock starting on the snap.
choice_d: A - 4/17 @ B-26 - Ready (0:04), with a 10-second runoff applied to the illegal touching.
explanation: A58 is ineligible (7-3-3), and therefore guilty of Illegal Touching of this legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-21 → B-26, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). Since this foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a).

question_text: 2/3 @ B-30. QB A12’s forward pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-1. B19 returns the interception to the B-4 where he fumbles. B35 recovers the fumble at the B-2 and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B35 runs around in his end zone where he is tackled. What if B19's fumble at the B-4 had been caused by A82's face mask foul? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-4 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot of B19's fumble with no penalty.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the basic spot the goal line.
choice_d: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since B35's momentum carried into the end zone.
explanation: The basic spot is the end of B19's run, the B-4 (10-2-2-d-1-c). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, B-4 → B-19 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/3 @ B-30. QB A12’s forward pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-1. B19 returns the interception to the B-4 where he fumbles. B35 recovers the fumble at the B-2 and his momentum takes him into the end zone. B35 runs around in his end zone where he is tackled. What if A82 had tackled B35 by the face mask in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-19 - Snap, with the basic spot the end of B19's run rather than the goal line.
choice_b: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since the ball became dead in the end zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot of B35's recovery.
explanation: Because the foul occurred after a change of possession in the field of play and the related run ended in the end zone, the basic spot is the goal line (10-2-2-d-2-b). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, GL → B-15 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The score is A-9, B-14 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter and no time outs for Team A. QB A11 scrambles outside the tackle box. Finding no receiver open, to conserve time, he throws a backwards pass from the B-16 which goes out of bounds at the B-18 (0:11). What is the most likely result? What if, in addition to A11's backward pass out of bounds, B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/11 @ B-21 - Ready (0:01), with a 10-second runoff applied to the backward pass.
choice_c: A - 2/5 @ B-15 - Ready (0:11), with the clock starting on the Ready after the offsetting fouls.
choice_d: A - 2/5 @ B-15 - Snap (0:01), with a runoff applied despite the offsetting fouls.
explanation: These fouls by A11 and B96 combine to make offsetting fouls, so the down is repeated (10-1-4). When there are offsetting fouls, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-e), and the clock will start on the Snap because of the backward pass out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The score is A-9, B-14 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter and no time outs for Team A. QB A11 scrambles outside the tackle box. Finding no receiver open, to conserve time, he throws a backwards pass from the B-16 which goes out of bounds at the B-18 (0:11). What is the most likely result? What if, in the original question, A11's pass went out bounds at the B-23? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/13 @ B-23 - Snap (0:11) only, with Team B declining the penalty.
choice_c: A - 3/11 @ B-21 - Ready (0:01) only, with no option to decline the runoff.
choice_d: A - 3/11 @ B-21 - Snap (0:11), with the penalty accepted but no runoff.
explanation: It may be more advantageous for Team B to accept the penalty and give up the two extra lost yards. If they accept the penalty, it will be enforced five yards from the spot of the foul, B-16 → B-21 (7-2-1-Penalty) and will include the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-4), which Team B will accept, 0:11 → 0:01, with the clock starting on the Ready (3-4-4-c). If they decline the penalty, the 10-second runoff is declined by rule (3-4-4-a), and the clock would start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-5. Punter A88 punts the ball and it is low, hits B45 at the A-9 and the ball bounces back into A’s end zone where A32 recovers the loose ball and carries it out of bounds at the A-10. What if A32 recovered the loose ball at the A-1? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety. 2 Points for B, since A32 recovered the kick in his own end zone.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-1 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the recovery.
choice_d: B - 1/G @ A-1 - Snap, with Team B awarded the ball since the kick crossed the neutral zone.
explanation: Since the scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone, the ball is dead when recovered by A32 (6-3-6-a), but since the kick touched B45, Team A is in legal possession of the ball (6-3-3) with a new series (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: 4/14 @ A-5. Punter A88 punts the ball and it is low, hits B45 at the A-9 and the ball bounces back into A’s end zone where A32 recovers the loose ball and carries it out of bounds at the A-10. What if the punt was blocked by B54 at the A-7, then recovered and advanced as in the original question? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since A32 recovered in his own end zone.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-10 - Snap, with Team A retaining the ball after the blocked kick.
choice_c: B - 1/G @ A-9 - Snap, with the ball placed at the spot where the kick was blocked.
explanation: The blocking of a scrimmage kick by an opponent of the kicking team who is not more than three yards beyond the neutral zone is considered to have occurred within or behind that zone (6-3-1-b), so this kick is considered to not have touched B54 beyond the neutral zone and, in fact, to not have even crossed the neutral zone (2-16-7-b-Exception). So, the ball remains in play when A32 recovers it (6-3-1-a), but when A32 does not reach the line to gain, the ball goes over to Team B (5-1-1-c).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-18. A99 receives the snap and attempts to punt the ball. A99 kicks the ball low and into the back of the punt shield where it rebounds back toward the punter. A99 picks the ball up at the A-2 and throws the ball out the back of the end zone. After the snap, B17 immediately grabbed A27 at the line of scrimmage and took him down to the ground. The Line Judge has a flag for holding by B17. What is the ruling?
explanation: A99's backward pass out of bounds is not a foul since he was not doing so to conserve time (7-2-1), but the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). For a variety of reasons (the foul did not occur during the kick, the kick did not cross the neutral zone, and Team B would not next put the ball into play), postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply (10-2-3-b), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-18 → A-28 (10-2-2-c-3) with an automatic first down (9-3-4-Penalty).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-42. Team A’s formation has five players (A8, A51, A26, A57, and A61) lined up to the left, outside the numbers, on the LOS with A20 positioned behind the linemen. A81 is set as the snapper on the right hash and A88 is on the LOS to his right. A99 is lined up 15 yards directly behind the snapper with two players in the punt shield. A99 receives the snap and passes the ball to A88. The catch is made behind the LOS and A88 runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation because it is not obvious that a kick will be attempted (2-16-10-a). This means that Team A may not take advantage of the numbering exception (7-1-4-a-5), and so they foul by not having five players numbered 50-79 on the line of scrimmage (7-1-4-a-3). The five-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot (7-1-4-Penalty), and the clock will start on the Ready because this was not a legal kick down (3-3-2-d-8 / 3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-43. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A29 runs a sweep to the left and is at the B-36 when he is hit and fumbles the ball. B99 gains control of the ball with his leg out of bounds at the B-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: When out of bounds B99 (4-2-1-a) touches the loose ball, then the ball is considered out of bounds (4-2-3-a), so this becomes a forward fumble out of bounds. The ball is returned to the spot of the fumble, the B-36 (7-2-4-b-1), and the clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-12. Snapped from the far hash, A7 completes a pass to A88 who is tackled at the B-7 on the near hash. B99 was in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the difference between the options to accept or decline?
choice_a: The ball will be spotted on different yard lines, and at different lateral positions.
choice_b: The ball will be spotted on different yard lines, but at the same lateral position.
choice_c: The ball will be spotted on the same yard line, and at the same lateral position.
choice_d: The ball will be spotted on the same yard line, but at different lateral positions.
explanation: If Team A declines the penalty, it will be A - 1/G @ B-7 - Near Hash. If Team A accepts the penalty, it will be enforced five yards from the previous spot, bringing up A - 1/G @ B-7 - Far Hash. Same yard line, different lateral positions.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10. A34 carries to the B-6 where he fumbles. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where B29 bats it across the end line. What is the ruling?
explanation: The result of the play is a Touchback because A34's fumble put the ball into B's end zone where it became dead (8-6-1-a); however, B29's bat -- while backward -- is illegal because it occurred in the end zone (9-4-1-c). The basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b), which is the spot of the fumble (2-25-8-b). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced half-the-distance from the basic spot, B-6 → B-3 (10-2-2-c-3). The clock starts on the snap because of the Touchback (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A23 bats A7's punt while it is in the air above B's end zone. B17 picks up the loose ball at the B-2 and runs to the B-10 where he is tackled. During the kick, B89 held at the B-12. What is the ruling?
explanation: A23's bat is legal (6-3-11), and all conditions of postscrimmage kick enforcement are satisfied (10-2-3-b) with a PSK spot at the B-20 (2-25-11-Special Case 2). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred behind the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the spot of the foul, B-12 → B-6 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 3/1 @ A-34. The score is A-28, B-24 late in the 4th quarter, and Team B has two timeouts remaining. A11 mishandles the snap, falls on the loose ball at the A-32, and Team B calls their second timeout (0:59). After the ready for play and with Team A in punt formation, B76 presents as injured. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-37 - Snap.  Team B is penalized for Delay of Game.
choice_b: A - 4/3 @ A-32 - Snap.  Team B is charged with their final timeout.
choice_c: A - 4/3 @ A-32 - Snap.  Officials timeout only.
explanation: If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted by officials, that team is charged a timeout or a delay penalty if all timeouts have been used (3-3-6-a-1). Under normal circumstances Team B would be charged with a timeout, but consecutive team timeouts are not allowed by the same team in any individual dead ball period (3-3-4), so Team B will be penalized for Delay of Game. (Note: AR 3-3-6-XII provides clarity for any potential conflict between 3-3-6-a-1 and 3-3-4). The five-yard penalty from the succeeding spot gives a new series to Team A.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A14 is ruled out of bounds at the B-25 (0:12). Video shows A14's knee was down inbounds with the ball at the B-26 (0:13). What is the ruling?
explanation: Replay will overturn to the runner down inbounds short of the line to gain, and since this reversal creates a running clock, there is a 10-second runoff which could only be avoided through a timeout (12-3-6-c). The clock will be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:13), and the runoff implemented, 0:13 → 0:03. The clock will start on the Ready.

question_text: 4/12 @ A-18. A99 receives the snap and attempts to punt the ball. A99 kicks the ball low and into the back of the punt shield where it rebounds back toward the punter. A99 picks the ball up at the A-2 and throws the ball out the back of the end zone. After the snap, B17 immediately grabbed A27 at the line of scrimmage and took him down to the ground. The Line Judge has a flag for holding by B17. What if A99's low kick was blocked by B78 at the A-20, then recovered by A99 at the A-2 and carried to the A-30? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-28 - Snap, with the ball placed at the previous spot after enforcement.
choice_c: A - 4/12 @ A-18 - Snap, with the holding penalty accepted and a re-kick.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, since B78's block of the kick gives Team B possession.
explanation: The blocking of a scrimmage kick by an opponent of the kicking team who is not more than three yards beyond the neutral zone is considered to have occurred within or behind that zone (6-3-1-b), so this kick is considered to not have touched B78 beyond the neutral zone and, in fact, to not have even crossed the neutral zone (2-16-7-b-Exception). So, the ball remains in play when A99 recovers it (6-3-1-a), and since he reached the line to gain, a new series is awarded to Team A (5-1-1-b). The penalty for B17's holding foul will be declined (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-18. A99 receives the snap and attempts to punt the ball. A99 kicks the ball low and into the back of the punt shield where it rebounds back toward the punter. A99 picks the ball up at the A-2 and throws the ball out the back of the end zone. After the snap, B17 immediately grabbed A27 at the line of scrimmage and took him down to the ground. The Line Judge has a flag for holding by B17. What if A99's grounded kick touched B78 at the A-20, then was recovered by A99 at the A-2 and carried to the A-30? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-2 - Snap, with Team A declining the penalty and keeping the ball at the recovery spot.
choice_c: A - 4/12 @ A-18 - Snap, with a re-kick after the accepted holding penalty.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-28 - Snap, since B78's touching beyond the neutral zone gives Team B the ball.
explanation: Since the scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone, the ball is dead when recovered by A99 at the A-2 (6-3-6-a), but since the kick touched B78, Team A is in legal possession of the ball (6-3-3) with a new series (5-1-1-e-2), A - 1/10 @ A-2. They will elect to accept the penalty for B17's holding foul, enforced from the previous spot, A-18 → A-28 (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 4/8 @ A-42. Team A’s formation has five players (A8, A51, A26, A57, and A61) lined up to the left, outside the numbers, on the LOS with A20 positioned behind the linemen. A81 is set as the snapper on the right hash and A88 is on the LOS to his right. A99 is lined up 15 yards directly behind the snapper with two players in the punt shield. A99 receives the snap and passes the ball to A88. The catch is made behind the LOS and A88 runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. What if the five players lined up to the left were A68, A51, A76, A57, and A61? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/13 @ A-37 - Ready, with an illegal-formation penalty since Team A is not in kick formation.
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ A-37 - Snap, with the five-yard penalty and the clock on the snap.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the play.
explanation: Although Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation because it is not obvious that a kick will be attempted (2-16-10-a), they do not need the numbering exception because they have five players numbered 50- 79 on the line of scrimmage (7-1-4-a-3). This is a legal play.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-42. Team A’s formation has five players (A8, A51, A26, A57, and A61) lined up to the left, outside the numbers, on the LOS with A20 positioned behind the linemen. A81 is set as the snapper on the right hash and A88 is on the LOS to his right. A99 is lined up 15 yards directly behind the snapper with two players in the punt shield. A99 receives the snap and passes the ball to A88. The catch is made behind the LOS and A88 runs to the B-45 where he is tackled. What if, with the same alignment as in (2a), B96 contacted A81 less than one second after the snap? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/9 @ A-38 - Ready, with a Roughing the Snapper penalty on B96.
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ A-37 - Ready, with an illegal-formation penalty since no kick was obvious.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with the clock stopped for the contact on A81.
explanation: Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation because it is not obvious that a kick will be attempted (2-16-10-a), so there can be no foul for Roughing the Snapper (9-1-14). There is also no foul for an Illegal Formation because Team A has five players 50-79 on the line of scrimmage (7-1-4-a-3).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-43. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A29 runs a sweep to the left and is at the B-36 when he is hit and fumbles the ball. B99 gains control of the ball with his leg out of bounds at the B-32. What if B99 gained control of the ball with his leg out of bounds at the B-38? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/3 @ B-38 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready as on a forward fumble.
choice_c: A - 4/1 @ B-36 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-38 - Snap, with a new series awarded at the out-of-bounds spot.
explanation: When out of bounds B99 (4-2-1-a) touches the loose ball, then the ball is considered out of bounds (4-2-3-a), so this becomes a backward fumble out of bounds. The ball remains at the out of bounds spot, the B-38 (7-2-4-b-2), and the clock starts on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/8 @ B-43. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A29 runs a sweep to the left and is at the B-36 when he is hit and fumbles the ball. B99 gains control of the ball with his leg out of bounds at the B-32. What if A29 ran out of bounds after the fumble, re-established and recovered the ball while grounded at the B-32? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-36 - Ready, with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble since A29 went out of bounds.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-32 - Snap, since A29 going out of bounds forfeits Team A's recovery.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the recovery.
explanation: There is no prohibition against A29 going out of bounds and then coming back inbounds to touch the ball on this running play, as there would be on a passing play (7-3-4). As long as A29 re-establishes inbounds, this is a legal recovery.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-12. Snapped from the far hash, A7 completes a pass to A88 who is tackled at the B-7 on the near hash. B99 was in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the difference between the options to accept or decline? What if -- snapped from the far hash -- A7's pass to A88 is incomplete along the near sideline in the end zone where B19 was guilty of both DPI and FMM against A88? What is the difference between accepting DPI vs. FMM? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Same yard line, different lateral positions.
choice_b: Different yard lines, different lateral positions.
choice_c: Different yard lines, same lateral position.
choice_d: Same yard line, same lateral position.
explanation: Team A will accept the penalty for DPI, enforced from the previous spot to the B-2 (7-3-8-c-Penalty) to bring up A - 1/G @ B-2 - Far Hash. This is more advantageous than the penalty for FMM, enforced half-the-distance from the previous spot, B-12 → B-6 (10-2-2-d-3), to bring up A - 1/G @ B-6 - Far Hash.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-12. Snapped from the far hash, A7 completes a pass to A88 who is tackled at the B-7 on the near hash. B99 was in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the difference between the options to accept or decline? What if -- snapped from the far hash -- A7's pass to A88 is incomplete along the near sideline at the B-8 where B19 was guilty of both DPI and FMM against A88? What is the difference between accepting DPI vs. FMM? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Different yard lines, same lateral position.
choice_b: Same yard line, different lateral positions.
choice_c: Same yard line, same lateral position.
choice_d: Different yard line, different lateral positions.
explanation: Team A will accept the penalty for FMM, enforced half-the-distance from the previous spot, B-12 → B-6 (10-2-2-d-3), to bring up A - 1/G @ B-6 - Far Hash. This is more advantageous than the penalty for DPI, which places the ball at the spot of the foul (7-3-8-c-Penalty) to bring up A - 1/G @ B-8 - Near Hash.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10. A34 carries to the B-6 where he fumbles. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where B29 bats it across the end line. What if A34's grounded fumble was at the B-2 when B29 batted it into B's end zone and across the end line? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, treating the end-zone bat as a touchback.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-3 - Snap, with the ball spotted where B29 batted it.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Snap, with the penalty enforced against Team B for the illegal bat.
explanation: B29's bat in the field of play is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), but the bat of this grounded ball imparts a new impetus (8-7-2-b-1). Since B29 is responsible for the ball going into his own end zone where it became dead, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10. A34 carries to the B-6 where he fumbles. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where B29 bats it across the end line. What if A34's fumble in flight was at the B-2 when B29 batted it into B's end zone and across the end line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - F/K @ B-20 - F/K Rules, with a safety since B29 batted the ball.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with the ball placed where B29 batted it.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-3 - Snap, with a penalty on B29 for the bat in flight.
explanation: B29's bat in the field of play is backward and therefore legal (9-4-1-c), and the bat of this loose ball in flight does not impart a new impetus (8-7-2-b-1). Since A34's fumble provided the impetus (8-7-2-a) which put the ball into his opponent's end zone where it became dead, the result of the play is a Touchback (8-6-1-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A23 bats A7's punt while it is in the air above B's end zone. B17 picks up the loose ball at the B-2 and runs to the B-10 where he is tackled. During the kick, B89 held at the B-12. What if B89's foul was at the B-24? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-1 - Snap, with the penalty enforced half-the-distance from the goal line.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-12 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with no enforcement since the foul was behind the PSK spot.
explanation: A23's bat is legal (6-3-11), and all conditions of PSK are satisfied (10-2-3-b) with a PSK spot at the B-20 (2-25-11-Special Case 2). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A23 bats A7's punt while it is in the air above B's end zone. B17 picks up the loose ball at the B-2 and runs to the B-10 where he is tackled. During the kick, B89 held at the B-12. What if A23's bat was at the B-1 rather than in B's end zone, and B89's foul was at the B-24? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with the PSK spot at the B-20 rather than the B-2.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-6 - Snap, with the penalty enforced half-the-distance from the B-12.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-2 - Snap, with no enforcement since the foul was beyond the PSK spot.
explanation: A23's bat is backward / legal (9-4-1-c), and all conditions of PSK are satisfied (10-2-3-b) with a PSK spot at the B-2 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic (PSK) spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 3/1 @ A-34. The score is A-28, B-24 late in the 4th quarter, and Team B has two timeouts remaining. A11 mishandles the snap, falls on the loose ball at the A-32, and Team B calls their second timeout (0:59). After the ready for play and with Team A in punt formation, B76 presents as injured. What if Team B had one timeout remaining, did not use a timeout after A11 was down at the A-32, and then B76 presented as injured after the ball was spotted (0:49)? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-37 - Snap (0:49), with Team B penalized for Delay of Game.
choice_c: A - 4/3 @ A-32 - Snap (0:49), with an officials timeout only and no charge to Team B.
choice_d: A - 4/3 @ A-32 - Ready (0:49), with the clock starting on the Ready after the timeout.
explanation: Since B76 presented as injured after the ball was spotted, Team B is charged with their final timeout (3-3-6-a-1). Even though a defensive injury, the play clock is set to 25 seconds because of the timeout (3-2-4-c-5), and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-5).

question_text: 3/1 @ A-34. The score is A-28, B-24 late in the 4th quarter, and Team B has two timeouts remaining. A11 mishandles the snap, falls on the loose ball at the A-32, and Team B calls their second timeout (0:59). After the ready for play and with Team A in punt formation, B76 presents as injured. What if Team B had no timeouts remaining, and B76 presented as injured after the ball was spotted (0:49)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-37 - Snap (0:49), with Team B charged a timeout instead of Delay of Game.
choice_c: A - 4/3 @ A-32 - Snap (0:49), with an officials timeout only.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-37 - Ready (0:49), with no runoff since the injury did not stop the clock.
explanation: Since B76 presented as injured after the ball was spotted and Team B has no time outs remaining, they will be charged with Delay of Game (3-3-6-a-1). The five-yard penalty from the succeeding spot gives a new series to Team A. Also, since the injury was the only reason for stopping the clock, Team A has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f) which they will accept, 0:49 → 0:39, with the clock starting on the Ready (3-3-6-f-2). Because the play clock will be set to 40 seconds for the defensive injury (3-3-6-f-1), Team A will not be required to snap the ball and the game will likely be over.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A14 is ruled out of bounds at the B-25 (0:12). Video shows A14's knee was down inbounds with the ball at the B-26 (0:13). What if A14 was ruled out of bounds at the B-26 (0:12) but video showed his knee down inbounds with the ball at the B-26 (0:13)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Ready (0:03), with a 10-second runoff applied.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Ready (0:13), with the clock reset and starting on the Ready.
choice_d: Half Over, since the reversal ends the half via runoff.
explanation: Whether a runner was down inbounds or out of bounds at the same spot is not reviewable unless a correct ruling of down inbounds would have ended the half via an option for a 10-second runoff (2025 Replay Case Book Play #155). Since the runner was down by rule inbounds with 0:13 remaining and a 10-second runoff would not end the half, this is not reviewable.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A14 is ruled out of bounds at the B-25 (0:12). Video shows A14's knee was down inbounds with the ball at the B-26 (0:13). What if A14 was ruled out of bounds at the B-26 (0:08) but video showed his knee down inbounds with the ball at the B-26 (0:09)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Snap (0:09), with the ruling not reviewable and the clock at the out-of-bounds time.
choice_b: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Ready (0:09), with the clock reset but no runoff.
choice_c: A - 4/1 @ B-26 - Ready (0:00), with time restored to the moment the ball was dead.
explanation: Whether a runner was down inbounds or out of bounds at the same spot is not reviewable unless a correct ruling of down inbounds would have ended the half via an option for a 10-second runoff (2025 Replay Case Book Play #155). Since the runner was down by rule inbounds with 0:09 remaining, a 10-second runoff would end the half, so this is reviewable.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-18 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: B96's foul occurs during a legal forward pass play (2-30-1), and even though the pass did not cross the neutral zone, the end of the last run (the spot of A81's recovery while grounded, 2-25-8-a) is beyond the neutral zone, and there was no change of team possession during the down. So, the penalty for B96's personal foul is enforced from the end of the last run, A-35 → 50 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 2/1 @ A-34. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A8 runs a down-the-line option. His pitch from the A-32 glances off A41's hands at the A-31, and the loose ball rolls out-of-bounds at the A-36. What is the ruling?
explanation: A8's pitch is a backward pass, and A41's muff does not change its status. When a backward pass goes out-of-bounds, the ball remains at the out-of-bounds spot (7-2-4-a). The clock starts on the Snap whenever a backward pass goes out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. The score is A-24, B-28 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter, and neither team with a timeout remaining. Before all Team A players come to a stop following the previous play, A81 goes in motion and is in motion at the snap (0:12). A11 muffs the snap and falls on the loose ball at the B-35 (0:10). What is the ruling?
explanation: Since all eleven Team A players never came to a one-second stop prior to the snap, this is an Illegal Shift that converts to a False Start (7-1-2-b-5). The penalty for this dead-ball foul is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-30 → B-35. Since the foul caused the clock to stop immediately, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-1), which Team B will accept. The clock should be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule, and the runoff implemented, 0:12 → 0:02. The clock will start on the Ready (3-4-4-c). Note that Team A may not "spike" the ball because there are fewer than three seconds remaining and the clock will start on the Ready (3-2-5).

question_text: 4/15 @ B-45. A3's punt is touched by A84 at the B-8, then muffed by B21 at the B-4. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where A12 recovers. During the kick, B19 held A28 at the B-30. What is the most likely outcome?
