explanation: Five- and 10-yard penalties for fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down (10-2-5-a-2). A84 is guilty of Illegal Touching (6-3-2-a), and while Team A would like to decline the penalty and keep the six points, if they do, Team B would elect the spot of Illegal Touching and take the ball at B-8 (6-3-2-b). So, Team A will accept the penalty. Postscrimmage kick enforcement does not apply since Team B will not next put the ball into play (10-2-3-b-4), and thus enforcement is from the previous spot, B-45 → B-35 (10-2-2-d-4-a), with an automatic first down (9-3-4-Penalty).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-40. Just prior to A9's punt arriving, A28 contacts B17 at the B-20 and is flagged for Kick Catch Interference. B17 muffs the kick, and the loose ball is recovered by B43 while grounded at the B-25. After the penalty is enforced, Replay stops the game because video shows A28 led with his head, forcibly contacting B17 in the neck area. What is the ruling?
explanation: B17 is defenseless (2-27-14-d), and Replay will add a foul for Targeting (9-1-4). On-field officials will have already enforced the penalty for KCI from the spot of the foul, B-20 → B-35, because "tack-on" enforcement does not apply to KCI (6-3-13). Replay will need to identify the spot of B43's recovery so that the penalty for TGT can now be enforced as a "tack-on", B-25 → B-40, with an appropriate Referee announcement.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A81 is beyond his free kick line at the time of A2's onside kick. As A41 is trailing the kick, he accidentally kicks the ball at the A-43. The ball then strikes B18 at the A-45, and is recovered by A41 while grounded at the A-44. What is the most likely outcome?
explanation: A player touched by a ball either batted or illegally kicked by an opponent is deemed not to have touched the ball (6-1-4-b). But, B18's contact with this accidental kick leaves Team A in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (6-1-3-a / 6-1-6-a), so "tack-on" enforcement is not possible (6-1-8). A41 still illegally touched the ball at the A-43 (6-1-3-b), so Team B may accept the ball at that spot. They may not, however, have the penalty enforced from there as acceptance of a penalty cancels the Illegal Touching privilege (6-1-3-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A4's pass is intercepted by B21 in B's end zone. B21 returns the ball to the B-10 where A67 brings him down with a crown of the helmet hit, confirmed as Targeting by Replay. At the snap, Team A was in an Illegal Formation and B98 was lined up in the neutral zone. What is the ruling?
explanation: If both teams foul during a Try and Team B fouls before the change of possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated, even if additional fouls occur after the change of possession (8-3-3-a). Even though A67's foul is flagrant and he will still be disqualified, this foul after the change of team possession will not be penalized as a FUDL foul because it is part of an offset.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. First possession series of the second extra period. A7's pass is complete to A89 in the end zone for a touchdown. B64 is flagged for Roughing the Passer. What option(s) is (are) available to Team A?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, followed by B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, followed by B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock.
choice_c: Neither A nor B is an option.
choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: Penalties for personal fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown may be enforced on the Try, on the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1). B64's foul is not flagrant, but the concept of FUDL only applies to fouls after a change of possession on a Try (8-3-4) or in extra periods (3-1-3-g). This personal foul by Team B during a Team A touchdown is penalized as it would be at any other time in the game -- on the Try, or where the ball will next be put in play after the Try.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-18 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What if A24's fumble from the A-30 was recovered by B18 at the A-35? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Ready, with tack-on enforcement from the end of the last run.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the fumble.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the change of possession.
explanation: "Tack-on" enforcement does not apply because there was a change of team possession during the down (10-2-2-e), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-18 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What if A24's fumble from the A-30 rolled out of bounds at the A-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from A81's original recovery spot.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the fumble out of bounds.
explanation: The end of the last run is the spot of A24's fumble, the A-30 (2-25-8-b). Since this is beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of team possession during the down, the penalty for B96's personal foul during this legal forward pass play (2-30-1) is enforced from the end of the last run, A-30 → A-45 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 2/1 @ A-34. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A8 runs a down-the-line option. His pitch from the A-32 glances off A41's hands at the A-31, and the loose ball rolls out-of-bounds at the A-36. What if A8's pitch was caught by A41 at the A-31, fumbled at the A-33, and rolled out of bounds at the A-36? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/5 @ A-30 - Snap, with the ball left at the out-of-bounds spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-36 - Ready, with a new series at the recovery spot.
choice_d: A - 3/2 @ A-33 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the fumble out of bounds.
explanation: When a fumble goes out of bounds between the goal lines in advance of the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the spot of the fumble (7-2-4-b-1). The clock starts on the Ready after a forward fumble out of bounds at any time during the game (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 2/1 @ A-34. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter. A8 runs a down-the-line option. His pitch from the A-32 glances off A41's hands at the A-31, and the loose ball rolls out-of-bounds at the A-36. What if A8's pitch was caught by A41 at the A-31, fumbled at the A-33, and rolled out of bounds at the A-30? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/2 @ A-33 - Ready, with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble.
choice_b: A - 3/5 @ A-30 - Ready, with the clock starting on the Ready.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-36 - Snap, with a new series at the out-of-bounds spot.
explanation: When a fumble goes out of bounds between the goal lines behind the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the out-of-bounds spot (7-2-4-b-2). The clock starts on the Snap after a backward fumble out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. The score is A-24, B-28 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter, and neither team with a timeout remaining. Before all Team A players come to a stop following the previous play, A81 goes in motion and is in motion at the snap (0:12). A11 muffs the snap and falls on the loose ball at the B-35 (0:10). What if all Team A players came to a stop, then A81 went in to motion as A76 was assuming his final set position? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/10 @ B-35 - Ready (0:02), with a 10-second runoff accepted for the shift.
choice_c: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Ready (0:10), with the Illegal Shift penalty enforced and the down replayed.
choice_d: Game Over, with a runoff ending the game.
explanation: Because all Team A players came to a stop, this will not be a dead-ball False Start (7-1-2-b-5) but rather a live-ball Illegal Shift (7-1-2-a / 7-1-4-c). Team B will decline the penalty (10-1-1-b). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. The score is A-24, B-28 with the clock running late in the 4th quarter, and neither team with a timeout remaining. Before all Team A players come to a stop following the previous play, A81 goes in motion and is in motion at the snap (0:12). A11 muffs the snap and falls on the loose ball at the B-35 (0:10). What if all Team A players came to a stop, then A81 went into motion, and after A11 was down, B96 piled on (0:09)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Ready (0:09), with the clock on the Ready after the personal foul.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap (0:00), with a 10-second runoff exhausting the clock.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Snap (0:09), with B96's foul declined.
explanation: There is no foul on Team A, but the penalty for B96's dead-ball personal foul is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-35 → B-20 (10-2-2-a). Because this foul caused the clock to stop immediately, Team A has an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a), which they will decline. The clock will then start on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 4/15 @ B-45. A3's punt is touched by A84 at the B-8, then muffed by B21 at the B-4. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where A12 recovers. During the kick, B19 held A28 at the B-30. What is the most likely outcome? What if B19's foul during the kick at the B-30 was an Illegal Block Below the Waist, rather than Holding? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the personal-foul penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock only, with the penalty enforced solely on the Try.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock followed by A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules -- or -- A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock followed by A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with the touchdown negated by the illegal block.
explanation: Fifteen-yard penalties for personal fouls and for unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are enforced on the try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods, at the option of the scoring team (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/15 @ B-45. A3's punt is touched by A84 at the B-8, then muffed by B21 at the B-4. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where A12 recovers. During the kick, B19 held A28 at the B-30. What is the most likely outcome? What if B19's foul during the kick at the B-30 was Holding, but A84 never touched the kick? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with the holding penalty enforced on the Try.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with the holding penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with Team B electing the spot of illegal touching.
explanation: Five- and 10-yard penalties for fouls by the non-scoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down (10-2-5-a-2).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-40. Just prior to A9's punt arriving, A28 contacts B17 at the B-20 and is flagged for Kick Catch Interference. B17 muffs the kick, and the loose ball is recovered by B43 while grounded at the B-25. After the penalty is enforced, Replay stops the game because video shows A28 led with his head, forcibly contacting B17 in the neck area. What if the loose ball had been recovered by A15 while grounded at the B-25? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the Targeting penalty tacked on from the recovery spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the KCI penalty enforced from the PSK spot.
choice_d: A - 4/20 @ A-25 - Snap, with the Targeting penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: Because the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (6-3-13). Team B will decline the penalty for TGT as it would be enforced from the previous spot and leave Team A in possession (10-2-2-d-4-a), and accept the penalty for KCI, enforced from the spot of the foul, B-20 → B-35 (6-4-1-Penalty).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-40. Just prior to A9's punt arriving, A28 contacts B17 at the B-20 and is flagged for Kick Catch Interference. B17 muffs the kick, and the loose ball is recovered by B43 while grounded at the B-25. After the penalty is enforced, Replay stops the game because video shows A28 led with his head, forcibly contacting B17 in the neck area. What if there had been no flag for KCI and the loose ball had been recovered by A15 while grounded at the B-25? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with Team B taking the ball at the recovery spot.
choice_c: A - 4/5 @ A-40 - Snap, with only a five-yard penalty for the Targeting.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement of the Targeting penalty.
explanation: Because the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (6-3-13). The penalty for TGT is enforced from the previous spot, A-40 → A-25 (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A81 is beyond his free kick line at the time of A2's onside kick. As A41 is trailing the kick, he accidentally kicks the ball at the A-43. The ball then strikes B18 at the A-45, and is recovered by A41 while grounded at the A-44. What is the most likely outcome? What if A41 intentionally kicked the ball at the A-43, striking B18 at the A-44½? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick after the offside penalty.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-44 - Snap, with the ball left at the spot of B18's contact.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-38 - Snap, with the illegal-kick penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: Because A41 illegally kicked the ball, B18 is deemed not to have touched the ball (6-1-4-b); therefore, Team A is not in legal possession at the end of the down (6-1-3-a) and the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B at the A-44 (6-1-6-a). Team B will decline the penalty for A81's Offside foul, and accept the penalty for A41's Illegally Kicking the Ball foul, enforced from that spot, A-44 → A-34 (6-1-8).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A81 is beyond his free kick line at the time of A2's onside kick. As A41 is trailing the kick, he accidentally kicks the ball at the A-43. The ball then strikes B18 at the A-45, and is recovered by A41 while grounded at the A-44. What is the most likely outcome? What if A41 never touched the ball, but it simply struck B18 at the A-45 before being recovered by A41 at the A-44? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with Team B taking the ball at the spot of B18's touching.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-35 - Snap, with a re-kick from the previous spot with no penalty.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-38 - Snap, with the offside penalty enforced against Team A.
explanation: Team A is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down (6-1-3-a / 6-1-6-a), and so "tack- on" enforcement does not apply (6-1-8). The penalty is enforced from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30 (10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A4's pass is intercepted by B21 in B's end zone. B21 returns the ball to the B-10 where A67 brings him down with a crown of the helmet hit, confirmed as Targeting by Replay. At the snap, Team A was in an Illegal Formation and B98 was lined up in the neutral zone. What if there had been no DOF on B98 -- just ILF on A and TGT on A67? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the Illegal Formation penalty enforced instead of declined.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the Targeting penalty declined since the fouls offset.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with A67 not disqualified since his foul was declined.
explanation: The penalty for Team A's Illegal Formation is declined by rule (8-3-3-c-3), but because A67's Targeting foul after the change of possession is flagrant, that penalty is enforced as a FUDL foul on the succeeding kickoff, A-35 → A-20 (8-3-4-a-Exception).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A4's pass is intercepted by B21 in B's end zone. B21 returns the ball to the B-10 where A67 brings him down with a crown of the helmet hit, confirmed as Targeting by Replay. At the snap, Team A was in an Illegal Formation and B98 was lined up in the neutral zone. What if there had been no ILF on A or TGT on A67 -- just DOF on B98 and a BSB w/TGT on B36 during the return? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the Blind Side Block penalty enforced instead of the offside.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with the Targeting penalty carried to the kickoff.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with B36 not disqualified since his foul was declined.
explanation: Team A may accept the penalty for only one of Team B's fouls (10-1-3), and they will opt for B98's Offside foul since that will give them a re-Try (8-3-3-b-2). The penalty for B36's Blind Side Both with Targeting foul will be declined, but B36 is still disqualified (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. First possession series of the second extra period. A7's pass is complete to A89 in the end zone for a touchdown. B64 is flagged for Roughing the Passer. What option(s) is (are) available to Team A? What if this foul occurred on a successful two-point Try in the first possession series of the second extra period? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with the personal-foul penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with Team A repeating the Try after the foul.
choice_d: B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced from the third-extra-period spot.
explanation: Since Team A has already scored two points, they will accept the score with the penalty for B64's personal foul enforced from the succeeding spot in extra periods, A-25 → A-40 (8-3-3-b-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. First possession series of the second extra period. A7's pass is complete to A89 in the end zone for a touchdown. B64 is flagged for Roughing the Passer. What option(s) is (are) available to Team A? What if this foul occurred on a successful two-point Try in the first possession series of the third extra period? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced from the second-extra-period spot.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with Team A repeating the Try.
choice_c: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with the personal-foul penalty declined by rule.
explanation: Since Team A has already scored two points, they will accept the score with the penalty for B64's personal foul enforced from the succeeding spot in extra periods, A-3 → A-18 (8-3-3-b-1).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Late in the first half, A13’s kickoff is intentionally driven low down the middle of the field to prevent a long runback. While the ball is rolling, B27 holds at the B-35. After the hold occurs, B11 recovers the kick at the B-20 and returns it to the B-40. Team A accepts the penalty. What is the ruling?
choice_d: Both A and B are options.
explanation: B27's foul occurred during a legal free kick play (2-30-2), and the basic spot on legal kick plays is the previous spot unless the foul is governed by postscrimmage kick rules (10-2-2-d-4-a). If accepted, the penalty for the foul by the team not in possession would be enforced from the basic spot, A-35 → A-45, with a re-kick (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Punter A91 is positioned on the A-30 when the snap goes over his head, and he recovers the ball at the A-23. He then stops and punts the ball, and is immediately hit in the plant leg by a diving B8. B47 completes a fair catch of the punt at the B-30. What is the ruling?
choice_d: Both B and C are options.
explanation: The kicker’s protection under the Roughing / Running Into the Kicker rule ends when the kicker carries or possesses the ball more than five yards behind the kicker’s initial position at the snap from scrimmage kick formation (9-1-16-a-4-c). So, there is no foul on this play.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A illegally shifts at the snap, and A12 throws an interception to B55 in the end zone. During the return, B9 commits an Illegal Blind Side Block at the B-20. B55 is tackled at the 50. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since Team B fouled after the change of possession, the fouls do not offset (8-3-3-a) and there will be no re-Try. The penalty for Team A's Illegal Shift is declined by rule (8-3-3-c-3), and since B9's foul was not a FUDL foul, that penalty is also declined by rule (8-3-4-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-22 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since this is not a legal forward pass, the action from the time of the snap until the pass is caught by A24 is considered a running play (2-30-4) which ends behind the neutral zone. So, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a), and the penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/9 @ A-5. From the end zone, punter A11 muffs the snap and kicks the loose ball to prevent an opponent from recovering it. The ball goes out of bounds at the A-2. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Both A and C are options.
explanation: A11 is guilty of illegally kicking a loose ball (9-4-4), and since the foul occurred in the end zone, the penalty would result in a Safety (8-5-1-b). Depending on the time and score, Team B might choose to decline the penalty and accept the ball at the out-of-bounds spot (10-1-1-b). The penalty may not be "tacked on" because this is not a legal scrimmage kick play (6-3-13).

question_text: 3/12 @ A-5. QB A11 takes the snap and throws the ball as he drops straight back. He is flagged for Intentional Grounding in his own end zone. Video shows that A11 released the ball at the A-1. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Not Reviewable.  A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
explanation: The location of the ball when it is obviously in the field of play or in the end zone, and there is a ruling on the field of Intentional Grounding, is reviewable (12-3-2-f). Replay will reverse the spot of the foul to the A-1, and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. While the kick is in-flight and untouched, A22 accidentally steps on the sideline on his own, but immediately returns inbounds and is blocked by B42. B21 then receives the ball at the B-10 and returns it to the B-30. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Foul.  A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: Both A and B are options.
explanation: It is a foul if a Team A player goes out of bounds on his own during a free kick play and then returns inbounds (6-1-11). The five-yard penalty may be enforced at the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, with a re-kick, or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, B-30 → B-35 (6-1-8).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-41. A22 runs downfield for a long gain. A77 tries to block defender B99 but misses and falls to the ground. A77 reaches out grabs B99’s foot at the B-25 causing B99 to fall to the ground. A22 scores on the play. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A77's actions "trip up" B99, they do not constitute Tripping as that requires the use of the lower leg or foot to obstruct an opponent (2-28). Still, A77 is guilty of Holding, and the spot of the foul is behind the basic spot, which is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-25 → B-35 (10-2-2-c-1), and the clock starts on the Snap because the result of the play was a Touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Late in the first half, A13’s kickoff is intentionally driven low down the middle of the field to prevent a long runback. While the ball is rolling, B27 holds at the B-35. After the hold occurs, B11 recovers the kick at the B-20 and returns it to the B-40. Team A accepts the penalty. What if B27's hold occurred after B11 recovered the kick at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap, with the ball left at the end of B11's run.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-10 - Snap, with PSK enforcement from the B-20.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick from the previous spot.
explanation: B27's foul occurred during a running play (2-30-4), and the basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-40 (10-2-2-d-1-c). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-35 → B-25 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Late in the first half, A13’s kickoff is intentionally driven low down the middle of the field to prevent a long runback. While the ball is rolling, B27 holds at the B-35. After the hold occurs, B11 recovers the kick at the B-20 and returns it to the B-40. Team A accepts the penalty. What if this occurred on 4/10 @ A-35, and B27's hold occurred before B11 recovered the kick at the B-20? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the foul.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-45 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick since PSK does not apply.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: B27's foul meets all the criteria for postscrimmage kick enforcement (10-2-3-b) with a PSK spot of the B-20 (2-25-11-a). Since the foul by the team considered to be in possession at the time (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic spot, the penalty is enforced from the basic (PSK) spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Punter A91 is positioned on the A-30 when the snap goes over his head, and he recovers the ball at the A-23. He then stops and punts the ball, and is immediately hit in the plant leg by a diving B8. B47 completes a fair catch of the punt at the B-30. What if A91 recovered the ball at the A-26, and B8 forcibly contacted A91 in the plant leg? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/5 @ A-45 - Snap, with only a five-yard Running Into the Kicker penalty.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with no foul since A91 recovered beyond five yards.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with the clock stopped for the penalty.
explanation: It is always Roughing the Kicker when there is forcible contact to the plant leg (Officiating Standard 9-3). The penalty is 15 yards from the previous spot, A-40 → B-45, and an automatic first down (9-1-16-a-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Punter A91 is positioned on the A-30 when the snap goes over his head, and he recovers the ball at the A-23. He then stops and punts the ball, and is immediately hit in the plant leg by a diving B8. B47 completes a fair catch of the punt at the B-30. What if A91 recovered the ball at the A-26, and B8 displaced A91 by contacting his kicking leg? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with a Roughing the Kicker penalty and automatic first down.
choice_b: A - 4/5 @ A-45 - Snap only, with no option for Team A to decline.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with no foul on the play.
choice_d: A - 4/5 @ A-45 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap.
explanation: Running into the Kicker occurs when the kicker is displaced but not roughed (9-1-16-a-2), or if the defender simply "runs through" the kicking leg and there is no forcible contact (Officiating Standard 9-3). The penalty, if accepted, is five yards from the previous spot, A-40 → A-45, with no automatic first down (9-1-16-a-Penalty). Team A might choose to simply to decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A illegally shifts at the snap, and A12 throws an interception to B55 in the end zone. During the return, B9 commits an Illegal Blind Side Block at the B-20. B55 is tackled at the 50. What if B9's foul was for an Illegal Blind Side Block with Targeting, confirmed by Replay? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the fouls offsetting and a re-Try.
choice_d: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, with B9 not disqualified for the Blind Side Block.
explanation: DQ B9. Since Team B fouled after the change of possession, the fouls do not offset (8-3-3-a) and there will be no re-Try. The penalty for Team A's Illegal Shift is declined by rule (8-3-3-c-3), but since B9's foul was a flagrant (FUDL) foul, that penalty is enforced on the succeeding kickoff, A-35 → 50 (8-3-4-a-Exception).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Team A illegally shifts at the snap, and A12 throws an interception to B55 in the end zone. During the return, B9 commits an Illegal Blind Side Block at the B-20. B55 is tackled at the 50. What if, in addition to the A's Illegal Shift and B9's BSB w/TGT, B55 was flagged for DPI in the end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules, with B9's Targeting enforced on the kickoff.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, with B55's DPI penalty enforced on the re-Try.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with the Illegal Shift penalty enforced.
explanation: DQ B9. If both teams foul during a Try and Team B fouls before the change of possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated, even if additional fouls occur after the change of possession (8-3-3-a). Even though B9's foul is flagrant and he will still be disqualified, this foul after the change of team possession will not be penalized as a FUDL foul because it is part of an offset.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-22 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What if B96's foul occurred after A24 had caught the pass? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the recovery.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the fumble.
explanation: B96's foul occurred during A24's run, and his run ended at the spot of his fumble (2-25-8-b). Since that occurred beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b), and the penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-30 → A-45 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A6's pass from the A-22 is complete to A24 at the A-20. He runs to the A-30 where he fumbles with A81 recovering while grounded at the A-35. Prior to the pass, B96 was flagged for Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. What if B96's foul occurred after A24 had caught the pass, and A24's fumble was recovered by B17? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot after the change of possession.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with Team B keeping the ball after B17's recovery.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the change of possession.
explanation: Unlike the "tack-on" rule for personal or unsportsmanlike fouls during a legal forward pass play (10-2-2-e), the change of possession is immaterial here. The enforcement is identical to that in (4a) above.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-5. From the end zone, punter A11 muffs the snap and kicks the loose ball to prevent an opponent from recovering it. The ball goes out of bounds at the A-2. What if A11's punt is partially blocked, and from the end zone he kicks the loose ball and it goes OOB at the A-6? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules or B - 1/G @ A-3 - Snap.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules only, with no tack-on option for Team B.
choice_c: B - 1/G @ A-3 - Snap only, with the safety-kick option unavailable.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules or B - 1/G @ A-6 - Snap, with the tack-on from the out-of-bounds spot.
explanation: A11 legally punted the ball, but is then guilty of illegally kicking a loose ball (9-4-4) for which the penalty would result in a Safety (8-5-1-b). The loose ball retains the status of A11's original legal scrimmage kick (8-7-2-c), and since this kick crossed the neutral zone, Team B could also opt to have the penalty enforced from where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them, A-6 → A-3 (6-3-13).

question_text: 4/9 @ A-5. From the end zone, punter A11 muffs the snap and kicks the loose ball to prevent an opponent from recovering it. The ball goes out of bounds at the A-2. What if A11's punt is partially blocked, and from the end zone he kicks the loose ball and it goes OOB at the A-4? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules only, with no option for Team B to take the out-of-bounds spot.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules or B - 1/G @ A-4 - Snap.
choice_c: B - 1/G @ A-4 - Snap only, with the safety-kick option unavailable.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules or B - 1/G @ A-2 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement toward the goal.
explanation: A11 legally punted the ball, but is then guilty of illegally kicking a loose ball (9-4-4) for which the penalty would result in a Safety (8-5-1-b). The loose ball retains the status of A11's original legal scrimmage kick (8-7-2-c), but it did not cross the neutral zone and so "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (6-3-13). Team B could decline the penalty and take the ball at the out-of-bounds spot (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 3/12 @ A-5. QB A11 takes the snap and throws the ball as he drops straight back. He is flagged for Intentional Grounding in his own end zone. Video shows that A11 released the ball at the A-1. What if the flag was dropped at the A-1, but video showed A11 released the ball from his own end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Not Reviewable. A - 4/16 @ A-1 - Snap, since the grounding ruling itself cannot be reviewed.
choice_b: Reviewable. A - 4/16½ @ A-½ - Snap, with the spot reversed to the half-yard line.
choice_c: Reviewable. A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: Reviewable. A - 4/16 @ A-1 - Snap, with a loss of down at the reversed spot.
explanation: The location of the ball when it is obviously in the field of play or in the end zone, and there is a ruling on the field of Intentional Grounding, is reviewable (12-3-2-f). Replay will reverse the spot of the foul to A's end zone, and since the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul (7-3-2-h-Penalty), the result is a Safety (8-5-1-b).

question_text: 3/12 @ A-5. QB A11 takes the snap and throws the ball as he drops straight back. He is flagged for Intentional Grounding in his own end zone. Video shows that A11 released the ball at the A-1. What if video was inconclusive as to where the pass was released, but showed the pass fell at the feet of eligible A84? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Reviewable. A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules, with the grounding ruling reversed to a safety.
choice_b: Not Reviewable. A - 4/16 @ A-1 - Snap, with the spot upheld as called on the field.
choice_c: Reviewable. A - 4/16 @ A-1 - Snap, with the location corrected but the foul upheld.
choice_d: Not Reviewable. A - F/K @ A-20 - F/K Rules.
explanation: Although the location of the ball is reviewable with the ruling of Intentional Grounding (12-3-2-f), the ruling of Intentional Grounding itself is not reviewable (12-3-7). Replay will uphold the location of the foul as called, but can not get involved with the fact that there should not have been a foul at all.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. While the kick is in-flight and untouched, A22 accidentally steps on the sideline on his own, but immediately returns inbounds and is blocked by B42. B21 then receives the ball at the B-10 and returns it to the B-30. What if B21 fumbled at the B-30 with A22 recovering while grounded at the B-35? What is the ruling?
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with Team B taking the tack-on spot.
choice_c: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules, with a re-kick and no penalty.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with no foul since the kick ended in B's possession.
explanation: A22 fouls by going out of bounds on his own and then returning inbounds (6-1-11), but because the subsequent dead ball does not belong to Team B, the only enforcement option is from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, with a re-kick (6-1-8).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. While the kick is in-flight and untouched, A22 accidentally steps on the sideline on his own, but immediately returns inbounds and is blocked by B42. B21 then receives the ball at the B-10 and returns it to the B-30. What if A22 stayed inbounds during the kick, stepped on the sideline during B21's return, then re-established to recover B21's fumble while grounded at the B-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules, with a foul on A22 for returning inbounds and a re-kick.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with Team B keeping the ball after B21's fumble.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Ready, with the clock stopped after the recovery.
explanation: The free kick play ended when B21 gained possession of the ball (2-30-2), so A22 did not foul (6-1-11). As long as A22 re-established prior to touching the ball, Team A is in legal possession at the end of the down.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-41. A22 runs downfield for a long gain. A77 tries to block defender B99 but misses and falls to the ground. A77 reaches out grabs B99’s foot at the B-25 causing B99 to fall to the ground. A22 scores on the play. What if A77 extended his leg out at the B-25 to obstruct B99, causing him to fall to the ground? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap, with a Holding penalty enforced instead of Tripping.
choice_b: A - 4/4 @ A-40 - Ready, with the clock on the Ready after the penalty.
choice_d: A - 4/4 @ B-40 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the end of the run.
explanation: A77 is guilty of Tripping (2-28), and the spot of the foul is behind the basic spot, which is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-25 → B-40 (10-2-2-c-1), and the clock starts on the Snap because the result of the play was a Touchdown (3-3-2-c).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-41. A22 runs downfield for a long gain. A77 tries to block defender B99 but misses and falls to the ground. A77 reaches out grabs B99’s foot at the B-25 causing B99 to fall to the ground. A22 scores on the play. What if A77 extended his leg out at the B-25 to obstruct B99, causing him to fall to the ground, and that A22 fumbled at the B-5 with A48 recovering the loose ball while grounded in B's end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/4 @ A-40 - Snap, with the clock on the snap since the result was a touchdown.
choice_b: A - 1/G @ B-5 - Ready, with the fumble recovery allowing a new series.
choice_c: A - 4/4 @ B-40 - Ready, with the Tripping penalty enforced from the end of the run.
explanation: The result of the play is A - 1/G @ B-5 because A22's fumble on 4th down was recovered by A48, and so the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-2-a-Exception 2). There was no Touchdown, so the only reason for stopping the clock is to complete the penalty for A77's Tripping foul (2-28). The spot of the foul is behind the basic spot, which is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, B-25 → B-35 (10-2-2-c-1), and the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: Try @ B-3. B23 intercepts A6's forward pass, returns the ball to the B-20, and fumbles. A42 recovers at the B-25 and advances to the B-15 where he fumbles. A35 recovers at the B-10 and carries the ball into B's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Score.  Ball is dead when A35 recovers.
choice_b: No Score.  Ball is dead when B23 intercepts.
choice_c: No Score.  Ball is dead when A42 recovers.
choice_d: Two Points for A.  Ball remains alive.
explanation: The Try ends when a Team A player fumbles and the ball is caught or recovered by any Team A player other than the fumbler (8-3-2-d-5). The ball remains alive when B23 intercepts and when A42 recovers (because he is recovering B23's fumble -- a Team B fumble), but becomes dead when A35 recovers A42's fumble -- a Team A fumble.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach informs the Side Judge that, after the kick, they would like the ball on the Far Hash. While the kick is in the air, B21 gives a “T signal”. The ball is caught by B44 at the B-5 on the Near Hash. What is the ruling?
explanation: B21's "T-signal" is an invalid fair catch signal (2-8-3-b), and when B44 catches the kick, it is not a fair catch, but the ball is dead (6-5-3) at that spot -- both the yard line and the lateral position.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-17. QB A1 keeps the ball and runs up the middle where he is tackled at the B-15. During the down, B7 and B34 both participated with green dots on their helmets. What is the ruling?
explanation: On a regular scrimmage down, if more than one green dot helmet per team is detected on the field by the game officials, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty enforced from the previous spot (1-4-11-b-Bullet 3). Team A would accept the penalty, enforced B-17 → B-12, with a repeat of the down.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. With the clock running and just over two minutes remaining in the 2nd quarter, A22 carries to the B-32 where is tackled (1:58). B54 is injured during the down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Option for 10-Second Runoff.  Play Clock at 40.
choice_b: Option for 10-Second Runoff.  Play Clock at 25.
choice_c: Option for 10-Second Runoff.  Play Clock at 40.
choice_d: No Option for 10-Second Runoff.  Play Clock at 25.
explanation: There is no option for a 10-second runoff because B54 was not injured after the Two-Minute Timeout (3-3-6-f). While an injury to a defensive player would normally require a 40-second play clock, it is set to 25 seconds because of the Two-Minute Timeout (3-2-4-c-5).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. QB A1 throws a pass from the A-22 toward receiver A84 at the A-24. B1 is playing the ball and pushes A84 in the back just prior to the ball arriving. The pass falls incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: B1 did not commit Defensive Pass Interference because the pass did not cross the neutral zone (7-3-8-a) and the contact was not beyond the neutral zone (7-3-8-c). B1 is also not guilty of an Illegal Block in the Back since he is an eligible player attempting to get to a forward pass (9-3-5-Exception 5). There is no foul on this play.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-45. A1, lined up 10 yards deep to punt, muffs the snap which falls to the ground. A1 picks up the loose ball, but before he is able to kick, he is knocked down by B1. B6 recovers the ball at the A-30. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since A1 never kicked the ball, he is not a kicker (2-27-3-a), and so there can be no foul for Roughing or Running Into the Kicker (9-1-16-a). This is a legal play. This was not a legal kick down, but the clock starts on the snap because Team B was awarded a first down and will next snap the ball (3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A8 carries to the A-42, then retreats behind the neutral zone. From the A-38, he completes a pass to A29 at the 50 where he runs out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: It is an Illegal Forward Pass if the pass is thrown from behind the neutral zone after a ball carrier's entire body and the ball have been beyond the neutral zone (7-3-2-e). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-38 → A-33, and a loss of down (7-3-2-e-Penalty).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-34. With the score tied late in the 4th quarter, Team A’s field goal attempt lands short in the end zone untouched as time expires. Video shows that when the ball touched the ground, 0:02 remained on the game clock. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Not Reviewable.  Go to extra periods.
choice_b: Reviewable.  Less than 0:03, so go to extra periods.
explanation: The ball is dead when it lands in B's end zone untouched by B (4-1-3-c), and the ball is returned to the previous spot (8-4-2-b-1). Since the clock would next start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-8) and the point differential is eight points or less (12-3-6-d-3), Replay may restore any amount of time to the 4th quarter clock (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: Try @ B-3. B23 intercepts A6's forward pass, returns the ball to the B-20, and fumbles. A42 recovers at the B-25 and advances to the B-15 where he fumbles. A35 recovers at the B-10 and carries the ball into B's end zone. What if this play occurred on 4/G @ B-3? What is the ruling?
choice_b: Two Points for A, with the recovery counting only as a Try.
choice_c: No Score, since a Team A player other than the fumbler recovered.
choice_d: No Score, with the ball dead when A35 recovered A42's fumble.
explanation: The "4th Down Fumble Rule" only applies before a change of team possession (7-2-2-a-Exception 2), so when A42's fumble after a change of team possession is recovered by A35, the ball continues in play (7-2-2-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. B23 intercepts A6's forward pass, returns the ball to the B-20, and fumbles. A42 recovers at the B-25 and advances to the B-15 where he fumbles. A35 recovers at the B-10 and carries the ball into B's end zone. What if, in the original play, A35 muffed A42's fumble with A42 then recovering and carrying the ball into B's end zone? What is the ruling?
choice_a: No Score, since A35 muffed A42's fumble.
choice_c: Six Points for A, with the recovery ruled a touchdown from scrimmage.
choice_d: No Score, with the ball dead when A42 recovered.
explanation: A35's muff does not change the status of A42's fumble, so A42 recovered and legally advanced his own fumble (8-3-2-d-5).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach informs the Side Judge that, after the kick, they would like the ball on the Far Hash. While the kick is in the air, B21 gives a “T signal”. The ball is caught by B44 at the B-5 on the Near Hash. What if the ball had been caught by B21 at the B-5 on the Near Hash? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap - Near Hash, with the ball dead where B21 caught it.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap - Near Hash, with no right to specify the hash.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap - Far Hash, with the ball spotted where the catch was made.
explanation: If, during a free kick, a Team B receiver gives any waving signal that does not meet all of the requirements of a valid fair catch signal, and subsequently catches the ball behind the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the B-25 (6-5-3-Exception). The "touchback-like" quality of this award at the B-25 includes the right of Team B to select the spot of lateral placement.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach informs the Side Judge that, after the kick, they would like the ball on the Far Hash. While the kick is in the air, B21 gives a “T signal”. The ball is caught by B44 at the B-5 on the Near Hash. What if the ball had been caught by B21 at the B-30 on the Near Hash? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap - Far Hash, with the ball awarded at the B-25 with hash choice.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap - Far Hash, with Team B choosing the lateral placement.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap - Near Hash, with the ball brought back to the B-25.
explanation: Because the kick was caught beyond the B-25, the ball is dead where caught (6-5-3). Since this spot does not have the "touchback-like" quality of an award at the B-25, Team B has no right to specify the lateral placement.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-17. QB A1 keeps the ball and runs up the middle where he is tackled at the B-15. During the down, B7 and B34 both participated with green dots on their helmets. What if A1 carried the ball to the B-10? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 2/2 @ B-12 - Ready, with the equipment-violation penalty accepted.
choice_c: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the run.
choice_d: A - 2/8 @ B-12 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: The penalty, if accepted, would result in A - 2/2 @ B-12 - Ready (1-11-4-b-Bullet 3), so Team A will decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 2/7 @ B-17. QB A1 keeps the ball and runs up the middle where he is tackled at the B-15. During the down, B7 and B34 both participated with green dots on their helmets. What if, in the original play, only A1, A82, and B7 participated with green does on their helmets? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 2/12 @ B-22 - Ready only, with no option to decline the penalty.
choice_b: A - 2/12 @ B-22 - Ready or A - 3/5 @ B-15 - Ready.
choice_c: A - 3/5 @ B-15 - Ready only, with no equipment violation since only three green dots showed.
choice_d: A - 2/2 @ B-12 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the tackle.
explanation: The penalty, if accepted, would be enforced five yards from the previous spot, B-17 → B-22 (1-11-4-b-Bullet 3), or Team B may elect to decline the penalty (10-1-1-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. With the clock running and just over two minutes remaining in the 2nd quarter, A22 carries to the B-32 where is tackled (1:58). B54 is injured during the down. What if the ball was snapped at 1:58, and A22 was tackled at the B-32 with 1:53 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/2 @ B-32 - Ready (1:43) only, with no option to decline the runoff.
choice_b: A - 3/2 @ B-32 - Snap (1:53) only, with no runoff since the injury was before the Two-Minute Timeout.
choice_d: A - 3/2 @ B-32 - Ready (1:43), with the play clock set to 25 seconds.
explanation: Play clock at 40 seconds. Since B54's injury is the only reason for stopping the clock, Team A has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). If the runoff is accepted, the clock will start on the Ready, and if it is declined the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-6-f-2). Team B may avoid the runoff by the use of a charged team timeout, if available (3-3-6-f-3), in which case the clock would start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-5). The play clock is set to 40 seconds for an injury to a defensive player (3-3-6-f-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. With the clock running and just over two minutes remaining in the 2nd quarter, A22 carries to the B-32 where is tackled (1:58). B54 is injured during the down. What if the ball was snapped at 1:58, and A22 was tackled at the B-30 with 1:53 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Ready (1:43), with a 10-second runoff for the injury.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap (1:53), with the clock on the snap after the injury.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-30 - Ready (1:53), with the play clock set to 25 seconds.
explanation: Play clock at 40 seconds. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock was stopped for both B54's injury and to award a first down to Team A (3-3-6-f). The play clock is set to 40 seconds for an injury to a defensive player (3-3-6-f-1), and the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-1 / 3-3-2-e-5).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. QB A1 throws a pass from the A-22 toward receiver A84 at the A-24. B1 is playing the ball and pushes A84 in the back just prior to the ball arriving. The pass falls incomplete. What if B1 tackled A84 just prior to the pass arriving? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with a 15-yard Defensive Pass Interference penalty.
choice_c: A - 3/5 @ A-30 - Snap, with no foul since the pass did not cross the neutral zone.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the clock stopped for the penalty.
explanation: B1 has committed a foul for Defensive Holding (9-3-4-e). The basic spot for this legal forward pass play is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and the 10-yard penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-30 → A-40 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. QB A1 throws a pass from the A-22 toward receiver A84 at the A-24. B1 is playing the ball and pushes A84 in the back just prior to the ball arriving. The pass falls incomplete. What if B1, who was off the line of scrimmage at the snap, blocked A84 below the waist just prior to the pass arriving? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with a 10-yard Defensive Holding penalty.
choice_c: A - 3/5 @ A-30 - Snap, with no foul since B1 was playing the ball.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Ready, with the clock stopped for the penalty.
explanation: B1 is guilty of an Illegal Block Below the Waist (9-1-6-b-2). The basic spot for this legal forward pass play is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and the 15-yard penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-30 → A-45 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 4/7 @ A-45. A1, lined up 10 yards deep to punt, muffs the snap which falls to the ground. A1 picks up the loose ball, but before he is able to kick, he is knocked down by B1. B6 recovers the ball at the A-30. What if, as A1 is attempting to recover the loose ball, B1 tackles him? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-40 - Ready, with a 15-yard personal-foul penalty for the tackle.
choice_b: A - 4/2 @ 50 - Ready, with no foul since B1 may block A1.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, with Team B keeping the ball after B6's recovery.
explanation: B1 may use his hands or arms to ward off or block A1 in attempt to get to the loose ball (9-3-4-b-1), but he may not tackle him (9-3-4-d). This a running play (2-30-4) behind the neutral zone, so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The 10-yard penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-45 → B-45 (10-2-2-c-3). This was not a legal kick down, so since the clock was stopped only to complete the penalty, the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 4/7 @ A-45. A1, lined up 10 yards deep to punt, muffs the snap which falls to the ground. A1 picks up the loose ball, but before he is able to kick, he is knocked down by B1. B6 recovers the ball at the A-30. What if, as A1 is attempting to recover the loose ball, B1 leads with his shoulder and forcibly contacts A1's head? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-45 - Ready, with a 10-yard Defensive Holding penalty instead of Targeting.
choice_b: A - 4/2 @ 50 - Ready, with no foul since A1 was not a defenseless player.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ A-30 - Snap, with Team B retaining the ball at the recovery spot.
explanation: A1 is defenseless since he is attempting to recover a loose ball (2-27-14-k), so B1 is guilty of Targeting (9-1-4). The basic spot for this running play (2-30-4) behind the neutral zone is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-1-a). The 15-yard penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-45 → B-40 (10-2-2-c-3). This was not a legal kick down, so since the clock was stopped only to complete the penalty, the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A8 carries to the A-42, then retreats behind the neutral zone. From the A-38, he completes a pass to A29 at the 50 where he runs out of bounds. What if this play occurred in the 4th quarter, and A29 ran out of bounds with 1:23 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/12 @ A-33 - Ready (1:13), with a 10-second runoff applied.
choice_c: A - 3/7 @ A-38 - Snap (1:23), with no loss of down for the illegal pass.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap (1:23), with no foul since A29 was beyond the neutral zone.
explanation: A8's forward pass is illegal (7-3-2-e), and the penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-38 → A-33, and a loss of down (7-3-2-e-Penalty). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because this is a complete Illegal Forward Pass (3-4-4-a-3). The clock starts on the Snap because A29 ran out of bounds (3-3-2-d-2). The Referee's option to start the clock on the Ready does not apply because this was not an illegal forward pass thrown to conserve time (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A8 carries to the A-42, then retreats behind the neutral zone. From the A-38, he completes a pass to A29 at the 50 where he runs out of bounds. What if this play occurred in the 4th quarter, and the pass to A29 fell incomplete with 1:23 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/12 @ A-33 - Snap (1:23) only, with no runoff option on an incomplete illegal pass.
choice_c: A - 3/12 @ A-33 - Ready (1:13) only, with no option to decline the runoff.
choice_d: A - 3/7 @ A-38 - Ready (1:13), with the penalty enforced without a loss of down.
explanation: A8's forward pass is illegal (7-3-2-e), and the penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-38 → A-33, and a loss of down (7-3-2-e-Penalty). Because this is an incomplete Illegal Forward Pass, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). If the runoff is accepted, the clock will start on the Ready, and if it is declined the clock will start on the Snap (3-4-4-c). Team A may avoid the runoff by the use of a charged team timeout, if available, in which case the clock would start on the Snap (3-4-4-d).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-34. With the score tied late in the 4th quarter, Team A’s field goal attempt lands short in the end zone untouched as time expires. Video shows that when the ball touched the ground, 0:02 remained on the game clock. What if the field goal was successful, and video showed that the kick touched the net with 0:02 in the 4th quarter? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Go to extra periods, since the successful field goal ends regulation tied.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules (0:00), with no time restored.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap (0:02), with the field goal ruled no good.
explanation: The ball is dead and when it strikes the net (4-1-3-a), and the clock should stop and not restart until the ensuing free kick is legally touched in the field (3-3-2-a). Since the point differential is eight points or less (12-3-6-d-3), Replay may restore any amount of time to the 4th quarter clock (12-3-6-d-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-34. With the score tied late in the 4th quarter, Team A’s field goal attempt lands short in the end zone untouched as time expires. Video shows that when the ball touched the ground, 0:02 remained on the game clock. What if the original play occurred in the 2nd quarter? What is the ruling?
choice_a: Reviewable. B - 1/10 @ B-34 - Snap (0:02), with time restored to the 2nd-quarter clock.
choice_b: Reviewable. B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap (0:02), with the ball at the 20 and time restored.
choice_c: Not Reviewable. Half Over, since down-by-contact spots are never reviewed.
explanation: Even though the ball is dead when it lands in B's end zone untouched by B (4-1-3-c), the clock would next start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-8), and the point differential is eight points or less (12-3-6-d-3), Replay uses the 40-yard line as a guideline for restoring time in the 2nd quarter (2025 Replay Case Book, p.35, next-to-last bullet). Because the ball would be returned to the B-34 (8-4-2-b-1), Replay will not restore the time (2025 Replay Case Book, Play 143).

question_text: 4/2 @ B-42. A1 attempts to hand the ball to running back A2 at the B-45 but A2 muffs the exchange. The ball bounces off A2’s hands, but just as the ball is near but not touching the ground, A2 gains possession at the B-43 and runs to the B-38 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: Loss of player possession by unsuccessful execution of attempted handing is a fumble by the last player in possession (2-13-1-c), but if the handoff is ultimately successful (i.e., it does not touch the ground), there is no fumble, and A2's advance is legal.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A45's punt is fielded by B3 at the B-35 and returned to the 50 where he is downed. During the return, A45 was running toward B3 when B54 approached A45 from the front and launched into him with his right shoulder, making forcible contact to A45’s head at the A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: Unless he is actively blocking or tackling, the kicker continues to be considered defenseless during the kick and any subsequent return (2-27-14-c). So, B54 is guilty of Targeting a defenseless player in the head / neck area (9-1-4). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the 50 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot (at the A-40), the penalty is enforced from the basic spot, 50 → B-35 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Both teams are lined up in position, and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. B21 signals for a fair catch, and completes the catch at the B-5. If there is a foul, Team B will accept the penalty. What is the ruling?
explanation: There is no foul because the coach-to-player communications rule is not in effect on free kick downs, so there is no limitation to the number of green dot helmets for either team (1-4-11-b-Bullet #4). Since B21 signaled for and completed a fair catch behind the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the B-25 (6-5-1-a-Exception).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A92’s punt is untouched and is bouncing at the B-3. A33 blocks B21 into the ball, forcing the ball into B's end zone where A33 falls on it. What is the ruling?
explanation: A33's block of B21 into the ball is considered forced touching, and so by rule B21 has not touched the ball (2-11-4-c-i). Thus, when the ball touches the ground in B's end zone untouched by B, the ball is dead (6-3-7-a), the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b), and because this is a scrimmage kick, the ball will next be put into play at the B-20 (8-6-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter with the score A-23, B-26 and neither team with a timeout remaining. A11's pass over the middle is complete to A88 where B27 tackles him inbounds at the B-15. B27 remains on the ground, and the BJ declares an official’s timeout for injury with 0:05 remaining on the game clock. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:05).  There is an option for a 10-second runoff, which Team A will decline.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:05).  There is no option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:05).  There is an option for a 10-second runoff, which Team A will decline.
choice_d: Game Over.  There is a 10-second runoff which cannot be avoided since neither team has a timeout.
explanation: If a player injury occurs on a play where there is a temporary stoppage of the clock for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f-Exception). Team A will decline the runoff for the Team B injury, and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-6-f-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is midway through the 3rd quarter with the clock running and both teams with all timeouts remaining. Team A is at the line and ready to snap the ball when the Team A head coach requests a timeout, which is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation, and the ball is ready for play. Just before the snap, A77 commits a false start, and the penalty is enforced. Both teams are back in formation with the ball ready, and just before the play clock expires, A1 requests a timeout. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Snap.  The time out is granted, but Team A is penalized for Delay of Game.
choice_b: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Snap.  The time out is granted, and Team A is not penalized for Delay of Game.
choice_c: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Snap.  The time out is not granted, and Team A is penalized for Delay of Game.
explanation: At no time during this sequence was the ball live; therefore, all of this action takes place during the same dead ball period. Since consecutive charged team timeouts are not allowed by the same team in any individual dead ball period (3-3-4), A1's request for a time will not be granted, and as a result, the play clock will expire. Team A was penalized B-30 → B-35 for the False Start (7-1-3-d-Penalty), and is now penalized B-35 → B-40 for the Delay of Game (3-4-2-a-Penalty). The clock starts on the Snap because of the first, properly-granted timeout (3-3-2-d-5).

question_text: 3/G @ B-½. With the clock running, center A55 slides the ball forward so the ball is at the goal line. A4 takes the snap and sneaks the ball across the goal line. What is the ruling?
explanation: The snapper shall not be permitted to move the ball more than ½ yard forward when taking his stance nor allow the ball to be moved over the line to gain or goal line. The play shall be shut down, the ball reset, and the player and his coach warned (Officiating Standards, Section 2, Number 11). Since the play was shut down, there was no touchdown, and the clock was stopped only for an officials timeout. So, the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-12).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. A88 catches a legal forward pass and fumbles when hit nine yards beyond the line of scrimmage. The fumble is recovered by grounded A24 five yards in advance of the fumble. During the pass, B54 is guilty of roughing the passer. What is the ruling?
explanation: A88 fumbled at the A-34 (25 + 9) with A24 recovering at the A-39 (34 + 5). A88's run ended at the spot of the fumble (2-25-8-b), but the last run ended at the spot of A24's recovery (2-25-8-a). Since the last run ended beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of possession during the down, the penalty for B54's personal foul is enforced from the end of the last run, A-39 → B-46 (10-2-2-e). After the penalty is completed, the clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-d-4).

question_text: 4/2 @ B-42. A1 attempts to hand the ball to running back A2 at the B-45 but A2 muffs the exchange. The ball bounces off A2’s hands, but just as the ball is near but not touching the ground, A2 gains possession at the B-43 and runs to the B-38 where he is tackled. What if, after the muffed exchange from A1 at the B-45, the ball hit the ground with A2 recovering at the B-43? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-43 - Running, with A2's recovery allowing a legal advance.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-43 - Snap, with the ball left at the spot of recovery.
choice_d: A - 4/2 @ B-42 - Snap, with the down replayed after the muffed exchange.
explanation: Because the ball hit the ground, this handoff is not considered successful, and this is a fumble attributed to A1 (2-13-1-c). Since it is 4th down and the ball was recovered by A2 beyond the spot of the fumble, the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-2-a-Exception 2).

question_text: 4/2 @ B-42. A1 attempts to hand the ball to running back A2 at the B-45 but A2 muffs the exchange. The ball bounces off A2’s hands, but just as the ball is near but not touching the ground, A2 gains possession at the B-43 and runs to the B-38 where he is tackled. What if, after the muffed exchange from A1 at the B-45, the ball hit the ground with A2 recovering at the B-47? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-47 - Running, with A2's recovery allowing a legal advance.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-45 - Snap, with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble.
choice_d: A - 4/2 @ B-42 - Snap, with the down replayed.
explanation: Because the ball hit the ground, this handoff is not considered successful, and this is a fumble attributed to A1 (2-13-1-c). Since it is 4th down and the ball was recovered by A2 behind the spot of the fumble, the ball remains at the spot of recovery (7-2-2-a-Exception 2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A45's punt is fielded by B3 at the B-35 and returned to the 50 where he is downed. During the return, A45 was running toward B3 when B54 approached A45 from the front and launched into him with his right shoulder, making forcible contact to A45’s head at the A-40. What if B3 returned the kick to the A-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-35 - Snap, with the ball left at the end of B3's return.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, with the penalty enforced from the basic spot at the end of the run.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-45 - Snap, with Team A retaining the ball after the Targeting foul.
explanation: B54 is guilty of Targeting a defenseless player in the head / neck area (2-27-14-c / 9-1-4). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-35 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot (at the A-40), the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, A-40 → B-45 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A45's punt is fielded by B3 at the B-35 and returned to the 50 where he is downed. During the return, A45 was running toward B3 when B54 approached A45 from the front and launched into him with his right shoulder, making forcible contact to A45’s head at the A-40. What if B3 returned the kick to the 50 where he fumbled, with A82 recovering and carrying across B's goal line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock only, with the Targeting penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap, with the touchdown negated by the Targeting foul.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock only, with the penalty enforced solely on the Try.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock, then A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules or A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, then A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
explanation: B54 is guilty of Targeting a defenseless player in the head / neck area (2-27-14-c / 9-1-4). Fifteen-yard penalties for personal or unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by the nonscoring team during a down that ends in a touchdown are enforced on the Try, the succeeding kickoff, or from the succeeding spot in extra periods (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Both teams are lined up in position, and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. B21 signals for a fair catch, and completes the catch at the B-5. If there is a foul, Team B will accept the penalty. What if Team A had two players wearing number 82 during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules only, with no tack-on option on a free kick.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the ball left at the fair-catch spot with no enforcement.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with the tack-on from the B-25 touchback spot.
explanation: No two players of the same team may participate in the same down wearing identical numbers (1-4-2-b). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-35 → A-30, with a re- kick (1-4-2-b-Penalty), but penalties for all fouls (except KCI) by the kicking team during a free kick play may also be enforced at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-1-8). Since B21 signaled for and completed a fair catch behind the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the B-25 (6-5-1-a-Exception), and the penalty for Team A's foul could then be enforced B-25 → B-30.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. Both teams are lined up in position, and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. B21 signals for a fair catch, and completes the catch at the B-5. If there is a foul, Team B will accept the penalty. What if Team B had two players wearing number 41 during the down? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - F/K @ A-40 - F/K Rules only, with no option for Team A to decline.
choice_b: A - F/K @ A-40 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with tack-on enforcement against Team B.
choice_d: A - F/K @ A-30 - F/K Rules or B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap, with the re-kick five yards toward Team A's goal.
explanation: No two players of the same team may participate in the same down wearing identical numbers (1-4-2-b). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-35 → A-40, with a re- kick (1-4-2-b-Penalty), or Team A may decline the penalty (10-1-1-b). Since B21 signaled for and completed a fair catch behind the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the B-25 (6-5-1-a-Exception).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A92’s punt is untouched and is bouncing at the B-3. A33 blocks B21 into the ball, forcing the ball into B's end zone where A33 falls on it. What if A33 and B21 were actively engaged in blocking one another when the loose ball touched B21? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with a touchback since B21 did not touch the ball.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the clock stopped for the touchdown.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-3 - Snap, with the ball dead where A33 recovered.
explanation: Because A33 and B21 were engaged in blocking, this is not considered forced touching, and so B21 did touch the ball (AR-2-11-4-I-a). So, the ball is not dead when it touches the ground in B's end zone (6-3-7-a), A33's recovery is legal (6-3-3), and the result of the play is a touchdown (8-2-1-d).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A92’s punt is untouched and is bouncing at the B-3. A33 blocks B21 into the ball, forcing the ball into B's end zone where A33 falls on it. What if A33 batted the loose ball at the B-5, into B21 at the B-3? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/20 @ A-35 - Snap only, with no tack-on option for Team B.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - Snap, with a touchdown since A33 recovered in the end zone.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-20 - Snap, with a touchback and the illegal-bat penalty declined.
choice_d: A - 4/20 @ A-35 - Snap or B - 1/10 @ B-30 - Snap.
explanation: A33's bat is forward and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c), and whenever a loose ball is batted (legally or illegally) into an opponent, it is considered forced touching, and so by rule B21 has not touched the kick (2-11-4-c-ii). Thus, when the ball touches the ground in B's end zone untouched by B, the ball is dead (6-3-7-a) and the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). The penalty for A33's foul may be enforced from the previous spot, A-45 → A-35 (10-2-2-d-4-a) with no loss of down since the foul occurred when a legal scrimmage kick was beyond the neutral zone (9-4-1-c-Penalty), or it may be enforced at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B (6-3-13), which would be the B-20 because of the touchback (8-6-2), and so B-20 → B-30.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter with the score A-23, B-26 and neither team with a timeout remaining. A11's pass over the middle is complete to A88 where B27 tackles him inbounds at the B-15. B27 remains on the ground, and the BJ declares an official’s timeout for injury with 0:05 remaining on the game clock. What if the injury was to A88 with 0:08 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:08), with the clock on the snap after the injury.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:08), with Team B declining the runoff.
choice_d: A - 1/G @ B-10 - Ready (0:08), with a Delay of Game foul for the injury timeout.
explanation: If a player injury occurs on a play where there is a temporary stoppage of the clock for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10- second runoff (3-3-6-f-Exception). Team B will accept the runoff for the Team A injury, ending the game.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter with the score A-23, B-26 and neither team with a timeout remaining. A11's pass over the middle is complete to A88 where B27 tackles him inbounds at the B-15. B27 remains on the ground, and the BJ declares an official’s timeout for injury with 0:05 remaining on the game clock. What if the injury was to A88 with 0:11 remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Snap (0:11), with the clock on the snap after the injury.
choice_c: End Of Regulation, with Team B accepting a 10-second runoff.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-15 - Ready (0:01), with a 10-second runoff applied.
explanation: The clock was stopped for two reasons -- to award a new series to Team A and because of the injury to A88. Since the player injury was not the only reason for stopping the clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, and since there are more than 10 seconds remaining, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-1 / 3-3-2-e-5).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is midway through the 3rd quarter with the clock running and both teams with all timeouts remaining. Team A is at the line and ready to snap the ball when the Team A head coach requests a timeout, which is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation, and the ball is ready for play. Just before the snap, A77 commits a false start, and the penalty is enforced. Both teams are back in formation with the ball ready, and just before the play clock expires, A1 requests a timeout. What if, just before the play clock expires, B23 requests a timeout? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/15 @ B-40 - Snap, with a Delay of Game penalty added for the second timeout request.
choice_b: A - 3/10 @ B-35 - Ready, with the clock on the Ready after the timeout.
choice_d: A - 3/10 @ B-30 - Snap, with the False Start penalty declined.
explanation: While consecutive charged team timeouts are not allowed by the same team in any individual dead ball period, there is no prohibition against a time out by each team (3-3-4). B23's request is granted, and the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-2-d-5). It is 3/10 because Team A was penalized B-30 → B-35 for the False Start.

question_text: 3/G @ B-½. With the clock running, center A55 slides the ball forward so the ball is at the goal line. A4 takes the snap and sneaks the ball across the goal line. What if Team A had previously been warned for moving the ball forward? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/G @ B-½ - Ready, with only a warning and no penalty.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-3 - No Clock, with the sneak counting as a touchdown.
choice_c: A - 3/G @ B-5½ - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the penalty.
choice_d: A - 3/G @ B-5½ - Ready, at the Referee's discretion.
explanation: A foul for a False Start shall be considered after a warning. (Officiating Standards, Section 2, Number 11). Assuming the Referee opts to penalize this action, the ball remains dead and the clock is started on the Ready after completion of the penalty (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. A88 catches a legal forward pass and fumbles when hit nine yards beyond the line of scrimmage. The fumble is recovered by grounded A24 five yards in advance of the fumble. During the pass, B54 is guilty of roughing the passer. What if A88's fumble had rolled out of bounds at the A-39? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-46 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the end of A24's run.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-49 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the fumble out of bounds.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: The end of the last run is the spot of A88's fumble, the A-34 (2-25-8-b).
