Since the last run ended beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of possession during the down, the penalty for B54's personal foul is enforced from the end of the last run, A-34 → A-49 (10-2-2-e). After both a forward fumble out of bounds and completion of the penalty, the clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2 / 3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. A88 catches a legal forward pass and fumbles when hit nine yards beyond the line of scrimmage. The fumble is recovered by grounded A24 five yards in advance of the fumble. During the pass, B54 is guilty of roughing the passer. What if A88's fumble had been recovered by B22 at the A-39? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-46 - Ready, with tack-on enforcement from the end of the last run.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ A-40 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the change of possession.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ A-49 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the fumble.
explanation: Because there was a change of possession during the down, "tack-on" enforcement does not apply (10-2-2-e), and so the penalty for B54's personal foul is enforced from the previous spot, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-d-3). Even though there was a change of possession during the down, Team B is not the next to snap the ball, so the clock will not start on the snap (3-3-2-d-3). After penalty completion, the clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 3/8 @ A-36. A7's pass is complete to slot receiver A81 at the 50 where he is tackled. A81 was covered up by outside receiver A12 who was on the same yard line. What is the ruling?
explanation: While A81 has a "good number", he is an ineligible receiver because he is neither a back nor on the end of the line of scrimmage (7-3-3-c). He commits two fouls: Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10) and Illegal Touching (7-3-11). Team B will decline the penalty for IDP as it is simply five yards from the previous spot (7-3-10-Penalty) and accept the penalty for ITP, which is five yards from the previous spot, A-36 → A-31 and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 1/15 @ B-47. Both teams have three timeouts remaining in the half. A4's pass is complete to A13 at the B-37 where he is downed. After the play and prior to the ball being spotted for the next play, B11 tells the officials he is having issues with his chin strap. He appears to get it fixed and the officials put the ball on the ground for the next play. After the ball is spotted, B11 takes his helmet off and goes down on a knee. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Ready.  Delay of Game.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Snap.  Delay of Game.
explanation: A chin strap is mandatory equipment (1-4-4-a-1), and so unless B11 can resolve the issue, he must either leave the game or his team must use a charged timeout (1-4-8-b). Here, in essence, B11 by doing neither is holding up the game by "presenting as injured after the ball is spotted", and Team B should be charged a timeout (3-3-6-a-1). From a preventative officiating standpoint, when B11 first reports equipment difficulties, officials might encourage him to leave the game.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-36. A17 escapes the pocket, and under duress at the A-28 throws a pass that is caught by A67 at the A-30 and advanced to the A-48 where he is tackled. There were no eligible offensive receivers in the area when A67 caught the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A17 was outside the tackle box, the pass did not cross the neutral zone (7-3-2-h-Exception), and since there were no eligible receivers in the area, this is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-h). A67 is ineligible (7-3-3-c), but there can be no foul for Illegal Touching because that rule applies only to legal forward passes (7-3-11). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the A-28 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-30, B-24 in the first possession series of the second extra period. B33 intercepts A12's pass in the end zone and returns the ball to the B-35 where he is tackled by the facemask. What is the ruling?
explanation: Penalties for fouls against either team after a change of possession are declined by rule in extra periods (3-1-3-g-1), unless the foul is a FUDL foul (3-1-3-g-1-Exception). A facemask foul is a personal foul, but not a flagrant personal foul, so the penalty is declined by rule, and Team B will begin their possession series at the A-25.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-4. A2 drops back in the end zone. While being tackled but prior to being down, A2 is stripped of the ball and it rolls forward to the A-2 where it is muffed by A7. The ball is scooped up by A7 at the A-4 and advanced to the A-6 where he is tackled. After A2 is tackled and he hits the ground, he loses his helmet. While the ball is loose, A2 picks up his helmet and pursues the loose ball with his helmet in his hands. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A2's fumble on 4th down is recovered by A7 beyond the spot of the fumble, the ball is dead when recovered and is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-2-a-Exception 2), which is in A's end zone. Since the ball is dead by rule in A's end zone, and A2 caused it to be there, the result of the play is a Safety (8-5-1-a). A2 fouled by continuing to participate after losing his helmet (9-1-17), but Team B will decline that penalty. There is no "carry-over" as that applies only to personal or unsportsmanlike fouls by the non-scoring team during a down which ends in a TD (10-2-5-a-1) or successful FG (10-2-5-d), but not a Safety.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach notifies the Field Judge that he wants the ball on the near hash. The kick is high near the far sideline. B18 signals for a fair catch and catches the ball at the B-5 near the sideline. The Line Judge rules B18 had his left foot inbounds and his right foot in the air out of bounds when he caught the ball. Video shows that B18's left foot was on the ground inbounds and his right foot was on the ground out of bounds when he caught the ball. What is the ruling?
explanation: B18 is out of bounds (4-2-1-a), and when he touches the kick, the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). Replay will overturn this to a free kick out of bounds untouched by an inbounds player of Team B (6-2-1). Team B will likely take the ball 30 yards from the previous spot, A-35 → B-35 (6-2-1-Penalty) with the ball at the nearest hash mark, the Far Hash (6-2-2).

question_text: 3/8 @ A-28. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A8 is immediately under duress and rolls out to his right, and as he is being tackled at the A-18 throws the ball incomplete near an eligible offensive receiver (0:54). Video shows that A8's knee was down prior to releasing the pass (0:57). What is the ruling?
explanation: Replay will overturn this to A8 down at the A-18, and properly officiated, the clock would have continued to run. Replay is therefore reversing to a running clock, and there will be a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:57) and implement the runoff, 0:57 → 0:47. The clock will then start of the Ready. A "replay runoff" may not be simply declined, but only avoided through the use of an available charged timeout.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-38. The score is A-23, B-27 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12's pass is complete to A3 at the B-28 where he is tackled (1:00). A69 is injured during the play. Video shows that A3's knee was down with the ball at the B-29 (1:02). What is the ruling?
explanation: Replay will overturn this to A3 down at the B-29, and properly officiated, the clock would have continued to run. Normally when Replay reverses to a running clock, there is the option for a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c); however, if there is a helmet off or an injury in conjunction with this, there is no runoff (2025 Replay Case Book, p.36, 4th bullet). Also, the clock is not reset (2023 CFO Instant Replay Memo 1). The clock remains at 1:00, and starts on the Ready.

question_text: 3/8 @ A-36. A7's pass is complete to slot receiver A81 at the 50 where he is tackled. A81 was covered up by outside receiver A12 who was on the same yard line. What if A81 caught the pass after it was tipped by B24, who was defending him? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/13 @ A-31 - Ready, with a loss of down for Illegal Touching added.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Running, with no foul since B24 tipped the pass.
choice_d: A - 3/18 @ A-26 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the catch.
explanation: When B24 touched the pass, A81 became eligible (7-3-5), so there is no foul for Illegal Touching (7-3-11); however, A81 was still ineligible when he went downfield (7-3-10). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-36 → A-31, with no loss of down (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 3/8 @ A-36. A7's pass is complete to slot receiver A81 at the 50 where he is tackled. A81 was covered up by outside receiver A12 who was on the same yard line. What if A81 stepped back and caught A7's forward pass at the A-34, then carried to the 50 where he was downed? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/13 @ A-31 - Ready, with no loss of down since the pass did not cross the neutral zone.
choice_c: A - 1/10 @ 50 - Running, with no foul since A81 stepped back inbounds.
choice_d: A - 4/18 @ A-26 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the spot of the touch.
explanation: A81 is not guilty of Ineligible Receiver Downfield because the pass did not cross the neutral zone (7-3-10); however, he is still guilty of Illegal Touching as this may occur anywhere on the field (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-36 → A-31, with a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 1/15 @ B-47. Both teams have three timeouts remaining in the half. A4's pass is complete to A13 at the B-37 where he is downed. After the play and prior to the ball being spotted for the next play, B11 tells the officials he is having issues with his chin strap. He appears to get it fixed and the officials put the ball on the ground for the next play. After the ball is spotted, B11 takes his helmet off and goes down on a knee. What if this occurred in the mid-2nd quarter with Team B already out of timeouts? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Snap, with the clock starting on the snap after the penalty.
choice_b: A - 2/5 @ B-37 - Ready, with an officials timeout and no penalty.
choice_d: A - 1/10 @ B-42 - Ready, with the penalty enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: Because B11 "presented as injured after the ball was spotted" and Team B has no timeouts remaining, they are charged with Delay of Game (3-3-6-a-1). The five-yard penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-37 → B-32 (10-2-2-a), and the clock will start on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 1/15 @ B-47. Both teams have three timeouts remaining in the half. A4's pass is complete to A13 at the B-37 where he is downed. After the play and prior to the ball being spotted for the next play, B11 tells the officials he is having issues with his chin strap. He appears to get it fixed and the officials put the ball on the ground for the next play. After the ball is spotted, B11 takes his helmet off and goes down on a knee. What if this occurred with Team B out of timeouts, and officials stopping the game with 0:54 in the 2nd quarter? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Ready (0:44) only, with no option to decline the runoff.
choice_b: A - 1/10 @ B-32 - Snap (0:54) only, with no runoff since the score is not close.
choice_c: A - 2/5 @ B-37 - Ready (0:54), with an officials timeout and no penalty.
explanation: Because B11 "presented as injured after the ball was spotted" and Team B has no timeouts remaining, they are charged with Delay of Game (3-3-6-a-1). The five-yard penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot, B-37 → B-32 (10-2-2-a). Since the injury / required penalty was the only reason for stopping the clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team A has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The clock will start on the Ready if they accept the runoff, 0:54 → 0:44, and on the Snap if they decline it (3-3-6-f-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-36. A17 escapes the pocket, and under duress at the A-28 throws a pass that is caught by A67 at the A-30 and advanced to the A-48 where he is tackled. There were no eligible offensive receivers in the area when A67 caught the pass. What if this occurred in the 4th quarter of a tie game with A67 catching the pass at 1:35 and being tackled at 1:29? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 3/18 @ A-28 - Ready (1:29), with no snap-clock option for the defense.
choice_c: A - 3/18 @ A-28 - Option (1:19), with a 10-second runoff applied.
choice_d: A - 3/15 @ A-31 - Option (1:29), with an Illegal Touching penalty instead of Illegal Forward Pass.
explanation: As in (3), this is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-h) -- not Illegal Touching (7-3-11) -- and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the A-28 (7-3-2-h-Penalty). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because this is a complete illegal forward pass (3-4-4-a-3), but because the score is tied and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, Team B has the option to start it on the Snap (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-36. A17 escapes the pocket, and under duress at the A-28 throws a pass that is caught by A67 at the A-30 and advanced to the A-48 where he is tackled. There were no eligible offensive receivers in the area when A67 caught the pass. What if everything in (3a) occurred with eligible A85 near A67? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/18 @ A-28 - Option (1:29), with an Illegal Forward Pass penalty since no receiver was eligible.
choice_c: A - 3/15 @ A-31 - Ready (1:29), with no snap-clock option for the defense.
choice_d: A - 3/15 @ A-31 - Option (1:19), with a 10-second runoff applied.
explanation: Because A85 was in the area, this is not an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-h), but A67 is is ineligible (7-3-3-c) and is therefore guilty of Illegal Touching (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-36 → A-31, with a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty). There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the foul did not cause the clock to stop immediately (3-4-4-a), but because the score is tied and the clock would start by rule on the Ready, Team B has the option to start it on the Snap (3-4-3-b).

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-30, B-24 in the first possession series of the second extra period. B33 intercepts A12's pass in the end zone and returns the ball to the B-35 where he is tackled by the facemask. What if B33 was brought down by a crown of the helmet hit, upheld by Replay as Targeting? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ A-25 - No Clock, with the Targeting penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-40 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced away from Team B's goal.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock, with B33 not disqualified for the Targeting.
explanation: Penalties for FUDL fouls against either team after a change of possession are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-1-Exception), so Team B will begin their possession series at the A-25 → A-12½.

question_text: Try @ B-3. The score is A-30, B-24 in the first possession series of the second extra period. B33 intercepts A12's pass in the end zone and returns the ball to the B-35 where he is tackled by the facemask. What if (4a) took place in the first possession series of the third extra period with the score A-24, B-24? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with the Targeting penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ A-12½ - No Clock, with the penalty enforced from the second-extra-period spot.
choice_c: B - Try @ A-18 - No Clock, with the penalty enforced away from Team B's goal.
explanation: Penalties for FUDL fouls against either team after a change of possession are enforced on the succeeding play (3-1-3-g-1-Exception), so Team B will begin their possession series at the A-3 → A-1½.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-4. A2 drops back in the end zone. While being tackled but prior to being down, A2 is stripped of the ball and it rolls forward to the A-2 where it is muffed by A7. The ball is scooped up by A7 at the A-4 and advanced to the A-6 where he is tackled. After A2 is tackled and he hits the ground, he loses his helmet. While the ball is loose, A2 picks up his helmet and pursues the loose ball with his helmet in his hands. What if A2's fumble from his end zone had been scooped up by B98 at the A-4, and carried across A's goal line? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - Try @ A-1½ - No Clock + B - F/K @ B-35 - F/K Rules or B - Try @ A-3- No Clock + B - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_b: B - Try @ A-3 - No Clock, with the helmet-participation penalty declined by rule.
choice_c: B - Try @ A-1½ - No Clock only, with the penalty enforceable solely on the Try.
choice_d: No score, with A2's foul negating the touchdown.
explanation: The ball continues in play when recovered by B98 (7-2-2-a), so the result of the play is a Touchdown. A2 fouled by continuing to participate after losing his helmet (9-1-17), and the penalty for this personal foul by the non-scoring team during a down which ends in a Touchdown is enforced either on the Try or the succeeding kickoff (10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/9 @ A-4. A2 drops back in the end zone. While being tackled but prior to being down, A2 is stripped of the ball and it rolls forward to the A-2 where it is muffed by A7. The ball is scooped up by A7 at the A-4 and advanced to the A-6 where he is tackled. After A2 is tackled and he hits the ground, he loses his helmet. While the ball is loose, A2 picks up his helmet and pursues the loose ball with his helmet in his hands. What if A2 carried to the A-14 where he fumbled, lost his helmet, and continued to participate, and A7 scooped up the ball at the A-17 and advanced to the A-20 where he was tackled? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 1/G @ A-14 - Ready, with A2's helmet foul declined by rule.
choice_c: A - 4/6 @ A-7 - Snap, with the clock on the snap after the recovery.
choice_d: A - 4/13 @ A-14 - Ready, with the penalty enforced the full 15 yards from the spot of participation.
explanation: When A2's fumble on 4th down is recovered by A7 beyond the spot of the fumble, the ball is dead when recovered and is returned to the spot of the fumble (7-2-2-a-Exception 2), which is the A-14, resulting in a first down for Team A. But, A2 fouled by continuing to participate after losing his helmet (9-1-17), and the penalty is enforced half-the-distance from the spot of his participation, A-14 → A-7.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach notifies the Field Judge that he wants the ball on the near hash. The kick is high near the far sideline. B18 signals for a fair catch and catches the ball at the B-5 near the sideline. The Line Judge rules B18 had his left foot inbounds and his right foot in the air out of bounds when he caught the ball. Video shows that B18's left foot was on the ground inbounds and his right foot was on the ground out of bounds when he caught the ball. What if the video evidence had not shown clearly and obviously that the ruling on the field was incorrect? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-5 - Snap - Near Hash, with the ball dead where B18 caught it.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ B-25 - Snap - Far Hash, with the ball placed at the coach's requested hash.
choice_d: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap - Far Hash, with a free-kick-out-of-bounds award since B18 was out of bounds.
explanation: Based on the original ruling on the field, B18 completed the catch at the B-5 after having given a fair catch signal. Whenever a player makes a fair catch of a free kick behind the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the B-25 (6-5-1-a-Exception), and the "touchback-like" nature of this result allows Team B to designate the lateral position of the ball, which they chose to be the Near Hash.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. The Team B head coach notifies the Field Judge that he wants the ball on the near hash. The kick is high near the far sideline. B18 signals for a fair catch and catches the ball at the B-5 near the sideline. The Line Judge rules B18 had his left foot inbounds and his right foot in the air out of bounds when he caught the ball. Video shows that B18's left foot was on the ground inbounds and his right foot was on the ground out of bounds when he caught the ball. What if all of B18's actions (including the overturning video evidence) had occurred at the B-35? What is the ruling?
choice_a: B - 1/10 @ B-35 - Snap - Far Hash, with the ball left where B18 touched it.
choice_b: B - 1/10 @ 50 - Snap - Far Hash, with a 30-yard award from the previous spot.
choice_c: B - 1/10 @ B-40 - Snap - Near Hash, with the ball placed at the coach's requested hash.
explanation: B18 is out of bounds (4-2-1-a), and when he touches the kick, the ball is out of bounds (4-2-3-a). Replay will overturn this to a free kick out of bounds untouched by an inbounds player of Team B (6-2-1). Team B will likely take the ball five yards from where the subsequent dead ball belongs to them, B-35 → B-40 (6-2-1-Penalty) with the ball at the nearest hash mark, the Far Hash (6-2-2).

question_text: 3/8 @ A-28. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A8 is immediately under duress and rolls out to his right, and as he is being tackled at the A-18 throws the ball incomplete near an eligible offensive receiver (0:54). Video shows that A8's knee was down prior to releasing the pass (0:57). What if both teams had one timeout remaining? What is the ruling?
choice_b: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Ready (0:47) only, with no option to avoid the runoff.
choice_c: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Snap (0:57) only, with no runoff since the clock was stopped.
choice_d: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Ready (0:44), with the runoff taken from the ruled-dead time of 0:54.
explanation: As in (7), Replay will put A8 down at the A-18, and in so doing is reversing to a running clock. This presents the opportunity for a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c), which either team may avoid through the use of an available charge timeout. The clock will be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:57), and the runoff implemented, 0:57 → 0:47, unless a charged timeout is used. The clock would then start on the Ready if there is a runoff, and on the Snap if a timeout is called (12-3-6-c).

question_text: 3/8 @ A-28. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A8 is immediately under duress and rolls out to his right, and as he is being tackled at the A-18 throws the ball incomplete near an eligible offensive receiver (0:54). Video shows that A8's knee was down prior to releasing the pass (0:57). What if, in the original question, Team A had been in an Illegal Formation at the snap, and the score is A-17, B-13? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Ready (0:47), with a 10-second runoff since the clock is reset to a running clock.
choice_c: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Ready (0:57), with no snap-clock option for the defense.
choice_d: A - 4/18 @ A-18 - Snap (0:54), with the Illegal Formation penalty enforced.
explanation: Replay will put A8 down at the A-18, but is reversing to a stopped clock since the clock would have stopped anyway to complete the penalty for Team A's foul. Team B will decline the penalty, but the clock will be reset to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:57), and there is no runoff (12-3-6-b). Since the clock would start by rule on the Ready and the foul was by the team ahead in score, Team B has the option to start on the Snap (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-38. The score is A-23, B-27 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12's pass is complete to A3 at the B-28 where he is tackled (1:00). A69 is injured during the play. Video shows that A3's knee was down with the ball at the B-29 (1:02). What if the ball had correctly been ruled dead at the B-29 (1:02), and the clock then stopped for A69's injury (1:00)? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Ready (0:50) only, with no option to decline the runoff.
choice_b: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Snap (1:00) only, with no runoff option after the injury.
choice_d: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Ready (1:00), with no runoff since the injury did not stop the clock.
explanation: Since A69's injury was the only reason for stopping the clock after the Two-Minute Timeout, Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). If they accept the runoff, 1:00 → 0:50, the clock will start on the Ready, but if they decline the runoff (1:00), the clock will start on the Snap (3-3-6-f-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-38. The score is A-23, B-27 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12's pass is complete to A3 at the B-28 where he is tackled (1:00). A69 is injured during the play. Video shows that A3's knee was down with the ball at the B-29 (1:02). What if the ball had been incorrectly spotted as above, but there was no injury to A69? What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Ready (1:00), with no runoff and the clock not reset.
choice_b: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Snap (1:02), with the clock on the snap after review.
choice_c: A - 3/1 @ B-29 - Ready (0:50), with the runoff taken from the ruled time of 1:00.
explanation: Replay will overturn this to A3 down at the B-29, and properly officiated, the clock would have continued to run. Replay is therefore reversing to a running clock, and there will be a 10-second runoff (12-3-6-c). Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (1:02) and implement the runoff, 1:02 → 0:52. The clock will then start of the Ready. A "replay runoff" may not be simply declined, but only avoided through the use of a charged timeout.

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4's kick: strikes the ground in B's end zone, untouched by any player. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball is dead when the kick untouched by Team B strikes the ground in B's end zone (6-1-7-a). Since A4's kick provided the impetus which put the ball into B's end zone where it became dead (8-7-1), the result of the play is a touchback (8-6-1-b). Team B will put the ball into play at the B-25 with a choice of lateral placement (8-6-2).

question_text: F/K @ A-35. A4's kick: is caught by B21 in B's end zone, and carried to the B-20 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since the kick did not strike the ground in B's end zone, the ball remains alive when caught by B21 (6-1-7-a). B21 could have taken a knee there, and since the impetus which put the ball into B's end zone was A4's kick (8-7-1), the result would have been a touchback (8-6-1-b). question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. A8's high short punt strikes the ground at the: A-21 and rebounds to the A-17 where A63 recovers the loose ball and carries it out of bounds at the A-26. What is the ruling?

question_text: 2/20 @ B-35. A3's pass is complete to A86 at the B-30 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass: A68 illegally blocked B79 below the waist at the B-33 and the penalty is accepted. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-35 → 50 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 2/20 @ B-35. A3's pass is complete to A86 at the B-30 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass: B19 held A84 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-35 → B-25 (10-2-2-c-3). The penalty also includes an automatic first down (9-3-4-d-Penalty).

question_text: 3/25 @ A-10. During A6's pass, B98 commits a hands to the face foul at the line of scrimmage. A6's pass is: complete to A82 at the A-15 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), but since the end of the last run was beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of team possession during the down, the penalty for B98's personal foul may be enforced from the end of the last run, A-15 → A-30 (10-2-2-e). The penalty includes an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty).

question_text: 3/25 @ A-10. During A6's pass, B98 commits a hands to the face foul at the line of scrimmage. A6's pass is: complete to A82 at the A-15. He runs to the A-20 where he fumbles with A46 recovering while grounded at the A-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), but since the end of the last run was beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of team possession during the down, the penalty for B98's personal foul may be enforced from the end of the last run, which is the spot of A46's recovery, A-25 → A-40 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 3/25 @ A-10. During A6's pass, B98 commits a hands to the face foul at the line of scrimmage. A6's pass is: complete to A82 at the A-15. He runs to the A-20 where he fumbles with B17 recovering while grounded at the A-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since there was a change of team possession during the down, 10-2-2-e does not apply. The penalty for B98's personal foul must be enforced from the basic spot, A-10 → A-25 (10-2-2-c-3). The penalty includes an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. A4's pass is complete to A31 at the A-35. He runs to the A-40 where he fumbles with: A24 recovering while grounded at the A-45. Prior to the pass, B96 twisted A78's face mask at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), but since the end of the last run was beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of team possession during the down, the penalty for B96's personal foul may be enforced from the end of the last run, which is the spot of A24's recovery, A-45 → B-40 (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. A4's pass is complete to A31 at the A-35. He runs to the A-40 where he fumbles with: B28 recovering while grounded at the A-45. Prior to the pass, B96 twisted A78's face mask at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since there was a change of team possession during the down, 10-2-2-e does not apply. The penalty for B96's personal foul must be enforced from the basic spot, A-20 → A-35 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. A4's pass is complete to A31 at the A-35. He runs to the A-40 where he fumbles with: A24 recovering while grounded at the A-45. During A31's run, B28 twists his face mask, causing him to fumble. What is the ruling?
explanation: The foul occurred during a running play, so the basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-40 (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty for the foul by team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-40 → A-45 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. A4's pass is complete to A31 at the A-35. He runs to the A-40 where he fumbles with: B28 recovering while grounded at the A-45. During A31's run, B28 twists his face mask, causing him to fumble. What is the ruling?
explanation: The foul occurred during a running play, so the basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-40 (10-2-2-d-1-b). The penalty for the foul by team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, A-40 → A-45 (10-2-2-c-3). The change of possession is irrelevant here since the foul did not occur during a legal forward pass play.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. A7's pass is complete to A89 at the B-10. He runs to the B-2 where he fumbles. While the pass was in flight, B65 roughed A7. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where: B21 recovers while grounded. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), and since there was a change of team possession during the down, 10-2-2-e does not apply. The penalty for B65's personal foul must be enforced from the basic spot, B-40 → B-25 (10-2-2-c-3). The clock will start on the snap because the result of the play was a touchback (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. A7's pass is complete to A89 at the B-10. He runs to the B-2 where he fumbles. While the pass was in flight, B65 roughed A7. The loose ball rolls into B's end zone where: it goes out of bounds. What is the ruling?
explanation: The basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3), but since the end of the last run was beyond the neutral zone and there was no change of team possession during the down, the penalty for B96's personal foul may be enforced from the end of the last run, which is the spot of A89's fumble (2-25-8-b), B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-e). The clock will start on the snap because the result of the play was a touchback (3-3-2-d-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-45. When A5's pass is thrown, A67 is at the B-40. A5's pass is: complete to A89 at the B-35, and he runs out of bounds at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: Because A5's legal forward pass crossed the neutral zone, A67 is guilty of being an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-45 → B-50 (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-45. When A5's pass is thrown, A67 is at the B-40. A5's pass is: complete to A31 at the B-47, and he runs out of bounds at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: A5's legal forward pass did not cross the neutral zone, so there can be no foul for A67 being downfield (7-3-10). The result of the play is a first down for Team A at the B-25.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-45. When A5's pass is thrown, A67 is at the B-40. A5's pass is: tipped by A31 at the B-46, then caught by A89 at the B-44, and he runs out of bounds at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A5's legal forward pass continued on beyond the neutral zone, it is considered not to have crossed the neutral zone because it first touched something (A31) behind the neutral zone (2-19-3-a). So there can be no foul for A67 being downfield (7-3-10). The result of the play is a first down for Team A at the B-25.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. A86 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up A42. At the snap: both A86 and A42 go well downfield. A9's pass is complete to A86 at the B-45 where he goes to the ground. What is the ruling?
explanation: Despite his "good" number (not 50-79), A42 is an originally ineligible receiver because he is not on the end of his scrimmage line (7-3-2-c). When he goes downfield during A9's legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone, he becomes an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-30 → A-25 (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. A86 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up A42. At the snap: both A86 and A42 go well downfield. A9's pass is complete to A42 at the B-45 where he goes to the ground. What is the ruling?
explanation: As in #16, A42 is an originally ineligible receiver. When he goes downfield during A9's legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone, he becomes an Ineligible Receiver Downfield (7-3-10)... and when he touches the pass, he is guilty of Illegal Touching (7-3-11). The more impactful penalty for the latter is five yards from the previous spot, A-30 → A-25, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. A86 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up A42. At the snap: A42 steps into the backfield. A9's pass from the A-25 is complete to A42 at the A-28. He runs out of bounds at the B-45. What is the ruling?
explanation: As in #16, A42 is an originally ineligible receiver. Even though A9's legal forward pass did not cross the neutral zone, A42 is still guilty of Illegal Touching because that rule does not require that the pass cross the neutral zone (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, A-30 → A-25, and a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Wide receiver A36 goes out of bounds on his own early in his route. He re-establishes inbounds, then is the first to touch A8's pass which he catches at the B-40 where he is downed. What is the ruling?
explanation: A36 is an originally eligible receiver because of both his number and position (7-3-2-c), but when he goes out of bounds on his own and then returns inbounds and touches the pass before it is touched by an opponent of official, he commits an Illegal Touching foul (7-3-4). The penalty is loss of down at the previous spot (7-3-4-Penalty).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Wide receiver A36 goes out of bounds on his own early in his route. He leaps from out of bounds, then is the first to touch A8's pass which he catches at the B-40 where he is downed. What is the ruling?
explanation: Because A36 leapt from out of bounds, he was still out of bounds when he first touched the pass (2-27-15-a-2). So, this is simply an incomplete pass (7-3-7) and not a foul for Illegal Touching (7-3-4). The clock starts on the snap because of the incomplete pass (3-2-3-d-4).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Wide receiver A36 goes out of bounds on his own early in his route. He re-establishes inbounds, then catches A8's pass at the B-40 after tipped by B21. A36 is downed at the B-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: A36 is an originally eligible receiver because of both his number and position (7-3-2-c), but even though he went out of bounds on his own, he re-established inbounds and did not touch the pass until it was first touched by an opponent (B21). So, there is no foul for Illegal Touching (7-3-4). The result of the play is a first down.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45. Wide receiver A36 goes out of bounds on his own early in his route. He re-establishes inbounds, then catches A8's pass at the B-40 after tipped by A21. A36 is downed at the B-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: A36 is an originally eligible receiver because of both his number and position (7-3-2-c), but when he goes out of bounds on his own and then returns inbounds and touches the pass before it is touched by an opponent of official, he commits an Illegal Touching foul (7-3-4). Note the pass was touched by a teammate (A21) and not an opponent. The penalty is loss of down at the previous spot (7-3-4-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Ball snapped from the far hash. A2's pass is: complete to A17 at the B-28, and he runs out of bounds at the B-25. Prior to the pass, A29 blocked B46 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: A29 has committed OPI by contacting B46 beyond the neutral zone during a legal forward pass play in which the pass crossed the neutral zone (7-3-8-b). The penalty is 15 yards from the previous spot, B-30 → B-45, with no loss of down (7-3-8-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Ball snapped from the far hash. A2's pass is: complete to A17 at the B-32, and he runs out of bounds at the B-25. Prior to the pass, A29 blocked B46 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
explanation: There can be no OPI because A2's pass did not cross the neutral zone (7-3-8-b). The result of the play is a first down for Team A at the dead ball spot.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Ball snapped from the far hash. A2's pass is: incomplete, intended for A17 in the near side zone. While the pass was in flight, B46 interfered with A17 at the B-20. What is the ruling?
explanation: B46 has committed DPI by contacting A17 beyond the neutral zone after a legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone was in flight (7-3-8-c). Since the foul occurred less than 15 yards beyond the previous spot, the ball is placed at the spot of the foul (yard line and lateral position), B-20 and Near Hash (7-3-8-c-Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. Ball snapped from the far hash. A2's pass is: incomplete, intended for A17 in the near side zone. While the pass was in flight, B46 interfered with A17 at the B-10. What is the ruling?
explanation: B46 has committed DPI by contacting A17 beyond the neutral zone after a legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone was in flight (7-3-8-c). Since the foul occurred 15 or more yards beyond the previous spot, the penalty is 15 yards from the previous spot (yard line and lateral position), B-30 → B-15 and Far Hash (7-3-8-c-Penalty).

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. Ball snapped from the near hash. A10's pass is: incomplete, intended for A84 in the far side zone. While the pass was in flight, B21 interfered with A84 at the B-4. What is the ruling?
explanation: B21 has committed DPI by contacting A84 beyond the neutral zone after a legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone was in flight (7-3-8-c). The ball was snapped from on or inside the B-17, but the foul did not occur on or inside the B-2, so the ball is placed at the spot of the foul (yard line and lateral position), B-4 and Far Hash (7-3-8-c-Penalty),

question_text: 2/G @ B-10. Ball snapped from the near hash. A10's pass is: incomplete, intended for A84 near the far sideline. While the pass was in flight, B21 interfered with A84 in the end zone. What is the ruling?
explanation: B21 has committed DPI by contacting A84 beyond the neutral zone after a legal forward pass which crossed the neutral zone was in flight (7-3-8-c). The ball was snapped from on or inside the B-17 and the foul occurred on or inside the B-2, so the penalty from the previous spot (same lateral position) places the ball the B-2, Near Hash (7-3-8-c-Penalty).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Ball snapped from the center of the field. A11's pass is: incomplete, intended for A45 in the near side zone. While the pass was in flight, B19 twisted A45's face mask at the A-35. What is the ruling?
explanation: B19 has committed DPI, but he has also committed a FMM foul. Since the DPI foul occurred less than 15 yards beyond the previous spot, that penalty would place the ball at the spot of the foul (A-35), but enforcement of the FMM penalty would be 15 yards from the previous spot (same lateral position), A-25 → A-40, Center (10-2-2-d-3 / 10-2-2-c-3). Team B would decline the DPI penalty and accept the FMM penalty.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Ball snapped from the center of the field. A11's pass is: complete to A45 at the A-35 in the near side zone where he is downed. While the pass was in flight, B19 twisted A45's face mask at the A-35. What is the ruling?
explanation: B19 has committed DPI, but he has also committed a FMM foul. Since the DPI foul occurred less than 15 yards beyond the previous spot, that penalty would place the ball at the spot of the foul (A-35), but the penalty for B19's FMM could be enforced from the spot (same lateral position) where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, A-35 → A-50, Near Hash (10-2-2-e). Team B would accept the FMM penalty.

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A12 runs to the A-41, and from there completes a pass to A48 at the B-40 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: At the A-41, A12 is one yard beyond the neutral zone, and this should have his entire body beyond the neutral zone at the time of his pass. Thus, A12 has thrown an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-a) for which the penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-41 → A-36, with a loss of down (7-3-2-a-Penalty).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A12 runs to the A-41, retreats to the A-39, and from there completes a pass to A48 at the B-40 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: At the A-41, A12 is one yard beyond the neutral zone, and this should have his entire body and the ball beyond the neutral zone. So even though he throws the pass from the A-39 (behind the neutral zone), this is an Illegal Forward Pass because his entire body and the ball had previously been beyond the neutral zone (7-3-2-e). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-39 → A-34, with a loss of down (7-3-2-e-Penalty).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. From the A-35, A12 passes to A48 at the A-37. From there, A48 completes a pass to A86 at the B-40 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
explanation: A12's pass from the A-35 to the A-37 is forward, and then A48's pass from the A-37 to the B-40 is also forward. This is two forward passes during the same down, so the second pass is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-d). The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul, A-37 → A-32, with a loss of down (7-3-2-d-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 muffs the snap. It bounces off the ground and back into his hands, and he spikes the ball at the B-22. What is the ruling?
explanation: A13 may legally spike the ball to conserve time, but only if he does so immediately and before the ball has otherwise hit the ground. So, this is an Illegal Forward Pass (7-3-2-f). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-22 (7-3-2-f-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball from the B-30 into an area not occupied by an eligible teammate. The ball lands near the hash mark at the B-21. A64 was at the B-15 when the pass was thrown. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A13 controlled the snap, did not relinquish possession, and carried the ball outside the tackle box, the pass did not cross the neutral zone, so 7-3-2-h-Exception does not apply for A13. So, this is a foul for Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h), for which the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-30 (7-3-2-h-Penalty). A64 is not illegally downfield for two reasons: (1) this was not a legal forward pass, and (2) the pass did not cross the neutral zone (7-3-10).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball from the B-30 into an area not occupied by an eligible teammate. The ball lands out of bounds at the B-19. A64 was at the B-15 when the pass was thrown. What is the ruling?
explanation: A13 controlled the snap, did not relinquish possession, carried the ball outside the tackle box, and threw the pass beyond the neutral zone, so this is not Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h-Exception). Furthermore, A64 is not an Ineligible Receiver Downfield because the pass was legally grounded near or beyond the sideline (7-3-10-Exception). The result of the play is simply an incomplete pass.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball from the B-30 into an area not occupied by an eligible teammate. The ball lands near the hash mark at the B-19. A64 was at the B-15 when the pass was thrown. What is the ruling?
explanation: A13 controlled the snap, did not relinquish possession, carried the ball outside the tackle box, and threw the pass beyond the neutral zone, so this is not Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h-Exception). But 7-3-10-Exception does not apply to A64 because the pass was not legally grounded near or beyond the sideline. The penalty for A64 being an Ineligible Receiver Downfield is five yards from the previous spot, B-20 → B-25 (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 muffs the snap which is then picked up by A24. He carries the ball outside the tackle box, and throws the ball from the B-30 into an area not occupied by an eligible teammate. The ball lands out of bounds at the B-19. What is the ruling?
explanation: A13 muffed the snap, but A24 controlled the resulting backward pass, did not relinquish possession, carried the ball outside the tackle box, and threw the pass beyond the neutral zone, so this is not Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h-Exception).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap and hands off to A24 who carries the ball outside the tackle box. He then hands off to A86 who tosses back to A13. From the B-30, he throws into an area not occupied by an eligible teammate. The ball lands near the hash mark at the B-19. What is the ruling?
explanation: Even though A13 controlled the snap, he relinquished possession to A24, and 7-3-2-h-Exception does not apply to A24. This is Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h) for which the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-30 (7-3-2-h-Penalty)

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, and from inside the tackle box at the B-30 throws a pass that strikes A79 in the back at the B-22 and falls to the ground. Tight End A86 was in the area where the pass fell incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: This is not Intentional Grounding because A13 threw into an area occupied by an eligible offensive receiver, A86 (7-3-2-h). There is also no foul for Illegal Touching because ineligible A79 did not intentionally touch the legal forward pass (7-3-11). The result of the play is simply an incomplete pass.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, and from inside the tackle box at the B-30 throws a pass that is muffed by A79 at the B-22 and falls to the ground. There was no eligible receiver in the area where the pass fell incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: A13 did not carry the ball outside the tackle box and did not throw the pass beyond the neutral zone, so 7-3-2-h-Exception does not apply and this is Intentional Grounding (7-3-2-h), which is an illegal forward pass. Even though ineligible A79 muffed the pass, there is no foul for Illegal Touching because that rule applies only to a legal forward pass (7-3-11). The penalty for A13's Intentional Grounding foul is loss of down at the spot of the foul, the B-30 (7-3-2-h-Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. Assume any penalty (the most advantageous, if more than one) will be accepted. A13 controls the snap, and from inside the tackle box at the B-30 throws a pass that is muffed by A79 at the B-22 and falls to the ground. Tight End A86 was in the area where the pass fell incomplete. What is the ruling?
explanation: This is not Intentional Grounding because A13 threw into an area occupied by an eligible offensive receiver, A86 (7-3-2-h). But when ineligible A79 muffed this legal forward pass, he was guilty of Illegal Touching (7-3-11). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-20 → B-25, with a loss of down (7-3-11-Penalty).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. Assume that any penalty will be accepted. A4's pass is complete to A86 at the A-25 where he is tackled. A79 was at the A-24 when the pass was thrown. B41 twisted A86's face mask in making the tackle. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (A = IDP / B = FMM) during a down in which there was no change of team possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. Assume that any penalty will be accepted. A4's pass is complete to A86 at the A-25 where he is tackled. A79 was at the A-24 when the pass was thrown. After B41 had legally tackled A86, B15 piled on. What is the ruling?
explanation: The penalties for A's live-ball foul (IDP) and B's dead-ball foul (UNR) will be administered separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). The IDP penalty is enforced from the previous spot, A-20 → A-15 (7-3-10), after which the UNR penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot, A-15 → A-30 (10-2-2-a) with an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. Assume that any penalty will be accepted. A4's pass is complete to A86 at the A-25 where he is tackled. B58 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. A86 pushed off B41 prior to catching the pass. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (B = DOF / A = OPI) during a down in which there was no change of team possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20. Assume that any penalty will be accepted. A4's pass is complete to A86 at the A-25 where he is tackled. B58 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. After the tackle, A86 got up and pushed B41 to the ground. What is the ruling?
explanation: The penalties for B's live-ball foul (DOF) and A's dead-ball foul (UNR) will be administered separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). The DOF penalty is enforced from the previous spot, A-20 → A-25 (7-1-5-b-2-Penalty), after which the UNR penalty is enforced half-the-distance (10-2-6) from the succeeding spot, A-25 → A-12½ (10-2-2-a). The down remains the same (5-2-3-a).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. A2's pass is intercepted by B17 at the B-10 and returned to the B-25 where he is tackled. B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. A59 brought B17 down with a horse collar tackle. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (B = DOF / A = HCT) during a down in which there was a change of team possession, but the team in final possession (Team B) fouled before the final change of team possession, 10-1-4-Exception 1 does not apply. The fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. A2's pass is intercepted by B17 at the B-10 and returned to the B-25 where he is tackled. Team A was in an illegal formation at the snap. During the return, B28 blocked A46 in the back at the B-20. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (A = ILM / B = IBB) during a down in which there was a change of team possession, and the team in final possession (Team B) fouled after the final change of team possession, they may decline offsetting fouls and retain possession (10-1-4-Exception 1). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-25 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. A2's pass is intercepted by B17 at the B-10 and returned to the B-25 where he is tackled. During the return, B28 blocked A46 in the back at the B-20. A59 brought B17 down with a horse collar tackle. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (B = IBB / A = HCT) during a down in which there was a change of team possession, and the team in final possession (Team B) fouled after the final change of team possession, they may decline offsetting fouls and retain possession (10-1-4-Exception 1). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the B-25 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-35. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. A3's pass is intercepted by B17 at the A-45 and returned to the A-30 where he fumbles. A85 recovers the loose ball at the A-25 and carries it to the A-40 where he is tackled. A29 was in illegal motion at the snap. During B17's run, B28 blocked A46 below the waist A-40. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (A = ILM / B = BBW) during a down in which there was a change of team possession, but the team in final possession (Team A) fouled before the final change of team possession, 10-1-4-Exception 1 does not apply. The fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-35. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. The basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-40 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-30 → A-15 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-35. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (A = BBW / B = TGT) during a down in which there was a change of team possession, and the team in final possession (Team A) fouled after the final change of team possession, they may decline offsetting fouls and retain possession (10-1-4-Exception 1). The basic spot is the end of the related run, the A-40 (10-2-2-d-1-c), and since the foul by the team in possession occurred behind the basic spot, enforcement is from the spot of the foul, A-30 → A-15 (10-2-2-c-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. A6's punt is fielded by B31 at the B-20 and returned to the B-40 where he: is tackled. Prior to the kick, A78 held B96 at the line of scrimmage. During the kick, B24 held A35 at the B-30. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since each team committed a live-ball foul (A = / B = HOLD) during a down in HOLD which there was a change of team possession, and the foul by the team in final possession (Team B) has PSK enforcement (10-2-3-b), they may decline offsetting fouls and retain possession (10-1-4-Exception 2). The basic spot is the B-20 (2-25-11-a), and since the foul by the team considered to be in possession (10-2-3-a) occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-20 → B-10 (10-2-2-c-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Assume that any penalty will be accepted, and that there will not be offsetting fouls unless that is the only option. The fouls offset and the down is repeated (10-1-4). Even though Team A is still in possession, the clock starts on the snap because of the legal kick down (3-3-2-d-8).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-30. With the clock running, A67 commits a false start, after which B98 commits a personal foul. What is the ruling?
explanation: Penalties for dead-ball fouls are administered separately and in order of occurrence (10-1-5). The penalty for A's FST is enforced, B-30 → B-35, after which the penalty for B's UNR is enforced, B-35 → B-20. The penalty for B's UNR includes an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-30. A5's pass is complete to A42 at the B-20 where he steps out of bounds and B36 hits him late. A42 then taunts B36. What is the ruling?
explanation: As a result of the live-ball action, it is 4/5 @ B-20. When dead-ball personal or unsportsmanlike fouls by both teams (B = UNR / A = UNS) are reported and none of the penalties have been completed, the yardage penalties cancel and the number or type of down established before the fouls occurred is unaffected (10-1-5-Exception).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-30. A5's pass is complete to A42 at the B-20 where he steps out of bounds and B36 hits him late. Prior to the next play and before the ball is declared ready for play, A42 taunts B36. What is the ruling?
explanation: The penalty for B's UNR foul would have already been completed before A's UNS foul, so 10-1-5-Exception does not apply. The penalty for B's UNR is enforced half-the- distance, B-20 → B-10, with an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty), after which the penalty for A's UNS is enforced, B-10 → B-25. Because A's foul occurred before the ready for play, the line to gain is reset to the B-15, and it will be 1/10 (5-2-7-a).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-30. A5's pass is complete to A42 at the B-20 where he steps out of bounds and B36 hits him late. Prior to the next play but after the ball is declared ready for play, A42 taunts B36. What is the ruling?
explanation: The penalty for B's UNR foul would have already been completed before A's UNS foul, so 10-1-5-Exception does not apply. The penalty for B's UNR is enforced half-the- distance, B-20 → B-10, with an automatic first down (9-1-Penalty), after which the penalty for A's UNS is enforced, B-10 → B-25. Because A's foul occurred after the ready for play, the line to gain remains the goal line, and it will remain 1/G (5-2-7-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. Assume that any penalty not declined by rule will be accepted. A7's pass is complete to A89 for a touchdown. While the pass was in flight, B43 interfered with A89. What is the ruling?

explanation: The penalty for B's DPI (which is neither a personal nor unsportsmanlike foul) is declined by rule (10-2-5-a-2), so the Try remains at the B-3 and the kickoff at the A-35.

question_text: 3/20 @ A-15. Assume that any penalty not declined by rule will be accepted. A8's pass is intercepted by B43 and returned for a touchdown. While the pass was in flight, A89 interfered with B43. What is the ruling?

question_text: 4/5 @ 50. A87 is the first to touch A9's punt at the B-20. B23 fields the punt at the B-10 and returns it to the B-30 where he fumbles. A45 recovers the loose ball at the B-40 and carries it across B's goal line. During the kick, B17 holds A82 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
choice_b: TD for A. Penalty declined by rule.
choice_c: TD for A. Penalty enforced on Try or KO.
explanation: If Team A declines the penalty for B's HOLD, Team B will elect the spot of illegal touching (6-3-2-a). PSK does not apply since Team B will not next put the ball into play (10-2-3-b-4), so the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-4-a). The penalty for the foul by the team not in possession is enforced from the basic spot, 50 → B-40 (10-2-2-c-3)..

question_text: 4/5 @ 50. A87 is the first to touch A9's punt at the B-20. B23 fields the punt at the B-10 and returns it to the B-30 where he fumbles. A45 recovers the loose ball at the B-40 and carries it across B's goal line. During B23's run, B17 clips A82 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
choice_a: TD for A. Penalty declined by rule.

explanation: The penalty for B's CLP (which is a personal foul) may be enforced on the Try or the succeeding kickoff at the option of Team A (10-2-5-a-1), and acceptance of the penalty cancels the illegal touching privilege. Thus, Team A may retain the six points.

(10-2-2-c-3), which is nonsensical. Therefore, enforcement is not possible, and the penalty is declined by rule (10-2-5-a-2). Team B will elect the spot of illegal touching (6-3-2-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Ball on the near hash for a two-point attempt. A67 false starts. Team A requests that the ball be placed in the center. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Near Hash even if Team A uses a timeout.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Center provided Team A uses a timeout.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-8 - No Clock - Center whether or not Team A uses a timeout.
explanation: On a try, the ball may be relocated after a charged timeout, but not if preceded by a Team A foul (8-3-2-c), so the request is denied.

question_text: Try @ B-3. Ball on the far hash for a two-point attempt. A11's pass, intended for A23, falls incomplete; however, B45 is flagged for Defensive Pass Interference dead-center in the end zone. Team A requests that the ball be placed on the near hash. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A - Try @ B-2 - No Clock - Center.
choice_b: A - Try @ B-2 - No Clock - Far Hash.
choice_c: A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock - Near Hash.
choice_d: A - Try @ B-2 - No Clock - Near Hash.
explanation: The penalty for defensive pass interference on a try from on or inside the B-3 is enforced half-the-distance to the goal line, B-3 → B-1½ (10-2-5-b). On a try, the ball may be relocated after a Team B foul (8-3-2-c), so the request is granted.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B36 in B's end zone and returned: to the B-40 where A74 brings him down by lowering his head and attacking with the crown of his helmet. After the pass, B98 roughed A12. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since both teams fouled during the try (B = RPS / A = TGT), and B's foul occurred before the change of team possession, the fouls offset and the down is repeated (8-3-3-a). Although A74 will still be disqualified for his flagrant foul (10-1-4), this penalty does not carry over as a FUDL foul because Team B fouled before the change of team possession (8-3-4-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B36 in B's end zone and returned: to the B-40 where A74 brings him down by lowering his head and attacking with the crown of his helmet. During the return, B98 blocked A65 below the waist. What is the ruling?
explanation: Since both teams fouled during the try (B = BBW / A = TGT), and B's foul occurred after the change of team possession, the penalty for B's non-flagrant personal foul is declined by rule (8-3-4-a), but the penalty for A's flagrant personal foul is enforced as a FUDL foul on the succeeding kickoff, A-35 → A-20 (8-3-4-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12's pass is intercepted by B36 in B's end zone and returned: across A's goal line for an apparent two points. During the return, a Team B assistant coach interfered with the Line Judge's movement to the goal line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: 0 points for B. A - Try @ B-1½ - No Clock.
choice_b: 2 points for B. A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
choice_c: 0 points for B. A - F/K @ A-35 - F/K Rules.
choice_d: 0 points for B. A - F/K @ 50 - F/K Rules.
explanation: Even though Team B committed a foul during the down, their score is not canceled (8-3-4-b-Exception) because this foul is a live-ball foul treated as a dead-ball foul (9-2-5-b-Penalty). This penalty will be enforced as a FUDL foul on the succeeding kickoff, A-35 → 50 (8-3-4-a).

question_text: First possession series of the indicated extra period. 2/10 @ B-25. First extra period. A34 carries to the B-10 where he fumbles. B19 recovers the loose ball at the B-5 and carries it to the B-15 where he fumbles. A67 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-20. What is the ruling?
explanation: The ball remains alive after the change of possession, but Team A may not have a first down if it regains possession after a change of possession (3-1-3-e). A's possession series is over, and B will begin their possession series at the A-25.

question_text: First possession series of the indicated extra period. Try @ B-3. Third extra period. A13's pass is complete to A86 for two points. B94 was flagged for hands to the face prior to the pass. Assume the penalty will be accepted unless declined by rule. What is the ruling?
explanation: The penalty for B's HTF (which is a personal foul) may be enforced to begin B's series, A-3 → A-18 (8-3-3-b-1) because the next play will be a two-point try (3-1-3-e).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25 in the first possession series of the second extra period. A14's pass is intercepted by B32 at the B-10 and returned across A's goal line for an apparent six points. During the return, B98 blocked A67 below the waist. What is the ruling?

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25 in the first possession series of the second extra period. A14's pass is intercepted by B32 at the B-10 and returned across A's goal line for an apparent six points. Team B committed both a live-ball foul and a live-ball foul that is treated as a dead-ball foul (9-2-5-a-Penalty), so those penalties will be enforced separately and in the order of occurrence (10-1-6-b). The penalty for the live-ball foul is enforced from the previous spot, B-25 → B-20, after which the penalty for the dead-ball foul is enforced half-the-distance (10-2-6) from the succeeding spot, B-20 → B-10.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A3 carries to the A-30 where he: fumbles and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the A-28. What is the ruling?
explanation: When a fumble goes out of bounds between the goal lines behind the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the out of bounds spot, the A-28 (7-2-4-b-2). For a "backward fumble" out of bounds after the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock stops and re-starts on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A3 carries to the A-30 where he: fumbles and the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the A-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: When a fumble goes out of bounds between the goal lines beyond the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the spot of the fumble, the A-30 (7-2-4-b-1). For a "forward fumble" out of bounds throughout the game, the clock stops and re-starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A3 carries to the A-30 where he: pitches back toward A89, who does not touch the loose ball and it goes out of bounds at the A-28. What is the ruling?
explanation: When a backward pass goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball belongs to the passing team at the out of bounds spot, the A-28 (7-2-4-a). For a backward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock stops and re-starts on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter. A3 carries to the A-30 where he: pitches back toward A89, who muffs the loose ball at the A-28 and it goes out of bounds at the A-32. What is the ruling?
explanation: A loose ball retains its original status until it is once again possessed (8-7-2-c), so this remains a backward pass despite the muff. When a backward pass goes out of bounds between the goal lines, the ball belongs to the passing team at the out of bounds spot, the A-32 (7-2-4-a). For a backward pass after the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock stops and re-starts on the Snap (3-3-2-d-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. At the B-33, A4 fumbles and the loose ball strikes the ground. To prevent B96 from recovering the loose ball, A78 bats it at the B-30 and it goes out of bounds at the B-27. What is the ruling?
explanation: A78's bat is forward (B-30 → B-27) and therefore illegal (9-4-1-c). The end of the related run is the spot of the fumble, the B-33 (2-25-8-b), and since the related run ended beyond the neutral zone, the basic spot is the end of the related run (10-2-2-d-1-b). Since the foul by the team in possession occurred beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot, B-33 → B-43 (10-2-2-c-2), with a loss of down.

explanation: Normally this would not qualify for a 10-second runoff since the clock was also stopped because A21 made the line to gain (3-3-6-f), but when there is a temporary stoppage for a first down and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less, the runoff still applies (3-3-6-f-Exception). Team B will accept, ending the game.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. The score is A-14, B-10 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A12's pass is complete to A89 at the A-44 where he is tackled inbounds (1:37). A89 was injured on the play. B76 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. Team A accepts the penalty. What is the ruling?
explanation: The clock was stopped for two reasons: for A89's injury (3-3-2-e-5) and to complete the penalty for B76's foul (3-3-2-e-3). Since the injury is not the only reason for stopping the clock, there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-3-6-f). The clock will remain at 1:37 and start by rule on the Ready. There is no Ready / Snap option because the foul is by the team behind in score (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A76 commits a false start (0:24). What is the ruling?
explanation: When A76 commits a false start, the clock will stop immediately and so there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-1). Team B will accept the runoff, and the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10's pass is complete to A43 at the B-22 where he is tackled inbounds (0:24). A76 held at the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
explanation: When A76 holds at the line of scrimmage, the clock continues to run, so there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-b). The clock will be stopped to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4), and Team B has a Ready / Snap option since the score is tied (3-4-3-b). They will elect Ready.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10's pass is complete to A43 at the B-22 where he is tackled inbounds (0:24). B17 is flagged for a late hit. What is the ruling?
explanation: The clock would have continued to run when A43 was tackled short of the line to gain, but when B17 hit him late, the clock would then stop immediately. So, there is an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a) which Team A will decline. The clock will then start by rule on the Snap (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10 runs to the B-29, then throws a pass which falls incomplete (0:24). What is the ruling?
explanation: An incomplete illegal forward pass is one of the specific fouls listed which triggers an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). Team B will accept the runoff, and the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10 runs to the B-29, then throws a pass which is complete to A43 at the B-22 where he is tackled inbounds (0:24). What is the ruling?
explanation: A complete illegal forward pass does not cause the clock to stop immediately, and so there is no option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a). The clock will be stopped to complete the penalty (3-3-2-e-4), and Team B has a Ready / Snap option since the score is tied (3-4-3-b). They will elect Ready.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10 runs to the B-29, then throws a pass which falls incomplete (0:24). B98 lost his helmet during the down. What is the ruling?
explanation: An incomplete illegal forward pass is one of the specific fouls listed which triggers an option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-3). B98's helmet does not negate the runoff because 3-4-4-a requires only that "the clock … stop immediately", and B98's helmet does not cause a runoff because it is not the only clock-stopper (3-3-10-b-2). Team B will accept, and the clock will start on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A10 runs to the B-29, then throws a pass which falls incomplete (0:24). B98 was flagged for hands to the face. What is the ruling?
explanation: When there are offsetting fouls (A = IFP, B = HTF), there is no option for a 10- second runoff (3-4-4-e). The clock is stopped both for the incomplete pass (3-3-2-d-4) and to complete the penalties for the fouls (3-3-2-e-4), and will then start by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-f).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-35. The score is A-28, B-28 with the clock running after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, and neither team has a timeout remaining. Assume Team B will exercise any option which consumes time while Team A will exercise any option which conserves time. A76 commits a false start (0:24), after which B98 comes across the line and forcibly knocks him to the ground. What is the ruling?
explanation: These are not offsetting (live-ball) fouls, but rather two dead-ball fouls for which the penalties will be enforced separately and in order of occurrence (10-1-5). The penalty for A76's false start will be enforced B-35 → B-40 and carries with it the option for a 10-second runoff (3-4-4-a-1). The penalty for B98's personal foul will then be enforced B-40 → B-25 and includes an automatic first down. Team B will accept the runoff, and the clock will start by rule on the Ready (3-4-4-c).

question_text: The score is A-9, B-7 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. 2/G @ B-10. A34 carries to near the goal line where the ruling on the field is a touchdown (0:13). Video shows that A34 was down at the B-1 (0:15). What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, A34 would have been declared down at the B-1 and the clock would have continued to run. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:15) and implement a 10-second runoff, 0:15 0:05. The clock starts on the Ready (12-3-6-c).

question_text: The score is A-9, B-7 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. 2/5 @ B-10. A34 carries to near the goal line where the ruling on the field is a touchdown (0:13). Video shows that A34 was down at the B-1 (0:15). What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, A34 would have been declared down at the B-1 and the clock would have been stopped to award a first down to Team A. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:15), but there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: The score is A-9, B-7 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. 2/G @ B-10. A34 carries to near the goal line where the ruling on the field is a touchdown (0:13). Video shows that A34 was down at the B-1 (0:15). B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. Team A declines the penalty. What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, A34 would have been declared down at the B-1 and the clock would have been stopped to complete the penalty for B96's foul. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:15), but there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-4). Team A does not have a Ready / Snap option because the foul is by the team behind in score (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. The score is A-27, B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining.
