A42 carries to the B-31 where the ruling on the field is a fumble, recovered by B17 near the sideline at the B-28 (0:36). Video shows that: A42 was down at the B-31 prior to the ball coming loose (0:39). What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, A42 would have been declared down at the B-31 and the clock would have continued to run. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:39) and implement a 10-second runoff, 0:39 0:29. The clock starts on the Ready (12-3-6-c).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. The score is A-27, B-30 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A42 carries to the B-31 where the ruling on the field is a fumble, recovered by B17 near the sideline at the B-28 (0:36). Video shows that: B17 had a foot on the sideline when he first touched the ball at the B-28 (0:37). What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, the clock would have stopped when the loose ball went out of bounds as a result of touching B17. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:37), but there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock starts on the Ready because this created a forward fumble out of bounds (3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-40. The score is A-19, B-6 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A13's pass is complete to A88 at the 50 where he is brought down by B16 (0:52). The clock continues to run, but just prior to the next snap, Replay stops the clock (0:38) and adds Targeting on B16. What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, B16 would have been flagged for Targeting and the clock would have been stopped to complete the penalty. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:52), but there is no 10-second runoff (12-3-6-b). The clock starts on the Ready (3-3-2-e-2). Team A does not have a Ready / Snap option because the foul is by the team behind in score (3-4-3-b).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-40. The score is A-19, B-6 after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout remaining. A13's pass is complete to A88 at the 50 where he is brought down by B16 (0:52). The clock stops (0:50) because the Back Judge has a flag for Targeting on B16. Replay overturns the Targeting foul. What is the ruling?
explanation: Properly officiated, no flag would have been thrown and the clock would have continued to run. Reset the clock to the time the ball was dead by rule (0:52) and implement a 10-second runoff, 0:52 0:42. The clock starts on the Ready (12-3-6-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. The score is A-26, B-31 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A86 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up A42. At the snap, both A86 and A42 go well downfield. The pass is complete to A86 for a touchdown (0:00). What is the most likely result?
explanation: A42 is guilty of being an ineligible receiver downfield (7-3-10). Team B must accept the penalty or Team A will win 32-31, but if the penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul during a down in which time expires, the period must be extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1). The penalty is five yards from the previous spot, B-20 → B-25 (7-3-10-Penalty).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. The score is A-26, B-31 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A86 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up A42. At the snap, both A86 and A42 go well downfield. The pass is complete to A42 for a touchdown (0:00). What is the most likely result?
explanation: A42 is guilty of illegal touching (7-3-11). Team B must accept the penalty or Team A will win 32-31, but since the statement of the penalty for Illegal Touching includes a loss of down, the period would only be extended if Team B requested it (3-2-3-a-1). They will not, and the game will be over.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-45. The score is A-34, B-37 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A14's pass is complete to A82 at the B-10. He runs to the B-2 where he is tackled (0:00). B96 roughed the passer. What is the most likely result?
explanation: The penalty for B96's roughing the passer foul is enforced from the end of the last run, B-2 → B-1 (10-2-2-e). Since the penalty was accepted for a live-ball foul during a down in which time expired, the period must be extended for an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-45. The score is A-34, B-37 with 0:05 remaining in the 4th quarter. A14's pass is complete to A82 at the B-10. He runs to the B-2 where he is tackled (0:00). B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. What is the most likely result?
explanation: Team A would like to decline the penalty and keep the 43-yard gain, but if they do the game will be over. Only through acceptance of the penalty from the previous spot, B-45 → B-40 (7-1-5-b-1-Penalty) can there be an untimed down (3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is late in the half and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team B head coach requests a timeout, and the timeout is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation and the ball is ready for play when the Team B head coach requests a time out.
choice_a: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_b: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials honor the request.
choice_c: B, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_d: A, 3/5, B-30, Ready. Officials do not honor the request.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request. The officials shall not acknowledge the timeout request by the Team B head coach (Rule 3-3-1-b). Team B still has 2 timeouts remaining, but since they have already called a timeout in the dead ball period, a timeout is not available for Team B (Rule 3-3-4-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is late in the half and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team B head coach requests a timeout, and the timeout is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation and the ball is ready for play and the Team A Head Coach requests a timeout.
choice_a: A, 3/5, B-30 Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_b: A, 3/5, B-30 Snap. Officials will honor the request.
choice_c: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. Officials will honor the request.
choice_d: B, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials will honor the request.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-30 Snap. Officials will honor the request. The Team A timeout request is granted. Since Team A has timeouts remaining and has not called a timeout in this dead ball period, and the officials will honor the request (Rule 3-3-4).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is late in the half and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team B head coach requests a timeout, and the time out is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation and the ball is ready for play. Just before the snap, A77 commits a false start. Team A is penalized 5 yards. Both teams are back in formation with the ball ready for play, and the Team B Head Coach requests a timeout.
choice_a: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. Officials honor the request.
choice_b: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_c: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_d: A, 3/10, B-35, Ready. Officials do not honor the request.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-35, Snap. Officials do not honor the request. The officials shall not acknowledge the Team B head coach's timeout request (Rule 3-3-1-b). Team B still has 2 timeouts remaining, but since they have already called a timeout in the dead ball period, a timeout is not available for Team B. Because the false start is a dead ball foul, we are still in the same dead ball period from when Team B called their 1st timeout, so a timeout is not available to Team B (Rule 3-3-4-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. Late in the 3rd quarter, QB A12 rolls deep and to the right and gets outside the tackle box. A12 is under heavy duress and throws the ball away from the B-30 into an area with no eligible receiver. The ball is headed back toward the line of scrimmage, but a strong wind holds the ball up and it lands at the B-23. The game clock expires during the play.
choice_a: A, 4/15, B-30, No clock and extend the 3rd quarter.
choice_b: At the option of Team B, the half is over.
choice_c: A, 4/15, B-30, Ready – Reset the clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter.
choice_d: A, 4/15, B-30, Snap – Reset the ball and clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, B-30, Snap – Reset the ball and clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter. The pass by A12 is an illegal forward pass and is a foul for intentional grounding (Rule 7-3-2-h). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul. Rule 3-2-3-a now states that only the 2nd and 4th quarters are extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live ball foul during a down in which time expires.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. Late in the 2nd quarter, QB A12 rolls deep and to the right and gets outside the tackle box. A12 is under heavy duress and throws the ball away from the B-30 into an area with no eligible receiver. The ball is headed back toward the line of scrimmage, but a strong wind holds the ball up and it lands at the B-23. The game clock expires during the play.
choice_a: At the option of Team B, the half is over – or – A, 4/15, B-30, No clock and extend the quarter.
choice_b: A, 4/15, B-30, No clock and extend the quarter, with no option to end the half.
choice_c: The half is over, with no option to extend.
choice_d: At the option of Team B, the half is over – or – A, 4/15, B-30, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: At the option of Team B, the half is over – or – A, 4/15, B-30, No clock and extend the quarter. The pass by A12 is an illegal forward pass and is a foul for intentional grounding (Rule 7-3-2-h). The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul. Rule 3-2-3-a now states that only the 2nd and 4th quarters are extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live ball foul during a down in which time expires; however, at the option of the offended team, the period is not extended if the foul is by the team in possession and the statement of the penalty includes loss of down (Rule 3-2-3-a-1).

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-10. Late in the game with Team A trailing by 3 points, back A44 catches A12's forward pass behind the line at the B-12. A44 runs to the B-2 where he is tackled inbounds, and the clock expires during the play. Before the pass by A12, B21 was guilty of holding receiver A88 in the end zone.
choice_a: A, 1/Goal, B-6, No clock, extend the 4th quarter.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal, B-5, No clock, extend the 4th quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-5, No clock, extend the 4th quarter. The holding foul by B21 is penalized half the distance to the goal from the previous spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-3) and includes an automatic 1st down. The 4th quarter is extended for an untimed down because of the accepted penalty for a live ball foul during a down in which time expires (Rule 3-2-3-a). The penalty must be accepted to extend the 4th quarter.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation with the game clock running. Center B5 legally starts the backward motion of the snap with a quick and continuous motion. During this backward motion the center clutches or pauses before releasing the ball and the ball subsequently slips from the snapper's hands and is loose at the B-29 and B54 recovers the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-30, Ready. Team A dead-ball foul, illegal snap (Rule 2-23-1-a). To be a legal snap, the ball must be handed or passed backward from its position on the ground with a quick and continuous motion and the ball leaving the snapper's hands in this motion. Any other action or motion by the snapper such as not releasing the ball, pausing, restarting, or breaking the continuous motion of the ball during the snap is considered an illegal snap.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation with the game clock running. Center B5 legally starts the backward motion of the snap with a quick and continuous motion. During this backward motion the ball slips from the snapper's hands and is loose at the B-29 and B54 recovers the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-29, Snap. This is a legal snap that becomes a backward pass (Rule 2-23-1-c). To be a legal snap, the ball must be handed or passed backward from its position on the ground with a quick and continuous motion and the ball leaving the snapper's hands in this motion. If the ball, during the backward motion of a legal snap, slips from the snapper's hand, it becomes a backward pass, and the ball is in play.

question_text: 3/10 @ 50. Late in the 4th quarter, Team A is out of timeouts and trails 21-17. Receiver A88 catches a pass and runs to the B-30 where he is tackled in bounds and the officials stop the clock for the 1st down with 1:58 remaining. A88's knee was down with 2:02 remaining on the game clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, 1:58, wind on ready. When the down ends as it relates to stopping the game clock for a 1st down near the two minute mark is not reviewable.

question_text: 4/2 @ B-47. Early in the 3rd quarter, QBA1 rolls out to his right and throws a forward pass to receiver A80 near the sideline. B34 steps in front of A80 and catches the pass at the B-45 with his right foot in bounds. The Line Judge rules the pass incomplete.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-47, Snap. Unless this is an obvious mistake highlighted on TV, replay should not stop the game for two yards in this situation.

question_text: 4/6 @ B-20. Late in the 3rd quarter, Team A lines up to kick a field goal. B75 is in the neutral zone at the snap and blocks the kick. The ball is recovered by B75 back at the B-30. When the play is over, the game clock reads 0:00.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-30, No clock – Extend the 3rd Quarter.
choice_b: A, 4/1, B-15, No clock – Extend the 3rd Quarter.
choice_c: A, 4/1, B-15, Snap – Reset the ball and clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter.
choice_d: A, 4/1, B-15, Ready – Reset the clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-15, Snap – Reset the ball and clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter. The result of the play is Team B's ball at the B-30. Team A will accept the live ball foul for offside by B75, and after the penalty enforcement will face 4/1. The 3rd quarter will not be extended, and the next snap will start the 4th quarter (Rule 3-2-3-a).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30. It is late in the half and both teams have all their timeouts remaining. With Team A at the line and ready to snap the ball, the Team B head coach requests a timeout, and the timeout is granted. After the timeout, both teams are in formation and the Team A head coach requests a timeout and the time out is granted. After the Team A timeout, both teams are back on the field with the ball ready for play when the Team B head coach requests a time out.
choice_a: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials honor the request since Team A called the previous timeout.
choice_b: B, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_c: A, 3/5, B-30, Ready. Officials do not honor the request.
choice_d: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. Officials do not honor the request. The officials shall not acknowledge the timeout request by the Team B head coach (Rule 3-3-1-b). Team B still has 2 timeouts remaining, but since they have already called a timeout in the dead ball period, a timeout is not available for Team B (Rule 3-3-4-a). The timeouts for a specific dead ball period will not reset if the opposing team calls a time out.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-44. Kicker A80 is lined up in scrimmage kick formation at the A-30. The snap goes straight over his head, and he retrieves the ball in the tackle box at the A-15. As A80 wheels around, he punts the ball. Just as A80's foot hits the ball, B55 tackles A80. The punt goes forward and rolls out of bounds at the A-35.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-30, Snap. Roughing the kicker on B55.
choice_c: A, 4/10, A-44, Snap. Running into the kicker on B55.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-35, Snap. The contact by B55 is not roughing the kicker. The kicker's protection under the roughing / running into the kicker rule now ends when the kicker carries or possesses the ball more than 5-yards behind their initial position from scrimmage kick formation (Rule 9-1-16-a-4).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-44. Kicker A80 is lined up in scrimmage kick formation at the A-30. The snap goes straight to A80, and he muffs the snap. A80 immediately picks the ball up from his initial position, and clearly goes into his punting motion and punts the ball. In an attempt to block the kick, B55 dives into A80 and makes hard contact to A80's plant leg. The punt goes out of bounds at the A-22.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-41, Snap. A80's action makes it clear that a kick will be attempted. When it is obvious a scrimmage kick will be made, no opponent shall rough the kicker (Rule 9-1-16-a). The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot and includes an automatic first down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-44. Kicker A80 is lined up in scrimmage kick formation at the A-30. The snap goes straight to A80, and he muffs the snap. A80 picks the ball up at the A-29 within the tackle box, and as he wheels around, A80 punts the ball. Just as A80's foot hits the ball, B55 makes strong contact to A80's kicking leg. The punt goes out of bounds at the B-40.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-44, Snap. Roughing the kicker on B55.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap. The contact by B55 is not roughing or running into the kicker. A80 is afforded protection under the roughing / running into the kicker rule only if it is obvious that a kick will be made (Rule 9-1-16-a). Team B will take over where Team A's scrimmage kick ends.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. QBA1 scrambles to the B-38 and throws a forward pass that falls incomplete at the B-31. The Referee rules A1 down before the ball was released. Replays show A1 was not down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/18, B-38, Running. If the ruling on the field is the player is down before a pass is thrown, replay cannot make it a pass. The ruling of down is not reviewable. The play is reviewable if the player fumbled and there was a clear recovery in the immediate continuing action.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Back A2 runs to the A-40 where he is contacted by B30 and falls forward toward the ground. B44 comes in and leads with his shoulder and attacks with forcible contact to the side of A2's head just before A2 hits the ground at the A-42.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ A-42, Running. No foul for targeting.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-42, Running. Targeting foul on B44, DQ B44.
choice_c: A, 1/10, A-27, Running. Targeting foul on B44, DQ B44.
choice_d: A, 2/8, A-42, Running. Personal foul on B44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ A-42, Running. No foul for targeting. A2 is a runner and not considered defenseless in this situation. He would transition to defenseless once he is clearly on the ground. If this same contact occurred after A2 clearly hit the ground, it would be 9-1-4 targeting.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-3. A44 sweeps right end and fumbles at the B-2. B21 recovers at the B-1 and returns to midfield and fumbles. A12 recovers at the B-48 and returns to the B-2 and fumbles the ball forward into the end zone. A88 recovers while grounded in the end zone.
choice_a: The try is over, no score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The 4th down fumble rule (Rule 7-2-2-a Exception 2) is not in effect on this play because there has been a change of team possession. The forward fumble by A12 is recovered in the end zone by A88 and results in a touchdown (Rule 7-2-2-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A44 sweeps right end and fumbles at the B-2. B21 recovers at the B-1 and returns to midfield and fumbles. A12 recovers at the B-48 and returns to the B-2 and fumbles the ball forward into the end zone. A88 recovers while grounded in the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_b: The try is good. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: The try is over, no score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: No score. Replay the try. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The try is over, no score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The new editorial change for the 2023 season removes the requirement of "before a change of possession" for a Team A fumble. Even though there has been a change of team possession on the play, A12's forward fumble into the end zone is recovered by teammate A88, there is no Team A score, and the try is over (Rule 8-3-2-d-5).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, late in the 4th Quarter with Team A trailing by 5 points. A11 carries to the B-14 where he is injured when tackled inbounds by B79. When stopped, the clock reads 0:05, and Team A has no time outs remaining.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-14, 10-Second Runoff and the game is over.
choice_b: There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff. A, 1/10, B-14, Snap.
choice_d: There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff. A, 1/10, B-14, Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff. A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock was stopped to award Team A's 1st down. Rule 3-3-5-f states that the 10-second runoff applies only if the player's injury is the only reason for stopping the clock (other than that player's helmet or a teammate's helmet coming off) with less than one minute in the half. A11 must leave the game for at least one down (Rule 3-3-5-a-1).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, late in the 4th Quarter with Team A trailing by 5 points. A11 carries to the B-16 where he is injured when tackled inbounds by B79. When stopped, the clock reads 0:05, and Team A has no time outs remaining.
choice_b: A, 4/1, B-16, Ready. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-16, 10-Second Runoff. Since the run by A11 is short of the line to gain, the only reason for stopping the clock was for the injury timeout for A11. With 5 seconds remaining, Team B will accept the 10-Second Runoff, and the game is over (Rule 3-3-5-f). If there had been any other reason for the clock to have stopped (other than that player's helmet or a teammate's helmet coming off), then there would have been no option for a 10-Second Runoff.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Late in the 3rd quarter, A90 punts and the ball is coming down to B21 who is in position to receive the ball at the B-18. A80 interferes with B21's opportunity to catch a kick and the ball bounces to the B-10 where the kick is dead. When the ball is declared dead, the game clock reads 0:00.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, No clock - extend the 3rd quarter.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-33, Snap. Reset the ball to the opposite end of the field & clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter.
choice_c: A, 4/10, A-25, Snap. Reset the clock to 15:00 for the 4th Quarter.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-33, No clock - extend the 3rd quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-33, Snap. Reset the ball to the opposite end of the field & clock to 15:00 for the start of the 4th Quarter. The penalty for kick-catching interference is enforced 15 yards from the spot of the foul which is the B-18 (Rule 6-4-1 PENALTY). The period is not extended. We will flip the field and the penalty will be enforced at the beginning of the 4th quarter (Rule 3-2-3-a).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the 2nd quarter, A90's squib kick is recovered by B80 at the B-25 and returned to the A-35. The game clock expires during the return and A44 was offside on the kick.
choice_a: B, 1/10, A-35, No clock - extend 2nd qtr., with no other options.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, No clock - extend 2nd qtr., as the only option.
choice_c: B, 1/10, A-30, No clock - extend 2nd qtr. <or> A, Free Kick, A-30, No clock - extend 2nd qtr. <or> half over.
choice_d: Half over, with no option to extend.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-30, No clock - extend 2nd qtr. <or> A, Free Kick, A-30, No clock - extend 2nd qtr. <or> half over. Rule 6-1-2-c-1 PENALTY allows three options to enforce the penalty - Five yards from the previous spot, five yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, or the touchback spot. If the penalty is accepted by Team B, the period is extended and there is an untimed down (Rule 3-2-3-a-1). If the penalty is declined the half is over.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Back A21 is stationary and is lined up in the tackle box. Just after the snap A21 immediately releases outside the tackle box. QB A12 is in the pocket to pass and A21 sees linebacker B54 blitzing. A21 returns into the tackle box and before A12 throws the pass, blocks B54 below the waist in the tackle box and the block is directed from the front. A12's pass is complete for a 15-yard gain.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, A-15, Ready. Foul by A21 for an illegal block below the waist. Once a player leaves the tackle box during the play, that player is considered outside the tackle box for the remainder of the down. A21 may not block below the waist once considered outside the tackle box (Rule 9-1-6-a-2 and AR 9-1-6 X).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Midway through the 2nd quarter, back A21 is stationary and is lined up in the tackle box. Just after the snap, A21 immediately steps up and blocks blitzing linebacker B55 1-yard beyond the neutral zone. The block is below the waist and directed from the front. QB A12 then throws a legal forward pass that is complete inbounds for a 15-yard gain.
choice_b: A, 3/25, A-15, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A21.
choice_d: A, 3/10, A-30, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A21.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Running. Legal block by A21. A block initiated 1-yard beyond the neutral zone is considered within the tackle box for both a lineman and back assuming that block meets all the other requirements for a legal block below the waist (Rule 9-1-6-a-2 and AR 9-1-6 XIII). Based on the rules change for 2023, the clock will not stop to award Team A's 1st down (Rule 3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. QBA1 throws a forward pass toward receiver A88 at the B-30 that falls incomplete. After the ball hit the ground B44 led with his head and attacked making forcible contact to the head of A88. There are no flags on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap, DQ B44. Replay must stop the game and add the foul for targeting as A88 is considered defenseless and all the elements for 9-1-4 are present. The foul occurred after the ball became dead, so the ball goes over to Team B on downs and the penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-12. Back A2 runs near the sideline and dives for the goal line pylon. The ball comes loose and B59 recovers it on the ground in the end zone. The Line Judge rules A2 stepped out of bounds at the B-3 before he dove for the goal line. Replays show A2 was in bounds, but there is no clear evidence as to whether the ball came loose before or after it broke the plane of the goal line.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/1, B-3, Ready. If the on-field ruling is a ball carrier out of bounds and the ball makes it to the end zone in the continuing action replay can rule on that action but must have clear video evidence to overturn the ruling. Here it is clear A2 was not out of bounds, but not clear as to whether it is a touchdown or touchback so the ruling on the field stands.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running late in the second half. Team A trails by four points and is out of timeouts. After the ball is ready for play lineman A66 commits a false start, and when the officials stop the game clock it reads 0:13.
choice_b: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff and the game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff. Team B will accept the 5-yard penalty and the 10-second subtraction (Rule 3-4-4-a-1). The game clock will be reset to 3 seconds and will start on the Referee's signal (Rule 3-4-4-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running late in the second half. Team A trails by four points and is out of timeouts. After the ball is ready for play lineman A66 commits a false start, and when the officials stop the game clock it reads 0:08.
choice_a: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff and the game is over.
choice_b: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff. Game clock set to 0:08 - Snap.
choice_c: A, 2/15, B-35, Ready. Game clock at 0:08.
choice_d: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff. Game clock set to 0:02 - Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff and the game is over. Team B will accept the 5-yard penalty and the 10-second subtraction (Rule 3-4-4-a-1). Since the game clock was stopped at 0:08, subtracting 10 seconds will leave no time remaining and the game is over and Team B wins.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running late in the second half. Team A trails by two points and is out of timeouts. At the snap Team A has five players in the backfield. A22 carries for a three-yard gain to the B-27. When the ball is declared dead the game clock reads 0:08.
choice_a: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-second runoff and the game is over.
choice_b: A, 2/15, B-35, Ready. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, Ready. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff. Team B will accept the 5-yard penalty for the illegal formation (Rule 7-1-4-a-4). Because the illegal formation is not a foul that causes the clock to stop immediately, the 10-second runoff does not apply (Rule 3-4-4) and there is no adjustment to the game clock. After the penalty is administered the game clock starts on the referee's signal (Rule 3-3-2-e-4).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. Late in the 4th quarter with the game clock running, Tackle B77 is in the neutral zone at the snap but does not make contact. QB A12 rolls out to pass, runs to the B-17 and throws a forward pass, which falls incomplete. The game clock reads 0:15 after the play.
choice_c: A, 2/5, B-20, Snap. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff.
choice_d: A, 2/10, B-25, Snap. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-20, Snap. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff. A12's pass is from beyond the neutral zone and is an illegal forward pass (Rule 7-3-2-a) and B77 is offside. This results in offsetting fouls (Rule 10-1-4). There is no 10-second runoff if there are offsetting fouls (Rule 3-4-4-e). The game clock remains at 15 seconds and starts on the snap (AR 3-4-4 IV).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-9. Team A is behind late in the 2nd quarter with the game clock running and neither team has a timeout remaining. A66 is in a three-point stance, misses the snap count and lurches forward, committing a false start. B77 then commits a flagrant dead-ball personal foul in retaliation. The game clock is stopped with 8 seconds remaining in the half.
choice_a: 10-second runoff, half over. B77's penalty is declined by rule. Eject B77.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal, B-7, Snap, as the only option. Eject B77.
choice_c: 10-second runoff, half over and B77's penalty is enforced on the 2nd half kickoff. Eject B77.
choice_d: 10-second runoff, half over and B77's penalty is carried over to the 2nd half – or – A, 1/Goal, B-7, Snap. Eject B77.
explanation: The correct ruling is: 10-second runoff, half over and B77's penalty is carried over to the 2nd half – or – A, 1/Goal, B-7, Snap. Eject B77. If Team B accepts the 10-second runoff associated with the false start, the half is over (Rule 3-4-4-a) and the penalty for B77's dead-ball foul carries over to the second half. Due to the 10-second runoff, by interpretation the Team B dead-ball foul effectively occurs after the half has ended and the penalty is thus carried over to the 3rd quarter (AR 3-4-4 V). An option but an unlikely choice - if Team B did not want the penalty for B77's actions to be carried over to the 3rd quarter, they can decline the runoff and both the penalty for the false start by A66 and the dead ball foul by B77 would be enforced. This would give Team A 1/Goal at the B-7 with 0:08 remaining in the 2nd quarter with the clock starting on the snap and there would be no carryover to the 3rd quarter. In either case, B77 is ejected.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-35. Late in the game with the game clock running, ball carrier A35 is tackled at the B-28. B79 goes to the ground with an injury and the game clock reads 0:45.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-28, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because since we are inside of two minutes in the 4th quarter the game clock stops to award the first down (Rule 3-3-2-e-1) as well as for the injury. The 10-second runoff applies to an injured player if that is the only reason to stop the clock (Rule 3-3-5-f). The play clock is set to 40 seconds for the defensive injury (Rule 3-2-4-c-4) and the game clock starts on the referee's signal.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. Late in the game, A44 runs to the B-27 and is down inbounds. During the down A22's helmet comes off (no helmet foul by the defense) and B77 goes down with an injury. When the clock is stopped it reads 0:58 in the fourth quarter.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-27, Ready. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-27, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock stops for both the helmet off and the injury, and these occur for opposing players (AR 3-3-9 III). The play clock is set at 40 seconds, due to the defensive injury, rather than 25 seconds due to the helmet coming off the offensive player and the game clock will start on the ready (Rule 3-3-2-e-5, 16). B77 must leave the game for one play and unless Team A takes a charged timeout, A22 must leave the game for one play as well.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. Late in the game, A44 runs to the B-27 and is down inbounds. During the down A22's helmet comes off (no helmet foul by the defense) and A45 goes down with an injury. When the clock is stopped it reads 0:58 in the fourth quarter. Team A has no timeouts remaining.
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-27, Ready. There is no option for a 10-Second Runoff.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-27, 10-second runoff. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-27, 10-second runoff. Because the injury and the helmet off occur to players on the same team, Team B has an option for a 10-second runoff (Rule 3-3-5-f). If Team B accepts the runoff, the game clock will be set to 0:48 and start on the Referee's signal. If Team B declines the runoff, the game clock will stay at 0:58 and will start on the snap (Rule 3-3-5-f-2). The play clock will be set to 25 seconds. A22 and A45 must leave the game for one play.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter the score is tied and both teams have one timeout remaining. A80 catches a pass and runs to the B-28 where he is tackled by B7 (0:40). There is a flag down for (a) targeting by B7; or (b) illegal substitution (12 players on field at snap) by Team B. Replays show there was no foul on the play.
choice_a: In both (a) and (b) A, 4/3, B-28, reset game clock to 0:40, Ready, with no runoff.
choice_c: In both (a) and (b) A, 4/3, B-28, 0:34, Snap.
choice_d: In both (a) and (b) A, 4/3, B-28, reset game clock to 0:40 and 10-second runoff applies. Either team may use a timeout to avoid the runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: In both (a) and (b) A, 4/3, B-28, reset game clock to 0:40 and 10-second runoff applies. Either team may use a timeout to avoid the runoff.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter the score is tied and both teams have one timeout remaining. A80 catches a pass and runs to the B-28 where he is tackled by B7 (0:40). There are no flags on the play. Replay Official stops the game (0:34) to review possible targeting by B7. Replays show B7 did commit a targeting foul.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B7, reset game clock to 0:40, no runoff, clock status at the option of Team A.
choice_b: A, 4/3, B-28, DQ B7, reset game clock to 0:40, 10-second runoff applies.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B7, reset game clock to 0:34, Snap.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B7, 0:40, 10-second runoff applies.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B7, reset game clock to 0:40, no runoff, clock status at the option of Team A.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, 4Q. A11 carries to the B-14 where he is injured when tackled inbounds by B79. When stopped, the clock reads 0:05, and Team A has no time outs remaining.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-14, 10-Second Runoff and the game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. There is no option for a ten-second runoff on this play because the clock is stopped to award Team A's first down and the injury to A11 is not the only reason to stop the clock (Rule 3-3-5-f). The game clock will remain at 0:05 and will start on the Referee's signal.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/3 @ B-40. A44 runs for a first down inbounds at the B-30 with 1:25 on the game clock in the 4th quarter. The Center Judge has placed the ball on the ground at the B-30. The referee waves the Center Judge away from the ball but before he signals the ball ready for play, snapper A55 snaps the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. Team A dead-ball foul for delay of game. Five-yard penalty, first and 10 at the B-35. NOTE: This is a foul between series in that it takes place before the ball is declared ready for the next series. Hence it is first and 10, not first and 15. (Rules 4-1-1, 4-1-4, 5-2-7-a and AR 5-2-7 V).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-17. Late in the 1st quarter, ball carrier A22 is tackled hard by B54 at the B-5, resulting in first and goal. After the ball is dead, A22 gets up and punches B54 multiple times.
choice_a: A, 1/Goal, B-5, Ready. A22 is ejected for fighting.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is disqualified for the remainder of the game and the 1st half of the next game.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-20, Snap. A22 is ejected for fighting.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected for fighting.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected for fighting. The action by A22 results in a 15-yard penalty against Team A and A22 is ejected from the game (Rules 2-32-1 and 9-5-1-a). After enforcement of the penalty, Team A will have first and 10 at the B-20 (Rule 5-2-7-a).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-17. Late in the 3rd quarter, ball carrier A22 is tackled hard by B54 at the B-5, resulting in first and goal. After the ball is dead, A22 gets up and punches B54 multiple times.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected for the remainder of the game and the 1st half of the next game for fighting.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected for the remainder of the game only.
choice_c: A, 1/Goal, B-5, Ready. A22 is ejected for fighting.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-20, Snap. A22 is ejected for the remainder of the game and the 1st half of the next game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is ejected for the remainder of the game and the 1st half of the next game for fighting. The action by A22 results in a 15-yard penalty against Team A and A22 is ejected for the remainder of the game and the 1st half of the next game. (Rules 2-32-1 and 9-5-1-b). After enforcement of the penalty, Team A will have first and 10 at the B-20 (Rule 5-2-7-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. A7's pass is intercepted by B49 deep in B's end zone. While still in the end zone, A81 twists B49's face mask, causing him to fumble. The loose ball is recovered by A86 while grounded at the B-5.
choice_a: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, Snap. The foul by A81 occurs during the run by B49 while in the end zone. The penalty for A81's foul will be enforced from the goal line, and will give Team B a 1st down at the B-15 (Rule 10-2-2-d-2-c).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A is offside, and the kickoff goes out of bounds at the B-20 after it has been touched by B80 at the B-25.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap – or – Re-kick from the A-30.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – Re-kick from the A-30.
choice_d: A, Re-kick from the A-30, FK Rules, as the only option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – Re-kick from the A-30. There is no foul for a free kick out of bounds because B80 touched the kick inbounds (Rule 6-2-1). Team B may elect to have the kick repeated at Team A's 30-yard line or snap the ball at the inbounds spot and enforce the penalty for the offside five yards from where the ball went out of bounds (Rule 6-1-8).

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. During A90's scrimmage kick the untouched ball is almost at rest at the B-3 when B22 blocks A80 into the ball, forcing the ball into the end zone where it touches the ground.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. The ball is dead when it touches the ground in the end zone and is a Touchback. The touching by A80 is ignored (Rule 2-11-4-c). By rule, neither team has touched the kicked ball; therefore, the ball is dead when it touches the ground in the end zone (Rule 6-3-9) and results in a touchback for Team B (Rule 8-6-1-b).

question_text: 3/G @ B-6. Team A trails 28-24 and both teams are out of timeouts late in the 4th quarter. Back A2 runs across the goal line and the Line Judge rules touchdown (0:12). Replays show A2 dropped the ball at the ½-yard line (0:13) and the ball rolled into the EZ where no player from either team attempted to recover it.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/G, B-½, 0:03, Ready. Without a clear recovery the ball is brought back to the spot of the fumble and Team A maintains possession. If the initial ruling on the field was a fumble the clock would have continued to run at that point so the overturn results in a running clock. Since the game clock is inside one minute at that point the 10-second runoff rule applies.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team B has two returners deep and A1's kickoff is caught by B13 at the B-2 and the officials blow the play dead there. After a huddle, the Referee announces B4 (the other returner) signaled for a fair catch, and it will be Team B's ball at the 2-yard line. Replays show both B13 and B4 signaled for a fair catch while the ball was in the air.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Since B13 signaled and completed the fair catch Team B gets the ball at the 25-yard line. Any signal by Team B during a free kick or scrimmage kick is reviewable.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. Ball carrier A22 is tackled hard by B44 at the B-5, resulting in first and goal. After the ball is dead, A22 gets up and drives his forearm hard into the face of B44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. The action by A22 is a dead ball personal foul and is flagrant so A22 is ejected. After the enforcement of the 15-yard penalty, Team A will have first and 10 at the B-20 because the foul occurred before the ball was ready for play for the next series. The penalty is completed before the line to gain is established (Rule 5-2-7-a).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game with the kicking team trailing by 2 points, they set up for an on-side kick. The kicking team has 6 players to the left of the kicker and 4 to the right of the kicker. Kicker A90 is lined up to kick the ball to the left side of the formation and as A90 approaches the ball he abruptly stops but the kicking team players to the left of the kicker continue and cross their restraining line. These players stop and then retreat back across their restraining line and A90 quickly turns and kicks the ball back to the right side of the formation with all players for Team A now back behind their restraining line.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. No foul.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Dead ball foul, five-yard penalty from the succeeding spot. By rule (6-1-2-c-1) each Team A player, except the kicker and potential holder, must be behind the ball when the ball is kicked. If they are beyond the ball and the ball is kicked – this is a live ball foul for offside on a kickoff. By interpretation, if a Team A player goes beyond their restraining line after the ball is ready for play and then returns back across their restraining line before the ball is kicked – this is a dead ball foul for offside by the kicking team (AR 6-1-2 IX). This interpretation does not impact situations when the ball falls from the tee (Rule 6-1-2-d). When the ball falls from the tee, the official shall sound their whistle immediately and reset both teams.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-20. While A12 is holding the ball for a field goal attempt at the B-27, B40 rushes off the corner and plays the ball by batting it from A12's hands. The ball is recovered by B55 at the B-30.
choice_a: A, 4/10, B-20, Snap. Illegal batting on B40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap. The batting by B40 is legal and results in a fumble (Rule 9-4-1-c and AR 4-1-3 I) that is recovered by Team B.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A90 executes an on-side kick toward the sideline, and A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. A33 leaps from out of bounds, and while airborne at the A-47, bats the ball, and it rolls forward where A88 recovers at the A-49.
choice_a: B, 1/10, A-49, Snap. Illegal bat on A33.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Illegal batting on A33.
choice_c: Re-kick @ A-30, as the only option.
choice_d: Re-kick @ A-30 – or - B, 1/10, A-42, Snap – or – B, 1/10, B-35, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Re-kick @ A-30 – or - B, 1/10, A-42, Snap – or – B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. Foul against Team A for free kick out of bounds at the A-47. Airborne A33 is out of bounds when he touches the ball because he has not established himself inbounds after going out of bounds (Rule 2-27-15-a-2). Not a foul for batting the ball forward because the ball is dead as soon as A33 touches it (Rule 4-2-3-a). Team B has 3 options for a free kick out of bounds - Five yards from the previous spot; or five yards from the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B; or the receiving team may put the ball in play 30 yards beyond Team A's restraining line at the hash mark (Rule 6-2-1).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. Team A is in scrimmage kick formation. Just after the snap, lineman B77 grabs guard A66 and pulls him to one side, allowing linebacker B43 to shoot the gap to try to block the kick. B44 catches the kick at the B-25 and returns to the B-40 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Snap. B77's holding foul is not covered by postscrimmage kick enforcement since it is before the kick (Rule 10-2-3). The 10-yard penalty is enforced at the previous spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a). The penalty yardage alone does not give Team A a first down, but defensive holding includes an automatic first down (Rule 9-3-4).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A90's punt is blocked, and the ball never crosses the neutral zone. A44 recovers while down at the A-30. B21 blocks gunner A80 below the waist at the A-45 while the ball was loose behind the line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Snap. Postscrimmage Kick enforcement (Rule 10-2-3) applies only when a scrimmage kick crosses the neutral zone. B21 is guilty of an illegal block below the waist (Rule 9-1-6-c) and Team A retains possession after the 15-yard penalty is enforced at the previous spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. Late in the 2nd quarter, B21 holds A80 at the B-10 during A44's touchdown run. A44 spikes the ball in the end zone after the score.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. Try @ B-3, succeeding kickoff at the A-35.
choice_b: No score. Replay the down. A, 2/25, B-40, Snap.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. Try @ B-3 and succeeding kickoff at the A-20 – or – Try @ B-18. Allow the score.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A. Try @ B-3, with Team B's holding penalty enforced on the try.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. Try @ B-3 and succeeding kickoff at the A-20 – or – Try @ B-18. Allow the score. The penalty for Team B's holding foul is declined by rule (Rule 10-2-5-a-2). Team B has the option of Team A being penalized for the spike by A44 15-yards on the try, the succeeding kickoff (or from the succeeding spot in extra periods when applicable) (Rule 10-2-5-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. During the successful kick try, B54 is offside. After the ball is dead, B55 commits a personal foul.
choice_a: Score 1 point for Team A and Free Kick @ A-35.
choice_b: Retry for Team A @ B-1½, as the only option.
choice_c: Score 1 point for Team A. Enforce B55's penalty on the succeeding kickoff, no retry option.
choice_d: Score 1 point for Team A and Free Kick @ 50 – or – Retry for Team A @ B-¾ Yard line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 1 point for Team A and Free Kick @ 50 – or – Retry for Team A @ B-¾ Yard line. If Team A declines the offside penalty and accepts the point, the penalty for Team B's dead-ball foul is then enforced on the succeeding kickoff (or at the succeeding spot in extra periods if applicable) (Rule 8-3-5). If Team A elects to replay the try, both penalties against Team B are enforced before the snap (Rule 10-1-6).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter the score is tied at 14 and team A is out of timeouts. QBA1 completes a pass to receiver A89 who is tackled inbounds at the B-28 and time expires. Video shows the clock was at 0:02 when A89's left knee touched the ground.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-28, reset clock to 0:02, charge Team A TO, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Half over. In this situation if Team A is out of timeouts there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the player is down for replay to reset the clock. If Team A has a timeout remaining, then replay can reset to less than 3 seconds and Team A is charged the timeout.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. QBA1's pass is ruled incomplete at the A-40. The Team A head coach takes a timeout to challenge the ruling on the field. After review, the ruling on the field stands. After the ball is made ready for the next down QBA1 comes to the line and as the play clock is running down, he turns to the Referee signaling timeout.
choice_a: A, 3/10, A-25, timeout granted to Team A.
choice_c: A, 3/15, A-20, no timeout granted. Delay of game on Team A.
choice_d: A, 3/10, A-25, Snap. Delay of game declined.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 3/10 @ A-25, no timeout granted. The timeout taken to challenge the ruling on the field is the only timeout allowed by Team A in that dead ball period. This is the case regardless of the outcome of the review (successful or not).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. Tight end A83 is on the end of his scrimmage line just outside the tackle box. Quarterback A12 is under center, receives the snap and at the A-34 immediately hands the ball forward to A83 who moved laterally just after the snap. A83 is tackled inbounds after gaining 12 yards on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/16, A-29, Ready. The action and handoff are immediately after the snap and it is not likely that a handoff would have had time to comply with the "turn'' requirement for A83 (Rule 7-1-6-b). The handoff clearly does not meet the "two yards behind the line'' requirement so this is a foul for Illegal forward handing and the Penalty is 5-yards from the spot of the foul and carries a loss of a down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air and A80 muffs the ball and the ball drops to the ground.
choice_a: A, 2/10, A-25, Ready. Backward pass, ball dead at the spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-25, Snap. The short flip of the ball by A12 is considered a legal forward pass unless it is clearly and obviously thrown backward. When the ball strikes the ground, it is ruled an incomplete forward pass and the play is dead (AR 7-3-1 I).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air but it is a fake jet sweep and A80 passes by and does not touch the ball. A12 catches the ball and then throws a forward pass from the A-20 to A88 who makes the catch at the A-40 and is downed inbounds at that spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/20, A-15, Ready. The short flip of the ball by A12 is considered a legal forward pass unless it is clearly and obviously thrown backward. The catch of the forward pass by A12 is legal, but A12's pass to A88 is the second forward pass during the same down and is an Illegal Forward Pass. The penalty is 5-yards from the spot of the foul and loss of down (Rule 7-3-2-d & AR 7-3-1 II).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-3. Late in the 3rd quarter, Ball carrier A21 is downed at the one-yard line and then B67 piles on. A21 then retaliates by punching B67 multiple times into the neck area.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-1, Ready. B67's and A21's penalties cancel since neither has been completed, and since both are dead ball fouls, it is 3rd down (Rule 10-1-5 Exception). A21 is ejected for fighting for the remainder of the game and for the 1st half of the next game (Rule 9-5-1-b and 10-1-1-b).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A91 is prepared to kick off. The ball is on the tee and the referee has signaled it ready for play. As A91 approaches the ball it begins to roll off the tee just as he starts his kicking motion. The kicker follows through and kicks the ball as it continues to roll off or near the tee. The ball rolls out of bounds untouched at the A-45.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap. Free kick out of bounds.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Illegal kick on A91.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. No foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. No foul. This is not a violation of either 9-4-4 or 9-2-1-a-2-a. The officials should stop play and have the teams line up for a new kickoff. If weather conditions dictate, Team A should have a player hold the ball on the tee (AR 9-4-1 XI).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. B21 intercepts a legal forward pass three yards deep in his end zone. B21 fumbles while still in the end zone and the ball rolls forward and out of bounds on Team B's two-yard line.
choice_b: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. B21 intercepted the ball in the end zone and never advanced out of the end zone so the impetus is imparted by Team A's pass (Rule 8-7-1). The fumble is a forward fumble out of bounds, so the ball belongs to Team B back at the spot of the fumble in Team B's end zone (Rule 7-2-4-b-1). The result of the play is a touchback (Rule 8-6-1).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. B21 intercepts a legal forward pass on his three-yard line, and his momentum carries him into his end zone. B21 fumbles in the end zone and the ball rolls forward and out of bounds on Team B's two-yard line.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-3, Snap. The fumble by B21 is a forward fumble out of bounds, so the ball belongs to Team B back at the spot of the fumble in Team B's end zone (Rule 7-2-4-b-1). B21 intercepted in the field of play and his original momentum carried B21 into his own end zone, so the momentum exception to a safety applies, and the ball belongs to Team B at the spot where B21 gained possession (Rule 8-5-1-a Exception) which is the B-3.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. B21 intercepts a legal forward pass at the B-3 and then, on his own, retreats into Team B's end zone. B21 fumbles while in the end zone and the ball rolls forward and out of bounds on Team B's two-yard line.
choice_b: Safety, Score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, Score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The fumble by B21 is a forward fumble out of bounds, so the ball belongs to Team B back at the spot of the fumble in Team B's end zone (Rule 7-2-4-b-1). Since B21 carried the ball into the end zone (no momentum), Team B's is responsible for the ball being in the end zone (Rule 8-7-1). Since the ball is dead by rule in Team B's end zone and Team B is responsible for the ball being there, it is a safety (Rule 8-5-1-a).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. Shotgun QBA1 controls the snap and flips the ball directly up into the air to receiver A87 who was in motion toward the ball at the snap. The ball hits the ground and B77 recovers and is downed at the B-34. The officials rule fumble.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-30, Snap. This is an incomplete pass. Game clock reset to when the ball hit the ground if under two minutes in the half.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter Team A trails 27-24 and has one timeout remaining. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-25 near the sideline and is tackled in bounds (0:02). The ball comes loose and B37 recovers and runs to the A-40 where he is pushed out of bounds with no time remaining on the game clock. The officials rule fumble. Replays show A80's left knee down before he lost possession of the ball.
choice_a: B, 1/10, A-40, no time remaining, game over.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-25, reset game clock to 0:02, Ready.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-25, reset game clock to 0:02, charge Team A TO, snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, reset game clock to 0:02, charge Team A TO, snap. Replay can put any amount of time back on the clock in this situation if Team A has a timeout remaining. If Team A was out of timeouts there would have to be at least 3 seconds to put time back on the clock.

question_text: 3/12 @ A-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A12 rolls out and throws a pass to A80 who makes the catch at the A-40 and runs out of bounds. Defender B21 holds receiver A88 early is his route at the A-38 and before the ball is thrown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Ready. The holding foul by B21 against A88 is during the forward pass play (Rule 2-30-1), and the basic spot is the previous spot since the foul occurred during a forward pass play (Rule 10-2-2-d-3). The penalty is 10-yards and when enforced from the previous spot gives Team A more yardage than the result of the play and also includes an automatic 1st down.

question_text: 3/12 @ A-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A12 rolls out and throws a pass to A80 who makes the catch at the A-40 and runs out of bounds. Defender B21 grabs and twists receiver A88's facemask early is his route at the A-38 and before the ball is thrown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. The face mask foul by B21 against A88 is during the forward pass play (Rule 2-30-1). Rule 9-1-8-b lists a face mask foul as a personal foul, and penalties for Team B personal fouls during a legal forward pass play are enforced from the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of possession (Rule 9-1 Penalty).

question_text: 3/12 @ A-35. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A12 rolls out and throws a pass to A80 who makes the catch at the A-40 and runs out of bounds. Defender B54 gives a throat slash that is directed at A77 at the A-35 before the ball is thrown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. The throat slash is an automatic unsportsmanlike conduct foul by B54 directed at A77 (Rule 9-2-1-a-1-a) and is during the forward pass play (Rule 2-30-1). Rule 9-2-1 Penalty states that penalties for Team B unsportsmanlike conduct fouls occurring during a legal forward pass play are enforced from the end of the last run when it ends beyond the neutral zone and there is no change of possession.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-45. During the down A22's helmet comes off (no helmet foul by the defense) and B77 goes down with an injury. Ball carrier A44 is tackled inbounds at the B-41. When the clock is stopped it reads 0:58 in the fourth quarter. Include the play clock setting as a part of the answer.
choice_a: A, 4/1, B-41, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 4/1, B-41, Ready. Play clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 4/1, B-41, Snap. Play clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 4/1, B-41, Ready. Play clock set to 40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-41, Ready. Play clock set to 40. The play clock is set at 40 seconds, due to the defensive injury, rather than 25 seconds due to the helmet coming off the offensive player. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock stops for both the helmet off and the injury, and these occur for opposing players (Rule 3-3-9-b & AR 3-3-9 III). Unless Team A takes a charged timeout, A22 must leave the game for one play (Rule 3-3-9-a). Regardless of any charged time out, B77 musty leave the game for 1 play.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score late in the fourth quarter. When ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play, the game clock reads 1:47. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-26. On the play, A22 gained 12 yards.
choice_a: A, 2/19, A-16, Snap or Ready at the option of Team B.
choice_c: A, 1/10, A-37, Snap or Ready at the option of Team B.
choice_d: A, 2/9, A-26, Snap or Ready at the option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/19, A-16, Snap or Ready at the option of Team B. The basic spot on the play is the end of the run (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-b). The holding during the running play occurred behind the basic spot and is penalized from the spot of the foul (Rule 10-2-2-c-1). The game clock starts on the snap or on the referee's signal, at the option of Team B (Rule 3-4-3-b).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. During the Free Kick, B21 signals for a fair catch at the B-5, muffs the ball and it rolls back into Team B's end zone where B44 recovers.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Since B21 signaled for a fair catch and muffed the ball, the ball is declared dead when B44 recovers (Rule 2-8-1-c). The kick provided the impetus for the ball being in the end zone (Rule 8-7-2), and it is declared dead in the end zone so the result is a touchback (Rule 8-6-1-b) and Team B will put the ball in play at their own 25-yard line.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Midway through the 4th quarter, QB A12 takes the snap and rolls out to the right. Finding no receiver open, A12 turns upfield and runs. After making the line to gain, A12 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Running. The live ball becomes dead at the point where A12 simulates or fakes a feet-first slide (Rule 4-1-3-r). Since the play results in a 1st down for Team A inbounds, the clock should continue to run (Rule 3-3-2-e-1). An official shall sound their whistle and declare the ball dead. A fake slide is not reviewable under Rule 12-3-3.

question_text: 4/15 @ 50. A90's punt is untouched beyond the neutral zone when A88 reaches across Team B's goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play. B22 muffs the ball at the B-2 and A43 recovers it at the B-6. While the ball is loose, B77 holds A21 at the B-10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Snap. Team A may cancel the illegal touching privilege by accepting the holding penalty, which is enforced at the previous spot with an automatic first down. Postscrimmage kick rules do not apply to B77's foul since Team B will not next put the ball in play (Rule 10-2-3).

question_text: Free kick @ A-35. A1 attempts an onside kick and the ball is first touched and recovered by A32 at the A-46. The on-field officials call a foul for an illegal block by A14. Replays show when the initial contact by A14 occurred the ball was at the A-45.
choice_b: A, 4/15, 50, Snap. Illegal block on A14.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-46, Snap. There is no foul for an illegal block as the ball had made the Team B restraining line and Team A is eligible to block at that point. Replay can review all aspects of the block including the timing.

question_text: 1/Goal @ B-8. QBA1 runs an option play to his right and pitches the ball from the B-10 to back A2 at the B-12. A2 runs out of bounds at the B-2. Just as A1 pitched the ball B56 forcibly hits him in the head with his right shoulder. There were no flags on the play.
choice_a: A, 2/Goal, B-2, Ready. Targeting on B56, DQ B56.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-2, Ready. Targeting on B56, DQ B56.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal, B-1, Ready. Personal foul on B56.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/Goal, B-2, Ready. An option QB is a runner and not defenseless unless he clearly establishes himself in a normal passing posture. The only type of targeting that can occur in this situation is 9-1-3 with the crown of the helmet.

question_text: 4/7 @ B-47. QB A12 takes the snap and runs in an attempt to gain a 1st down. A12 slides feet first and his knees hit down with the ball at the B-40. He began lowering his backside and entered the slide with the ball at the B-41. Just after A12 hits the ground, B54 is guilty of a personal foul with targeting against A12.
choice_d: A, 4/1, B-40, Snap. Targeting on B54.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-26, Snap. The play is declared dead when A12 begins to lower his backside entering the slide at the B-41. Since Team A did not make the line to gain, a new series is awarded to Team B (Rule 5-1-1-c). The dead ball personal foul with Targeting will be enforced 15-yards from the dead ball spot and it will be Team B's ball 1st and 10 (Rules 10-2-2-a & 5-2-7-a). B54 will be disqualified.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. During the first possession series of the first extra period, B21 intercepts a forward pass at the B-5, returns, and has a clear field to Team A's goal line when he flips into the end zone. B21 leaped from the B-3 and landed in the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No Clock. The action by B21 is a live ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul (Rule 9-2-1-e & Penalty). Team B's score is canceled, and the unsportsmanlike conduct penalty is carried over to their possession series (Rules 3-1-3-g-1 and 2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A12 throws a forward pass and Team A is flagged for an illegal formation. B21 intercepts the pass, and B38 commits a personal foul for facemask during B21's return and before B21 crosses Team A's goal line.
choice_b: B, 1/10, 15-yard line, No Clock. Down repeated.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. Team B's score is not allowed. Both teams fouled during the down, but Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, so the fouls cancel and the down is not repeated (Rule 3-1-3-g-3). Team A's possession series is ended and since the fouls cancel, Team B will begin its possession series at the 25-yard line.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A12 throws a forward pass and Team A is flagged for an illegal formation. B21 intercepts the pass, and B38 commits a flagrant personal foul during B21's return and before B21 crosses Team A's goal line.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock. Penalty on succeeding play.
choice_d: B, 1/10, 15-yard line, No Clock. Down repeated.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No Clock. Team B's score is not allowed. Both teams fouled during the down, but Team B had not fouled before the change of possession, so the fouls will cancel and the down is not repeated, but the penalty for the flagrant personal foul will be enforced on the succeeding play (Rule 3-1-3-g-3 Exception). Team A's possession series is ended and Team B will begin its possession series at the 40-yard line. B38 will be ejected.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. During the first possession series of the first extra period, B10 intercepts a forward pass on his six-yard line and ultimately downs the ball in his end zone.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. Team A's possession series is over and Team B will begin their possession series first and 10 on the 25-yard line at the same end of the field (Rule 3-1-3-e).

question_text: 4/3 @ B-20. Late in the 3rd quarter, A90's field goal attempt is good and B70 was lined up offside.
choice_a: Score 3 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules, as the only option.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: Score 3 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules – or – A, 4/3, B-15, Snap.
choice_d: Score 3 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules – or – A, 1/10, B-15, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 3 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules – or – A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Team A has the option to accept the 5-yard penalty for offside at the previous spot which will result in a 1st down for Team A, or to keep the points by declining the penalty (Rule 10-2-5-d).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A90's kick is unsuccessful, and Team A lined up with 5 players in the backfield. After the ball becomes dead, B21 commits a dead ball personal foul.
choice_a: Try is unsuccessful. A, Free Kick, 50, FK Rules – or – A, Try, B-4, No Clock.
choice_b: Try is unsuccessful. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules, as the only option.
choice_c: Try is successful. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Try, B-4, No Clock, as the only option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Try is unsuccessful. A, Free Kick, 50, FK Rules – or – A, Try, B-4, No Clock. With Team A not scoring on the try, Team B will almost always refuse the penalty for the Team A foul and thus a replay of the try, and have Team B penalized on the succeeding kickoff. The option is there for Team B to allow Team A to repeat the try and then the dead ball personal foul by B21 would be enforced before the repeat of the try and the succeeding kickoff would be unaffected (Rule 8-3-5).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-3. Ball carrier A21 is down at the one-yard line and then B67 piles on. A21 then retaliates by punching B67 multiple times in the head.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal, B-1, Ready. The yardage penalties by A21 and B67 cancel since neither has been completed and the down established before the fouls occurred is unaffected. A21 is ejected for fighting (Rule 10-1-5).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-4. Late in the 2nd quarter Team A trails 17-10 and has two timeouts remaining. Back A2 takes a handoff and is tackled right at the goal line (0:45). The officials rule A2 down short. Team A rushes to the line to snap the ball, but before they can do so B77 jumps across the line and contacts left guard A68. The officials shut the play down and the Referee announces offside on B77. Replays show that on the 2nd down play, A2 reached the ball across the goal line before he was down.
choice_a: A, 3/Goal, B-1, 0:45, Snap. Offside on B77.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, reset clock to 0:45.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal, B-2, reset clock to 0:45, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, reset clock to 0:45. The 2nd down play is reviewable until there is a legal snap. The encroachment foul prevented the snap so replay can go back and review whether A2 was down or scored. The overturn creates a stopped clock and inside two minutes of the half the clock is reset.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. QBA1 drops back to pass but has to scramble to avoid the rush. He runs to the B-31 where he starts a feet-first slide and is then contacted by B44. The officials spot the ball at the B-30 and there are no flags on the play. Replays show as A1 was sliding B44 led with his shoulder and made forcible contact to A1's head in an attacking fashion.
choice_c: A, 4/9, B-31, Snap. Targeting on B44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-16, Snap, DQ B44. The ball is dead once A1 begins the slide at the B-31 and the ball is short of the line to gain. B34 does target A1, but it is a dead ball foul and Team A failed to make the line to gain so the ball goes over to Team B and the penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 2/10 @ 50. Late in the game with the score tied, Team B substitutes with the play clock winding down. B44 is the 12th player and while running off the field is just outside the numbers when the ball is snapped. A12 takes the snap and throws to A88 who catches the ball at the B-20 and is tackled at the B-10. The game clock expired during the play.
choice_a: A, 2/5, B-45, No Clock (One untimed down).
choice_c: A, 2/10, B-20, No Clock (One untimed down).
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-45, No Clock (One untimed down).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-45, No Clock (One untimed down). The 2023 rule change concerning extending quarters still allows the 2nd and 4th quarters to be extended for an untimed down if a penalty is accepted for a live ball foul (Rule 3-2-3-a-1). The action by B44 is a substitution infraction because he did not get off the field before the ball was snapped. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot and is not tacked on to the end of the last run (Rule 3-5-2-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the second period, ball carrier A44 runs around left end to the B-20 where he throws a backward pass out of bounds to stop the clock. The ball crosses the boundary at the B-22. The game clock stopped at 0:15.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, B-25, 10-second runoff. Because the ball is thrown intentionally out of bounds to conserve time, it is an Illegal pass and the penalty is 5-yards from the spot of the foul and includes loss of down (Rule 7-2-1). This foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule (Rule 3-4-4). The clock will be reset to 0:05 and will start on the Referee's ready-for-play signal.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score late in the fourth quarter. When ball carrier A21 is tackled in the field of play, the game clock reads 1:47. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-28. A21 gained nine yards on the play.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-34, Snap / Ready at the option of Team B.
choice_c: A, 2/14, A-18, Snap / Ready at the option of Team B.
choice_d: A, 2/17, A-15, Snap / Ready at the option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/14, A-18, Snap / Ready at the option of Team B. The holding penalty will be 10-yards and is enforced from the spot of the foul which is the A-28 (Rule 10-2-2-c-1). After enforcement of the penalty, the game clock starts on the snap or on the referee's signal, at the option of Team B (Rule 3-4-3-b).

question_text: 3/3 @ A-32. With the game clock running, snapper A55 snaps the ball before the ball is made ready for play. A12 muffs the snap and B55 recovers the ball at the A-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/8, A-27, Ready. This is a dead ball foul, and the ball does not become alive (Rule 4-1-4) and the 5-yard penalty is enforced from the A-32. All action should be stopped immediately by the game officials.

question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. Midway through the second quarter, A12 takes the snap, runs forward, and fumbles the ball at the A-38. The fumbled ball goes out of bounds at the A-40. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, Team A commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-23, Ready. A12 has made the line to gain before his forward fumble goes out of bounds. The ball is returned to the spot of the fumble which is the A-38 (Rule 7-2-4-b-1), and Team A is awarded a 1st down. The dead ball foul will be penalized 15-yards from this spot, and Team A will have a 1/10 at the A-23 (Rule 5-2-7-a). The clock starts on the ready for play (Rule 3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. Midway through the second quarter, A12 takes the snap, runs forward, and fumbles the ball at the A-38. The fumbled ball goes out of bounds at the A-36. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, Team A commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-21, Snap. A12 has made the line to gain before the fumble, but the backward fumble goes out of bounds at the A-36 and the ball belongs to Team A at that point which is short of the line to gain (Rule 7-2-4-b-2). Since this spot (A-36) is short of the line to gain, Team B is awarded a 1st down. The dead ball foul will be penalized 15-yards from the A-36, and Team B will have a 1/10 at the A-21 (Rule 5-2-7-a). The clock starts on the snap as Team B will next put the ball in play (Rule 3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 3/2 @ A-35. Midway through the second quarter, A12 takes the snap, runs forward, and fumbles the ball at the A-38. The fumbled ball goes out of bounds at the A-36. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, Team A commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/16, A-21, Ready. A12 has made the line to gain before the fumble, but the backward fumble goes out of bounds at the A-36 and the ball belongs to Team A at that point which is short of the line to gain (Rule 7-2-4-b-2). The down counts, and Team A is penalized 15-yards from the dead ball spot (A-36) and will have 4th down (Rule 10-1-5). The clock will start on the ready for play (Rule 3-3-2-e-3).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. A12 throws a pass to A88 at the B-1, and the receiver stretches and grasps the ball in the field of play and is going to the ground on his own as he is attempting to complete the catch. A88 crosses the goal line with firm control of the ball, and as he falls to the ground in the end zone, the ball immediately comes loose and rolls along the ground.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-10, Snap.
