Any receiver going to the ground on his own in the process of making a catch, including the end zone, must maintain complete and continuous control of the ball when he hits the ground to complete the catch (Rule 2-4-3-b). This pass is incomplete.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Returner B17 catches the airborne kick near the sideline at the B-1. The officials huddle then throw a flag for a kickoff out of bounds. Replays show B17 giving an invalid fair catch signal and then completing the catch inbounds before going out of bounds.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap. Free kick out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The signal and possession of the kick are reviewable. Since the ball was caught in the field of play and B17 signaled (valid or invalid) the ball belongs to Team B at its own 25-yard line.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-1. QBA1 attempts a QB sneak but the ball is loose and A2 recovers at the B-3 and advances the ball into the end zone. The officials rule A1 muffed the snap and Team A is given a TD. Replays show A1 possessed the ball then lost it and then A2 recovered.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-3, Snap. Whether A1 possessed the ball is reviewable and since he did it is a fumble that can only be advanced by the fumbling player. The ball is dead when A2 recovers, and Team B will get possession at the spot of the recovery.

question_text: 1/20 @ A-15. A11 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the play and just before the pass defensive end B88 uses his palm to slap A79 in the side of the helmet. The pass to A88 is complete for a 10-yard gain to the A-25 where A88 is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The action by B88 is a personal foul for striking the opponent's helmet (Rule 9-1-2-a). Since the foul was during a forward pass play the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the end of A88's run and includes an automatic first down (Rule 9-1 PENALTY).

question_text: 1/20 @ A-15. A11 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the play and just before the pass defensive end B88 uses his palm to slap A79 in the side of the helmet. The pass to A88 is incomplete.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-30, Snap. The action by B88 is a personal foul for striking the opponent's helmet (Rule 9-1-2-a). Since the foul was during a forward pass play that is incomplete, the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot and includes an automatic first down (Rule 10-2-2-d-3).

question_text: 1/20 @ A-15. A11 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the play and just before the pass defensive end B88 is legally blocked to the ground by A79, and B88 reacts with obscene language and gesture directed to A79. The pass to A88 is complete for a 10-yard gain to the A-25 where A88 is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The action by B88 is a foul for unsportsmanlike conduct (Rule 9-2-1-a-1). Since the foul was during a forward pass play the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the end of A88's run and includes an automatic first down (Rule 9-2-1 PENALTY).

question_text: 4/9 @ A-6. A12 drops straight back to pass and is under heavy duress and throws a forward pass from 3-yards deep in his end zone. The ball lands at the A-10 with no receiver in the area.
choice_a: B, 1/Goal, A-6, Snap, as the only option.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B and Free Kick from the A-20, as the only option.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team B and Free Kick from the A-20 – or – B, 1/Goal, A-6, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B and Free Kick from the A-20 – or – B, 1/Goal, A-6, Snap. The pass by A12 is a foul for intentional grounding (Rule 7-3-2-h). Team B may accept the penalty which will result in a safety since A12 threw the ball from his own end zone. If they decline the penalty, it is Team B's ball, first and goal on the six-yard line (Rule 7-3-7-c).

question_text: 3/2 @ B-45. Team A is in formation with the clock running and the snap is imminent. Team A QB is in shotgun formation and is using a clap as a starting signal. Team B assistant coach claps causing the Team A wideout to flinch and move just before the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. The action by the assistant coach is a dead-ball foul, delay of game for disconcerting signals [S21]. Team A will have 1/10 @ B-40. The rule states that no player may call defensive signals that simulate the sound or cadence of, or otherwise interfere with, offensive starting signals. The spirit of the rule would include squad members and coaches that clap when the snap is imminent and could cause the offense to false start (AR 7-1-5 V).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-25. Punt receiver B22 is at the B-30 awaiting the punt as it makes its downward flight and his teammate B88 is three yards in front of him at the B-33. Down field to cover the kick, A44 legally blocks B88 into B22 just as the ball reaches him. The ball hits B22 in the shoulder and bounces away. Team A recovers at the B-25.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-30, Ready. Kick-catching interference on A44.
choice_c: A, 1/10, A-25, Snap. Kick-catching interference on A44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. This is not kick-catching interference. The action by A44 is against B88 who is not in position to catch the kick, and not against B22. Thus A44 is not deemed to have interfered with B22's opportunity to catch the ball. B22's touching of the ball allows Team A to recover legally at the B-25 (AR 6-4-1 XI).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. Team A punts, and B21 is attempting to catch a kick (no fair catch signal) at the B-25, muffs the ball which is then is recovered by A80 at the B-25. A80 was not interfering with the opportunity of the receiver when B21 was in position to initially make the catch.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, Ready. Kick-catching interference on A80.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. A80's actions are not kick-catching interference. In the absence of a fair catch signal, the protection against interference with the opportunity to catch a kick ends when B21 muffs the ball (Rule 6-4-1-c). The recovery by A80 at the B-25 is legal.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Receiver A80 catches a pass and runs near the sideline to the B-20 where he is tackled, and the ball comes loose. B41 recovers the loose ball in bounds at the B-18 and runs to the 50 where he is pushed out of bounds. The Line Judge ruled A80 stepped out of bounds before the ball came loose. Replays show the ball came out before A80 stepped out.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B,1/10, B-18, Snap. If the ball is loose before the runner steps out of bounds, replay can make it a fumble and give the ball to the defense with a clear recovery in the continuing action. There is no advance allowed after the recovery.

question_text: 1/Goal@ B-8. A79 is on the end of the line and at the snap he retreats into the backfield. QBA1 throws a pass from the B-11 to A79 who catches it at the B-10 and runs the ball across Team B's goal line. The officials rule TD.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/Goal, B-13, Snap. A79 is ineligible to touch a forward pass. Replay can review the direction of the pass and since it is forward it is a foul for illegal touching. Penalty enforced from previous spot and includes a loss of down.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 4 points and neither team has a time out remaining, A44 runs to the B-15 where he is tackled with 40 seconds remaining on the game clock. A77 uses forcible contact to push B55 off the pile while the Umpire is getting the ball from A44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. The action by A77 is a dead ball foul for Unsportsmanlike Conduct (Rule 9-2-1-a-1-k). A44's run for 15-yards is beyond the line to gain and completes that series, and since the foul occurs after the play but before the ball is next ready for play, the penalty is enforced before the line to gain is established, resulting in 1/10 for Team A (Rule 5-2-7-a). There is no 10-second runoff because the clock was stopped to award team A's 1st down, and the foul was not the only reason to stop the clock (Rule 3-4-4-a).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 4 points and neither team has a time out remaining, A44 runs to the B-22 where he is tackled with 40 seconds remaining on the game clock. A77 uses forcible contact to push B55 off the pile while the Umpire is getting the ball from A44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/17, B-37, 10-Second Runoff. The action by A77 is a dead ball foul for Unsportsmanlike Conduct (Rule 9-2-1-a-1-k). A44's run for 8-yards is short of the line to gain and since the foul occurs after the play while the clock is running, the foul is the only reason to stop the clock, and this results in a 10-second runoff (Rule 3-4-4-a). The penalty is enforced 15-yards from the end of the run and the down counts (Rule 5-2-6).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. Late in the 4th quarter with Team A trailing by 4 points, the clock is running and neither team has a time out remaining. B55 jumps into the neutral zone and makes contact with A77. A77 reacts and slams B55 into the ground. The clock reads 0:40 when it is stopped.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/20, B-25, 10-Second Runoff. B55's foul for offside stops the clock immediately, and is subject to a 10-second runoff (Rule 3-4-4-a). Both B55's 5-yard penalty and A77's 15-yard dead ball personal foul penalty will be enforced, and since the ball was ready for play, both are completed after the line to gain is established (Rule 5-2-7-b). Team A will most likely decline the 10-second runoff and if they do the clock will start on the snap (3-4-4-c). If A77's foul is flagrant, he should be disqualified.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game, the kickers need to try and recover an onside kick. A90 initially brings out a tee, but then throws the tee back to his sideline and sets the ball down on the ground without a tee. The Referee signals the ball ready for play, and A90 kicks the ball from the ground. It rolls along the ground and is touched and recovered by A21 at the A-46.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Illegal kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-46, Snap. Legal kick as Rule 2-16-4-c makes it clear that a free kick can be made with or without the use of a tee. If a tee is utilized, it must be in contact with the ball for the kick to be legal. The kick and recovery by A21 is legal.

question_text: 3/10 @ 50. A21 leaves the field of play just after the previous play. Team A huddles with 10 players. Substitute A80 enters, and A88 simulates leaving the field but sets just inside the sideline. After the snap, QB A12 throws deep to A88 who catches the pass and runs into the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, A-35, Snap. A88's action is a simulated replacement of a player, and this action uses the substitution process to confuse Team B. This is an unfair act and penalized as a team Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul (Rule 9-2-2-b). The clock starts on the snap as the result of the play is a touchdown (Rule 3-3-2-c).

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. Team A lines up in scrimmage kick formation with gunner A88 flanked out to the right side of the formation. After the snap, B21 jams gunner A88 and then holds him as A88 tries to work free down the field during the kick. The punt is downed at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Holding by B21. The penalty for this foul is governed by Postscrimmage Kick Enforcement and the postscrimmage kick spot serves as the basic spot for enforcement. The 10-yard penalty will be enforced from the end of the kick which is the B-35 and it will be Team B's ball.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter the score is tied and both teams are out of timeouts. QBA1 scrambles out of the pocket to the B-47 and as he is being tackled attempts to throw the ball away. (0:45). The ball lands out of bounds at the B-42 with no eligible receiver in the area. The Referee comes in and points to the ground ruling A1 down before he released the pass. Replays show A1 was not down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/17, B-47, Running. If the on-field ruling is down, then replay cannot create a pass. Replay should not stop the game and the clock should continue to run.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Punter A1 takes the snap and throws a forward pass to end A80 who catches it at the A-44 and runs out of bounds at the B-40. There are flags down and the Referee announces ineligible player downfield on Team A.
choice_b: A, 4/10, A-45, Snap. Ineligible downfield on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. Since the pass did not cross the line of scrimmage there was no foul for ineligible downfield. Replay can review the spot where the ball was touched in relation to the line of scrimmage. The ball is the key, not the player's body. If it is clear, then replay should use O2O review in a situation like this.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A's kickoff is caught by B21 at the goal line and he breaks free for a long return. Kicker A90 pulls B21 down by the face mask at the A-45. During the return, the Side Judge runs into the Team A head coach who is out on the field of play near the sideline.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-15, Snap. A90's action of grasping and pulling B21's facemask is a live ball foul, and the 15-yard penalty will be added to the end of the return (Rule 9-1-8-a). The action by the coach is a team foul for unsportsmanlike conduct and the penalty is administered as a dead-ball foul. Penalize 15 yards at the succeeding spot which is the A-30. This is a team foul and is not charged as an unsportsmanlike conduct foul against the head coach (Rule 9-2-5-b).

question_text: 4/8 @ B-30. Team A attempts a field goal, and B55, who is lined up 4 yards beyond the neutral zone, runs forward and leaps straight up at the B-28 and partially blocks the kick. The ball continues downfield and rolls dead on Team B's seven-yard line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap. The partial block of the field goal attempt by B55 is legal and the touching is not more than three yards beyond the neutral zone, so the touching is considered within or behind the neutral zone (Rule 6-3-1-b). The kick is considered untouched by the receiving team beyond the neutral zone, so the ball will belong to Team B at the previous spot which is the B-30 (Rule 8-4-2-b-1).

question_text: 4/3 @ B-15. Team A attempts a field goal and the kick is blocked behind the neutral zone. The ball crosses the neutral zone and lands at the B-12. Before any player touches it beyond the neutral zone, the ball rebounds behind the neutral zone and goes out of bounds at the B-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The kick is untouched beyond the neutral zone by Team B, and rebounds behind the neutral zone so all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (Rule 8-4-2-b-3). Since the scrimmage kick is declared dead out of bounds between the goal lines at the B-25, the ball belongs to Team B at the dead ball spot (Rule 6-3-7).

question_text: 4/3 @ B-15. Team A attempts a field goal and the kick is blocked behind the neutral zone. The ball crosses the neutral zone and lands at the B-12. Before any player touches it beyond the neutral zone, the ball rebounds behind the neutral zone and goes out of bounds at the B-17.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-17, Snap. The kick is untouched beyond the neutral zone by Team B, and rebounds behind the neutral zone so all rules pertaining to scrimmage kicks apply (Rule 8-4-2-b-3). Since the scrimmage kick is declared dead out of bounds between the goal lines at the B-17, the ball belongs to Team B at the dead ball spot (Rule 6-3-7) and would not go back to the B-20 (AR 8-4-2 VII).

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-10. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked and hits the ground at the B-6. It then rebounds back to the B-13 where B54 muffs it. While the ball is still loose, A44 recovers at the B-11.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-11, Snap. Even though the touching by B54 is behind the neutral zone, the kick had crossed the neutral zone, so any player may legally recover the ball (Rule 6-3-3). The recovery gives Team A the ball 1/10 at the B-11 (AR 8-4-2 VIII).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20. Defensive back B44 intercepts a forward pass in his own end zone. While still in the end zone he fumbles the ball. It rolls forward, goes into the field of play, and in the scramble the ball goes back into the end zone where B44 recovers while grounded.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The impetus for the ball going back into the end zone the second time is from B44's fumble. The scramble to recover the ball does not add new impetus (Rule 8-7-2-a). The ball became dead in Team B's own end zone, and they provided the impetus for the ball being there so it is a safety (Rule 8-5-1-a).

question_text: 2/20 @ A-30. QB A11 drops back in the pocket and sets up to pass. He then scrambles to his right, sets up again and is in a passing posture when he is hit by end B88 at the A-25 who drives his shoulder into A11's knee. A11 then pulls the ball in and goes to the ground at the A-25 because of being tackled by B88.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Ready. The action by B88 is a foul for roughing the passer (Rule 9-1-9-b). While A11 is technically not a passer because he did not release the ball, the action by B88 is a foul under 9-1-9-b because of the vulnerability of A11 as a potential passer in a passing posture (AR 9-1-9 III). The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot which is the basic spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-a), and results in an automatic 1st down (Rule 9-1 Penalty).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. Late in the 1st quarter, Team A's formation at the snap includes only 10 players: five players on the line of scrimmage numbered 50-79, one player on the end of the line wearing number 82, leaving A77 on the other end of the line. There are four players in the backfield wearing numbers 0-49. A44 takes the handoff and runs to the A-39 on the play.
choice_b: A, 3/5, A-30, Snap. Illegal formation on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-39, Running. The formation is legal because Team A has no more than four players in the backfield and the required number of players (five) on the line wearing 50-79 (Rule 7-1-4-a-3 & 4). There is no restriction to A77 being on the end of the line. A44's run results in a 1st down.

question_text: 3/1 @ 50. A2 takes a handoff and breaks through the line with a clear path to Team B's end zone. At the B-6 near the sideline B24 catches A2 and punches the ball loose. The ball bounces out of bounds near the goal line pylon. The officials rule fumble forward out of bounds at the 1-yard line and spot the ball at the B-6. Replays clearly show the ball bounced directly over the top of the pylon before touching the ground out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Where a fumble or backward pass goes out of bounds in relation to the pylon is reviewable and since the ball crossed over the pylon it is out of bounds in the end zone and a touchback.

question_text: Free kick @ A-35. On a surprise onside kick to start the 2nd half kicker A1 kicks a slow roller that stops at the A-43 where he picks it up. The officials rule illegal touching. There are two questionable blocks by Team A before they were eligible to touch the football, but no flags.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-43, Snap. The illegal touch gives Team B the ball at the A-43. Since Team B has possession the potential fouls for blocking are not significant in this situation and replay should not stop the game. If Team A has possession at the end of the down, then those blocks become significant.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-34. QB A12 completes a pass to A80 who is downed at the B-28. B55 is flagged for roughing the passer.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. Since the roughing the passer foul by B55 is a personal foul and is committed during a legal forward pass play and the run ends beyond the neutral zone, the penalty is added to the end of the run by A80 (Rule 9-1 PENALTY). The penalty will be half the distance to the goal from the B-28.

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. B21 was disqualified late in the 3rd quarter for Targeting and legally remains in the team area. QB A12 looks for a receiver, scrambles and runs out of bounds on Team B's sideline at the A-33. B21 is flagged in the team area for Unsportsmanlike Conduct as he taunts A12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-48, Ready. The action by B21 is a dead ball foul for Unsportsmanlike Conduct and includes a 15-yard penalty from the succeeding spot and an automatic first down for Team A. B21 is ejected from the game and must leave the playing enclosure under team supervision. Any player that has been disqualified and is subsequently penalized for Unsportsmanlike Conduct is automatically ejected (AR 2-27-12 III).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. The score is tied during the second possession series of the first extra period. B25 intercepts a pass at the B-10 and carries the ball across Team A's goal line. During B25's run, B79 commits an illegal blind side block at midfield.
choice_a: The 1st possession series of the 2nd extra period will begin at the 40-yard line.
choice_b: The 1st possession series of the 2nd extra period will begin at the 25-yard line.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
choice_d: The down is replayed. B, 1/10, B-15, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The 1st possession series of the 2nd extra period will begin at the 25-yard line. The score by B25 is cancelled and the next period will start with first and 10 at the 25-yard line, since the penalty by B79 is not carried over (Rules 3-1-3-g-1 & 2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. During the first possession series of the first extra period, B37 intercepts a forward pass at the B-10 and has a clear field to the goal line when he makes an obscene gesture toward the nearest opponent at midfield. B37 continues into the end zone for a touchdown.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
choice_d: B, 1/10, 15-yard line, No Clock. Down repeated.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No Clock. The first possession series is over and the score by B37 is canceled. The 15-yard penalty is carried over to the second possession series since the foul is unsportsmanlike conduct (Rules 3-1-3-g-1 & 2). Team B will begin its possession series after the 15-yard penalty.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-25. After the snap A12 is holding the ball for a place kick at the B-32. As kicker A90 approaches the ball, B55 bats the ball from A12's hands. The ball is recovered by B80 at the B-45 where he is down.
choice_a: A, 4/10, B-25, Snap. Illegal bat on B55.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-45, Snap. The batting by B55 is legal and results in a fumble and the ball remains alive (Rule 4-1-3-b Exception). The recovery by B80 is legal and Team B will gain possession and next put the ball in play at the B-45.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. QB A12 takes the snap and drops back to pass. A12 scrambles outside the tackle box, and he fumbles the ball at the A-30, which bounces directly back up into his hands. A12 then passes the ball forward from the A-33, and the ball lands at the A-45 in an open area where there are no eligible receivers with an opportunity to make a catch.
choice_b: A, 2/20, A-25, Snap. Illegal forward pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-35, Snap. The pass by A12 is legal since A12 did not relinquish possession to another player and the pass was from outside the tackle box and landed beyond the neutral zone (Rule 7-3-2-h Exception).

question_text: Try @ B-3. Kicker A90's kick is partially blocked by B55 who leaps straight up from his position at the B-2. B19 is the first player beyond the neutral zone to touch Team A's partially blocked kick try when B19 muffs the in-flight kick 2-yards deep the end zone. The kick then bounces in the end zone and A66 recovers the ball while it is still in the end zone.
choice_a: Try is no good. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: Touchdown for A66, 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: Touchdown for A66, 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for A66, 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The touching of the kick by B55 is considered to have occurred behind the neutral zone and is ignored after the ball crosses the neutral zone (Rule 6-3-1-b). B19 muffs the kick before the kick touches the ground in the end zone, so the ball remains live (Rules 6-3-3 & 6-3-9). The recovery by A66 is legal and results in a 2-point touchdown (Rule 8-1-1).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-8. B21 intercepts QB A12's legal forward pass deep in his end zone and advances but does not get out of the end zone, where he is downed 5-yards deep in the end zone. During B21's run, B55 holds A80 2-yards deep in the end zone.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The result of the play is a touchback since the pass by Team A was the initial impetus for the ball being in the end zone (Rule 8-7-1). The foul by B55 was in the end zone, and the penalty leaves the ball in Team B's possession in the end zone and results in a Safety (Rule 8-5-1-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. Early in the 3rd quarter, QBA1 is hit as he throws a pass to receiver A80 near the sideline at the B-18. The Field Judge rules the pass incomplete signaling that A80 was out of bounds. There is a flag down on the play and the Referee announces roughing the passer on B78. Replays show B78 lead with his head and attack with the crown of the helmet to A1's chest with force and A80 gain firm control of the ball with his left foot down in bounds before falling to the ground where he maintains control.
choice_c: Incomplete pass. A, 2/10, B-30, Ready - DQ B78.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-9, Ready - DQ B78. Replay must look at all reviewable aspects of the play which in this situation include potential targeting and catch/no catch at the sideline. B76 attacks with the crown of the helmet so it is targeting, and replay should add the foul and B78 is disqualified. A80 did complete the catch so replay must give the Referee the spot of the catch and the penalty is enforced half the distance to the goal line.

question_text: 4/5 at B-35. Early in the 2nd quarter, A1 completes a pass to back A2 who runs to the B-31 where he is hit and loses the football. B56 recovers the loose ball and is downed at the B-30. The officials rule fumble and signal Team B's ball. A2's right knee may have been down before the ball came loose.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap. Although this is a turnover replay should not stop the game as the difference will be one yard of field position for Team B. Since it was 4th down and A2 did not make the line to gain before he lost the football there is no significant competitive effect. Had this not been 4th down or A2 had made the line to gain before he lost the football then replay must stop the game to review whether A2 was down.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-8. B21 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-3, and his momentum carries him into the end zone. Before the ball is declared dead, B52 holds in the end zone. B21 does not get out of the end zone, and the ball is declared dead.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The momentum exception applies to B21's interception (Rule 8-5-1-a Exception a) and the basic spot for B52's holding penalty is the end of the run where B21 gained possession at the B-3. The foul is behind the basic spot, so the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul and this results in a safety by penalty (Rule 8-5-1-b).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-7. QB A12 drops back and then scrambles from the pocket. A12 throws to A88, and the ball is caught at the A-30. A88 runs to the A-33 where he is downed. A12 released the ball at the A-8 with his right heel still in the neutral zone.
choice_a: A, 3/13, A-4, Ready. Illegal forward pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-33, Running. For A12's pass to be an illegal forward pass, his entire body must be beyond the neutral zone when the pass is released, so the pass by A12 is legal (Rule 7-3-2-a). Since the pass is legal, Team A will have 1/10.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-7. QB A12 drops back and then scrambles from the pocket. A12 throws to A88, and the ball is caught at the A-30. A88 runs to the A-33 where he is downed. A12 released the ball at the A-8 with his entire body beyond the neutral zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/13, A-4, Ready. A12's pass is an illegal forward pass because his entire body is beyond the neutral zone when the pass is released (Rule 7-3-2-a). The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul which is the A-8, and the penalty also includes loss of down.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-8. QB A12's legal forward pass is intercepted by B21 in the end zone, and he runs it back to midfield where he is downed. During the interception return, B52 clips in Team B's end zone.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The foul by B52 occurs behind the basic spot on the play which is the end of B21's run, and the penalty will be enforced from the spot of B52's foul. Since the spot of the foul is in the end zone, the accepted penalty will leave the ball behind the offending team's goal line, resulting in a safety (Rule 8-5-1-b).

question_text: Try @ B-3. QB A12's legal forward pass is intercepted by B21 in the end zone and he runs it back to midfield where he is downed. During the interception return, B52 clips in Team B's end zone.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
choice_b: Try over, no score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Try over, no score. Retry for Team A, B-3, No Clock.
choice_d: Score 1 point for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Try over, no score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The penalty for B52's foul is declined by rule and the try is over (Rule 8-3-4-a). Team A will kickoff from the A-35.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. A21 takes the handoff and runs for a Touchdown. B55 holds at the B-35 during A21's touchdown run. A21 spikes the ball in the back of the end zone after the score.
choice_a: TD for Team A w/ Try, B-3, No Clock, with Team B's holding penalty enforced.
choice_b: No score. A, 1/10, B-35, Snap. Holding on B55.
choice_c: TD for Team A w/ Try, B-18, No Clock -or- A, Try, B-3, No Clock and A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules
choice_d: TD for Team A w/ Try, B-18, No Clock, as the only option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team A w/ Try, B-18, No Clock -or- A, Try, B-3, No Clock and A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules Allow the score for Team A because the penalty for Team B's live ball holding foul is declined by rule (Rule 10-2-5-a-2). Team B has the option penalizing the dead ball unsportsmanlike conduct 15-yards on the try or on the succeeding kickoff (Rules 10-2-5-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-5. A21 takes the handoff and is hit and fumbles at the A-6. The ball strikes the ground and B55 muffs the ball in an attempt to recover at the A-7. The ball rolls back and crosses Team A's goal line, where A12 falls on the ball in the end zone.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. The fumble by A21 provides the impetus for the ball rolling back into the end zone even though it was muffed by B55 (Rule 8-7-2-a). Since Team A is responsible for the ball being in their own end zone and the ball is declared dead there – it is a safety (Rule 8-5-1-a).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A1's kick lands at the B-15 and bounces toward the sideline near the goal line. Returner B21 runs to the B-1 and straddles the sideline with his left foot out of bounds and his right foot in bounds. The ball rolls toward him and he has to lean toward the field of play to field it. As he leans his left foot comes up off the ground (right foot stays on the ground) and he then recovers the ball before immediately touching out of bounds again with his left foot. The officials rule kickoff out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-1, Snap. The touching of the kick is reviewable. B21 touches the ball in the field of play and then goes out of bounds so the ball is placed at the OB spot. Once his left foot comes off the ground he has re-established back in bounds. At least one second should run off the game clock.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10. Early in the 2nd quarter, A1's punt lands at the 50 and returner B13 has to retreat in an attempt to field it. B13 reaches for the ball at the B-45 but quickly pulls his hands away. The ball rolls to the B-40 where A28 (who had been in bounds throughout the down) falls on it. The officials rule B13 did not touch the ball. There is a flag for an illegal formation on Team A. Replays show B13 clearly touched the ball at the B-45. Team B elects to enforce the penalty from the dead ball spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, A-5, Snap. The touching of the kick is reviewable. The ruling on the field with the penalty will result in Team B's ball at the B-45. Since B13 touched and Team A recovered the only option for the penalty is the previous spot. If Team B initially elected to enforce the penalty from the previous spot there would no significant competitive effect and replay does not need to get involved.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. During the kick by A90, B55 holds A22 at the B-35, just before the kick is caught by B21 who is straddling the sideline with the left foot out of bounds and the right foot down inbounds at the B-4.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-40, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. Since B21 has one foot out of bounds, he is considered a player out of bounds (Rule 4-2-1-a). B21 catches the kick and is an out of bounds player, so the kick is considered out of bounds (Rule 4-2-3-a), and thus a foul for a free kick out of bounds (Rule 6-2-1). The foul by B55 occurs before the change of possession so offsetting fouls is the result of the play, no options (Rule 10-1-4). Team A must re-kick at the previous spot.

question_text: 4/8 @ B-35. A21 runs and gains four yards, and the ball is declared dead, after which B55 is called for a dead ball personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-16, Snap. A21's run is short of the line to gain, so Team A's series is over and a new series is awarded to Team B (Rule 5-1-1-c). The dead ball personal foul penalty will be administered from the succeeding spot which is the B-31 (Rule 10-1-5). The clock starts on the snap (Rule 3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-3. A90 punts from his end zone and the ball is returned by B21 to the A-16 where it is fumbled. A23 grabs and twists B55's facemask in Team A's end zone during the fumble. The ball is recovered in the field of play by B44 at the A-12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/Goal, A-8, Snap. The facemask foul by A23 is during the fumble and the basic spot is the A-16 which is the spot of the fumble and is considered the end of the related run (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-c). The penalty will be enforced half the distance to the goal and results in 1/Goal for Team B.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-3. A90 punts from his end zone and the ball is returned by B21 to the A-16 where it is fumbled. A23 grabs and twists B55's facemask in Team A's end zone during the fumble. The ball is recovered in the field of play by A44 at the A-12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/Goal, A-8, Snap. The facemask foul by A23 is during the fumble and the basic spot is the A-16 which is the spot of the fumble and is considered the end of the related run (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-c). Even though Team A recovered the ball, the recovery is after the foul by A23 and Team A will not be able to keep the ball (Rule 5-2-4). The penalty will be enforced half the distance to the goal and results in 1/Goal for Team B.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A90's free kick is muffed in flight by B21 in his end zone. While the ball is loose in the end zone, B21 bats the ball out of the end zone to keep Team A players from recovering.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-45, FK Rules.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-40, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-45, FK Rules. The result of the play is a touchback. B21 commits a foul for illegally batting a ball in the end zone (Rule 9-4-1-c) during a free kick play (Rule 2-30-2). If the penalty is accepted, it will be enforced 10-yards from the previous spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A90's kick is high and deep, and it lands just beyond Team B's end line. Just after the kick, B55 charges across his restraining line, goes directly to A90 and violently contacts the kicker in an attempt to punish the kicker. All action occurs before the kick lands.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-45, FK Rules. Eject B55.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-50, FK Rules. Eject B55.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap, as the only option. Eject B55.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-50, FK Rules. Eject B55, as the only option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-50, FK Rules. Eject B55. The result of the play is a touchback. B55 commits a flagrant personal foul during a free kick play (Rule 2-30-2). If the penalty is accepted, it will be enforced 15-yards from the previous spot (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Back A21 is stationary and is lined up in the tackle box. Just after the snap A21 immediately releases outside the tackle box. QB A12 is in the pocket to pass and A21 sees linebacker B54 blitzing. A21 returns into the tackle box and before A12 throws the pass, blocks B54 below the waist in the tackle box and the block is directed from the front. A12's pass is complete to A88 at the A-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, A-15, Ready. Foul by A21 for an illegal block below the waist. Once a player leaves the tackle box during the play, that player is considered outside the tackle box for the remainder of the down (Rule 9-1-6-a-2 & AR 9-1-6 X). A21 may not block below the waist once considered outside the tackle box.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Early in the 2nd quarter, left tackle A77 is lined up inside the tackle box and defender B60 is lined up near the line of scrimmage and inside of A77 and is head up on left guard. The play is a toss sweep to the right and as B60 on his initial line charge starts his pursuit, A77 releases to the right and blocks B60 below the waist from the side. The ball carrier gains 15 yards on the play.
choice_a: A, 3/25, A-10, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A77.
choice_c: A, 3/10, A-25, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A77.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Running. Legal block by A77. This action is considered part of the initial line charge and A77 can block below the waist and is not restricted to only blocking below the waist from the front. There is no step count for initial line charge, just a judgment if the action becomes a second act (Rule 9-1-6-a-1 & AR 9-1-6 XII). After the initial line charge, and if it is clearly a second act, then A77's block below the waist in the tackle box must be directed from the front.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QBA1 throws a forward pass to receiver A80 at the B-28. The pass falls incomplete and there is a flag down for offensive holding on A78. Replays show B6 lower his head and attack with it making forcible contact to the side of A80's head after the ball hit the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Snap, DQ B6. This is targeting by B6 for leading with his head and attacking with force to the head/neck area of a defenseless player. The foul occurred after the ball hit the ground, so it is considered a dead ball foul. The penalty for the live ball hold is enforced first and then the dead ball penalty for targeting resulting in a 1st down for Team A.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-12. With 13 minutes left in the 3rd quarter, QBA1 is hit as he tries to throw a pass to receiver A80 in the end zone and the ball comes loose. It is recovered by B78 at the B-18, and he is downed there. The Referee rules fumble, but there is a flag down for defensive holding. Replays show A1's hand came forward with control before the ball came loose.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-6, Ready. There is no need to stop the game to review the pass/fumble as Team A will accept the penalty either way and the result is the same (previous spot enforcement).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-3. A12 throws a legal forward pass to A88, and B21 is called for defensive pass interference against A88 eight yards deep in the end zone. The catchable ball falls incomplete.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-2, Snap. When the ball is snapped on or inside the Team B 17-yard line and outside the Team B two-yard line, and the spot of the foul is on or inside the two-yard line, the penalty from the previous spot shall place the ball at the two-yard line, first down (Rule 7-3-8-c PENALTY and A.R. 7-3-8-XIV).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A12 throws a legal forward pass to A88, and B21 is called for defensive pass interference against A88 eight yards deep in the end zone. The catchable ball falls incomplete.
choice_d: A, Try, B-1 ½, No clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try, B-1 ½, No clock. Rule 7-3-8-c PENALTY states that no penalty for defensive pass interference enforced from outside the two-yard line may place the ball inside the two-yard line with the exception that on the Try when the snap is on or inside the three-yard line, Rule 10-2-5-b. This rule states that penalties for defensive pass interference fouls on a try from on or inside the three-yard line are enforced one-half the distance to the goal line. If the try is successful, the penalty is declined by rule.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-40. After the previous play and with the clock running, Team A rushes to the line and Snapper A55 snaps the ball before the ball is made ready for play. QB A12 muffs the snap and B50 recovers the ball at the B-44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/6, B-45, Ready. The snap before the ball is ready for play by A55 is a dead-ball foul, and Team A is penalized for delay of game. Since the ball does not become alive, all action should be stopped immediately by the game officials and there is no legal recovery by B50 (Rule 4-1-4).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. After the previous play which resulted in a 1st down inbounds for Team A, Team A rushes to the line and Snapper A55 snaps the ball before the ball is made ready for play. QB A12 muffs the snap and B50 recovers the ball at the B-44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. The snap before the ball is ready for play by A55 is a dead-ball foul, and Team A is penalized for delay of game. Since the ball does not become alive, all action should be stopped immediately by the game officials and there is no legal recovery by B50 (Rule 4-1-4). This is a foul between series in that it takes place before the ball is declared ready for the next series. Hence it is first and 10, not first and 15. (Rule 5-2-7-a and AR 5-2-7 V).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. QB A12 scrambles from the pocket and runs out of bounds at the B-22. After A12 is out of bounds, linebacker B55 chasing A12 delivers a strong hit to A12 in the sideline area.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-11, Snap. The 4th down run by A12 is short of the line to gain when he runs out of bounds. The late hit by B55 is after Team A's series is over as the continuity of downs is broken (Rule 5-1-4-f). The dead ball foul by B55 is clearly before the ready for play, so the penalty for B55's late hit is enforced half the distance to the goal from the B-22 and it will be 1/10 for Team B (Rule 5-2-7-a).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A90's kick goes out of bounds at the B-20 after it has been touched B88 at the B-22. A21 was flagged by the BJ for being offside.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules, as the only option.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-15, Snap.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-25, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. There is no foul for a free kick out of bounds because B88 touched the ball inbounds (Rule 6-2-1). Team B has two options to enforce the offside penalty. They may elect to have the kick repeated after a 5-yard penalty enforced from the Team A 35-yard line or snap the ball at the inbounds spot, five yards from the B-20 which was where the ball went out of bounds (Rule 6-1-8).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. With the game clock running, Team B has 12 players in the formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field. The ball is ready for play, both teams are in formation and the snap is imminent. Quarterback A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense and calls timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. When the deep officials count 12 Team B players, both teams are in formation, no Team B player is attempting to leave the field and the snap is imminent, the crew will offer Team A their time out back and penalize Team B for a substitution foul (AR 3-5-3 VII).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. With the game clock running, Team B has 12 players in the formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field. The ball is ready for play, both teams are in formation and the snap is imminent. Quarterback A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense and the play clock expires.
choice_a: A, 3/10, B-40, Ready. Delay of game on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, Ready. When the deep officials count 12 Team B players, both teams are in formation, no Team B player is attempting to leave the field and the snap is imminent, there is no foul for Team A delay of game. Penalize Team B for a substitution foul (AR 3-5-3 VII).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. QBA1's pass to receiver A80 near the sideline is ruled complete and A80 is tackled at the A-45. There are no flags on the play and the ball is spotted at the A-45 with the clock running. Replays show A80 go out of bounds after some minor contact with B21 then re-establish back in bounds before catching the pass. The Side Judge threw her hat during the down.
choice_a: A, 2/10, A-25, Snap. Illegal touching on A80.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Running. If there is any type of contact between a defender and a receiver in this situation, then replay cannot create a foul for illegal touching. To put the receiver out of bounds on his own there cannot be any contact whatsoever. Once contact is established the only other reviewable aspect is whether the receiver re-established back in bounds before touching the ball.

question_text: 3/G @ B-4. 0:11 remaining in the 2nd quarter, the score is tied, and Team A is out of timeouts. QBA1 throws a quick pass to A80 at the B-2 who catches the ball with his left knee near the ground (0:09). A80 then dives forward into the EZ (0:07). The officials rule TD. Replays show A80's knee was down when he gained control of the ball.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: 10-second runoff, half over. A80 is down by rule after gaining control with his knee on the ground. The overturn results in a running clock so there is a 10-second runoff applied from the time A80 was down (0:09). Team A cannot avoid the runoff since they are out of timeouts, so the half is over.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-10. A90's punt travels five yards beyond the neutral zone where B33 touches the ball. The ball then rebounds behind the neutral zone, where A33 recovers it in the field of play at the A-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-6, Snap. The ball is legally recovered by A33 since the kick was touched by B33 beyond the neutral zone. The ball is dead when recovered by A33 and may not be advanced. It will be first down for Team A (Rules 6-3-3 & 6-3-6-a). The clock will start on the snap following a legal kick down (Rule 3-3-2-d-8).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-10. A90's punt travels five yards beyond the neutral zone where B33 touches the ball. The ball then rebounds behind the neutral zone, where A33 recovers it 3-yards deep in his end zone. After recovering, A33 runs back into the field of play to the A-3 where he goes down.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. The ball is legally recovered by A33 since the kick was touched by B33 beyond the neutral zone. The ball is dead when recovered by A33 in his own end zone and may not be advanced. The action results in a Safety (Rules 6-3-3, 6-3-6-a and 8-5-1-a).

question_text: 3/2 @ B-30. Before the snap, Team B has 11 players on the field and legal substitute B21 comes running onto the field from the bench. B21 stops and requests a timeout from the LJ before being inside the top of the numbers. Team A snaps the ball and A44 runs for a 1-yard gain.
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-30, Ready. Timeout granted to Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready. The timeout request by B21 is not granted as a legal substitute may request a timeout only after he is between the nine-yard marks (Rule 3-3-4-b). Team B is guilty of illegal substitution with 12 players on the field, a live ball foul penalized from the previous spot (Rule 3-5-3-b).

question_text: 3/2 @ B-30. Before the snap, Team B has 11 players on the field and legal substitute B21 comes running onto the field from the bench. B21 requests a timeout just after he crosses the top of the numbers. After the time out request, Team A snaps the ball and A44 runs for a 1-yard gain.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready. Illegal substitution on Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-30, Snap. The timeout request by B21 is granted as he is between the nine-yard marks (Rule 3-3-4-b). After granting the time out, officials should work to shut any dead ball action down as soon as possible.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-9. Late in the game, ball carrier A22 heads for the right-hand pylon at the goal line. At the B-2, A22 goes airborne and the ball is in A22's right hand and crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. While still airborne, A22 touches the pylon with his left hand and the ball is beyond the goal line but out of bounds. The clock reads 0:55 when the ball is declared dead.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The goal line plane is extended since A22 touches the pylon with his left hand and since the ball is beyond the goal line extended at that moment, it is ruled a Touchdown (Rule 8-2-1-a).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-9. Late in the game, ball carrier A22 heads for the right-hand pylon at the goal line. At the B-2 he goes airborne and the ball is in A22's right hand and crosses the sideline at the B-1 and passes outside the pylon. A22 first touches the ground out of bounds with the ball three yards beyond the goal line. The clock reads 0:55 when the ball is declared dead.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-1, Snap. The goal line plane is not extended because A22 did not touch either the pylon or the ground in the end zone (Rule 8-2-1-a). The ball is ruled out of bounds at the B-1 where it crossed the sideline (Rule 4-2-4-d Exception).

question_text: Try @ B-3. On a one-point attempt, A90's kick is blocked, and the ball remains behind the neutral zone. The ball is rolling at the B-5 and B50 attempts to recover the kick but muffs the ball and it rolls forward to the B-1 where A21 picks up the ball and runs into the end zone.
choice_a: Try successful, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: Touchdown, 2 points for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: The Unsuccessful Try is ended. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: The Unsuccessful Try is ended. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The Unsuccessful Try is ended. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. The ball is declared dead at the B-1 when A21 recovers the ball (Rule 6-3-6-a). There is no score, and the try has ended.

question_text: Try @ B-3. On a one-point attempt, A90's kick is blocked, and the ball remains behind the neutral zone. The ball is rolling at the B-5 and B50 attempts to recover the kick but muffs the ball and it rolls forward to the B-4 where A21 picks up the ball and runs into the end zone.
choice_a: The Unsuccessful Try is ended. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: Try successful, score 1 point for Team A. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: No score. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: Try successful, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Try successful, score 2 points for Team A. A, Free kick, A-35, FK Rules. The recovery of the kick that has never crossed the neutral zone is legal by A21 and A21 may legally advance (Rule 6-3-1-a). A21 scores a 2-point Touchdown on the try.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-40. Early in the 3rd quarter, back A2 runs up the middle and breaks through the line with a clear path to the goal line. As he crosses the goal line, he drops the ball, and it comes to rest in the EZ with no player from either team going after it in the continuing action. The officials rule TD. Replays show A2 dropped the ball at the B-1.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-1, Ready. The ball in relation to the goal line is reviewable and since it came loose short of the line there is no score. There is no clear recovery, so the ball is brought back to the spot of the fumble and Team A retains possession.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. Receiver A80 catches a pass and is tackled near the line to gain. The officials spot the ball at the B-36. Replays show the ball is clearly at the B-35 ½ when A80 was down. The Team A head coach challenges the ruling of short of the line to gain.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-35½, Charge Team A TO, Snap.
choice_b: A, 4/ ½, B-35 ½, Charge Team A TO, Snap.
choice_c: A, 4/½, B-35½, Charge Team A TO, Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/ ½, B-35 ½, Charge Team A TO, Snap. Team A can challenge that the line to gain was reached. If it is clear the spot is incorrect, replay can move the ball closer to the line to gain, but the down must change for the challenge to be successful. Team A now has no challenge remaining.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A12's legal forward pass is intercepted by B21 at the goal line. B21 returns to the B-15 where he fumbles and A88 scoops the ball up at the B-14 and runs into the end zone.
choice_a: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 25-Yard Line, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Running Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The recovery is legal and the touchdown by A88 counts (Rule 3-1-3-e). Team A's possession series ends, and Team B will have 1/10 at the 25-yard line after the try.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A12's legal forward pass is intercepted by B21 at the goal line. B21 returns to the B-15 where he fumbles and A88 recovers at the B-14 where he is down.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 40-Yard Line, No Clock.
choice_d: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 25-Yard Line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 25-Yard Line, No Clock. Even though the recovery by A88 would typically give Team A a new series, in extra periods Team A will not have a 1st down if it regains possession after a change of team possession (Rule 3-1-3-e). Team A's possession series ends, and Team B will have 1/10 at the 25-yard line.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, A12 throws a legal forward pass and Team A is flagged for an illegal shift. B25 intercepts the pass at the goal line, and B38 blocks an opponent in the back at the 50 during B25's return. B25 carries the ball all the way back across Team A's goal line.
choice_a: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try, A-3, No Clock.
choice_c: The down is repeated. A, 1/10, B-25, No Clock.
choice_d: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 40-yard line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A's possession series ends. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. The fouls cancel and the down is not repeated. Team A's possession series is ended, and Team B begins its possession series at the 25-yard line (Rule 3-1-3-g-3).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-34. A44's run gains 3 yards and he is tackled inbounds. B55 is offside on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-29, Ready. B55's foul is a live ball foul and Team A will accept the penalty. The penalty yardage will result in a 1st down and the clock starts on the referee's signal (Rule 3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A99's kick goes out of bounds at the B-20 after it has been touched by B80 at the B-22. A18 was offside on the play.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules, as the only option.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-25, Snap.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-15, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team B has 2 options to enforce the penalty on the play. They may elect to have the kick repeated after a 5-yard penalty or snap the ball at the inbounds spot five yards from where the ball went out of bounds (Rule 6-1-8).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. Ball carrier A33 runs to the A-40 and is in the grasp of two defenders. A33's forward progress has clearly been stopped and he is being driven back. Linebacker B55 crouches and thrusts himself forward, driving his forearm into the side of A33's helmet at the A-37.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. The action by B55 is targeting a defenseless opponent (Rule 9-1-4). A33 is a defenseless player since he is in the grasp of the opponents and his forward progress has been stopped (Rule 2-27-14-h). B55 is disqualified.

question_text: 3/4 @ A-36. A81 catches a pass at the 50 and runs toward Team B's goal line. A81 drops the ball straight down at the B-1 after assuming he has already crossed his opponent's goal for a touchdown. The officials make no signal and the loose ball bounces into the end zone where it comes to rest with no player trying to possess it.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-1, Ready. When a fumble comes to rest inbounds and no player attempts to secure the ball in advance of the spot of the fumble, the ball belongs to the fumbling team at the spot of the fumble. Team A's ball at the 1-yard line (Rule 7-2-5-b-1 & AR 8-2-1 X).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. QB A12 takes the snap and drops back to pass. He scrambles outside the tackle box and fumbles the ball at the A-28, which bounces back up into his hands. He then passes the ball forward from the A-30, and it lands beyond the neutral zone in an open area where there are no eligible receivers with an opportunity to make a catch.
choice_a: A, 3/20, A-30, Snap. Intentional grounding on A12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, A-35, Snap. A12 is outside the tackle box and throws the ball that lands beyond the neutral zone, so there is no foul for intentional grounding. Since A12 did not relinquish possession to another player, this action is legal (Rule 7-3-2-h Exception).

question_text: 4/1 @ B-31. QBA1 attempts a sneak with teammates pushing him from behind into the line. He is stacked up near the line to gain and driven backward. The officials rule A1's forward progress was stopped and place the ball at the B-30. Replays show when A1's progress was stopped the ball was at the B-30 ½.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30 ½, Snap. When the on-field officials rule the runner's forward progress is stopped replay can review the position of the ball in relation to the line to gain (or goal line) when the progress is stopped.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A1's low punt hits the ground at the B-40 and is fielded by B12 at the B-35 who immediately goes to the ground. The officials spot the ball at the B-35. Replays show the ball hit the right shoulder of A5 at the 50-yard line before it hit the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 50, Snap. Touching of a kick is reviewable and the illegal touch by Team A gives Team B the option to take the ball at that spot.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-40. Midway through the 4th quarter, Team A lines up with A11 ten yards directly behind the snapper. The other three backs are split wide, all outside the tackle box. Only four Team A linemen have uniform numbers between 50 and 79. A11 receives a backward pass from the snapper with Team A in this formation. A11 throws a legal forward pass to A21, and the pass is complete for a 5-yard gain.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, B-45, Ready. Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation, because in this alignment it is not obvious that a kick will be attempted. Since there are not five players numbered 50-79 on the line of scrimmage, this is an illegal formation, and it is a live ball foul (Rule 2-16-10 & AR 7-1-4 VIII).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. Team A is in a scrimmage kick formation, and the snap goes over punter A90's head. The ball is rolling free and as A90 retreats, he kicks the loose ball back through the end zone. The kick by A90 occurred just after the ball broke the plane of Team A's goal line.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-1, Snap.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. Since the ball goes back through the end zone, the result of the play is a safety (Rule 8-5-1-a). The action by A90 is a foul for illegally kicking the ball (Rule 9-4-4). The basic spot is the previous spot (the A-30) and the foul occurred behind the basic spot. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul which is in the end zone, so the penalty results in a safety as well (Rule 10-2-2-c-1). Team B has no option here as both the play and penalty enforcement result in a safety.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. Team A is in a scrimmage kick formation, and the snap goes over punter A90's head. The ball is rolling free and as A90 retreats, he kicks the loose ball back through the end zone. The kick by A90 occurred at the A-2.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules, as the only option.
choice_b: B, 1/Goal, A-1, Snap, as the only option.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-1, Snap.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-15, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/Goal, A-1, Snap. Since the ball goes back through the end zone, the result of the play is a safety (Rule 8-5-1-a). The action by A90 is a foul for illegally kicking the ball (Rule 9-4-4). The basic spot is the previous spot (the A-30) and the kick occurred behind the basic spot at the A-2 (Rule 10-2-2-c-1). The penalty, if accepted, is enforced half the distance to the goal from the spot of the foul which is the A-2. The penalty carries loss of down as well, so if Team B elects to enforce the penalty, it will be their ball since it was 4th down for Team A.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. A12's legal forward pass is intended for A88 at the 50. While airborne and inbounds, eligible receiver A88 touches the pass when he propels it toward eligible receiver A80, who catches the pass at the A-45 and is down at the 50.
choice_a: A, 3/10, A-35, Snap. Illegal bat on A88.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Running. A88 has batted a legal forward pass, and this batting is legal (Rule 9-4-1-a). The ball remains live and the catch by A80 is legal as well (Rule 2-4-3).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. Midway through the 4th quarter, QB A12's legal forward pass is intended for airborne receiver A80, and A80 gains firm control of the pass at the pass at the A-48. While still in the air, A80 is hit by B10 and driven backward and lands on his knees at the A-44, where the ball is declared dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-48, Running. The catch by A80 is completed when he returns to the ground and maintains complete and continuous control of the ball (Rule 2-4-3-b). When an airborne pass receiver of either team completes a catch inbounds after an opponent has driven the receiver backward and the ball is declared dead at the spot of the catch, the forward progress is where the player gained and maintained firm control of the ball, so the ball is spotted where A80 gains firm control of the pass at the A-48 (Rule 5-1-3-a Exception 2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. Wide receiver A88 is blocked out of bounds by B21 at the A-40 and then A88 runs 20 yards downfield before returning inbounds to the field of play at the B-40. A88 catches a legal pass at the B-35 and runs to the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-35, Snap. A88 commits a foul for illegal touching due to his failure to return inbounds immediately (Rule 7-3-4 Exception). The penalty is loss of down at the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap as the result of the play was a Touchdown (Rule 3-3-2-c).

question_text: 3/25 @ 50. QB A12 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the pass defensive tackle B77 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct. The pass to A88 is complete for an 8-yard gain to the B-42 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Ready. The penalty for B77's unsportsmanlike conduct foul is enforced at the end of the run which is the B-42 (Rule 9-2-1 Penalty). Even though the penalty yardage leaves the ball short of the line to gain, unsportsmanlike conduct fouls by Team B carries an automatic first down as a part of the penalty.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. B21 intercepts a pass at the B-3 and his momentum carries him into the end zone. Before the ball is declared dead, B50 holds an opponent in the end zone. B21 does not get out of the end zone, and the ball is declared dead in the end zone.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The basic spot is the end of the run which is the B-3 where B21 gained possession (Rule 8-5-1-a Exception). The foul by B50 occurs behind the basic spot in the end zone and that is the enforcement spot (Rule 10-2-2-c-1), so accepting the penalty results in a safety by penalty (Rule 8-5-1-b).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A1 throws a quick screen pass from the A-4 to receiver A80 that deflects off his hands and hits the ground. B23 immediately muffs the ball knocking it into the goal line pylon. The Line Judge signals incomplete forward pass. Replays show A80 touching the ball at the A-5 and then it hits the ground at the A-3.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, A-6, Snap. The direction of the pass is reviewable and since it was first touched in advance of where it was released, it is forward and incomplete once it hits the ground. Had the pass been backward replay could overturn to a safety since the ball hit the goal line pylon in the immediate continuing action.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QBA1's pass to receiver A80 is ruled complete near the sideline at the B-20. A80 may have been bobbling the ball as he went out of bounds and Team A rushes to the line to snap the ball. Team A gets set and legally snaps the ball with back A2 taking a handoff and getting tackled at the B-18. The Replay Official recognized a potential issue and attempted to stop the game but the notification to the on-field crew came well into the next play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/8, B-18, Running. The ruling of catch at the sideline is reviewable, but the notification (O2O or page) must come prior to the next legal snap. If the notification occurs clearly after the next play has begun the on-field officials should ignore it and continue to officiate the play. If the notification comes simultaneous with the snap the on-field officials should immediately kill the play.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the half, quarterback A12, who has not been outside the tackle box, is under heavy duress and throws a desperation pass at the B-35. The pass falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball at the B-31 in an attempt to catch it. There was no eligible receiver in the area and the clock stopped at 0:31 after the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. A12's pass is a foul for intentional grounding and the penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass (Rule 7-3-2-h). There is no foul for illegal touching since the pass is illegal (Rule 7-3-11). A 10-second runoff applies since there is less than one minute remaining on the game clock (Rule 3-4-4-a-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Midway through the 2nd quarter, A12 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the pass defensive tackle B78 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct. The pass to A88 is complete for a 10-yard gain to the A-35 where A88 is tackled inbounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Ready. The penalty for B78's Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul is enforced 15-yards from the end of A88's run, which is the A-35 (Rule 9-2-1-a-1 Penalty).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Midway through the 2nd quarter, A12 throws a forward pass intended for A88. During the pass defensive tackle B78 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct. The pass to A88 is intercepted by B21 at the 50 and B21 returns to the A-15 where he is out of bounds. A54 contacts B21 well after B21 is out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-25, Ready. There is a live ball foul by B78 and a dead ball foul by A54 and both will be penalized separately and in the order of occurrence (Rule 10-1-6). The penalty for B78's foul is enforced at the previous spot, 15-yards and will include a first and 10 for Team A (Rule 9-2-1-a-1 Penalty). Then the dead ball foul by A54 will be administered 15-yards, and it will remain 1/10 (Rule 5-2-7-a). The clock will start on the referee's signal (Rule 3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: 4/20 @ A-30. Team A's punt is blocked behind the neutral zone and while the kick is loose, B21 holds at the A-40. The ball never crosses the neutral zone during the play and is recovered by B50 at the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Snap. Team A retains possession after the 10-yard penalty for holding is enforced at the previous spot. The penalty also includes an automatic 1st down (Rule 9-3-4 Penalty). Postscrimmage Kick enforcement (Rule 10-2-3) applies only when a scrimmage kick crosses the neutral zone. The clock starts on the snap after a legal kick down (Rule 3-3-2-d-8).

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. After the previous down was over, Team A sends in three substitutes, and three players begin to leave the field. A88, who participated in the previous play, trails the three replaced players toward the Team A sideline. The three replaced players continue into the team area, but A88 stops and sets up on the line of scrimmage very close to the sideline. After the ball is snapped A88 runs down the sideline and catches a forward pass at the B-20 and runs into the end zone.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25, A-35, Snap. A88 commits a foul at the snap for unsportsmanlike conduct for unfair tactics by using the substitution process to deceive the opponents (Rule 9-2-2-b & AR 9-2-2 VI). This is a live-ball foul, and the penalty is enforced 15-yards from the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a Touchdown (Rule 3-3-2-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. After the previous down was over, Team A does not substitute. A88, who was in motion toward his sideline on the previous play is still inside the numbers when the ball was declared dead. A88 continues toward his sideline and sets up well off the ball just inside the boundary and no defender sees or moves to cover A88. After the ball is snapped from the far hash away from Team A, A88 runs down the sideline and catches a forward pass at the B-20 and runs into the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25, A-35, Snap. A88 commits a foul at the snap for unsportsmanlike conduct for unfair tactics by using the substitution process to deceive the opponents (Rule 9-2-2-b). Even though there was no substitution by Team A, this tactic is still using a simulated replacement to confuse opponents. This is a live-ball foul, and the penalty is enforced 15-yards from the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a Touchdown (Rule 3-3-2-c).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-30. Early in the 2nd quarter, Back A21 is stationary and is lined up in the tackle box. Just after the snap A21 immediately releases outside the tackle box. QB A12 is in the pocket to pass and A21 sees linebacker B54 blitzing. A21 returns into the tackle box and before A12 throws the pass, blocks B54 below the waist in the tackle box at the A-22 and the block is directed from the front. The pass is completed to A88 for a 15-yard gain.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/25, A-15, Ready. The action by A21 is a foul for an illegal block below the waist. Once a player leaves the tackle box during the play, that player is considered outside the tackle box for the remainder of the down. A21 may not block below the waist once considered outside the tackle box (AR 9-1-6 X).

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Early in the 2nd quarter, Back A21 is stationary and is lined up in the tackle box. Just after the snap, A21 immediately steps up and blocks blitzing linebacker B55 1-yard beyond the neutral zone. The block is below the waist and directed from the front. A12's pass is complete to A88 for a 15-yard gain.
choice_a: A, 3/25, A-10, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A21.
choice_c: A, 3/10, A-25, Ready. Illegal block below the waist on A21.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Running. The block by A21 is legal. A block initiated 1-yard beyond the neutral zone is considered within the tackle box for both a lineman and back assuming that block meets all the other requirements for a legal block below the waist (AR 9-1-6 XIII).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, QBA1 scrambles out of the pocket and runs toward the sideline before throwing a forward pass that is caught by A80 at the B-25. A80 steps out of bounds after making the catch, but the Referee rules A1 stepped out of bounds at the B-45 before releasing the pass. Replays show A1 did not step out of bounds prior to the pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15, B-45, Ready. The ruling of out of bounds is not reviewable in this situation. If the on-field officials rule a player out of bounds before a pass is thrown, replay cannot rule on whether the player was out and make it a pass. 12-3-7 may be used if it is an obvious mistake that will have a significant impact on the outcome of the game.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. Receiver A80 controls a forward pass as he goes to the ground near the end line. B34 is covering A80, and the ball comes loose after they both hit the ground. The Back Judge rules the pass incomplete. Replays show A80 controlling the ball with his left knee in bounds and maintaining control as his body hits the ground. Clearly after he hits the ground B34 pulls the ball loose.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The ruling of incomplete is reviewable and since the receiver maintained control of the ball when he hit the ground the time requirement has been met. If the ball comes loose prior to or simultaneously with the receiver hitting the ground the pass is incomplete in this situation.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-12. Ball carrier A22 is tackled hard by B44 at the B-5, resulting in first and goal. After the ball is dead, A22 slams B44's head into the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A22 is guilty of a flagrant personal foul and in addition to the 15-yard penalty will be ejected (Rules 2-10-3 & 9-1-1). Team A will have 1/10 after the administration of the penalty (Rule 5-2-7-a).
