question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. A12 takes the direct snap, circles right end and is hit and fumbles the ball on Team A's 38-yard line. The ball rolls out of bounds on Team A's 40-yard line. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, Team A71 commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-23, Ready. The fumble is forward and out of bounds, so the ball belongs to Team A at the fumbling spot (A-38) which is beyond the line to gain, and this results in a 1st down for Team A (Rule 7-2-4-b-1). The 15-yard penalty against Team A will be administered from the A-38 and will still result in a 1/10 (Rule 5-2-7-a). The clock will start on the ready for play (Rule 3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 4/2 @ A-35. A12 takes the direct snap, circles right end and is hit and fumbles the ball on Team A's 38-yard line. The ball rolls out of bounds on Team A's 30-yard line. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, Team A commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-15, Snap. The fumble is backward and out of bounds, so the ball belongs to Team A at the out-of-bounds spot (A-30) which is short the line to gain, and this results in a 1st down for Team B (Rule 7-2-4-b-2). The 15-yard penalty against Team A will be administered from the A-30. The clock will start on the snap (Rule 3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game with the score tied, Kicker A10 places the ball down at the right hash for an apparent onside kick. After the Referee's ready-for-play signal, A10 approaches the ball and then picks up the ball runs to the left hash at the A-35, places the ball down and quickly kicks the ball.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. No foul.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Once the ball position between the hash marks has been selected by Team A and after the ready-for-play, the ball may be relocated only after a charged team timeout or before any re-kick (AR 6-1-2 VIII). This is a dead-ball foul and the five-yard penalty is enforced from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A90 punts, and the untouched kick is bouncing at Team B's three-yard line when A80 blocks B21 into the ball, forcing it into the end zone where A84 recovers the grounded ball.
choice_d: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Since A80 blocked B21 into the ball, B21 is deemed not to have touched it (Rules 2-11-4-c & 6-3-4-a). Since the touching is ignored, the ball is dead when it touches the ground in Team B's end zone (Rule 6-3-9) and the result of the play is a touchback (Rule 8-7-2).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. During A90's punt, B21 gives a valid fair catch signal at the B-10 and does not touch the punt. While the untouched ball is loose at the B-3, B21 blocks A80 at the B-6 and the ball then bounds into the endzone where it is declared dead
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-3, Snap. The action by B21 is a foul for an illegal block and postscrimmage kick enforcement will govern the enforcement (Rule 6-5-4). Since the spot of the foul (B-6) is behind the postscrimmage kick enforcement spot (B-20), the penalty will be enforced half the distance to the goal from the B-6 (Rule 10-2-3).

question_text: 3/2 @ B-45. With the clock running, Team A is in formation and the snap is imminent. Team A QB is in shotgun formation and is using a clap as a starting signal. Team B assistant coach claps causing the Team A wideout to false start just before the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. The action by the assistant coach is a dead-ball foul, delay of game for disconcerting signals [S21]. The rule states that no player may call defensive signals that simulate the sound or cadence of, or otherwise interfere with, offensive starting signals. The spirit of the rule would include squad members or coaches that clap when the snap is imminent and could cause the offense to false start (AR 7-1-5 V).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter the score is tied at 24. QBA1's pass to receiver A80 near the sideline at the B-29 is ruled complete and A88 runs to the B-20 where he is pushed out of bounds (1:57). Replays show after gaining control A80's left foot hit the sideline before his right foot came down in bounds (2:04).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, B-40, 1:57, Snap. The catch ruling is reviewable and since A80 did not get a body part down in bounds it is incomplete. The overturn creates a stopped clock, but since the clock was not inside 2:00 when the pass was incomplete there is no adjustment.

question_text: 4/4 @ A-36. A1's punt is blocked, and the ball hits the ground at the A-38 before bouncing to the A-34 where A77 picks it up and runs to the A-42 where is tackled. The officials huddle and the Referee announces A77 recovered the ball behind the line of scrimmage and advanced it beyond the line to gain, 1st down for Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-34, Snap. Whether the kick crossed the line of scrimmage is reviewable and since it did, the ball is dead when A77 recovers it. The ball will go over to Team B at that spot, 1 st and 10.

question_text: A 2/10 @ A-30. After the Two Minute Timeout, A1 hands the ball to A22, A22 gains 5 yards where he is ruled out of bounds at the A-35 with the game clock at :32, the clock continues to run and is stopped at :25. Replay shows the runner was inbounds with the game clock at :32.
choice_a: Not reviewable. No clock adjustment by Replay.
choice_b: Reviewable. Reset the game clock to :32.
choice_c: Reviewable. Reset the clock to :32 and charge Team A a timeout.
choice_d: Not reviewable, but Replay puts :32 back on the clock.
explanation: Not Reviewable, No clock adjustment by Replay. However, O2O to the SJ that there was :32 on the game clock.

question_text: A 2/25 @ A-30. It is late in the 3rd quarter, A1 throws a pass to A88 for a Touchdown and the game clock reads 0:00. Video shows that the clock was at 0:04 when the Touchdown occurred. Reviewable??
choice_a: Not reviewable. The touchdown stands with 0:00 on the clock.
choice_b: Reviewable. A, Try @ B-3, no clock. Reset the clock to :04.
choice_c: Reviewable, but no time can be restored - the quarter is over.
choice_d: Reviewable. A, Try @ B-3, reset the clock to :00.
explanation: Yes Reviewable, Rule 12-3-6-d-1. A Try B-3, no clock. Reset Clock to :04 (Recommended for RC to communicate to Crew via O2O to put :04 on the clock this is also consistent with the Shaw/Blandino Quiz Version 5)

question_text: A 3/G @ B-9. QB A2 takes the snap and is flushed outside the tackle box. Just before A2 reaches the sideline he throws the ball in the end zone where B23 catches the ball close to the sideline, while B23 is Airborne he throws the ball to B44 who runs it out of bounds at the B-1. The SJ rules that B23 was out of bounds before going airborne.
choice_b: A 4/G @ B-9, but B44's advance to the B-1 counts.
explanation: A 4/G @ B-9. This is an incomplete pass as B23 never re-established back in bounds.

question_text: A 4/5 @ A-37. A23 punts the ball to the B-13 where B6 receives the punt. B6 returns the ball to the A-15 where he fumbles the ball. A59 recovers the fumble at the A-14. During the return A43 commits a blind side block at the B-30.
choice_a: B 1/10 @ A-14 (enforced from the recovery spot).
choice_b: A 1/10 @ A-14 (Team A keeps the ball).
choice_c: B 1/G @ A-7.5, enforced from the spot of the foul at the B-30.
explanation: B 1/G A-7.5. This is enforced half the distance from the end of the related run since the foul occurred during the run. The end of the related run is the A-15 the spot of the fumble. Rule 10-2-2-d-1-c.

question_text: A 1/G @ B-4. QB A16 takes the snap and rolls to his right, just as he gets to the sideline, he throws the ball to A17 who catches the ball at the B-1 then his left foot steps on the goal line, A17 then extends the ball outside the pylon past the goal line.
choice_b: A 1/G @ B-1 (down at the 1, no score).
explanation: Touchdown. When A17 steps on the goal line he gets goal line extended, as the entire goal line is in the end zone. Rule 2-12-2 and Rule 8-2-1-a.

question_text: A F/K @ A-35. B17 leaps from inbounds and is the first player to touch Team A's free kick when he grasps the ball while airborne. He then lands out of bounds with the ball in his control.
choice_a: Free kick out of bounds - Team B's three options apply.
choice_b: Not a foul. Team B's ball at the spot where B17 crossed the sideline.
choice_c: Touchback. Team B's ball at the B-25.
explanation: Not a foul for free kick out of bounds. B17 is inbounds when he touches the kick. Team B will have the ball at the yard line where B17 crossed the sideline. AR 6-2-2-IV

question_text: A 4/15 from A-35. A's scrimmage kick is fielded by B22 at the B-10. B22 slips but remains on his feet and advances to the B-38. Team B is flagged for a block in the back at the B-20. After review Replay indicates that B22's knee touched down when he slipped at the B-10.
choice_a: B 1/10 @ B-38 (block in the back declined).
choice_b: B 1/10 @ B-28 (block in the back enforced from the B-20).
choice_d: B 1/10 @ B-10, block in the back enforced 10 yards.
explanation: B's ball 1/10 at the B-10. After Replay indicates the ball carrier is down by rule, any foul committed is not enforced unless it is a personal foul or unsportsmanlike.

question_text: A 4/10 @ A-15. Punter A49's kick is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A89 recovers the bouncing ball at the A-4 and his momentum carries him into the end zone where he is tackled.
choice_d: Safety, 2 points Team B. B, F/K @ B-20.
explanation: Safety A F/K @ A-20. The momentum rule applies only to the catch or recovery of an opponent's pass, fumble, or kick (8-5-1-a-Exception a). So, the ball became dead in A89's possession behind his own goal line, and Team A was responsible for the ball being there, thus resulting in a Safety (8-5-1-a).

question_text: A 4/7 A-45: B-32 is in position to catch Team A's scrimmage kick at the B-20 when A44 makes contact with him prior to the ball arriving causing B32 to muff the kick. The ball rolls to the B-10 where it is picked up by B33 and returned across team A's goal line for a touchdown.
choice_a: Touchdown Team B. Try @ A-3 (KCI penalty declined by rule).
choice_b: No score. Team A's KCI penalty is enforced and Team A re-kicks.
choice_c: Touchdown Team B, with A44's KCI enforced on the Try.
choice_d: A 1/10 @ B-20 (KCI enforced from the spot of the foul).
explanation: Try A-3. The KCI penalty is declined by rule since only 15 yard penalties for personal fouls and for UNS are enforced on the Try or succeeding kickoff. Rule 10-2-5-a. Rule 6-4-1-e

question_text: A 1/10 @ 50. A23 catches a pass at the B-45 and runs downfield. As he gets to the B-5, B27 hits the ball out of his hand. The ball bounces towards the pylon when B10 dives to try to recover it. The SJ rules that B10 was touching the sideline when he recovered the ball. During the run, B58 taunted A59 well behind the play. Replay has indisputable evidence that B10 recovered the ball while inbounds.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ B-20 (UNS enforced from the previous spot).
choice_c: A 1/G @ B-5 (enforced from the spot of the foul).
choice_d: B 1/10 @ B-2.5 (Team B recovers, fouls offset).
explanation: A 1/G @ B-2.5. Because the UNS occurs during the run play (vs the pass play), it will be enforced from the end of the related run. Rule 10-2-2-d-1-b. B58 also has one UNS towards DQ.

question_text: A 4/5 @ B-40. With :09 left on the clock in the 2nd Quarter. Team A has no timeouts remaining. A8 takes a handoff and runs to the B-32 where he is tackled. The clock runs out to :00. After a quick review Replay shows :02 were on the clock when the runner was ruled down.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ B-32, reset clock to :02, Snap.
choice_b: A 1/10 @ B-32, reset clock to :02, Ready.
choice_d: A 1/10 @ B-32, :02, charge Team A a timeout.
explanation: Half is over. Replay can review the play, but they cannot put :02 back on the clock because the clock will start on the ready. Since Team A does not have a timeout, we go to Halftime. Rule 12-3-6-d-2

question_text: A FK A-35. A91's kick, while still airborne and never touching the ground, flies low over the shoulder of the H standing at the goal line pylon and then strikes the ground just behind him OOB. The H rules a touchback. As the teams are setting up for 1/10 B-25, the Team B head coach calls timeout, which is granted by the S, and he then tells the S he wants to initiate a replay challenge, claiming the kick went out of bounds over the field of play, and thus a KOB foul. Replay has IVE that the airborne ball crossed the sideline at the B-1.
choice_a: Reviewable - free kick out of bounds, Team B's options apply.
choice_c: Not reviewable, but Team B keeps its timeout.
choice_d: Not reviewable. Team B loses the timeout.
explanation: Not reviewable (Replay Casebook, No. 15). Team B loses that TO (12-5-1-b-5).

question_text: A 1/G B-8. B40 intercepts the pass in the end zone. About to be tackled and still in the end zone, B40 throws a backward pass to teammate B22. B22, seeing A45 rapidly approaching, bats the airborne backward pass out of the back of the EZ.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points Team A. A, F/K @ A-20.

question_text: A 4/8 @ B-40. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked by B99 at the B-41. B4 jumps at the B-38 and grabs the ball midair. Just as B4 is returning to the ground, the ball is hit by the helmet of a teammate and becomes loose. A42 scoops up the loose ball at the B-39 and runs to the B-32.
explanation: A 1/10 @ B-39, Snap. B4's action is not that of blocking a scrimmage kick (6-3-1-b), so when he touches the ball beyond the neutral zone, all players become eligible to catch or recover (6-3-3). Since B4 did not complete the catch (2-4-3), this is a muff, and A42's recovery at the B-39 caused the ball to become dead (6-3-6-a) and a new series is awarded to Team A (5-1-1-e-2).

question_text: A 2/8 @ B-48. The score is tied with 1:36 remaining in the 4th quarter. QB A12 throws a completed pass to A84 at the B-39. The FJ has a hat down and a flag for A84 stepping out of bounds on his own then re-establishing inbounds and catching the pass. The CJ has a flag on B69 for Illegal Hands to the Face at the line of scrimmage. Replay shows that A84 jumped from out of bounds and never reestablished inbounds prior to touching the pass.
explanation: A 1/10 @ B-33, Snap. When A84 touched the pass before returning inbounds, it was an incomplete pass and not a foul for illegal touching (7-3-4 , 7-3-7-a), and the clock starts by rule on the Snap (3-3-2-d-4). The only foul that remains is B69's Illegal Use of Hands, and since it occurred during a legal forward pass play, the basic spot is the previous spot (10-2-2-d-3). Since the foul is by the team not in possession, enforcement of the penalty is from the basic spot, B-48 > B-33 (10-2-2-c-3).

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-25. With :10 remaining in the 4th Quarter, Team A does not have any timeouts and is trailing in the score. A4 is in shotgun formation and takes the snap at the A-20. A4 is scrambling in the tackle box and flushed out to the A-26 where he throws the ball downfield to A12 who catches the ball and goes to the ground with :03 seconds left on the clock, however the clock continues to run to :02 when the clock is stopped.
choice_a: A 2/14 @ A-21, reset clock to :03, Ready.
choice_b: A 2/14 @ A-21, reset clock to :03, with a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A 2/14 @ A-21, reset clock to :02, Ready.
choice_d: A 2/20 @ A-15, reset clock to :03, Ready.
explanation: A 2/14 @ A-21, Reset Clock to :03, Ready. There is no 10 second runoff since this was a completed illegal forward pass. Rule 7-3-2-a, Rule 3-4-4-a.

question_text: A 4/19 @ A-35. Punter A13 receives the snap and punts the ball to the B-27, where B12 receives the punt and runs to the B-42 and is tackled by A42. Shortly after the snap A17 grabs the facemask of B36 at the LOS.
choice_a: B 1/10 @ A-43, as the only option.
choice_b: B 1/10 @ A-43 (end of run) - or - A 4/34 @ A-20, repeat the down, at Team B's option.
choice_c: A 4/34 @ A-20, repeat the down, as the only option.
choice_d: B 1/10 @ B-27 (enforced from the previous spot).
explanation: B 1/10 @ A-43 enforce from the end of the run where Team B will next put the ball in play OR A 4/34 @ A-20 enforced at the previous spot and repeat the down at the option of Team B. Rule 6-3-13

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-42. A16 is in shotgun formation receives the snap and hands the ball to A3 where he runs to the A-45 and is tackled by B19 at that spot. During the play A79 grabs the facemask of B45 at the A-42.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ A-30 (enforced from the previous spot).
choice_d: A 1/25 @ A-20 (enforced from the end of the run).
explanation: A 1/25 @ A-27, enforcement is at the spot of the foul since the team in possession commits a foul behind the basic spot. The Basic Spot is the end of the related run. Rule 10-2-2-c.

question_text: A 4/8 @ B-42. Punter A34 is set to receive the snap, the play clock runs to zero before the snap occurs. With the game clock running it is stopped due to the play clock running out. The game clock is stopped at 7:17. Ruling, and Game Clock status?
choice_a: A 4/13 @ B-47, game clock starts on the ready.
choice_c: A 4/8 @ B-42, replay the down, clock on the snap.
choice_d: A 4/13 @ B-47, game clock correct at 7:17, starts on the snap.
explanation: A 4/13 @ B-47, game clock is correct at 7:17 and starts on the snap. Rule 3-3-2-d-11.

question_text: A 2/3 @ B-18. QB A9 is in shotgun formation and receives the snap. A9 throws the ball to the end zone where A3 drops the ball. The U has a flag down for two Team B Players with green dots on the field.
explanation: A 1/10 @ B-13, Snap. If more than one green dot helmet per team is detected on the field by the game officials, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty. Rule 1-4-11-b Exception. Since the Team Not in Possession committed the foul either behind or beyond the basic spot the penalty is enforced from the basic spot. Rule 10-2-2-c-3, The basic spot on pass plays is the previous spot. Rule 10-2-2-d-3.

question_text: A 2/6 @ A-7. QB A9 is in shotgun formation, receives the snap and passes the ball to A85, the ball falls incomplete. After the play in the end zone A61 hits B49 who is laying on the ground late, the hit was in the head or neck area.
choice_c: A 3/3 @ A-8 (enforced from the end of the run).
choice_d: A 3/9.5 @ A-3.5, enforced from the spot of the foul in the end zone.
explanation: A 3/9.5 @ A-3.5. This is targeting a defenseless player, B49 is on the ground then gets hit in the head or neck area. A player on the ground is considered defenseless. Rule 9-1-4. The enforcement spot is the previous spot since this is a dead ball foul. Rule 10-2-2-a. A61 is DQ'd after Replay Confirmation.

question_text: A 3/14 @ B-44. Receiver A88 catches a pass and runs near the line to gain where he is tackled in bounds. The officials spot the ball @ B-31. The offense rushes up the line, but before they can snap the ball, the Replay Official stops the game to review the spot. Replays show when A88 is down the ball is at the 30 1/2 - yard line.
choice_a: Reviewable. Overturn to a first down for Team A.
choice_b: Not reviewable - the ball stays at the B-31.
choice_c: Reviewable. The no-first-down ruling stands, but Replay moves the ball to the corrected spot.
choice_d: Reviewable, but the spot cannot be changed, only the down.
explanation: Reviewable play, regarding whether the ball made the line to gain. The ruling of no 1st down stands, but replay should move the ball to correct the spot closer to the line of gain. (Caseplay #20)

question_text: A 1/10 @ B-20. With time running out in the second quarter, A10 receives the snap and immediately attempts to spike the ball. Before his hand starts forward, the ball pops out and lands at the B-23 where B77 recovers. The referee rules incomplete pass.
choice_a: Not reviewable - the incomplete pass stands.
choice_b: Reviewable. Overturn to a fumble. A retains the ball at the B-23.
choice_d: Reviewable. Overturn to a fumble. B 1/10 @ B-23.
explanation: Reviewable play. Overturn to fumble, Team B 1-10 on B-23 (Rule 12-3-3-b). (Caseplay #69)

question_text: A 3/8 @ B-40. QB A8 passes to A88 at the B-30. While the ball is in the air, B21 makes early contact on A88, grabbing and pulling his facemask in the process. The pass is deflected and falls incomplete.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ B-25, snap, :25. Rule references: 7-3-9-e (DPI w/ a Personal Foul), 9-1-8-b (Face Mask), 3-3-2-d-4 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/10 @ B-18. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked behind the line of scrimmage by Team B, but continues downfield to the B-10, where B28 picks up the ball and advances to the B-15. Team A was flagged for an Illegal Formation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-15, snap, :25. Rule references: 6-3-5 (Catch or Recovery by Receiving Team), 6-3-13 and 10-2-4 (Fouls by Kicking Team), 3-3-2-d-3 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-6 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/10 @ A-5. Punter A2's kick from the end zone is blocked by B44. The blocked kick flutters to the A-7 where B-21 attempts to catch it, but muffs the ball, which then bounces into A's end zone. A2 recovers the ball in the end zone and advances to the A-2 where he is tackled.
choice_c: A FK @ A-20, On Legal Touch, :25
choice_d: Safety, 2 pts Team B. B FK @ B-20, :25
explanation: The correct ruling is: A FK @ A-20, On Legal Touch, :25. Rule references: 8-5-1-a (Safety), 8-5-2 (Kick After Safety), 8-7-1 and 8-7-2 (Impetus), 6-3-6-a (Recovery by Kicking Team), 3-3-2-a (Timing), 3-2-4-c-7 and 3-2-4-c-8 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/2 @ B-37. A11 takes a shotgun snap, is under heavy pressure at the B-45, and attempts to dump the ball to A28. A28 muffs the balls into the air, A50 catches it at the B-40 and advances to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-42, snap, :25. Rule references: 7-3-11 (Illegal Touching), 3-3-2-d-3 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-6 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/2 @ A-30. QB A12 takes the snap and runs to the A-35 where he fumbles the ball out of bounds at the A-40. Immediately after the ball goes out of bounds, A55 commits a personal foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ A-20, ready, :25. Rule references: 7-2-4-b-1 (Forward Fumble Out of Bounds), 9-1 (Personal Foul), 3-3-2-e-2 and 3-3-2-e-4 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/10 at A-20. Rugby style kicker A1 rolls outside the tackle box and attempts to kick the ball, but loses control at the A-9 as he is being tackled by B99. Teammate A2 picks up the loose ball at the A-10, and punts the ball. The poorly punted ball rolls out of bounds at the A-30. At the snap, Team A had 5 players in the backfield.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/Goal @ A-9, snap, :25. Rule references: 7-2-2-a-Exception2 (4th down fumble), 7-1-4-a-4 (No more than 4 Backs), 3-3-2-d-3 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-6 and 3-2-4-c-7 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/9 @ B-40. Punter A2 kicks the ball. B80 catches the punt in the end zone and returns it to the B-8 where he is tackled. During the kick, B8 blocked gunner A28 blocked below the waist at the B-36 yard line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-8, snap, :25. Rule references: 9-1-6 (Blocking Below the Waist), 10-2-3 (Postscrimmage Kick Enforcement), 2-25-11-b (Postscrimmage Kick Spot), 3-3-2-d-3 and 3-3-2-d-8 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-6 and 3-2-4-c-7 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 3/10 @ B-20. A8's pass is intercepted by B28 at the B-4. B28 takes two steps is hit and fumbles the ball. The bouncing ball is recovered by teammate B30 at the B-3 and his momentum takes him into his end zone, where he is tackled by two A players.
choice_a: Safety, 2 pts Team A. A FK @ A-20, snap, :25
choice_d: A FK @ A-20, On Legal Touch, :25
explanation: The correct ruling is: A FK @ A-20, On Legal Touch, :25. Rule references: 8-5-1-a-Exception-a (Safety), 8-5-2 (Kick After Safety), 8-7-1 and 8-7-2 (Impetus), 3-3-2-a (Timing), 3-2-4-c-8 (25 second clock)

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. During the kick B21 signals for a fair catch at the B-32 and does not touch the ball during the play. B21 proceeds to block A81 at the B-30. B42 catches the ball at the B-24 and immediately takes a knee.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-20, snap, :25. Rule references: 6-5-4 (Illegal Block or Contact - Fair Catch), 3-3-2-d-3 and 3-3-2-d-8 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 and 3-2-4-c-6 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 3/7 @ B-40. A11 takes a direct snap from center A50, and rolls to his right. He throws a pass to tight end A85 who is at the B-30. A85 catches the pass and advances down the sideline. B24 is trailing him and swipes the ball causing A85 to fumbles at the B-8 yard line. The ball rolls into the end zone where B-22 recovers the ball. A11 was roughed by B77 just after he threw the pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ B-25, snap, :25. Rule references: 9-1-9 (Roughing the Passer), 3-3-2-d-1 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/10 at B-45. A0's punt takes a high bounce at the B-10 and is rolling toward the end zone. After the ball breaks the goal line plane, but before the ball touches the ground in the end zone, A24 bats the ball back into the field of play. B4 picks up the ball at the B-6 and shimmers his way for a crowd pleasing touchdown. During the Kick, B62 was holding at the B-34.
choice_a: Touchdown Team B. B Try @ B-3, :25
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-10, snap, :25. Rule references: 6-3-11 (Loose Behind Goal Line), 2-25-11-b-Special cases-2 (Postscrimmage Kick Spot), 9-3-3-b (Holding), 3-3-2-d-3 and 3-3-2-d-8 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-6 and 3-2-4-c-7 (25 second clock)

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A22 is flagged for being offside before the ball was kicked. B20 catches the ball at the B-10 and returns it to the B-40 where he is tackled. During B20's return B44 blocked airborne A22 below the waist at the B-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ B-45, snap, :25. Rule references: 2-3-2-a (Definition- Block Below Waist), 6-1-2-c-1 (Free Kick Formation), 3-3-2-d-3 and 3-3-2-d-8 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 and 3-2-4-c-6 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 3/5 @ B-25. A28 takes a handoff and runs to the B-16 where he is tackled by B44. Clearly after A28 was down, B48 came in late and also hit A28. A28 was angered by this, got up off the ground, and shoved B48 with two hands to the face mask.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ B-16, ready, :25. Rule references: 10-1-5-Exception (Dead-Ball Fouls), 3-3-2-e-1 and 3-3-2-e-4 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/10 @ A-26. Punter A1 is under heavy pressure and runs with the ball to the A-28 where he punts the ball. The punt is blocked and rolls out of bounds at the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B 1/10 @ A-21, snap, :25. Rule references: 6-3-10-c (Illegal Kick), 3-3-2-d-10 and 3-3-2-d-3 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 and 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/9 at A-35. A7's pass is intercepted by B21 at the 50. He returns the ball to the A-42 where he fumbles the ball. A24 recovers the ball at the A-40, then advances to the A-45 and tackled there.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ A-45, ready, :40. Rule references: 7-2-2-a (Fumble), 3-3-2-e-1 (timing), 3-2-4-b-1 (40 second clock)

question_text: A 2/10 at A-40. A11 takes a 7 step drop and sets up in the pocket. B99 is bringing big pressure from his left, but A11 waits until that last second to pull the ball down and scramble. While still in the tackle box, B99 grabs the name plate area of A11's jersey and pulls him immediately and directly to the ground, causing his knees to buckle, at the A-32.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 3/18 @ A-32, running, :40. Rule references: 9-1-15 (Horse Collar Tackle), 2-34 (Tackle Box), 3-2-4-b-1 (40 second play clock)

question_text: A 2/5 @ B-20. A27 takes a handoff over right guard. At the snap, Linebacker B44 moved forward from the B-17 and takes out lead blocker A33 inside the tackle box with a block below the waist at the B-20. This block frees up B55 to make the tackle at the B-18.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/Goal @ B-9, snap, :25. Rule references: 9-1-6-b-1 and 9-1-6-b-2 (Blocking Below the Waist), 2-34 (Tackle Box), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 2/14 @ B-40. QB A7 drops back looking to his right for A80. A80 is grabbed by B21 and thrown to the ground at the B-42. A7 then scrambles to his left, sees A83 open and throws a pass. A83 catches the ball at the B-32 and runs out of bounds there.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ B-30, ready, :25. Rule references: 9-3-4 (Defensive Holding), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-15. A7 takes the snap and hands off to A32 at the A-10. A32 runs right, outside the tackle box, and sets up in a passing posture at the A-12. A32 is looking for a receiver when he is hit forcibly below the knee by B65. A32 stumbles but manages to regain his balance and advance the ball to the A-35 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/10 @ 50, ready, :25. Rule references: 9-1-9-b (Roughing the Passer), AR 9-1-9-III (Potential Passer qualifies for this foul under 9-1-9-b), 10-2-2-d-1-b (Enforcement spot - end of related run), 3-3-2-e-4 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 3/8 @ B-20. QB A8 passes to A88 in the end zone. While the ball is in the air, B21 plays through the back of A88. A88 is unable to make a play on the ball and the ball falls incomplete in the end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A 1/Goal @ B-5, snap, :25. Rule references: 7-3-8-c (DPI), 3-3-2-d-4 (Timing), 3-2-4-c-1 (25 second clock)

question_text: A 4/12 @ A-30. Punter A44 receives the ball from the snapper, runs to the A-32, and punts. Receiver B22 makes a fair catch at the B-35
choice_a: Team B 1/10 @ A-25 (illegal kick - loss of down).
explanation: Team B 1/10 @ A-25. Since the players entire body is beyond the neutral zone this is an illegal kick. The enforcement is 5 yds from the previous spot and loss of down. Rule 6-3-10-c

question_text: A 1/10 @ B-45. A9 takes the snap in shot gun formation and throws a quick pass towards A2 but B22 catches the ball at the B-30. B22 is running side by side with B11 to the A-33, At the A-33, A74 Blocks Below the Waist on B11 to try getting to B22 to make the tackle. B22 continues to run where he is tackled at the A-29 by A3.
choice_a: B 1/10 @ A-24 (enforced from the spot of the foul).
explanation: B 1/10 @ A-14.5. Blocking Below the Waist is illegal after a change of team possession except against a ball carrier. This block was against a player next to the ball carrier and players are not allowed to block below the waist through other players to get to the ball carrier. Rule 9-1-6-d; This is considered a run play. The penalty is enforced at the basic spot since the team not in possession committed the foul either behind or beyond the basic spot. On run plays the basic spot is the end of the related run when there is not a neutral zone. Rule 10-2-2-c-3; Rule 10-2-2-d-1-c

question_text: A 2/13 @ A-34. A5 takes the snap in shot gun formation from the near hash, not able to find an open receiver A5 is flushed out of the tackle box to his left toward the far hash as A9 arrives at the far hash he throws a pass to A80 that falls incomplete. During the pass B27 makes contact with A80 at the B-43.
choice_b: A 1/10 @ A-43, Snap, Near Hash (spot of foul).
explanation: A 1/10 @ A-49, Snap, Near Hash. Since the foul happened beyond 15 yards the foul is enforced 15 yds from the previous spot. Rule 7-3-8-c; The previous spot also includes the spot on the field in which the ball was snapped from. Since the spot of the snap was on the near hash the foul will be enforced from the A-34 near hash.

question_text: A 3/2 @ A-34. After the Two Minute Time Out in the 2nd Quarter with the game clock running B95 jumps into the NZ causing A77 to react. B95 was lined up in the gap with A69 to his left and A77 to his right. Clock is stopped with 1:31 remaining.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ A-39, Snap, no runoff option.
choice_d: A 1/10 @ A-39, with an option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: A 1/10 @ A-39, Option for a 10 Second Runoff if Team A elects the runoff then the clock starts on the Ready, if there is no 10 second runoff the clock starts on the snap (Rule 10-4-4-a) (Rule 10-4-4-b) (Rule 10-4-4-c). This is a defensive offside foul as the Team B player enters the NZ and causes an offensive lineman to react immediately (Rule 7-1-5-a-2).

question_text: A 3/3 @ A-36 right hash. QB16 takes the snap in shotgun formation, he quickly throws a pass to A5 where he was contacted by B24 before the ball arrives at the A-38 left hash. The ball falls incomplete.
choice_c: A 1/10 @ A-36 right hash, Snap (previous spot).
explanation: A 1/10 @ A-38 left hash, Snap. Since the foul happened less than 15 yards the penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, this includes the hash or position on the field (7-3-8-c) (Rule 7-3-8 Penalty Statement).

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-25. After the Two Minute Time Out in the 2nd Quarter with the clock running both teams have timeouts, A76 moves before the snap, clock stops with :43 remaining on the clock. What are the options and elements for a 10 second runoff?
choice_a: A 1/15 @ A-20, Snap, no runoff option.
choice_b: A 1/15 @ A-20, with an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A 1/15 @ A-20, mandatory 10-second runoff, clock on ready.
choice_d: A 1/15 @ A-20, clock adjusted to :33 regardless.
explanation: A 1/15 @ A-20. Since the foul immediately stopped the clock there is an option for a 10 second runoff at option of the offended Team. If the offended team elects to take the 10 second runoff the clock would be adjusted to :33 seconds and the clock will be started on the Ready. If Team B elects to decline the 10 second runoff the clock starts on the snap. Team A can avoid the 10 second runoff by using a Timeout, if Team A elects to take a timeout then the clock will start on the snap after the timeout (Rule 3-4-4-(a-d)).

question_text: A 2/10 @ B-38. With 10 seconds remaining in the second quarter, QBA1 throws a pass to receiver A86 at the B-27 near the sideline. The officials rule complete and time expires on the play. Replays show that after completing the catch A86 stepped out of bounds with 2 seconds remaining on the game clock.
choice_a: Not reviewable - the catch and 0:00 stand.
choice_b: Reviewable. A 1/10 @ B-27, reset to :02, Ready.
choice_c: Reviewable. A 1/10 @ B-27, reset the clock to :02, Snap.
choice_d: Reviewable, but no time can be restored - half over.
explanation: Reviewable play, regarding whether the pass was complete and whether there should be time remaining on the game clock at the end of the play. A 1-10 on B-27, reset game clock to 0:02 and start on the snap (Rule 12-3-6-c) (Case Play #137).

question_text: A 1/G @ B-5. Ball carrier A22 runs to the one-yard line and dives for the goal line. The officials rule A22 out of bounds at the B-1. Replays show that the ball crossed the plane of the goal line outside the pylon and then A22 landed in the end zone.
choice_a: Not reviewable - out of bounds at the B-1 stands.
explanation: Reviewable play. Overturn to touchdown. Reset clock if after the Two-Minute Timeout. If any part of the player lands in the end zone before he touches out of bounds, he gets the goal line extended (Rule 12-3-1-a) (Rule 8-2-1-a) (Case Play #116).

question_text: A 3/4 @ B-14. QBA1 throws a pass which B4 intercepts at the B2. B4's first step is at the B2, and his momentum carries him into B's End Zone. B4 attempts to run the ball out of the end zone but fumbles the ball, which goes out the back of the end zone. During B4's run, B8 blocked A8 low at the B6.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-3, Snap (enforced from the spot of the foul).
explanation: B, 1/10, B-1, Snap. The penalty for the BBW is enforced half the distance from the momentum spot. Clock starts on Snap.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A4's pass is intercepted by B16 in B's end zone. B16 runs around in the end zone, then fumbles the ball into the field of play with B27 recovering while grounded at the B-2. While B16 is running around in the end zone, B39 clips A84 at the B-10.
choice_b: Safety, B, F/K @ B-20, F/K Rules.
explanation: Safety, B, F/K, B-20, F/K Rules. The basic spot is the GL (10-2-2-d-2-c), and since the spot of the foul (B-10) by the team in possession is beyond the basic spot, enforcement is from the basic spot (10-2-2-c-2). This leaves the ball behind the goal line for a Safety by penalty (8-5-1-b)

question_text: 3/5 @ A-30. Immediately after the snap, B97 is legally blocked and loses his helmet at the line of scrimmage. Quarterback A6 reverses field, and B97 continues in pursuit of A6. A6 completes a pass to A86 at the A-40 where he is tackled.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ A-45, Ready (no foul - helmet replaced).
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. Personal foul on B97.
explanation: A, 1/10, B-45, Ready. Personal Foul on B97. A player whose helmet comes completely off during a down may not continue to participate beyond the immediate action, putting the helmet back on does not resolve the foul (9-1-17). The Penalty is enforced at the end of the last run since this was a legal pass play (10-2-2-e).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-35. Prior to the snap the officials shut the play down ruling Team A had 12 players in formation. Replays show Team A had 11 players.
choice_b: Reviewable, but the number of players is not reviewable.
choice_c: Reviewable. A 2/10 @ B-40 after the penalty.
choice_d: Reviewable. Overturn to no foul (Team A had 11 players).
explanation: Reviewable play regarding the number of players in formation when a flag is thrown. Overturn to no foul. (Case Play #172)

question_text: Second and 20 on the B-40. With 12 seconds remaining in the fourth quarter, Team A is behind by four points and has one timeout remaining. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B28 and runs out of bounds at the B15 as time expires. Replays show that A80's left knee was down when he caught the pass, and there were 5 seconds remaining on the game clock at that point.
choice_a: Reviewable. A 3/8 @ B-28, reset clock to :05, 10-second runoff (Team A may use its timeout).
choice_b: Reviewable. A 3/8 @ B-28, reset to :05, Snap, no runoff.
choice_c: Not reviewable - the play and 0:00 stand.
choice_d: Reviewable. A 3/8 @ B-28, reset to :05, Ready, no runoff option.
explanation: Reviewable play, regarding whether A80 was down after he caught the pass. Overturn to A 3-8 on B-28, reset game clock to 0:05 a 10-second runoff is applied. Team A may elect to use its final timeout to avoid the runoff and the game clock will start on the snap (Rules 12-3-3-c and 12-3-6-b). If Team A is out of timeouts the game is over. When an overturned ruling results in a running clock after the Two-Minute Timeout and there is no other reason the clock would have stopped (foul, injury, or helmet off ), a 10-second runoff applies. (Case Play #144)

question_text: 4/2 @ B-23. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked at the line of scrimmage and the ball remains behind the neutral zone. B8 muffs the ball behind the neutral zone at the B-25 before the loose ball rolls out of bounds at the B-19.
explanation: B 1/10 @ B23. The kick is blocked behind the neutral zone (6-3-1-b) and went beyond the neutral zone and out of bounds but wasn't touched by B beyond the neutral zone. Therefore, the kick is returned to the previous spot for this missed FG (8-4-2-b-1).

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-25. Without being contacted, A88 steps on sideline at the A-35. A88 has re-established inbounds when he jumps for a pass at the A-45. A88, realizing he will be unable to come down inbounds, controls the ball while airborne and flips it to WR A82 who catches the ball at the A-48 where he is tackled.
explanation: A 2/20 @ A-15. This is both illegal batting (2-11-3) and illegal touching; A88 lost his eligibility and therefore may not bat a pass forward. Both penalties include LOD; B will choose the batting penalty as it also includes 10 yards.

question_text: A 4/10 @ B-25. A77 commits PF-HTF during an unsuccessful FG attempt that lands in the EZ. What are Team B's enforcement options?
choice_a: A 4/25 @ B-40, as the only option.
choice_b: B 1/10 @ B-25, as the only option.
choice_d: A 4/25 @ B-40 - or - B 1/10 @ B-25, at Team B's option.

question_text: A 3/G @ B-7. Team A is trailing by 4 points with 7 seconds remaining in the fourth quarter. QBA1 throws an incomplete pass into the end zone and time expires on the play. Replays show that when the ball hit the ground there was 1 second remaining on the game clock.
choice_a: Reviewable. A 4/G @ B-7, reset clock to :01, Snap.
choice_c: Reviewable. A 4/G @ B-7, reset to :01, Ready.
choice_d: Reviewable, but no time can be restored - game over.
explanation: Reviewable play, regarding whether the pass was incomplete and whether there should be time remaining on the game clock at the end of the play. A 4-G on B-7, reset game clock to 0:01 and start on the snap (Rule 12-3-6-c). (Case Play #140)

question_text: A 1/G @ B-5. Ball carrier A22 runs and dives for the goal line pylon. The officials rule A22 out of bounds at the B-1. Replays show A22's left leg touching the pylon and the ball across the goal line outside the pylon at that point.
choice_a: Not reviewable - out of bounds at the B-1 stands.
explanation: Reviewable play. Overturn to touchdown. Reset clock if after the Two-Minute Timeout. A player touching the pylon with any part of his body is treated the same as a player with a body part down in the end zone. They both get the goal line extended (Rule 12-3-1-a). (Case Play #117)

question_text: A 3/10 @ A-36. A8 takes the snap and hands the ball off to A21, A21 runs to the A-39 where he is tackled. The BJ has a flag down for two Team B players with green dots on the field and actively participated in the play.
choice_b: A 3/5 @ A-44 (enforced from the end of the run).
explanation: A 3/5 @ A-41. If more than one green dot helmet per team is detected on the field by the game officials, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty. (Rule 1-4-11-b Exception). Per clarification from Steve Shaw this will be a previous spot enforcement.

question_text: A 4/4 @ A-42. In the 4th Quarter with :53 on the game clock, Team A is in scrimmage kick formation with the clock running. Team A has no time outs remaining and Team B has three timeouts remaining. Team A commits a Delay of Game foul and the clock is stopped at :43.
choice_a: A 4/9 @ A-37, Snap, no runoff option.
choice_d: A 4/9 @ A-37, with Team B's option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: A 4/9 @ A-37. Since the clock stopped immediately for the Delay of Game foul after the Two Minute Timeout, Team B has the option for a 10 second runoff and the clock starts on the Ready (:33). If Team B declines the runoff the clock starts on the snap (:43). (Rule 3-4-4) (Rule 3-3-2-f)

question_text: A F/K @ A-35. A95 kicks the ball to the B-1 where B2 receives the kick and runs to the B-40 where he is tackled. During the kick B-37 takes down A27 at the B-35.
choice_a: A, F/K @ A-45 (enforced from the previous spot).
choice_b: A, F/K @ A-30 (enforced from the spot of the foul).
choice_c: B 1/10 @ B-40 (Team B keeps the ball).
explanation: A F/K @ A-45. The foul occurred during the kick, since the foul happened during the kick the basic spot on a kick is the previous spot. (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a) Since the team not in possession commits the foul either beyond or behind the basic spot the foul is enforced from the basic spot. (Rule 10-2-2-c-3)

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-33. A0 takes the snap in shotgun formation, and quickly throws a pass to A7 who is at the A-35, A7 runs to the A-42 where he is tackled. During the run A65 commits a foul for Hands to the Face at the A-34.
choice_a: A 1/10 @ A-42 (enforced from the end of the run).
choice_d: A 1/17 @ A-26 (enforced from the previous spot).
explanation: A 1/24 @ A-19, Ready. Since the foul happened during the run the basic spot is the end of the related run. (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-b) When the foul is committed behind the basic spot by the team in possession the penalty is enforced at the spot of the foul. (Rule 10-2-2-c-1)

question_text: A 3/9 @ B-11. The game clock is running with 2:01 in the 2nd Quarter. The play clock runs out with 1:52 remaining.
choice_a: A 3/9 @ B-11, Snap (delay of game on Team A).
choice_b: A 4/9 @ B-16, Snap (delay of game penalty).
choice_c: A 3/9 @ B-11, Snap, play clock 25 (Replay voids the play for the Two-Minute Timeout).
explanation: A 3/9 @ B-11, Snap, Play Clock 25. There is no foul on the play, the on-field crew should have stopped the clock for the Two Minute Timeout when the clock reads 2:00. Replay will void the play and go to the Two-Minute Timeout. This is treated the same as the end of a period. (2024 Shaw/Blandino Weekly Quiz - Week 4 Question #9)

question_text: A 4/10 @ A-40. A80 recovers the rolling punt at the B-9. The officials rule illegal touching by A80 and award the ball to Team B. The ball grazed off the fingertips of B45 at the B-13 prior to A80 recovering it.
choice_a: Not reviewable - Team B's ball at the B-9.
choice_c: Reviewable. A 4/10 @ A-40, replay the down.
choice_d: Reviewable. Overturn to no illegal touching. A 1/G @ B-9.
explanation: Reviewable play, regarding whether B45 touched the ball. Overturn to no illegal touching. The ball belongs to Team A 1-G on B-9 (Rule 12-3-4-a). (Case Play #95)

question_text: A 3/20 @ B-30. A12 drops back to pass is flushed out of the tackle box and runs downfield to the B-15 where A12 throws a backward pass to A80. A80 muffs the backward pass and the ball rolls out of bounds at the B-11.
choice_b: A 4/1 @ B-15 (backward pass returns to the previous spot).
explanation: A 4/1 @ B-11, Ready. Since the backward pass went out of bounds between the goal lines it belongs to the passing team at the out of bounds spot. (Rule 7-2-4-a). Game Clock is on the Ready since the ball went out of bounds before the Two Minute Timeout. (Rule 3-3-2-e-3)

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-34. QB A2 takes the snap from the shotgun formation and throws a backward pass to A31 and the ball falls to the ground at the A-28. B13 then kicks the loose ball at the A-25 then recovers it at the A-18 and runs for a Touchdown.
choice_d: A 1/10 @ A-38 (enforced from the spot of the foul).
explanation: A 1/10 @ A-44, Ready. This is a foul for illegally kicking since the ball was loose. (Rule 9-4-4) The basic spot is the previous spot since the end of the run ended behind the neutral zone. (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-a) Since the team not in possession committed the foul either behind or beyond the basic spot the enforcement spot is the basic spot. (Rule 10-2-2-c-3)

question_text: A 3/5 @ B-40. Score A17 and B20. Team A has one timeout remaining. The game clock reads 1:30 in the 4th Quarter with the game clock running. A1 runs around the left end and is tackled at the B-36. A1's helmet comes off when he hits the ground. The clock shows 1:15 at the end of the play.
choice_a: A 4/1 @ B-36, Snap, no runoff option.
choice_c: A 4/1 @ B-36, play clock 25 (A1 sits one play; Team B has a 10-second runoff option).
explanation: A 4/1 @ B-36. Play clock set to :25, A1 must sit out for one play unless Team A uses their timeout. Team B has the option of a 10 second runoff. Team A may use its timeout to avoid the 10 second runoff. Clock will start depending on what Team A opts to do.

question_text: A 4/8 @ A-45. Team A's punt is caught at the B-26 and returned to the A-3 where the ball is fumbled. Prone A88 recovers the fumble at the A-5. During the kick, B66 holds.
choice_b: A 1/10 @ A-30 (enforced from the previous spot).
explanation: A 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. Team B fouls during the kick (9-3-3) however, this foul does not qualify for PSK enforcement (10-2-3) as Team B is not in legal possession at the end of the play and will not next snap the ball. By rule 10-2-2-d-4-a fouls that occur during the kick play that are not governed by PSK enforcement are enforced from the previous spot. Clock will start on the snap.

question_text: A 2/10 @ B-30, 2nd Quarter (0:06). QB A12 throws a desperation pass that is caught by A88 for a TD (0:00). The CJ has a flag down for 2 players on Team A with the unbranded green dot on the back middle line of the helmet indicating that the helmets were equipped with Coach to Player communications.
choice_b: Touchdown stands. A, Try @ B-3, No Clock.
explanation: A 2/5 @ B-25, One untimed down. If more than 1 green dot is detected on the field by the same team, the result is a live ball 5 yd equipment violation penalty. (Rule 1-4-11-b Exception) The 2nd Quarter is extended since the penalty is accepted for a live ball foul on the last play of the 2nd Quarter. (Rule 3-2-3-a-1) (2024 National Rules Test #1)

question_text: A 1/10 @ A-34. QB A2 takes the snap from the shotgun formation and throws a backward pass to A31. A31 runs to the A-39 where he fumbles. After the ball hits the ground, B13 kicks the loose ball at the A-40 then recovers it at the A-45 where he is down. The on-field officials rule fumble recovered by Team B at the A-45. Replay has IVE that A31 was down at the A-39.
choice_b: A 2/5 @ A-39, Ready (illegal-kick foul ignored - 10-yard penalty).
explanation: A 2/5 @ A-39, Ready. The foul for illegally kicking a loose ball becomes a dead ball foul since the foul happened after the runner being down and it is a 10-yard penalty, and 5- and 10-yard penalties are ignored. (Rule 12-3-6-i) Another way to look at this is that if properly officiated on the field the runner would have been declared down, and the foul would not have occurred.
